Title: Data Analyst I
Description:
About Us:

At Booking.com, data drives our decisions. Technology is at our core. And innovation is everywhere. But our company is more than datasets, lines of code or A/B tests. We’re the thrill of the first night in a new place. The excitement of the next morning. The friends you encounter. The journeys you take. The sights you see. And the memories you make. Through our products, partners and people, we make it easier for everyone to experience the world.

Role Description:

The Central Tech Analytics Team is the primary data and insights partner for Leadership, Tech, and Product teams. The team is responsible for transforming operational, financial, engineering, and workforce data into clear, actionable insights, providing stakeholders with the dashboards, analysis, and insights necessary for informed decision-making.

As a Data Analyst I you’ll work on analytics tasks such as reporting, visualization and analysis within the scope of the team. You are expected to combine your understanding of the business with the knowledge of data, workflows, methods and tooling to largely autonomously drive analysis and reporting. You will also collaborate with peers in the craft and follow best practices ensuring data quality and stability.

Key Job Responsibilities and Duties:


Owning the design and delivery of parts of Data Analytics solutions based on business requirements, contributing to bringing them from the initial idea-generation phase to implementation with some supervision
Working independently on consuming data, preparing it for analytical use and translating business problems to data stories
Delivering work that informs business decisions for the product / business topic you work on
Leveraging foundational skills in data analysis and data visualization to answer business questions
Incorporating stakeholder requirements when creating intuitive and insightful visualizations that translate data into actionable insights
Connecting and collaborating with stakeholders in operational and project-based roles
Using effective communication to address stakeholders’ needs and presenting data insights in an accessible, business-friendly manner.
Being flexible in adopting existing internal Data Analytics approaches and expanding your technical competencies when a more efficient way presents itself
Networking and proactively connecting with craft peers and peers in related crafts (ie. Data Engineering) beyond the team's scope to ensure successful outcomes.
Ensuring quality of your own work by validating it through peer review.



Qualifications & Skills:


Minimum of 2 year of experience in a data or software adjacent field, working with systems and data infrastructure at scale.
Strongly preferably experienced in working in a technical/engineering scope rather than commercial analytics scope
Proficiency in data analysis languages (e.g., SQL, Python)
Proficiency in building dashboards and using visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI)
Independent in working with ETL/ELT tools and methodologies
Independent in working with relational databases and any flavor of SQL in an analytical context
Ability to explain analyses clearly and present ideas in a structured way
Experienced in working with stakeholders or cross-functional partners, preferably experienced in working in an engineering/technical heavy environment
Ability to work independently with limited support in an ambiguous environment
Ability to identify blockers early and communicate them well



Benefits & Perks - Global Impact, Personal Relevance:

Booking.com’s Total Rewards Philosophy is not only about compensation but also about benefits. We offer a competitive compensation and benefits package, as well unique-to-Booking.com benefits which include:


Annual paid time off and generous paid leave scheme including: parent, grandparent, bereavement, and care leave
Hybrid working including flexible working arrangements, and up to 20 days per year working from abroad (home country) Industry leading product discounts - up to 1400 per year - for yourself, including automatic Genius Level 3 status and Booking.com wallet credit



Inclusion at Booking.com:

Inclusion has been a core part of our company culture since day one. This ongoing journey starts with our very own employees, who represent over 140 nationalities and a wide range of ethnic and social backgrounds, genders and sexual orientations.

Take it from our Chief People Officer, Paulo Pisano: “At Booking.com, the diversity of our people doesn’t just build an outstanding workplace, it also creates a better and more inclusive travel experience for everyone. Inclusion is at the heart of everything we do. It’s a place where you can make your mark and have a real impact in travel and tech.”

We ensure that colleagues with disabilities are provided the adjustments and tools they need to participate in the job application and interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment.

Application Process:


Let’s go places together: How we Hire
This role does not come with relocation assistance.



Booking.com is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We strive to move well beyond traditional equal opportunity and work to create an environment that allows everyone to thrive.

Pre-Employment Screening

If your application is successful, your personal data may be used for a pre-employment screening check by a third party as permitted by applicable law. Depending on the vacancy and applicable law, a pre-employment screening may include employment history, education and other information (such as media information) that may be necessary for determining your qualifications and suitability for the position.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




We are seeking a skilled Data Analyst to join our growing data and analytics team. You will be responsible for developing high-quality reporting and analytics solutions, transforming data into actionable insights, and working closely with both business and technical stakeholders to support data-driven decision-making.




Key Responsibilities




Design, develop, and maintain Power BI reports and dashboards.
Analyse data to identify trends, patterns, and business insights.
Develop and optimise SQL queries for data extraction, validation, and analysis.
Work with Google BigQuery and modern cloud-based data platforms.
Collaborate with Data Engineers to build curated datasets using dbt and modern data architecture practices.
Support data quality, reconciliation, root cause analysis, and reporting governance.
Document reporting solutions and technical processes.
Participate in Agile delivery practices and stakeholder engagement activities.




Essential Skills & Experience




Strong SQL development and data analysis experience.
Hands-on experience with Power BI dashboard and report development.
Experience working with Google BigQuery or a similar cloud data warehouse.
Understanding of star schema and dimensional data modelling.
Experience with dbt and modern data transformation practices.
Knowledge of Medallion Architecture and layered data platforms.
Experience using GitHub, pull requests, and version control best practices.
Experience working with Microsoft SQL Server or similar relational databases.
Experience working within Agile delivery teams.
Strong communication, stakeholder management, analytical, and problem-solving skills.




If you are interested please apply quickly!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. 



With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.





Job Description



Want to use data intelligently to deliver maximum value to clients? It's a challenge that suits you perfectly and energizes you. You're looking for challenges where you can not only advise but also deliver tangible results. You want to make a difference. Are you ready for the next phase? Ready to tackle and solve complex data challenges and further specialize in your data management field? Then we'd love to get to know you.



What you'll be doing at the client's site



As a Data Analyst, you'll play a key role with our clients. You'll advise on their data management, develop data strategies, and translate these into workable solutions to maximize the value of their data. You'll be involved in all phases of the project, from developing data governance frameworks to implementing data quality processes and advising on data analysis. Interested in a project like this?



Moreover:



You'll identify improvement opportunities through thorough analyses of data sources and processes.
You'll design and implement data architecture and models for improved interoperability and scalability.
You'll ensure more efficient and effective systems; you'll advise and support data integration, migration, and transformation projects.
You'll provide insights that support your clients in making the right business decisions.
You'll provide training and support to users to help them manage and use data effectively.


Qualifications



This is you



You're naturally curious, enjoy a bit of fun, but are also deadly serious when it comes to the content. Sound familiar? Great, that's what we appreciate in a colleague. Furthermore, in this role, we'd like you to:



Enjoy your work!
Enjoy collaborating with colleagues and clients on interesting issues. In fact, that's a key part of your job.
You want to develop yourself every day with your positive attitude and drive.
You have strong communication skills and, above all, understand the importance of listening. To the client, colleagues, and of course, yourself!
You don't do the same thing every day; you prefer diverse and complex projects.
You do what you promise. You're accurate and ensure your work is completed on time, without compromising on quality.
You're a true team player. As a part of our team, you can always count on support from your colleagues. Achievements are the result of successful collaboration, and we celebrate them together! We value this culture highly. What you bring

What you bring



Completed HBO (higher professional education) or WO (university education) degree
You speak fluent Dutch and English. If you speak German that's a plus!
At least one year of knowledge and experience with (meta)data management and data quality solutions
You possess relevant data and technical skills, such as data cleaning, data visualization, machine learning, knowledge of big data tools, and data warehousing. Knowledge of programming languages ​​is a plus!
You have a strong vision for data management within organizations.





What you can expect from us



Your development is our priority. Whether it’s through challenging projects, inspiring colleagues or personal growth, we invest in your success, every step of the way.



We offer:



An attractive salary that grows with your experience and performance.
A yearly bonus based on your individual and team results.
Sustainable and practical mobility options, whether you prefer to travel by train, bike, or your own vehicle.
A solid pension plan and additional benefits to support your well-being.
Freedom in where you work, from home, at the client’s office, or in one of our inspiring hubs in Amsterdam or Maastricht, including a home working allowance to make remote work comfortable.
26 days of holiday per year, with the opportunity to earn extra days as your seniority grows.
Plenty of learning opportunities, from mentoring and training programs to international projects that broaden your perspective.
A strong community, where connection matters – we organize regular social and sports activities to celebrate successes together.

Do you have the drive and enthusiasm to help build our great company, and does this position sound like a perfect fit for you? We'd love to get to know you!





Additional Information



Unfortunately, we are unable to provide a work permit for this position.



Sia is an equal opportunity employer. Employment decisions, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or disciplinary actions, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, and business needs.



Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs.

Title: Junior Data & AI Analyst
Description:
About Stokke – Here We Grow

Founded in Norway in 1932, Stokke creates premium products that bring parents and children closer together. With offices across Europe, the U.S., and Asia, we offer a dynamic international environment focused on innovation, growth, and making a positive impact.




The Position

Parent & Child at the Heart

As Junior Data & AI Analyst, you will support Stokke's global Data, Insights & AI team by helping transform data into actionable insights. You will work with Marketing, Ecommerce, and regional teams to analyze performance, build dashboards, and explore how AI can improve reporting and decision-making.

Based in Amsterdam | The Netherlands | Reporting to Head of Data, Insights & AI

7-Month Fixed-Term Contract (with potential to become permanent) | Visa Sponsorship not included




Main Responsibilities

Support reporting and analysis across digital marketing and Ecommerce.
Build and maintain dashboards in Power BI.
Identify trends and insights to support business decisions.
Help improve data quality and reporting processes.
Explore AI tools and automation opportunities to improve efficiency.
Collaborate with stakeholders across Marketing, Ecommerce, and regional teams.




The Ideal Profile

Curious & Analytical: You enjoy working with data and turning insights into action.

Innovative & Eager to Learn: You have an interest in AI, digital analytics, and finding smarter ways of working.




Experience & Skills

Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Analytics, Data Science, Business Analytics, Economics, Statistics, Computer Science, Marketing Analytics, or a related field.
1+ year of experience in digital marketing, eCommerce, business intelligence, or marketing analytics, gained through an internship, placement, in-house, or agency role
Hands-on experience with Power BI, or a related data and BI tool (e.g. Looker Studio, Tableau)
Strong analytical skills — able to work with data, spot patterns, and translate findings into clear recommendations
Confident with Excel for data manipulation and analysis
Fluent in English.

Nice to Have

Experience with AI tools (e.g. vibe coding).
Familiarity with web and campaign analytics tools (e.g. Google Analytics).
Exposure to eCommerce or digital media KPIs and attribution




What We Offer

Hands-on experience in Stokke's global Data, Insights & AI team.
Exposure to analytics, Ecommerce, and AI initiatives.
Mentorship, learning opportunities, and real business impact.
A collaborative and international working environment.




A workplace guided by our values: Parent & Child at the Heart, Agility Speed & Execution, Pioneering Spirit, and Everyone Has a Seat at the Table.

Title: Data Analyst - Finance
Description:
Finance Data Analyst | Nutricia Research

📍 Location: Nutricia Research (Utrecht)

📅 Assignment Period: August 1, 2026 - April 30, 2027

About the Assignment

For Nutricia Research, we are looking for a Finance Data Analyst to join the Research & Innovation (R&I) organization. In this role, you will operate at the intersection of data, business, and IT, contributing to the development, testing, and implementation of data-driven solutions.

You will be responsible for preparing, transforming, and validating data while supporting the deliveryofanalytics solutions that create business value. Working closely with R&I and IT stakeholders, you will help ensure projects are delivered on time and meet quality expectations.




Key Responsibilities

Develop and maintain Python scripts for data transformation and data preparation.
Pilot data processing solutions and support their handover to IT for industrialization.
Support, adapt, and optimize existing applications.
Coordinate data mapping activities with business stakeholders.
Translate business requirements into clear inputs for analytics and dashboard development.
Validate data and analytics solutions from a business perspective.
Monitor project deliverables, timelines, and overall progress.
Collaborate with end-users to test, validate, and refine solutions.
Ensure data quality, consistency, and reliability across datasets.

Profile

Bachelor's or Master's degree in a relevant technical, scientific, or business-related field.
Strong Python programming skills.
Hands-on experience with:
Pandas
NumPy
SciPy
Streamlit
Proven experience in data transformation, data preparation, and data mapping.
Basic understanding of statistical concepts, including mean, standard deviation, and coefficient of variation.
Familiarity with GitHub or other version control systems.
Experience gathering and structuring business requirements.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
Comfortable working with both business and IT teams.
Proactive, pragmatic, and results-oriented mindset.

Nice to Have

Experience within the food, FMCG, chemical, or R&D industries.
Previous involvement in business-facing analytics projects.
Knowledge of dashboarding and reporting processes.

Personal Competencies

Strong analytical skills
Structured and detail-oriented
Independent and proactive
Excellent communication skills
Stakeholder-focused
Solution-oriented and hands-on

Title: Digital Data Analyst
Description:
Marketing Team | HQ Netherlands, Amsterdam | 32–40 hrs/week

The Role

As our Digital Data Analyst, you will be the driving force behind reliable data tracking, meaningful performance insights, and data-driven decision-making across all of our digital marketing channels. You will sit within the Marketing team and work closely with our Performance Specialist, Website Specialist, and Developer to ensure we always have a clear, accurate, and actionable view of what is happening across our digital ecosystem.

This is a high-ownership role. You will not just report numbers, you will shape how the team understands and acts on data.

What You Will Do

Tracking & Data Integrity


Own and maintain our full tracking setup across GA4, GTM, pixels, and events
Ensure accurate and consistent data collection across all digital channels
Validate data quality, troubleshoot discrepancies, and work with developers on implementation


Performance Analysis & Insights


Analyse performance across paid, organic, and website channels
Identify trends, patterns, and anomalies and translate them into clear recommendations
Proactively flag risks and opportunities that impact performance
Leverage AI tools to accelerate insight generation and data exploration


Reporting & Dashboards


Own weekly and monthly performance reporting across key KPIs
Ensure clear visibility on efficiency metrics
Provide structured insights alongside data, not just numbers


Attribution & A/B Testing


Validate performance data across platforms and clarify what is driving results
Design and support A/B tests in collaboration with the Website Specialist and


Developer


Document test learnings and ensure they are applied to future decisions


What We Are Looking For

Must-Haves


3+ years of experience in digital analytics, web analytics, or marketing analytics
Strong hands-on experience with GA4 and Google Tag Manager
Proven ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable business insights
Strong analytical mindset with sharp attention to detail
Experience with A/B testing and experimentation methodologies
Fluent in English
In possession of, or willing to obtain, a Certificate of Good Conduct (VOG)


Nice to Have


Basic knowledge of SQL or HTML/CSS
Experience using AI tools to support data workflows
GA4 or GTM certification
Background in performance marketing, e-commerce, or subscription businesses


Education


Bachelor's degree in Analytics, Data Science, Marketing, Business, Economics, or a related field


What We Offer


A role with real ownership and direct impact on marketing performance
An international, data-driven team of 4–5 digital specialists within a broader Marketing team of ~15
A modern analytics stack and the freedom to improve it
Hybrid working in line with our Amsterdam office policy
Opportunity to grow into a senior analytics, insights, or marketing performance role
Access to Verisure's learning & development framework and company mobility policy


About Verisure

Verisure has been established in the Netherlands since 2018 and employs over 700 people. At Verisure, we believe that people have the right to feel safe and secure. We offer families and small businesses the best protection with a comprehensive security solution. We do this with innovative products that Verisure develops in-house, fully taking care of our customers from alarm placement to maintenance and alarm response. Verisure is the market leader in security services in Europe and Latin America and, with 35 years of insight, experience, and innovation, protects 6.3 million people across 18 countries.

We believe it is important that our company reflects the communities and customers we protect and support worldwide. Diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging (DEIB) are an essential part of who we are, firmly embedded in both our operations and our ESG strategy. Everyone with a Certificate of Good Conduct (VOG), and where required a VVB, is welcome at our company. We offer equal opportunities to all employees to develop their talents and strive for an open, safe, and inclusive working environment.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




As a preferred supplier to one of our biggest Financial Clients, I am seeking for a Data Analyst for a position in Amsterdam, Netherlands. As a Data Analyst, you play a key role in improving and safeguarding the quality of customer data. You combine analytical skills with a product mindset and work closely with stakeholders across the bank.




Responsibilities:

Monitor and improve data quality by defining and applying data quality rules and controls
Analyze data issues (e.g. duplicates, missing or incorrect data) and identify root causes across processes and systems
Drive structural improvements by collaborating with upstream and downstream teams
Support consumers of customer data and help them use data effectively
Translate business needs into clear data requirements and user stories
Increase data awareness and promote data quality practices within the bank




Required knowledge and experience

Have strong analytical skills and take decisions based on data and insights
Are proficient in SQL and experienced in working with large datasets
Have hands-on experience with Power BI for building dashboards and visualizing insights
Are familiar with analytical tools such as Python (pandas) and/or Excel for deeper data analysis
Communicate effectively with both technical and business stakeholders
Translate data into clear, actionable insights that drive impact
Take ownership within your domain and proactively drive improvements
Thrive in multidisciplinary, agile teams
Are fluent in Dutch and English (both verbal and written)




Contract: 12 months+

Rates: Excellent

Location: Amsterdam

Interview: 2 stages, 1 technical + 1 assessment

Title: Data Analyst Excel - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Data Analyst Excel

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Conduct in-depth data analysis using Microsoft Excel to extract actionable insights and support decision-making across teams.
Collaborate with project stakeholders to understand data requirements and translate them into meaningful analyses.
Review, clean, and validate datasets to ensure accuracy, consistency, and integrity.
Develop clear, concise reports and visualizations to communicate findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities in complex datasets, contributing to strategic initiatives.
Document methodologies, results, and recommendations with meticulous attention to detail.
Contribute expertise to enhance data-driven decision-making processes within the customer's team.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Proven experience performing data analysis in a business environment using Microsoft Excel as the primary tool.
Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy in data processing and reporting.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly articulate analytical findings.
Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively across teams to drive business outcomes.
Solid understanding of Excel functions, formulas, and data visualization tools.
Proficiency in identifying data inconsistencies and implementing corrective measures.
Experience working in fast-paced, data-driven organizations.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience supporting cross-functional teams with data insights to inform business strategy.
Background in presenting analytical outcomes to diverse audiences.
Familiarity with additional data analysis tools or business intelligence platforms.

Title: Senior Data Analyst | Digital & Web Analytics | Hoofddorp
Description:
For an internationally operating organization within the logistics sector, we are looking for an experienced Senior Data Analyst. In this role, you will be responsible for analyzing and translating digital and web data into actionable insights, while playing a key role in connecting business, product, and technology.

You will join a highly data-driven environment where innovation and collaboration are central. The team is actively working on evolving the data landscape, with a strong focus on cloud solutions and AI integration. You will be part of a multidisciplinary team and work closely with product and business stakeholders.

Your responsibilities

Analyze web and digital data (e.g. Adobe Analytics)
Translate data into clear insights and actionable recommendations for business and product teams
Act as a bridge between technical teams and non-technical stakeholders
Contribute to data-driven decision-making and optimization initiatives
Support and execute experimentation and A/B testing
Work within and contribute to the transition towards Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
Explore and apply AI solutions within data analytics
Collaborate cross-functionally and act as a trusted data partner

Your profile

Minimum of 5 years of experience as a Data Analyst in a digital or product-driven environment
Strong experience with web analytics tools such as Adobe Analytics
Knowledge of modern data platforms (e.g. GCP, Azure, Databricks)
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
Proactive, independent, and able to take ownership
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to translate insights into business value
Experience with experimentation and optimization is a plus
Affinity with AI and data innovation

What we offer

A competitive salary based on your experience.
Flexible (hybrid) working environment
Visa sponsorship available
Opportunity to work in an international and data-driven organization
High level of ownership and impact
Exposure to modern data and AI developments

Interested?

Are you ready for your next step as a Senior Data Analyst in an international environment? Get in touch with Remy van Kesteren at +31 6 27 15 29 27 or send an email to r.vankesteren@progressive-nl.com.


Let op: vacaturefraude




Helaas komt vacaturefraude steeds vaker voor. We waarschuwen je voor mogelijke misleiding:
* Wij zullen nooit via WhatsApp of in een videogesprek vragen om jouw persoonlijke gegevens (zoals een kopie van je ID, bankgegevens of BSN).
* Twijfel je over de echtheid van een vacature of contactpersoon? Neem dan altijd rechtstreeks contact met ons op via de officiële contactgegevens op onze website.

Important: job fraud

Unfortunately, job fraud is becoming more common. Beware of such scams:
* We will never ask for personal information (such as a copy of your ID, bank details, or social security number) via WhatsApp or during a video call.
* If you're unsure whether a vacancy or contact person is legitimate, please reach out to us directly using the official contact details on our website.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




We bouwen met Just Good People een nextgen data team met leuke mensen met sterke data, AI, human & biz skills. Geen PPT-consultancy, maar datakansen kraken en echt waarde realiseren bij toffe klanten. En veel opleiding.




Zie jij in bergen info – naast getallen – vooral kansen? Wil je die liever omzetten in actie dan in rapporten die in een la verdwijnen?




Wil je maximaal leren door in korte tijd bij meerdere bedrijven in de keuken te kijken? Terwijl wij je helpen ontwikkelen op het snijvlak van data, business en people?




Dan hebben we de uitdaging voor jou.




🟡 Just Good People

Bij JGP hebben we twee simpele regels: We nemen goede mensen aan. En we zorgen goed voor deze mensen. Dat betekent dat we samen optrekken om jou maximaal in je kracht te krijgen op een manier die bij jou past.




🌱 Unieke data filosofie

“As machines get better at being machines, humans have to get better at being more human.”

Deze nieuwe tijd vraagt om een nieuw soort data professional. Bij Just Good People Data helpen we bedrijven versnellen met top notch data professionals met goed ontwikkelde data, business én human skills. En trainen je naar nog hoger niveau. Met aandacht voor:




Volgende levels in Data | AI
“Waarde denken” voor klanten/ gebruikers
Stakeholder management
Communiceren & inspireren




⚡ Wat je gaat doen

Je werkt aan projecten bij mooie bedrijven zoals Bol.com, Defensie, Action, Rabo, NS, AMC, ASML, Essent.




Denk aan:




Het maken van veel impact met data
Stakeholders meenemen in de kracht van data – op een begrijpelijke manier
Verbinden van mensen, processen en tech
Vertalen van data naar heldere inzichten, dashboards en rapportages die helpen bij besluitvorming
Signaleren van trends, kansen en verbeterpunten en stakeholders adviseren op basis van data
Meedenken over slimme oplossingen voor complexe vraagstukken




👩 Wie je bent




Je bent technisch sterk maar ook nieuwsgierig naar de mens achter de vraag
Je vindt het leuk om complexe vraagstukken simpel te maken
Je krijgt energie van verandering en nieuwe omgevingen
Je wilt blijven groeien in data, business én human skills
Je hebt 4+ jaar ervaring als Data Analist
Je hebt een WO-opleiding, bijvoorbeeld in Data Science of Econometrie
Je bent ondernemend en denkt in zowel risico’s als kansen
Je schakelt gemakkelijk tussen strategie, business en data
Je durft vragen te stellen en bestaande werkwijzen uit te dagen
Je bent een echte teamspeler en communicatief sterk
Je spreekt en schrijft vloeiend Nederlands en Engels




🚀 Dit ga je krijgen

Vast dienstverband met goed salaris, bonusregeling en aantrekkelijke voorwaarden
Veel persoonlijke aandacht voor waardegroei, inzetbaarheid en “in je kracht komen“ als data professional. Maar ook data inhoudelijke mentoring.
Opleiding en kruisbestuiving met andere professionals in state of the art AI skills
De kans om in diverse keukens te kijken en veel te leren
Leaseauto of NS-Businesscard (ook privégebruik)
Gezellige borrels, kennissessies en team-events
Collega’s die naast slimme analyses ook goed zijn in slechte grappen




Wij zoeken goede data-mensen, die goede dingen doen. Van die types die je er gewoon heel graag bij wil hebben – net zo goed met mensen als met code!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
For ROSEN Europe BV in Oldenzaal, we are looking for two analytical and ambitious Data Analysts with 0–3 years of experience.




Due to the continued growth of our organization and increasing demand for data-driven insights, we currently have two vacancies available within our Data Analytics team.

This is a broad data-driven role within an organization that is rapidly evolving in the areas of data and digitization. You will have the opportunity to help build this further and make a direct impact on how data is utilized within the organization.




Description




In this role, the focus is on analyzing, structuring, and visualizing data, as well as developing dashboards and reports. You will work with data from various systems and translate it into insights used for better decision-making and tangible improvements within the organization. At the same time, you will be involved in improving processes, systems, and ways of working, ensuring that data is effectively applied in practice.

Depending on your experience and profile, the exact role will be tailored within the organization. This could be more corporate-focused (central data, reporting, and strategic analyses) or positioned in one of our Regions responsible for Europe, Central Asia, and Africa.




ROSEN is an international and innovative organization focused on high-end technology and data-driven solutions for industrial applications. Within ROSEN Europe, a culture of quality, innovation, and continuous development is key. The organization is experiencing strong growth and offers ample opportunities for initiative, development, and long-term career progression.




Your tasks




• Conduct data analyses and develop dashboards and reports (Power BI and similar tools)

• Combine, structure, and analyze data from different systems

• Identify trends, patterns, and deviations and translate these into actionable insights and improvements for the organization

• Actively contribute to improving data quality, reporting, and data-driven decision-making

• Support teams and management with clear analyses and practical insights

• Identify and implement improvements in processes and ways of working, including through automation

• Write and optimize policies and documentation, supported by AI tools such as Copilot

• Contribute to various data and business intelligence projects within the organization




What you bring




• Completed Master’s degree in, for example, Mathematics, Business IT, Data Science, or a comparable field

• Strong analytical skills and a high level of abstract thinking

• Experience or strong interest in data analytics and AI tools

• Good knowledge of Excel and Power BI (SQL or similar tools is a plus)

• Affinity with AI tools such as Copilot and modern data practices

• Excellent communication skills and a team-oriented mindset

• Eager to learn, independent, and proactive

• Able to easily switch between analysis, investigation, and practical process improvements

• Living in the Twente region (or willing to commute within 45 minutes) and fluently speaking Dutch




Please note that, due to the composition and balance of the current team, we are specifically looking for candidates who are fluent in Dutch and already have permanent eligibility to work within the European Union. Please note that an orientation year (zoekjaar) permit does not meet this requirement.




If you do not meet these requirements, we kindly ask you not to apply, as your application cannot be considered for this position.




Offer




• A challenging entry-level position with significant responsibility and impact

• Working within an international, innovative, and fast-growing organization

• Plenty of opportunities for personal development and career growth

• Collaboration with experienced professionals in data, engineering, and business

• A competitive salary and attractive secondary benefits

• A professional yet informal working environment with short communication lines

• A role with long-term prospects and international career opportunities




Company profile




ROSEN is an international and innovative organization focused on high-end technology and data-driven solutions for industrial applications. Within ROSEN Europe, a culture of quality, innovation, and continuous development is key. The organization is experiencing strong growth and offers ample opportunities for initiative, development, and long-term career progression.

Title: Digital Analyst
HTML:
Turn B2C data into sharper journeys, smarter trading and stronger growth.

Lijnden | B2C (Ecommerce, IT, UX & Online Merchandising) | 40 hours | Full-time

At Just Brands, we build menswear brands with character. PME Legend, Cast Iron and Vanguard each have their own voice, their own audience and their own place in the market. In B2C, growth is shaped by thousands of decisions across ecommerce, retail, CRM, digital marketing, site merchandising, site experience, IT, UX and marketplaces.

The challenge is not having more data. The challenge is knowing what matters, what it means and what to do next. That is where ecommerce performance, customer behaviour, trading insights and business intelligence come together.

But what really sets us apart is how we work together.

We’re team first. No ego, no unnecessary layers, no endless talking, no corporate theatre. We back each other, speak up, take ownership and keep pushing for better. We work hard, stay sharp and make sure there’s room to enjoy the ride too.

That’s where you jump onboard.

Why this role matters

This is where ecommerce analytics, B2C performance and digital decision-making become commercial impact. As our Ecommerce Performance Analyst, you support data-driven decision-making across all B2C channels: Retail, E-Commerce, Digital Marketing & CRM, Site Merchandising, Site Experience, IT & UX and Marketplaces.

You collect, analyse and interpret data to generate actionable insights that improve performance, customer experience and operational efficiency. You help teams understand what is happening, why it is happening and which actions will create better results. You are not here to build dashboards that quietly gather dust.

You are here to turn B2C data into decisions that teams trust, use and act on.

What you’ll do

  • Support data-driven decision-making across all B2C channels, including Retail, E-Commerce, Digital Marketing & CRM, Site Merchandising, Site Experience, IT & UX and marketplaces;
  • Collect, analyze and interpret data to generate actionable insights that improve performance, customer experience and operational efficiency;
  • Build, maintain and improve dashboards for ecommerce performance, customer behavior, campaign performance, trading, funnel performance and channel results;
  • Design forecasting tools to anticipate customer behavior, sales trends, demand patterns and commercial opportunities;
  • Provide performance analytics to senior stakeholders, supporting decision-making and P&L accountability across online channels;
  • Lead department-wide projects that improve data collection, data integration and financial insight generation across B2C operations;
  • Analyze ecommerce KPIs such as revenue, conversion rate, traffic quality, average order value, margin, retention, customer lifetime value, funnel drop-off and product/category performance;
  • Translate data into clear recommendations for Ecommerce, CRM, Digital Marketing, Site Merchandising, Retail, Marketplaces, UX and IT;
  • Monitor digital marketing and CRM performance across paid media, email, automation flows, campaign traffic and customer segments;
  • Support ecommerce trading rhythms with weekly and monthly insights on what is selling, what is slowing down and where commercial opportunities sit;
  • Analyze customer journeys and identify friction points across web shops, apps, landing pages, checkout, content and onsite experience;
  • Support A/B testing and experimentation, including test setup, measurement, analysis and learnings;
  • Improve data quality, tracking consistency, reporting definitions and governance across B2C;
  • Help teams understand the story behind the numbers by presenting insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

What success looks like in your first year

  • B2C teams use your dashboards and insights in weekly and monthly decision rhythms;
  • Ecommerce, CRM, marketing, site experience and marketplace teams make better decisions based on trusted data;
  • You identify conversion, trading or customer journey opportunities that lead to measurable business improvements;
  • Forecasting becomes sharper and helps teams anticipate sales trends, customer behavior and commercial risks;
  • Data collection and integration improve, reducing noise, manual work and reporting confusion;
  • Senior stakeholders receive clearer performance analytics that support P&L ownership and better prioritization;
  • A/B tests and experiments create learnings that change how we build campaigns, pages and customer journeys.

What makes this role exciting

  • You work across the full B2C ecosystem: ecommerce, retail, CRM, digital marketing, marketplaces, site experience, UX and IT;
  • You sit close to revenue, conversion, customer behaviour, trading performance and P&L decisions;
  • You influence both commercial performance and customer experience for three strong menswear brands;
  • You work on dashboards, forecasting, experimentation and strategic B2C projects;
  • You help shape how a growing international business becomes more data-driven;
  • You turn scattered data into clear direction, which is where the magic gets wonderfully operational.

You’ll probably love this role if you…

  • enjoy turning messy data into clear direction and practical next steps;
  • like investigating why performance changes, not just reporting that it changed;
  • understand that good analytics is part numbers, part business sense and part storytelling;
  • get energy from working with ecommerce, marketing, CRM, UX, IT and marketplace teams;
  • care about both performance and customer experience;
  • prefer impact over perfection and want your work to change decisions.

What you bring

  • Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Business Analytics, Statistics, Economics, Marketing, Ecommerce or a related field;
  • 5+ years of experience in ecommerce analytics, digital analytics, business intelligence, performance analysis or B2C data analysis;
  • Strong knowledge of ecommerce KPIs: conversion rate, revenue, AOV, traffic quality, margin, retention, CLV, funnel drop-off and product/category performance;
  • Strong experience with GA4, SQL, Excel and Power BI or Tableau;
  • Experience building dashboards, reporting structures and forecasting models;
  • Experience with digital marketing analytics, CRM analytics, campaign performance and customer journey analysis;
  • Experience with A/B testing, experimentation, CRO or UX analytics is a strong plus;
  • Strong analytical skills and the ability to connect commercial, financial and customer data;
  • Comfortable working with cross-functional teams in a fast-moving retail or ecommerce environment;
  • Strong communication skills: you can explain insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders;
  • Fluent English; Dutch or German is a plus.

What Just Brands is really like

We’re team first. In B2C, that means we challenge assumptions with data, keep conversations sharp and turn insight into action. No ego. Just practical collaboration, honest decisions and a team that likes improving what actually matters.

You’ll Join a Company Where

  • People help each other out;
  • Ideas and people matter more than job titles;
  • Hard work matters, and so does enjoying the ride;
  • If something can be better, we improve it.

What you get

  • Salary depending on experience and background;
  • End-of-year bonus equal to one gross monthly salary;
  • Healthy and varied lunch every day;
  • Exclusive access to our own Gym, including classes such as boxing, yoga and padel;
  • Parties and drinks that will be talked about for years to come;
  • Staff discount on our clothing.

Ready to turn ecommerce performance data into decisions that change results?

Still reading? Good sign.

If you want to work close to ecommerce, CRM, marketing, marketplaces, customer behaviour and real commercial decisions, this could be your next step.

Ready to apply?

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Wil jij werken met grote hoeveelheden data én analyses ontwikkelen die direct bijdragen aan de groei van Pricewise? Dan is dit jouw kans. Binnen Mavriq en Pricewise hebben we ambitieuze groeiplannen, waarbij data een centrale rol speelt.




Als Data Analist werk je op het snijvlak van business en BI. Het BI-team legt de basis voor een diepgaande data-infrastructuur. Jij bouwt hierop voort door onze enorme hoeveelheid data inzichtelijk te maken, te analyseren en te vertalen naar modellen en inzichten die invloed hebben op hoe we werken en de beslissingen die we nemen.




We zoeken iemand die graag meebouwt aan de toekomst. Iemand die nieuwsgierig is, wil experimenteren, en bestaande werkwijzen durft uit te dagen en met behulp van onder andere AI-toepassingen steeds meer waarde uit data weet te halen.




Wat doe je als Data Analist bij Pricewise?

Als Data Analist vertaal je ruwe en complexe data naar heldere inzichten en oplossingen. Je werkt op het snijvlak van data, BI en business en helpt collega's om datagedreven beslissingen te nemen.




Dit vind je leuk om te doen

Ruwe en complexe data analyseren en vertalen naar inzichten en concrete oplossingen voor de business.
Kwantitatieve en voorspellende modellen ontwikkelen die bijdragen aan betere besluitvorming.
Dashboards en rapportages ontwikkelen waarmee collega's eenvoudig kunnen sturen op data.
Dataprocessen optimaliseren en bijdragen aan betrouwbare, goed gestructureerde en breed inzetbare data binnen de organisatie.
Nieuwe toepassingen ontdekken op het gebied van AI en analytics om nog meer waarde uit data te halen.




Dit zoek jij in een baan

Een rijke dataomgeving waarin jouw analyses direct bijdragen aan de groei van Pricewise én miljoenen consumenten helpen betere keuzes te maken.
Een bruto maandsalaris van €4.000 tot €5.500 (exclusief vakantiegeld), passend bij jouw ervaring.
Een aantrekkelijke bonusregeling die aansluit bij de impact die je maakt.
Reiskostenvergoeding, een premievrij pensioen en aantrekkelijke extra's, zoals een sportschoolvergoeding.
Veel aandacht voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling, met volop mogelijkheden om te leren van internationale collega's met expertise in data, analytics en digital commerce.
De mogelijkheid om mee te bouwen aan een snelgroeiende internationale organisatie binnen een dynamisch e-commercelandschap.
Een ondernemende cultuur met korte lijnen, veel verantwoordelijkheid en ruimte om te experimenteren en te innoveren.
Gezellige teamuitjes, borrels en een dagelijks verzorgde gezamenlijke lunch.
Een prettige balans tussen samenwerken op kantoor en geconcentreerd werken vanuit huis.




Dit breng jij mee

Een afgeronde hbo- of wo-opleiding, in Data Analytics, Econometrie of een vergelijkbare richting.
Minimaal twee jaar ervaring in een datarol. Ervaring binnen e-commerce of digital marketing is een pré.
Je bent analytisch, nieuwsgierig en proactief. Je ziet kansen, neemt initiatief en werkt graag samen met collega's uit verschillende disciplines.
Sterke vaardigheden in Python, SQL en Excel. Je schrijft schone, production-ready code en bent goed thuis in data libraries.
Ervaring met (statistische) analyses en het ontwikkelen van voorspellende of kwantitatieve modellen.
Ervaring met BI-tools, zoals Looker, en het ontwikkelen van dashboards en rapportages.
Kennis van data-infrastructuur. Je begrijpt hoe datapipelines en datamodellen werken, kunt werken met tools als BigQuery en schakelt gemakkelijk met data engineers.
Affiniteit met (generatieve) AI. Je hebt ervaring met LLM's en AI-tools of bent enthousiast om je hierin verder te ontwikkelen.




Bedrijfsbeschrijving

Pricewise is een van de grootste vergelijkingssites op het gebied van energie, verzekeringen, internet, tv, leningen en meer. Vanuit ons hoofdkantoor in Amsterdam bieden wij consumenten de mogelijkheid om gemakkelijk over te stappen naar de beste aanbieding in de markt. Via ons vergelijkingsplatform bespaarden al miljoenen mensen honderden euro’s per jaar.

Sinds 2024 is Pricewise onderdeel van Mavriq: een snelgroeiende internationale groep met meer dan vijftien vergelijkingsplatformen in Nederland, Spanje, Italië, Frankrijk, Duitsland en Mexico. Vanuit onze leidende positie bouwen we aan de toekomst van online vergelijken, waarin data een essentiële rol speelt.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Rotterdam (three days onsite).

Salary €65,000 to €80,000 plus benefits.




This is a great opportunity to step into a business-critical data analyst role where your insights will directly influence priorities, decision-making and growth. You will join at a moment of change, with the chance to shape how data is used across the organisation and take real ownership of analytical projects.




The Company

They are a growing organisation operating in a dynamic market environment, where data plays an increasingly important role in strategy and performance. The business values clear communication, initiative and practical insight over theory alone. With a lean data setup, they offer a close-knit environment where your work is visible and impactful. Collaboration with senior stakeholders is a core part of how they operate.




The Role

You will be responsible for delivering actionable insights and managing BI-focused data initiatives across the business.
You will work closely with stakeholders to understand needs, set priorities and translate questions into analysis.
You will take ownership of both larger analytical initiatives and smaller ad hoc projects.
You will support the evolution of reporting and dashboards using SQL-based data models and Power BI.
You will help reassess team focus and objectives following recent organisational changes.

Your Skills and Experience

Strong commercial experience in SQL for data analysis and data modelling.
Hands-on experience building reports and dashboards in Power BI.
Confidence working independently and managing analytical projects end to end.
Proven ability to engage with stakeholders and communicate insights clearly.
Exposure to Python or advanced analytics is beneficial but not essential.




What They Offer

A competitive salary between €65,000 and €80,000 with flexibility based on experience.
Recently updated salary bands with clear room for growth.
Hybrid working with a strong in-office collaboration culture in Rotterdam.
A high-impact role with autonomy, ownership and visibility at senior level.




How to Apply

If you are a Data Analyst looking for a role where you can truly influence how data is used, apply now to learn more.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. 



With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.





Job Description



Want to use data intelligently to deliver maximum value to clients? It's a challenge that suits you perfectly and energizes you. You're looking for challenges where you can not only advise but also deliver tangible results. You want to make a difference. Are you ready for the next phase? Ready to tackle and solve complex data challenges and further specialize in your data management field? Then we'd love to get to know you.



What you'll be doing at the client's site



As a Data Analyst, you'll play a key role with our clients. You'll advise on their data management, develop data strategies, and translate these into workable solutions to maximize the value of their data. You'll be involved in all phases of the project, from developing data governance frameworks to implementing data quality processes and advising on data analysis. Interested in a project like this?



Moreover:



You'll identify improvement opportunities through thorough analyses of data sources and processes.
You'll design and implement data architecture and models for improved interoperability and scalability.
You'll ensure more efficient and effective systems; you'll advise and support data integration, migration, and transformation projects.
You'll provide insights that support your clients in making the right business decisions.
You'll provide training and support to users to help them manage and use data effectively.


Qualifications



This is you



You're naturally curious, enjoy a bit of fun, but are also deadly serious when it comes to the content. Sound familiar? Great, that's what we appreciate in a colleague. Furthermore, in this role, we'd like you to:



Enjoy your work!
Enjoy collaborating with colleagues and clients on interesting issues. In fact, that's a key part of your job.
You want to develop yourself every day with your positive attitude and drive.
You have strong communication skills and, above all, understand the importance of listening. To the client, colleagues, and of course, yourself!
You don't do the same thing every day; you prefer diverse and complex projects.
You do what you promise. You're accurate and ensure your work is completed on time, without compromising on quality.
You're a true team player. As a part of our team, you can always count on support from your colleagues. Achievements are the result of successful collaboration, and we celebrate them together! We value this culture highly. What you bring

What you bring



Completed HBO (higher professional education) or WO (university education) degree
You speak fluent Dutch and English. If you speak German that's a plus.
At least one year of knowledge and experience with (meta)data management and data quality solutions
You possess relevant data and technical skills, such as data cleaning, data visualization, machine learning, knowledge of big data tools, and data warehousing. Knowledge of programming languages ​​is a plus!
You have a strong vision for data management within organizations.





What you can expect from us



Your development is our priority. Whether it’s through challenging projects, inspiring colleagues or personal growth, we invest in your success, every step of the way.



We offer:



An attractive salary that grows with your experience and performance.
A yearly bonus based on your individual and team results.
Sustainable and practical mobility options, whether you prefer to travel by train, bike, or your own vehicle.
A solid pension plan and additional benefits to support your well-being.
Freedom in where you work, from home, at the client’s office, or in one of our inspiring hubs in Amsterdam or Maastricht, including a home working allowance to make remote work comfortable.
26 days of holiday per year, with the opportunity to earn extra days as your seniority grows.
Plenty of learning opportunities, from mentoring and training programs to international projects that broaden your perspective.
A strong community, where connection matters – we organize regular social and sports activities to celebrate successes together.

Do you have the drive and enthusiasm to help build our great company, and does this position sound like a perfect fit for you? We'd love to get to know you!





Additional Information

Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. 

With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.



Job Description

Want to use data intelligently to deliver maximum value to clients? It's a challenge that suits you perfectly and energizes you. You're looking for challenges where you can not only advise but also deliver tangible results. You want to make a difference. Are you ready for the next phase? Ready to tackle and solve complex data challenges and further specialize in your data management field? Then we'd love to get to know you.

What you'll be doing at the client's site

As a Data Analyst, you'll play a key role with our clients. You'll advise on their data management, develop data strategies, and translate these into workable solutions to maximize the value of their data. You'll be involved in all phases of the project, from developing data governance frameworks to implementing data quality processes and advising on data analysis. Interested in a project like this?

Moreover:

  • You'll identify improvement opportunities through thorough analyses of data sources and processes.
  • You'll design and implement data architecture and models for improved interoperability and scalability.
  • You'll ensure more efficient and effective systems; you'll advise and support data integration, migration, and transformation projects.
  • You'll provide insights that support your clients in making the right business decisions.
  • You'll provide training and support to users to help them manage and use data effectively.

Qualifications

This is you

You're naturally curious, enjoy a bit of fun, but are also deadly serious when it comes to the content. Sound familiar? Great, that's what we appreciate in a colleague. Furthermore, in this role, we'd like you to:

  • Enjoy your work!
  • Enjoy collaborating with colleagues and clients on interesting issues. In fact, that's a key part of your job.
  • You want to develop yourself every day with your positive attitude and drive.
  • You have strong communication skills and, above all, understand the importance of listening. To the client, colleagues, and of course, yourself!
  • You don't do the same thing every day; you prefer diverse and complex projects.
  • You do what you promise. You're accurate and ensure your work is completed on time, without compromising on quality.
  • You're a true team player. As a part of our team, you can always count on support from your colleagues. Achievements are the result of successful collaboration, and we celebrate them together! We value this culture highly. What you bring

What you bring

  • Completed HBO (higher professional education) or WO (university education) degree
  • You speak fluent Dutch and English. If you speak German that's a plus.
  • At least one year of knowledge and experience with (meta)data management and data quality solutions
  • You possess relevant data and technical skills, such as data cleaning, data visualization, machine learning, knowledge of big data tools, and data warehousing. Knowledge of programming languages ​​is a plus!
  • You have a strong vision for data management within organizations.



What you can expect from us

Your development is our priority. Whether it’s through challenging projects, inspiring colleagues or personal growth, we invest in your success, every step of the way.

We offer:

  • An attractive salary that grows with your experience and performance.
  • A yearly bonus based on your individual and team results.

  • A solid pension plan and additional benefits to support your well-being.
  • Freedom in where you work, from home, at the client’s office, or in one of our inspiring hubs in Amsterdam or Maastricht, including a home working allowance to make remote work comfortable.
  • 26 days of holiday per year, with the opportunity to earn extra days as your seniority grows.
  • Plenty of learning opportunities, from mentoring and training programs to international projects that broaden your perspective.
  • A strong community, where connection matters – we organize regular social and sports activities to celebrate successes together.

Title: Junior Data Analyst Retail
Description:
Note: This job posting is only open to native level or near-native level Dutch speakers. If you are not proficient in Dutch, please have a look at our other job postings instead!)

Your role in our team

As a Junior Data Analyst Retail, you support client teams with analysis, dashboards, KPI insights and data quality checks. You work with data from retail processes such as sales, customer behavior, inventory, promotions, e-commerce or supply chain and help make insights understandable for business stakeholders. At CGI, you grow by working in projects with experienced data consultants, project managers and technical specialists.

How You Strengthen The Team


Analyze datasets and translate findings into clear insights and visualizations.
Build and maintain dashboards and reports in Power BI, Tableau or comparable tooling.
Perform basic data quality checks and document definitions, assumptions and issues.
Support requirements, user stories, KPI definitions and stakeholder questions.
Work with senior consultants, data engineers, business analysts and client teams.
Contribute to presentations, workshops and analyses for retail and consumer-services clients.


What You Bring


1-2 years of experience, internship or traineeship in data analysis, BI, business analysis or analytics.
Solid foundation in SQL, Excel and at least one BI tool such as Power BI, Tableau or Looker.
Affinity with retail, e-commerce, supply chain, customer analytics or commercial analysis.
Able to explain data outcomes clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
Accurate, eager to learn, communicative and comfortable with client contact.
Professional Dutch and English, or demonstrably quick to develop to that level.


We would also value


Microsoft Power BI Data Analyst Associate (PL-300) or demonstrable Power BI experience.
Microsoft Azure Data Fundamentals (DP-900), Azure AI Fundamentals (AI-900) or comparable fundamentals.
Basic Python or R for data cleaning, analysis or automation.
Experience with KPIs such as revenue, margin, conversion, inventory, customer segments or promotion effectiveness.
Experience with Jira, Azure DevOps, Confluence or working in an agile team.


Where will you work?

You will join CGI’s retail team and work on assignments where data, business questions and technology come together. You will have room to learn, while contributing from the start to analyses, dashboards and insights that help clients make better decisions.

What We Offer


A learning environment where you can grow quickly towards data consulting in retail.
Guidance from experienced consultants and access to CGI’s broader knowledge network.
Varied assignments where data directly influences commercial and operational decisions.
Room to obtain certifications and further develop your technical and consulting skills.
A team culture centered on collaboration, knowledge sharing and ownership.


Why CGI?

CGI is one of the world's largest IT consultancy firms. Our people keep the Netherlands running. Government agencies, banks, (aero-) space, infrastructure organizations and energy and manufacturing companies are clients of CGI. We work on interesting projects that touch the daily lives of millions of people. We are present regionally, augmented with global domain knowledge, our ecosystem, and colleagues spread all over the world. We are proud of the impactful projects that we, together with our colleagues, make a success.

At CGI, we place great value on diversity and inclusion, as this not only promotes collaboration but often leads to better results. We look forward to your contribution to our team!


Work in small self-managing teams consisting of experts in your field
Take control of your own career with the space to continue developing yourself
You work with the latest technologies
An excellent salary and attractive secondary employment conditions
Immediate permanent contract
Part-time possibilities
And the opportunity to become a CGI shareholder


Join our team of experts and apply now!

We will contact you as soon as possible. Questions about the position? Ask them to Floris Diemel, Director Consulting Services via floris.diemel@cgi.com

For more information about the application procedure, please contact recruiter Jana Kreckova via jana.kreckova@cgi.com

We werken niet samen met externe recruitment bureau’s. Acquisitie wordt daarom niet op prijs gesteld.

Your future duties and responsibilities

Required Qualifications To Be Successful In This Role

Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.

Life at CGI is rooted in ownership, teamwork, respect and belonging. Here, you’ll reach your full potential because…

You are invited to be an owner from day 1 as we work together to bring our Dream to life. That’s why we call ourselves CGI Partners rather than employees. We benefit from our collective success and actively shape our company’s strategy and direction.

Your work creates value. You’ll develop innovative solutions and build relationships with teammates and clients while accessing global capabilities to scale your ideas, embrace new opportunities, and benefit from expansive industry and technology expertise.

You’ll shape your career by joining a company built to grow and last. You’ll be supported by leaders who care about your health and well-being and provide you with opportunities to deepen your skills and broaden your horizons.

Come join our team—one of the largest IT and business consulting services firms in the world.

Title: Data Analyst / Analytics Engineer
Description:
About Insify
We are a high-growth scale-up backed by €43M in total funding from world-class investors like Accel, Munich Re Ventures, and Formula 1 champion Nico Rosberg. While we started our journey five years ago in the Netherlands, we have since expanded into France and Germany, and we’re just getting started. Our mission is to dominate the European market and become a global leader in AI-powered SME insurance.

We aren't just building a company; we’re leading a revolution. Our culture is intense, high-stakes, and mission-driven. We hire smart, ambitious "owners" who possess a relentless drive to win. This is an environment for those who thrive on challenges, value intellectual honesty, and share our mindset of becoming a global category leader.

Our founding team consists of seasoned entrepreneurs who have previously built iconic businesses like HelloFresh, Bloomon, and SumUp. We combine that deep fintech and e-commerce expertise with cutting-edge AI to stay ahead of the curve.

We're looking for a highly motivated Analytics Engineer who will work closely with Product and Insurance departments to bring instant, tailored and affordable financial safety products to entrepreneurs.

What you'll be up to (some examples):


Define and calculate KPIs for product and feature launches to track our trajectory toward 10x simplicity
Identify opportunities and provide data-driven recommendations that influence our roadmap
Answer analytical questions and analyse A/B tests to ensure we draw the right conclusions from every experiment
Create and maintain interactive dashboards (using Databricks and QuickSight) for real-time metrics monitoring
Contribute to the data transformation code base (using dbt and Git) to ensure our data infrastructure is as modern as our software
Own outcomes within your scope — from problem definition to delivery
Operate in ambiguity and make decisions without perfect information
Collaborate closely with colleagues across functions to bridge the gap between "the floor" and "the lab."
Improve how we work by spotting inefficiencies in our data workflows and proposing better ways
Balance speed and quality in a fast-moving environment




This role is a strong fit if you:


Have excellent SQL skills: You can calculate any metric in a readable and efficient way
Are proficient with dashboarding and code tools: You have experience with tools like Databricks, QuickSight, and Git to organise your work
Understand the "Why": You have a strong grasp of Statistics and A/B testing to draw meaningful conclusions
Possess a growth mindset: You have a passion for continuous learning and seeking feedback to drive personal growth
Take ownership naturally: You don’t wait for perfect instructions and thrive in the "grey area"
Can turn messy problems into clear, actionable plans: You are hyper-organised and process-obsessed




This role is not for you if:


You are looking for a strict 40-hour work week
You need fully defined scopes and stable priorities to perform well
You prefer staying high-level and avoiding hands-on execution
You expect long ramp-up periods before taking ownership




We offer:


Competitive compensation that reflects responsibility and impact
Trust and autonomy: you’ll have real ownership and room to make decisions Space to grow and shape your role as Insify evolves
A steep learning curve, with support from experienced teammates
The opportunity to share in what we’re building, with equity for eligible roles once there’s mutual conviction on fit and impact
A welcoming, central Amsterdam HQ where we work closely together: shared lunches, dogs in the office, whiteboards, and fun end-of-week drinks
An international, ambitious team that enjoys building meaningful things together

Description:
📊 Ben jij een Data Analist die verder kijkt dan dashboards?




Bij Ebicus zoeken we een Data Analist die organisaties helpt betere beslissingen te nemen op basis van data.




Van het ondersteunen van oplossingen die financiële criminaliteit helpen bestrijden bij banken tot het creëren van inzichten voor overheidsorganisaties en non-profits: jouw analyses maken impact.

Je werkt onder andere voor organisaties zoals Rabobank, ING en de Raad voor de rechtspraak.

✔ Data analyseren en vertalen naar concrete inzichten

✔ Dashboards en visualisaties bouwen in Power BI, Tableau of vergelijkbare tools

✔ Stakeholders adviseren op basis van data

✔ Kansen ontdekken die organisaties helpen groeien en verbeteren




Wat wij zoeken:

🔹 Minimaal 4 jaar ervaring als Data Analist

🔹 Ervaring met SQL

🔹 Ervaring met Power BI, Tableau, Qlik, Python of R

🔹 Sterke communicatieve vaardigheden

🎯 Maandelijkse inzetbonus van gemiddeld €600 – €1.000

Title: Data-analist
Description:
Your mission




You’ll join a team that is in the process of further building the organization’s data capabilities. Together with your team of 4, you will work on developing and improving dashboards and data solutions used across different departments, such as logistics, finance, and HR.




In this role, you won’t just focus on building dashboards in Power BI. A big part of your work will involve understanding and preparing data. You’ll investigate where data comes from, how it is structured, and how it can be transformed into insights the business can actually use. To do this, you’ll work closely with stakeholders and the IT department to ensure the right data is available and usable.




Why you should consider this opportunity

You work at the intersection of business and data, with direct impact on domains such as logistics, finance, and HR
Plenty of room to contribute ideas on how data can be used more effectively across the organization
Close interaction with stakeholders, allowing you to see how your work directly supports the business
An environment where the data function is still developing and where you can contribute to its growth




Organization

You will join an international organization within the logistics sector. The company combines years of experience in transportation and logistics services with an increasing focus on data and digitalization.




This is what is expected from you

Experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar role (minimum 1.5 years)
Strong knowledge of SQL and Power BI
Experience with cloud environments (they are on Azure)
Strong communication skills and the ability to work with different stakeholders
A proactive mindset and willingness to think along with the business




In return for your hard work, you will receive

Salary between €3,470 and €4,500 based on full-time employment (40 hours)
25 vacation days
Solid pension scheme
Travel expense reimbursement
Hybrid working with three days per week in the office
Tailored learning and development budget for your professional growth

Title: Data Analyst/ Researcher
Description:
Data Analyst / Researcher – Robidus

Ben jij een analytische Data Analyst/Researcher die met data en technologie impact wil maken? Bij Robidus werk je aan vraagstukken rondom gezondheid, preventie en duurzame inzetbaarheid.

Je analyseert gezondheids- en gedragsdata, ontwikkelt dashboards (o.a. Power BI) en helpt bij het verbeteren van voorspellende modellen. Je vertaalt complexe data naar heldere inzichten voor stakeholders en signaleert kansen om meer waarde uit data en AI te halen.

Je werkt in het Data & AI team en in de beginfase nauw samen met niped, een health-tech organisatie binnen de Robidus Groep.




Wat breng je mee?

WO werk- en denkniveau
3–5+ jaar ervaring in data analyse / research
Sterk in SQL en Python (R is een pré)
Ervaring met BI tooling (bijv. Power BI)
Sterk analytisch en communicatief




Wat krijg je?

Betekenisvol werk met maatschappelijke impact
Hybride werken (Breukelen / Zaandam)
Salaris tussen €4300 en €6200 bruto per maand

HTML:
Data Analyst / Researcher – Robidus

Ben jij een analytische Data Analyst/Researcher die met data en technologie impact wil maken? Bij Robidus werk je aan vraagstukken rondom gezondheid, preventie en duurzame inzetbaarheid.

Je analyseert gezondheids- en gedragsdata, ontwikkelt dashboards (o.a. Power BI) en helpt bij het verbeteren van voorspellende modellen. Je vertaalt complexe data naar heldere inzichten voor stakeholders en signaleert kansen om meer waarde uit data en AI te halen.

Je werkt in het Data & AI team en in de beginfase nauw samen met niped, een health-tech organisatie binnen de Robidus Groep.


Wat breng je mee?

  • WO werk- en denkniveau
  • 3–5+ jaar ervaring in data analyse / research
  • Sterk in SQL en Python (R is een pré)
  • Ervaring met BI tooling (bijv. Power BI)
  • Sterk analytisch en communicatief


Wat krijg je?

  • Betekenisvol werk met maatschappelijke impact
  • Hybride werken (Breukelen / Zaandam)
  • Salaris tussen €4300 en €6200 bruto per maand
  • 30 vakantiedagen
  • Opleidingsbudget en RobiSchool

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Ben jij een data professional die verder kijkt dan alleen de data? Iemand die niet alleen analyses levert, maar ook snapt wat de business ermee wilt? Dan zoeken wij jou.




Over Opcharge

Opcharge is een snelgroeiende speler binnen de energietransitie en richt zich op slimme laadoplossingen voor gemeenten en bedrijven. Als jonge en ondernemende organisatie opereert Opcharge zonder fossiele achtergrond en bouwt het actief aan een toekomst met duurzame mobiliteit. De ambitie is helder: 50.000 laadpunten realiseren in 2030.

De organisatie bevindt zich in een sterke groeifase, wat zorgt voor een dynamische omgeving waarin processen continu in ontwikkeling zijn en nieuwe kansen zich snel aandienen. Die energie zie je ook terug in de werksfeer, ondernemend en betrokken. Eind 2026 verhuizen we naar ons nieuwe kantoor in hartje Rotterdam, klaar voor de volgende fase.




De functie

Opcharge groeit hard en data speelt daarin een centrale rol. Als Business Data Analyst ben je onderdeel van de Data & IT afdeling en werk je dagelijks samen met business en engineering. Je zorgt dat data uit ons warehouse niet blijft liggen, maar wordt omgezet naar inzichten die er echt toe doen, van operationele verbeteringen tot strategische keuzes op MT-niveau.




Je verantwoordelijkheden bestaan uit:

Je analyseert data om trends, patronen en kansen te identificeren
Je werkt effectief samen met business- en technische teams om inzichten te vertalen naar concrete acties
Je ontwikkelt en verbetert rapportages en dashboards die data toegankelijk maken
Je bouwt voorspellende modellen die bijdragen aan strategische keuzes rondom de uitbreiding van ons laadpalenportfolio
Je ontwerpt en bouwt robuuste datastructuren in Snowflake







Wat breng jij mee?

Je hebt 1 tot 5 jaar werkervaring binnen het datadomein
Je werkt projectmatig en neemt eigenaarschap over voortgang en resultaat
Je bent vaardig in SQL en hebt ervaring met BI-tools zoals Power BI. Kennis van Python is een pré
Je bent sterk in presenteren en weet de businessbehoefte achter data te achterhalen door de juiste vragen te stellen
Je reflecteert op je werk en gebruikt feedback om je analyses en aanpak continu te verbeteren




Arbeidsvoorwaarden

Wat bieden wij?

Salaris tussen €4.200 en €5.000 bruto per maand afhankelijk van jouw ervaring
Een functie voor 32 tot 40 uur per week
Flexibel werken mogelijk, met 2 dagen per week op kantoor
Werken in een snelgroeiende, ondernemende organisatie met echte impact
Veel ruimte voor eigen initiatief en procesverbetering
Een informele en betrokken werksfeer







Herken jij jezelf hierin? Stuur je sollicitatie naar Opcharge en bouw mee aan de duurzame laadinfrastructuur van morgen.
