HTML:
Institutionen för data- och systemvetenskap. Med över 200 anställda och 4 500 studenter är Institutionen för data- och systemvetenskap (DSV) en stark och dynamisk forsknings- och utbildningsmiljö. Ämnet data- och systemvetenskap rör sig mellan teknik, humaniora, samhällsvetenskap och beteendevetenskap, med stor relevans för hur vi ska leva i dag och i framtiden. Vår forskning handlar om hur IT ska utformas för att vara till nytta för människor, organisationer och hela samhällen.

Mer information om oss finns på: Institutionen för data- och systemvetenskap.

Projektbeskrivning

Forskningsprojektet ”Sektorkoppling och verksamhetsforskning för miljömässig hållbarhet (SCORES): Effekter på klimatutsläpp, energipriser och ekonomisk nytta som en följd av Nordens energiomställning” kommer att ta itu med energiomställningen i Sverige innebär ett avsteg från fossila bränslen samt en ökad elektrifiering av industrin, där kopplingen mellan elproduktion och konsumtion (sektorkoppling) blir av större betydelse när icke-reglerbar förnybar energi (VRE) som vind- och solkraft får en större proportion. Då VRE är intermittent och platsspecifik krävs ett mer flexibelt energisystem, med reglerbar produktion som komplement till exempel i form av vattenkraft. Här kan flexibla producenter ha större förmåga för att påverka elpriser, vilket kräver mekanismer för att mildra välfärdsförluster. På samma sätt kommer överföringsplaneringen att behöva anpassas för att dämpa producenternas marknadsstyrka. I projektet ska spelteoretiska modeller tillämpas för att identifiera hur producenternas marknadsmakt påverkas av sektorskoppling och ta fram metoder att förutse ekonomiska snedvridningar på elmarknaden. Dessa insikter kommer att stödja policyutveckling för en välfärdshöjande övergång till ett hållbart energisystem.

Arbetsuppgifter

En tvärvetenskaplig ansats som omfattar ekonomiska, tekniska och samhällsvetenskapliga perspektiv utgör ramen för analys. En forskningsassistent i projektet kommer att undersöka incitament för marknadsmakt inom energilagring. Exempel på forskningsuppgifter är:

  • Uppdatera och konsolidera en databas över de nordiska värme- och kraftsektorernas attribut.
  • Använda Nash-Cournot-modeller för att undersöka strategiskt beteende inom energilagring.
  • Utveckla både öppna och slutna modeller som syftar till analytiska lösningar med kompletterande numeriska exempel.

Vi välkomnar sökande från olika metodologiska bakgrunder. 

Kvalifikationer

Vi söker en kandidat med följande kvalifikationer:

  • En meritlista som visar utmärkt modelleringsarbete relaterat till energisystem eller miljöpolitik publicerat i vetenskapliga tidskrifter.
  • Metodologisk verktygslåda relaterad till tillämpad matematik, miljöekonomi, spelteori eller operationsanalys.
  • Färdigheter i analytiska lösningsmetoder för spelteoretiska problem och beräkningsmässig implementering av optimeringsmodeller i GAMS eller liknande plattformar.
  • Domänkännedom om kraftsektorn och energipolitik.

  • Utmärkt engelska i tal och skrift (svenska är inte ett krav).

Vi letar efter en kandidat som kan arbeta inom ett mycket tvärvetenskapligt team och agendasättande forskningsprojekt. Du är ansvarsfull, lyhörd och en bra lagspelare. Title: Senior Data Analyst - Enterprise GTM
Description:
Join our global force of 400+ innovators, blending the latest in tech with the greatest in soundtracking — from our Stockholm HQ to offices in London, New York, Los Angeles, Berlin, Paris, Oslo, and Seoul. We're an industry leader with a startup mentality. We take what we do seriously, but we don't take ourselves too seriously. Come create with us, and let the world feel your work.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you'll join an embedded, AI-native analytics team that sits inside the commercial and product domains driving our growth. You'll work shoulder-to-shoulder with the VP of Revenue Operations and Global Sales Leadership as we scale our enterprise business — defining what success looks like across the full bow-tie funnel, from pipeline generation to expansion revenue, and proving it with data. You'll have a dedicated mandate within the Enterprise GTM org while drawing on the shared infrastructure, engineering support, and peers of our central Data & Analytics team.

What you'll do


Partner with RevOps and Sales leadership to define success metrics, surface growth opportunities across the enterprise bow-tie funnel, and shape GTM strategy with evidence — proactively raising the questions stakeholders haven't thought to ask.
Build and refine the models that steer the business — customer segmentation for territory and capacity planning, lead and account scoring for pipeline quality, and forecasting for pipeline and revenue.
Make our data trustworthy for agentic analytics — enrich context and metadata, and use AI coding tools to ship instrumentation and data-quality fixes directly so both people and AI tools can rely on it.
Own the analytical backbone of how the enterprise business is steered — KPI and metric definitions aligned across Sales, Marketing, and Finance, and the monthly, quarterly, and annual performance forums, owning the narrative, not just the slides.
Close data gaps and build scalable, self-serve measurement infrastructure so GTM teams can monitor long-term performance without you in the loop.
Uphold rigor as self-serve expands — push back on false precision in pipeline and forecast numbers, set the bar for what good analysis looks like, and resist sophistication, statistical or AI, where a simpler approach serves the decision better.


What you'll bring


Senior-level influence. You've driven real business impact by shaping decisions at the leadership level and moving revenue outcomes.
High agency. Habit of driving decisions through tailored communication and data storytelling.
Sharp analytical instincts. You scope vague questions with vetted frameworks, state hypotheses up front, and reason to a defensible answer even with imperfect data.
Sound judgement about data. A solid grasp of statistics and the discernment to know when past data predicts the future and when to override it for where the business is heading.
Technical versatility. Proficiency across SQL and Python (notebooks in Hex), dbt (on BigQuery), plus experience with AI coding tools and techniques including context engineering and evals.
PLG/SaaS fluency. Hands-on experience in product-led and SaaS businesses, with a solid grasp of subscription economics and the metrics that drive them.


Music to our ears


Experience across the B2B GTM funnel — Sales, Marketing, and RevOps — with funnel, segmentation, and forecasting analytics.
Data modelling know-how and experience with semantic layers.
A track record of defining metrics and steering frameworks for enterprise or 0→1 commercial motions.


What we'll offer


A fast-paced environment with real room for personal growth and tangible impact on the business.
The chance to help write the new playbook for running Analytics in the AI era, combining timeless principles with fresh learnings from emerging tooling.
A diverse, collaborative team of Analysts and Engineers building together.


Equal opportunity employer

We believe that bringing people together from different backgrounds, experiences and perspectives makes for a healthy workplace, a more successful business and a better world. We value diversity and encourage everyone to come and soundtrack the world with us.

Application

Ready to make the world feel your work? Please apply, in English.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Playground Data


Stockholm
Hybrid


Data Engineer

Some data problems are neat and tidy. Ours usually aren’t. They are messy, evolving, and tied to real decisions. We work closely with our clients to understand what actually needs to be solved and build from there.

At Playground Data, we build modern data platforms for analytics, AI and machine learning. We are a collaborative team that values solid engineering and shared growth. Our engineers take ownership and work closely with clients to deliver high-quality solutions.

What you'll actually do

You’ll work closely with our clients on high-impact initiatives where data is critical to the business. Many of our engagements involve greenfield builds or major platform transformations. This role suits engineers who enjoy creating structure from complexity and taking end-to-end ownership.

In Practice, You’ll


Design and build modern data platforms from scratch.
Ingest, model, and structure data so it’s usable for analytics, AI and ML workloads.
Work close to production, with growing responsibility for operating, evolving, and improving platforms over time.
Contribute internally by improving how we work, helping build and manage our data engineering frameworks, shaping our technical approaches, being part of presales and supporting Playground’s continued growth.


You’ll never sit idle, there’s always something meaningful to build, improve, or untangle.

Who are you?

You’ve been building data platforms for a few years and know that data doesn’t magically organize itself. Python and SQL are part of your daily toolkit, and you’ve wrestled with at least one modern data stack (AWS, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake etc.) and lived to tell the tale.

You Would Probably Describe Yourself As Someone Who

Understands data engineering in practice: You know how data needs to flow, scale, and hold up in production for analytics and AI and ML workloads.

Enjoys building from scratch: Greenfield work doesn’t scare you, it motivates you.

Stays calm when things are unclear: You don’t panic when requirements are fuzzy and the system diagram needs three whiteboards.

Is comfortable in a consultant role: You handle customer dialogue well, can contribute in presales, and help guide clients through difficult decisions.

As a person, you’re humble, genuinely curious about technology, and motivated, while knowing that asking for help is a strength, not a weakness. Friendly, curious, and playful.

You must be based in the Stockholm area and have a valid work permit for Sweden (no visa sponsorship or relocation).

Buzzword bingo: AWS, Azure, GCP, Databricks, Snowflake, DBT, Python, SQL, Terraform

Language requirement

You need to be fluent in Swedish and able to use it confidently in a professional environment. Swedish citizenship is required.

Why? Most of our collaborations and client projects are conducted in Swedish, and several of our clients have formal requirements for both Swedish citizenship and full professional fluency in the language.

Let's Play!

Applying to us is a breeze!


Upload your resumé or use your Linkedin profile
Answer the choice-questions (no "please-rewrite-your-resume" nonsense, promise)
Feel free to include links to your portfolio, GitHub or similar if you have stuff you're proud of that you want to share with us


Note

Playground Group does not provide visa sponsorship or relocation support.

For this role applicants must have a swedish citizenship

Welcome to Playground!

At Playground, you work on real, complex assignments for well-established and ambitious clients, not bench projects or short-lived experiments. Our teams are trusted with production systems, platforms, and data that actually matter. Here growth comes from ownership, curiosity, and doing the work properly. You’re trusted to think, contribute, and keep getting better, one meaningful step at a time.

Playground brings together specialized domains under one umbrella, with a shared culture and way of working, and easy access to broad technical expertise when it helps move the work forward.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Data Engineer

Build the future of data, not just the pipelines.




Most data engineering roles are centred around moving data from A to B.




This isn't one of them.




We're working with one of the Nordics' most exciting technology businesses as they continue to invest heavily in their data platform, AI capabilities and engineering function. They're looking for a Data Engineer who enjoys building complete solutions - someone equally comfortable modelling data, building APIs, automating workflows and solving business problems with technology.




The data is already flowing. The challenge now is deciding what to build with it.




You'll join a small, highly collaborative team where you'll have genuine ownership over the direction of the platform. Rather than sitting behind tickets all day, you'll work closely with stakeholders across the business to understand problems, design scalable solutions and build the tools that allow the company to make smarter decisions every day.




If you've ever found yourself wanting to build more than pipelines, this is the role you've been looking for.




The Opportunity:

The business has spent the last few years modernising its data platform, creating a cloud-native data warehouse and establishing strong engineering foundations.




Now comes the exciting part.




The focus has shifted from simply collecting data to building intelligent services around it.




You'll help design scalable data models that become the foundation for reporting, analytics and AI initiatives. You'll build lightweight applications that allow business users to interact with data. You'll expose datasets through APIs, automate repetitive processes and help create the infrastructure that powers a growing number of AI-driven workflows.




Because the team is small, you'll see the direct impact of everything you build.




Ideas don't spend months in approval chains. Engineers are trusted to identify problems, propose solutions and own them from design through to production.




What you'll be working on:

Your work will span far more than traditional data engineering.




You'll be involved in:

Designing scalable, well-structured data models using SQL and dbt
Building Python services that automate business processes
Creating APIs that expose trusted data to internal applications and AI services
Developing custom integrations where off-the-shelf tooling isn't enough
Building cloud infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code
Improving data quality, testing and platform reliability
Working closely with commercial teams to understand how data can solve real business challenges
Helping shape the next generation of AI-enabled products and internal tooling




The engineering philosophy is simple:




Solve business problems with technology.




Sometimes that's a data model.

Sometimes it's an API.

Sometimes it's a small application.




Whatever creates the most value.




What they're looking for:

This role would suit someone who's naturally curious and enjoys solving problems.

You're probably someone who starts with "What are we trying to achieve?" rather than "Which technology are we using?"




You'll bring:

Strong Data Engineering Fundamentals

Excellent SQL skills
Strong experience with dbt
Solid Python development experience
Experience working with cloud data platforms
An understanding of modern data architecture and best practices




Broader Engineering Experience

The ideal person isn't purely a pipeline engineer.

You've probably built some combination of:

REST APIs
Internal tooling
Automation services
Backend applications
Infrastructure with Terraform or similar
Data integrations




You don't need to be an expert software engineer.

You simply enjoy building whatever is needed to solve the problem.




Mindset matters more than tools!

The hiring manager is far more interested in engineers who can learn than people who've memorised a specific technology stack.




Whether you've worked with Google Cloud, Azure or AWS isn't particularly important. What matters is that you've worked in environments where you've had to think for yourself, adapt quickly and wear multiple hats. Experience in start-ups, scale-ups or fast-moving product businesses will be highly valued.




Likewise, engineers who understand both the technical and commercial side of data tend to thrive here.




If you've spent time working closely with stakeholders, translating business questions into technical solutions and making data genuinely useful, you'll fit right in.




Tech Stack:

Current technologies include:

Python
SQL
dbt
BigQuery
Google Cloud Platform
Docker
Terraform
Airbyte

Experience with every tool isn't expected.

Strong engineering principles and a willingness to learn are far more important than matching every technology.




Why this role?

This is an opportunity to join a company at exactly the right stage.




The foundations have been built.




Now the team is focused on creating a truly modern data platform - one that enables self-service analytics, intelligent automation and AI-driven products across the business




You'll join a highly visible team where your work is used daily by stakeholders throughout the organisation.




There are no endless approval processes.

No rigid role boundaries.

No spending months maintaining legacy pipelines.




Instead you'll work alongside ambitious engineers and business stakeholders who genuinely care about building great products and using data to drive better decisions.




It's the kind of environment where people who enjoy ownership, autonomy and variety do their best work.




Salary & Benefits

55,000–70,000 SEK per month (Dependent on experience)
Hybrid working (Tuesday–Thursday in the Stockholm office)
Comprehensive benefits package
Permanent position




Interview Process

Initial conversation with the Hiring Manager
Technical reasoning and problem-solving case study (discussion-based, no live coding)
Final technical and cultural interview

If you've ever found yourself wanting to build more than pipelines, this is the role you've been looking for.


The Opportunity:

The business has spent the last few years modernising its data platform, creating a cloud-native data warehouse and establishing strong engineering foundations.


Now comes the exciting part.


The focus has shifted from simply collecting data to building intelligent services around it.


You'll help design scalable data models that become the foundation for reporting, analytics and AI initiatives. You'll build lightweight applications that allow business users to interact with data. You'll expose datasets through APIs, automate repetitive processes and help create the infrastructure that powers a growing number of AI-driven workflows.


Because the team is small, you'll see the direct impact of everything you build.


Ideas don't spend months in approval chains. Engineers are trusted to identify problems, propose solutions and own them from design through to production.


What you'll be working on:

Your work will span far more than traditional data engineering.


You'll be involved in:

  • Designing scalable, well-structured data models using SQL and dbt
  • Building Python services that automate business processes
  • Creating APIs that expose trusted data to internal applications and AI services
  • Developing custom integrations where off-the-shelf tooling isn't enough
  • Building cloud infrastructure using Infrastructure as Code
  • Improving data quality, testing and platform reliability
  • Working closely with commercial teams to understand how data can solve real business challenges
  • Helping shape the next generation of AI-enabled products and internal tooling


The engineering philosophy is simple:


Solve business problems with technology.


Sometimes that's a data model.

Sometimes it's an API.

Sometimes it's a small application.


Whatever creates the most value.


What they're looking for:

This role would suit someone who's naturally curious and enjoys solving problems.

You're probably someone who starts with "What are we trying to achieve?" rather than "Which technology are we using?"


You'll bring:

Strong Data Engineering Fundamentals

  • Excellent SQL skills
  • Strong experience with dbt
  • Solid Python development experience
  • Experience working with cloud data platforms
  • An understanding of modern data architecture and best practices


Broader Engineering Experience

The ideal person isn't purely a pipeline engineer.

You've probably built some combination of:

  • REST APIs
  • Internal tooling
  • Automation services
  • Backend applications
  • Infrastructure with Terraform or similar
  • Data integrations


You don't need to be an expert software engineer.

You simply enjoy building whatever is needed to solve the problem.


Mindset matters more than tools!

The hiring manager is far more interested in engineers who can learn than people who've memorised a specific technology stack.


Whether you've worked with Google Cloud, Azure or AWS isn't particularly important. What matters is that you've worked in environments where you've had to think for yourself, adapt quickly and wear multiple hats. Experience in start-ups, scale-ups or fast-moving product businesses will be highly valued.


Likewise, engineers who understand both the technical and commercial side of data tend to thrive here.


If you've spent time working closely with stakeholders, translating business questions into technical solutions and making data genuinely useful, you'll fit right in.


Tech Stack:

Current technologies include:

  • Python
  • SQL
  • dbt
  • BigQuery
  • Google Cloud Platform
  • Docker
  • Terraform
  • Airbyte

Experience with every tool isn't expected.

Strong engineering principles and a willingness to learn are far more important than matching every technology.


Why this role?

This is an opportunity to join a company at exactly the right stage.


The foundations have been built.


Now the team is focused on creating a truly modern data platform - one that enables self-service analytics, intelligent automation and AI-driven products across the business


You'll join a highly visible team where your work is used daily by stakeholders throughout the organisation.


There are no endless approval processes.

No rigid role boundaries.

No spending months maintaining legacy pipelines.


Instead you'll work alongside ambitious engineers and business stakeholders who genuinely care about building great products and using data to drive better decisions.


It's the kind of environment where people who enjoy ownership, autonomy and variety do their best work.


Salary & Benefits

  • 55,000–70,000 SEK per month (Dependent on experience)
  • Hybrid working (Tuesday–Thursday in the Stockholm office)
  • Comprehensive benefits package
  • Permanent position


Interview Process

  • Initial conversation with the Hiring Manager
  • Technical reasoning and problem-solving case study (discussion-based, no live coding)
  • Final technical and cultural interview

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Data Engineer




We're partnered with a well-established Nordic financial services organisation that's investing heavily in its modern data platform. Combining the stability of a long-standing business with the agility of a much smaller organisation, they're looking for a Data Engineer to help shape the next generation of their cloud-based data landscape.




Data is becoming increasingly central to how the business makes decisions, delivers services and develops new capabilities. As part of a collaborative Data & Analytics team, you'll play a key role in building the platforms and pipelines that make this possible.




About the role:

This is an opportunity to join a business where engineering quality, collaboration and long-term thinking are genuinely valued.




Working with Microsoft Azure and Snowflake, you'll help design, develop and maintain modern data solutions that enable analytics, reporting and future AI initiatives across the organisation.




Rather than simply maintaining existing infrastructure, you'll have the opportunity to influence how the platform evolves, working alongside data professionals, architects and business stakeholders to build reliable, scalable and well-governed data products.




What you'll do:

Design, build and optimise data pipelines using modern cloud technologies.
Develop and maintain scalable data solutions across Azure and Snowflake.
Improve the reliability, performance and quality of the organisation's data platform.
Collaborate with analysts, data specialists and business stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver practical solutions.
Support data modelling, transformation and integration across multiple business domains.
Contribute to platform improvements, automation and engineering best practices.
Help ensure data is secure, trusted and governed within a regulated environment.




About you:

You'll enjoy solving technical problems but also understand the importance of building data platforms that deliver value for the wider business.




You'll likely have:

Experience as a Data Engineer or in a similar data platform role.
Strong experience working with Microsoft Azure data services.
Hands-on experience with Snowflake.
Good SQL skills and experience building scalable ETL/ELT pipelines.
Experience working with cloud-based data platforms and modern engineering practices.
An understanding of data modelling, performance optimisation and data quality.
A collaborative approach and the ability to work closely with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.




Experience within financial services or other regulated industries would be beneficial but isn't essential.




Why join?

This is a rare opportunity to join an organisation that combines the security and stability of an established financial institution with the culture and accessibility of a much smaller business.




You'll work in an environment where decisions are made quickly, ideas are listened to, and engineers have genuine influence over both technology and direction. The team values collaboration over hierarchy, encouraging knowledge sharing, continuous learning and long-term career development.




The organisation is investing significantly in its cloud data platform, meaning you'll be joining at an exciting stage where you'll help shape the future rather than simply inherit the past.




Alongside modern technologies including Microsoft Azure and Snowflake, you'll benefit from a supportive team, flexible hybrid working, excellent employee benefits and a culture built around trust, responsibility and sustainable growth.




If you're looking for a Data Engineering role where you'll have real ownership, work with modern cloud technologies and build solutions that make a genuine impact across the business, we'd love to hear from you.

Title: Data Engineer - Data
Description:
Varje vecka använder omkring åtta miljoner människor TV4 Play och MTV Katsomo. Bakom varje produktbeslut, innehållsrekommendation, abonnemang, annonsaffär och användarupplevelse finns data.

Nu söker vi en Data Engineer som vill vara med och utveckla dataplattformen och dataförmågorna som hjälper hela organisationen att fatta bättre beslut.

Varför den här rollen är viktig

Data är en central del av hur TV4 och MTV utvecklar produkter, förstår användarbeteenden och skapar affärsvärde.

Som Data Engineer är du med och bygger grunden som gör det möjligt för analytiker, data scientists, produktteam och verksamheten att arbeta effektivt med data.

Du kommer att arbeta i ett nordiskt datateam och bidra till både nya datalösningar och vidareutveckling av befintliga plattformar, pipelines och datamodeller.

Det här är en möjlighet att påverka hur en av Nordens största medieaktörer använder data för att utveckla produkter, stärka affären och skapa bättre upplevelser för miljontals användare.

Varför du vill kliva ombord


Du gillar att bygga saker som andra är beroende av.
Du motiveras av att skapa robusta och välstrukturerade datalösningar som hjälper andra team att fatta bättre beslut och arbeta snabbare.
Du tycker om att röra dig mellan teknik, verksamhet och användarbehov och vill förstå hur data faktiskt används för att skapa värde.
Du lockas av möjligheten att påverka både plattform, arbetssätt och framtida datalösningar i en organisation där data blir allt viktigare.
Du ser AI som ett naturligt verktyg i modern utveckling och är nyfiken på hur nya verktyg och arbetssätt kan hjälpa team att skapa mer värde.


Om teamet

Vi är ett nordiskt datateam som arbetar nära produktorganisationen, analytiker, data scientists och verksamheten.

Teamet ansvarar för dataplattformen och de dataförmågor som används för analys, rapportering, produktutveckling och affärsinsikter.

Vi arbetar tvärfunktionellt och nära våra stakeholders för att säkerställa att rätt data finns tillgänglig på rätt plats, med rätt kvalitet.

Det här är en möjlighet att vara med och utveckla både dataplattformen och hur data används för att skapa värde i hela organisationen.

Vad du kommer att göra


Designa, bygga och vidareutveckla datapipelines och dataplattformskomponenter
Utveckla datamodeller som används för analys, rapportering och beslutsfattande
Bygga och förbättra ELT-processer
Säkerställa datakvalitet, tillförlitlighet och observability
Arbeta nära analytiker, data scientists och andra team för att förstå behov och omsätta dem till skalbara datalösningar
Bidra till tekniska vägval och utvecklingen av vår dataplattform
Förbättra utvecklarupplevelsen genom automatisering, standardisering och moderna arbetssätt
Bidra till best practices inom data engineering, datamodellering och dataarkitektur


Om dig

Du har erfarenhet av data engineering och tycker om att bygga lösningar som används av andra. Du är nyfiken, lösningsorienterad och trivs i miljöer där du får ta ansvar och vara med och påverka.

Vi tror att du har flera års erfarenhet av data engineering och känner dig trygg i att självständigt driva tekniska initiativ och leverera lösningar med hög kvalitet. Du ser helheten kring dina lösningar och förstår hur tekniska beslut påverkar användare, verksamhet och andra team.

Vi tror också att du:


Har erfarenhet av SQL, Python och moderna dataplattformar
Har arbetat med datamodellering, datapipelines och ETL/ELT-flöden
Har erfarenhet av molnbaserade lösningar, gärna AWS
Trivs med att samarbeta tvärfunktionellt
Tar ansvar för kvalitet, struktur och långsiktig hållbarhet i det du bygger
Är nyfiken, framåtlutad och gillar att lära dig nya saker
Har anammat AI som verktyg för att höja kvalitet, lärande och produktivitet


Extra meriterande


Snowflake
Airflow
dbt
Terraform
CI/CD
Monitoring och observability


Nuvarande tech stack


AWS
Snowflake
Airflow
dbt
Python
SQL
Terraform
GitHub Actions
Monitoring & observability tooling


Vad TV4 erbjuder dig


Möjlighet att påverka hur data används i en av Nordens största medieorganisationer
Hög grad av autonomi och kort väg från idé till beslut
Ett nordiskt team med stark kompetens och höga ambitioner
Stora möjligheter att påverka plattform, arbetssätt och tekniska vägval
Kollegor som gärna delar kunskap och hjälper varandra att lyckas
Du loggar ut på riktigt efter jobbet. Vi har dedikerad on-call där det är relevant och förväntar oss inte att du ska vara tillgänglig utanför arbetstid
Modernt kontor med gym, restaurang, garage och takterrass
Kollektivavtal, tjänstepension, försäkringar, friskvårdsbidrag, Benify, valfri telefon och MacBook Pro


Praktisk information


TV4 är office-first och vi arbetar från kontoret fyra dagar i veckan.
Vissa resor inom Norden förekommer som en naturlig del av samarbetet mellan våra kontor.
Tillsvidareanställning med sex månaders provanställning.
Bakgrundskontroll ingår i processen.


Vi ser fram emot din ansökan!

Avdelning: Produkt, Teknik & Operations

Plats: Stockholm

Title: Business IT Analyst
Description:
About Justera Group

Justera Group is a leading Swedish IT consulting company specializing in building high-performing technology teams and delivering end-to-end IT solutions. We help organizations accelerate their digital transformation through expert consultancy, software development, and talent solutions. Our consultants work on impactful projects across industries, helping clients achieve sustainable business success through innovation and technology.

About The Role

We are currently looking for a Senior Business IT Analyst to join an exciting assignment with one of Sweden's leading automotive companies in Södertälje.

In this role, you will be responsible for identifying and analyzing information used across business processes and applications, translating business needs into clear requirements, and supporting implementation initiatives. You will work closely with stakeholders across multiple functions and global teams to ensure business and technical requirements are aligned and effectively delivered.

The assignment also includes aspects of data privacy and information governance, making it ideal for candidates with experience in regulatory and compliance-related initiatives.

Responsibilities


Analyze business processes, information flows, and application usage
Gather, document, and manage business and system requirements
Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to define solutions
Support implementation and continuous improvement initiatives
Ensure alignment between business needs and IT solutions
Contribute to data privacy and information governance activities
Work closely with global cross-functional teams within the organization


Required Qualifications


Academic degree in a relevant field
Extensive experience as a Business IT Analyst or similar role
Experience working within automotive projects
Previous experience working with our automotive client in Södertälje
Strong analytical and stakeholder management skills
Excellent communication and documentation abilities


Meritorious Qualifications


Experience within information governance, including legal, architecture, or information security perspectives
Experience working with Data Privacy initiatives
Knowledge of vehicle electrical systems and/or embedded systems
C/CE driver's license
Experience working in global and cross-functional environments


What We Offer


A dynamic and innovative workplace
Opportunities to work on impactful projects with leading industry clients
Collaboration with talented and experienced professionals
Competitive compensation and benefits package
Continuous learning and professional development opportunities
Flexible hybrid working model supporting work-life balance
25 days of annual leave
Annual wellness allowance of 3,500 SEK


If you are a driven and experienced Business IT Analyst with a passion for the automotive industry, we would love to hear from you.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
For a Sustainable Future Together

Coop is Sweden's largest cooperative and a leading player in the grocery retail sector, with over 4 million members. We focus heavily on sustainability and work to create a greener and more just future by offering sustainable products and services.

We believe that a diversity of perspectives and experiences is key to creating a more inclusive and successful workplace. Therefore, we welcome everyone, regardless of gender, age, background, or ethnic origin.

Coop Sverige – Together we make food-Sweden sustainable

At Coop we are building the data backbone of a truly data-driven company. We run stores for our 4.2 million members, and our motto is “Relevance in every touchpoint.” Understanding our customers’ needs and behaviours is what fuels our innovation – from purchasing and logistics to personalised customer experiences.

We are now strengthening our Data Platform team with a skilled Data Engineer to help us build scalable, cloud-native data solutions on Azure.

About The Role

You will be part of our Data Platform team, which sits within Coop’s AI & Business Intelligence organisation – the team building the data foundation that powers analytics, reporting, operational intelligence and AI across the company.

In this role you will design, build and optimise enterprise-grade data platforms using Azure Data Services, Databricks, modern Data Lakehouse architectures, real-time streaming and DevOps automation. You’ll turn data from across the business into trusted, high-quality data products that the whole organisation can rely on.

What You Will Do


Design and implement scalable batch and real-time data pipelines using Azure-native technologies.
Build and maintain robust data ingestion frameworks from multiple source systems, APIs and event streams.
Develop and manage Medallion Architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold) within the enterprise Data Lake.
Implement streaming solutions with Kafka, Event Hub and Service Bus for event-driven processing.
Develop transformation frameworks using Databricks, PySpark and Delta Lake.
Collaborate with business stakeholders, data analysts and data scientists to deliver trusted, high-quality data products.
Enable CI/CD pipelines and automate deployments using Azure DevOps best practices.
Drive platform optimization focused on performance, scalability, reliability and cost efficiency.
Ensure adherence to data governance, security, monitoring and operational excellence standards.


Who are you?

To succeed in this role, we believe you have a genuine passion for getting value from massive datasets and love working with state-of-the-art technologies that make completely new data-driven solutions possible. You enjoy working with different tasks and stakeholders, you are result-oriented, and you spark when given room to take initiative and create solutions. As a team, we believe in supporting and challenging each other and growing a positive, learning culture.

We believe that you


Have strong hands-on experience with the Azure data platform – Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Data Lake Storage Gen2 (ADLS), Azure Functions, Event Hub and Service Bus.
Are highly skilled in Databricks and the Lakehouse – PySpark, Spark SQL, Delta Lake, Databricks Workflows, Unity Catalog and performance tuning, applying Medallion and Lakehouse architecture in practice.
Are strong in data architecture and modelling – Lakehouse and Medallion design, ETL/ELT frameworks, and data quality & validation frameworks.
Have experience with streaming and integration – Apache Kafka, event-driven architecture, Service Bus, batch ingestion / Autoloader and REST APIs.
Are a confident programmer in Python, PySpark and SQL.
Work the DevOps way – Azure DevOps, CI/CD pipeline development and Infrastructure as Code (Terraform preferred).
Are comfortable with monitoring and operations – Log Analytics, Application Insights, and performance tuning & troubleshooting.
Can communicate effectively with both technical specialists and non-technical stakeholders.


It’s meritorious if you


Have 5+ years of experience in data engineering.
Have built enterprise-scale Azure data platforms hands-on.
Have worked with retail, logistics or large-scale operational data environments.
Have supported machine learning and advanced analytics workloads.
Have a strong understanding of distributed data processing and streaming architectures.


About Us

The grocery trade is a stable, long-term industry, and Coop’s offering is essential to the transition toward a greener, more sustainable future. You’ll join a culture defined by humility, initiative and strong values, where we take joint responsibility and genuinely want to help each other succeed. Our office in Solna Business Park offers a modern work environment with great transport links, a fantastic staff restaurant, and the option of hybrid work in agreement with your manager. At Coop, we work for our 4.2 million members, making us by far the largest cooperation in Sweden.

If you have questions about the position, you are welcome to contact Hiring Manager Jakob Liljedahl (jakob.liljedahl@coop.se) or the recruiter Andreas Nilsson (andreas.nilsson2@coop.se)

For a Sustainable Future Together

Coop is Sweden's largest cooperative and a leading player in the grocery retail sector, with over 4 million members. We focus heavily on sustainability and work to create a greener and more just future by offering sustainable products and services.

We believe that a diversity of perspectives and experiences is key to creating a more inclusive and successful workplace. Therefore, we welcome everyone, regardless of gender, age, background, or ethnic origin.

Coop Sverige – Together we make food-Sweden sustainable

At Coop we are building the data backbone of a truly data-driven company. We run stores for our 4.2 million members, and our motto is “Relevance in every touchpoint.” Understanding our customers’ needs and behaviours is what fuels our innovation – from purchasing and logistics to personalised customer experiences.

We are now strengthening our Data Platform team with a skilled Data Engineer to help us build scalable, cloud-native data solutions on Azure.

About The Role

You will be part of our Data Platform team, which sits within Coop’s AI & Business Intelligence organisation – the team building the data foundation that powers analytics, reporting, operational intelligence and AI across the company.

In this role you will design, build and optimise enterprise-grade data platforms using Azure Data Services, Databricks, modern Data Lakehouse architectures, real-time streaming and DevOps automation. You’ll turn data from across the business into trusted, high-quality data products that the whole organisation can rely on.

What You Will Do

  • Design and implement scalable batch and real-time data pipelines using Azure-native technologies.
  • Build and maintain robust data ingestion frameworks from multiple source systems, APIs and event streams.
  • Develop and manage Medallion Architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold) within the enterprise Data Lake.
  • Implement streaming solutions with Kafka, Event Hub and Service Bus for event-driven processing.
  • Develop transformation frameworks using Databricks, PySpark and Delta Lake.
  • Collaborate with business stakeholders, data analysts and data scientists to deliver trusted, high-quality data products.
  • Enable CI/CD pipelines and automate deployments using Azure DevOps best practices.
  • Drive platform optimization focused on performance, scalability, reliability and cost efficiency.
  • Ensure adherence to data governance, security, monitoring and operational excellence standards.

Who are you?

To succeed in this role, we believe you have a genuine passion for getting value from massive datasets and love working with state-of-the-art technologies that make completely new data-driven solutions possible. You enjoy working with different tasks and stakeholders, you are result-oriented, and you spark when given room to take initiative and create solutions. As a team, we believe in supporting and challenging each other and growing a positive, learning culture.

We believe that you

  • Have strong hands-on experience with the Azure data platform – Azure Data Factory, Azure Databricks, Data Lake Storage Gen2 (ADLS), Azure Functions, Event Hub and Service Bus.
  • Are highly skilled in Databricks and the Lakehouse – PySpark, Spark SQL, Delta Lake, Databricks Workflows, Unity Catalog and performance tuning, applying Medallion and Lakehouse architecture in practice.
  • Are strong in data architecture and modelling – Lakehouse and Medallion design, ETL/ELT frameworks, and data quality & validation frameworks.
  • Have experience with streaming and integration – Apache Kafka, event-driven architecture, Service Bus, batch ingestion / Autoloader and REST APIs.
  • Are a confident programmer in Python, PySpark and SQL.
  • Work the DevOps way – Azure DevOps, CI/CD pipeline development and Infrastructure as Code (Terraform preferred).
  • Are comfortable with monitoring and operations – Log Analytics, Application Insights, and performance tuning & troubleshooting.
  • Can communicate effectively with both technical specialists and non-technical stakeholders.

It’s meritorious if you

  • Have 5+ years of experience in data engineering.
  • Have built enterprise-scale Azure data platforms hands-on.
  • Have worked with retail, logistics or large-scale operational data environments.
  • Have supported machine learning and advanced analytics workloads.
  • Have a strong understanding of distributed data processing and streaming architectures.

About Us

The grocery trade is a stable, long-term industry, and Coop’s offering is essential to the transition toward a greener, more sustainable future. You’ll join a culture defined by humility, initiative and strong values, where we take joint responsibility and genuinely want to help each other succeed. Title: Data Engineer
Description:
About Pophouse

Pophouse is a pioneering entertainment and investment company focused on iconic music catalogues and artist legacies – behind names like ABBA, Avicii, Swedish House Mafia, and KISS. By combining data‑driven insights with cutting‑edge technology, we unlock long‑term value in some of the world’s most beloved music IP.




About the role

As our investment portfolio continues to grow, so does our ambition. We’re building a world‑class data engineering capability to power how we evaluate catalogues, understand music consumption at scale, and make even smarter, faster, more informed investment decisions.

We’re now looking for a Data Engineer to build Pophouse’s intelligence platform – the layer where information is filtered, connected, and turned into investment‑ready intelligence. This is an opportunity to build the foundation that enables smarter investment decisions from the ground up.




You will design and engineer the intelligence platform that powers our deal teams by turning fragmented signals from across the music ecosystem into clear, actionable insight.

Working closely with our investment team, you have a deep understanding of what truly drives value in music investments, from streaming dynamics and platform behaviour to emerging signals that others haven’t yet spotted. Your mission is to create systems that surface the right information at the right moment, while continuously evolving the underlying technology, data sources, and approaches as the landscape changes.




You will also work closely with our Catalog team, extending your impact beyond the investment decision itself and into the data that matters once an investment has been made – helping ensure the same intelligence-driven approach carries through into how our catalogues are managed and developed over time.




This role is for someone who is technically strong, commercially curious, and genuinely excited by building from scratch. You’re comfortable operating in fast‑moving environments, happy to challenge existing approaches, and not afraid to change tools or start again when better solutions emerge.




If you’re driven by building intelligence, passionate about music and technology, and motivated by creating something foundational in an ever‑evolving tech landscape, this is your opportunity to shape how Pophouse makes data‑driven investment decisions.




More about Pophouse

At Pophouse, data is not a support function, it’s a strategic capability. You’ll be joining a company that combines storytelling, technology, and investment expertise to shape the future of music IP. This is an opportunity to build and influence a modern analytics capability from the ground up, working closely with decision-makers and contributing directly to high-value investments in globally recognized catalogues and artist legacies.




If you’re excited about building world-class data infrastructure at the heart of music, culture, and investment, we’d love to hear from you.




In this recruitment process, we are collaborating with Safemind, and all applications will be handled through the application link.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Kreditz is transforming how businesses assess credit and risk using real-time data and NextGen Credit Scoring solutions.

As one of Europe’s fastest-growing FinTech scaleups - we’ve earned a place on the Sifted 100: Nordics & Benelux list in both 2024 and 2025 and most recently, we were proud to receive a double win at the Credit Management & Compliance Awards 2025, being named both "Fintech Rocket of the Year" and "Breakthrough of the Year".

With strong growth across the Nordics and Europe, our clients range from leading banks and iGaming giants to fast-moving digital innovators.

About The Position

At Kreditz, data is not just a support function, it is the core of our product. We turn raw bank transactions into real-time insight that powers credit & risk decisions across Europe.

As we scale, we’re strengthening our data foundation and building a modern, reliable, and cost-efficient data platform. We’re now looking for a Data Engineer to join our Product team and take ownership of our Data Warehouse, pipelines, and reporting enablement across the business.

This is a hands-on role for someone who loves building scalable data infrastructure and wants to work close to both product and real business impact.

Your mission is to build and evolve a scalable, reliable, and high-performing data environment that supports both analytics and operational needs.

What Can Kreditz Offer


A once-in-a-lifetime growth journey, from startup to Global scaleup
A journey where everyone in the team plays a crucial role for our company’s success
Fun-loving, driven, and passionate colleagues who support each other’s success
A culture that celebrates progress, humility, and teamwork
We win together
24/7 access to gym, sauna and sports facilities at our office, plus additional wellness allowance
Competitive base salary


RESPONSIBILITES

Some Of The Main Responsibilities You Will Cover


Maintain & Enhance the DWH: Own the architecture of our Data Warehouse, ensuring it is scalable, cost-effective, and optimized for both analytical and operational needs.
Pipeline Automation: Design, build, and maintain robust ETL/ELT pipelines to ingest data from various sources into our AWS ecosystem. Work closely with our MLOPs Engineering team to assist the data pipelines for our AI / ML models
Stakeholder Enablement: Work closely with Product, Finance, and Commercial teams to automate internal reporting and create self-service data environments.
AWS Infrastructure: Leverage and manage AWS services (such as Glue, Redshift, Athena, Lambda, and S3) to ensure a high-performing data environment.
Data Quality & Governance: Implement monitoring and validation frameworks to ensure data integrity and lineage across all systems.
Collaborate: Partner with our DevOps and Backend teams to align infrastructure and ensure smooth data flow across the entire Kreditz platform.


WE ARE LOOKING FOR

We focus on practical engineering skill, strong foundations, and the ability to build systems that last.

In Addition To That We Believe You Have


BSc / MSc in a quantitative subject, i.e., Physics, Engineering, Mathematics, Computer Science or equivalent.
3+ years of experience in Data Engineering or a similar role focused on data infrastructure.
Expert-level SQL skills for complex transformations and performance tuning.
Proficiency in Python for building pipelines and automating tasks.
Strong hands-on experience with AWS (specifically services like Redshift/Snowflake, S3, Glue, and Lambda).
Solid understanding of data warehousing concepts (e.g., Star Schema, Kimball) and modern data stack tools.
Experience with orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow, Dagster) and CI/CD workflows (i.e., GitHub Actions).
Ability to translate complex technical data structures into clear insights for non-technical stakeholders.


Meritorious Skills


Experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform).
Experience from FinTech, Open Banking, or working with highly regulated data.
Familiarity with BI tools (e.g., AWS QuickSight, PowerBI...etc)


WHY JOIN NOW?

At Kreditz, you’ll work with data that matters, powering real-time financial decisions across Europe. You’ll have real ownership, real impact, and the opportunity to shape the data foundation of a fast-growing FinTech scaleup.

APPOINTMENT OF ROLE

Q2/Q3 2026

SCOPE

Full-time, permanent employment (tillsvidareanställning)

SALARY

Competitive salary

REPORTING

You will be reporting to the company’s CPO

WORKPLACE

Danderyd, Stockholm

RECRUITMENT PROCESS

Ongoing selection, we review candidates continuously.

Last day for applications 31st March

CRIMINAL RECORDS EXTRACT

As part of our recruitment process we will ask you to present a clean criminal records extract

CONTACT

Lorenzo Puccio, VP Strategic Operations, Lorenzo@kreditz.com

For more information about the role feel free to reach out to our CPO, Spilios Tzouras, spilios.tzouras@kreditz.com

Intelligent credit- & risk decisions made easy with open banking & AI-technology

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
VVi söker Data Engineers

Vi rekryterar inte till en specifik roll, utan söker ständigt efter nya duktiga kollegor med en positiv inställning och en vilja att lära och dela med sig av sina kunskaper. Med den inställningen vet vi att du kan lyckas som konsult hos ten10. Därför lyssnar vi på dig och ser, utifrån din erfarenhet och kompetens, vilken typ av spännande uppdrag där du trivs och utvecklas bäst.

Hos ten10 blir du en del av en organisation som värdesätter den personliga relationen, där varje individ får vara med och påverka både sin egen och ten10s utveckling.

Vi tror på att bygga ett bolag av olika personer som bidrar på olika sätt till att vi alla trivs och lyckas. Utöver att göra skillnad i de uppdrag vi gemensamt väljer utifrån ditt intresse och din utveckling är du en viktig del av ett framåtlutat bolag där våra medarbetare alltid står i fokus.

Hos oss ser man…

…positivt på utmaningar

…positivt på samarbete men klarar också av att vara självgående när det behövs

…positivt på nya teknologier, data och komplexa problem

…positivt på kompetensutveckling och att ständigt bli bättre

…positivt på att vara med och påverka

Att vara en konsult hos ten10

Vi vill ständigt bli bättre och kunna hjälpa fler av våra kunder i deras utmaningar. Vi därför söker vi nu fler Data Engineers som vill vara med på en spännande resa, tillsammans med oss och våra kunder.

Som Data Engineer-konsult hos ten10 har du en nyckelroll i att demokratisera data ute hos våra kunder. Tillgänglig, tillförlitlig och välstrukturerad data är grunden för både Analytics, Machine Learning och AI-lösningar och där gör du verklig skillnad.

Våra uppdrag varierar, men gemensamt är att du arbetar nära både verksamhet, analytiker, data scientists och utvecklingsteam för att skapa hållbara dataplattformar och datadrivna lösningar.

I din roll som Data Engineer…


flytande i både Svenska och Engelska


…har du vilja och intresse av att lösa kundernas problem och se det som en intressant utmaning.

…har du minst tre års arbetslivserfarenhet inom rollen eller liknande roller


har du gärna erfarenhet eller ett stort intresse av Databricks


…är du skicklig i ett skriptspråk som Python.

…gillar du att bygga pipelines med (med ApacheSpark, Flink eller liknande).

…har du en djup förståelse för systemdesign, datastrukturer och algoritmer och kunskapen att tillämpa dem för att skapa pragmatiska lösningar.

…har du erfarenhet av molnplattformar som AWS, Azure, GCP eller liknande.

…har du erfarenhet av av big data-teknologier som Hadoop, Kafka, SQL, Spark m.m.

…är du noggrann men också lösningsorienterad

…trivs du att jobba i agila team och gärna också i en mer ledande roll.

…har du ett stort intresse för Data och Analytics området i allmänhet

Vad får man ut av att vara en ten10are

Hos ten10 är det viktigt att hitta en sund balans mellan privatliv och arbetsliv. Vi arbetar tillsammans för att hitta ett upplägg som fungerar i din vardag – både när det gäller uppdrag, arbetssätt och ersättning.

Vi erbjuder en av marknadens mest attraktiva lönemodeller, där du får tydlig kompensation för det värde du skapar.

Vi lägger också stor vikt vid gemenskap. Du får möjlighet att dela erfarenheter, idéer och perspektiv när vi ses på AWs, resor, julbord och andra gemensamma aktiviteter.

Vi pratar inte bara om att vi bryr oss om våra medarbetare – hela vår verksamhet bygger på dem. Vår ambition är att skapa rätt förutsättningar för just dig.
Show more

Hos ten10 är det viktigt att hitta en sund balans mellan privatliv och arbetsliv. Vi arbetar tillsammans för att hitta ett upplägg som fungerar i din vardag – både när det gäller uppdrag, arbetssätt och ersättning.

Vi erbjuder en av marknadens mest attraktiva lönemodeller, där du får tydlig kompensation för det värde du skapar.

Vi lägger också stor vikt vid gemenskap. Du får möjlighet att dela erfarenheter, idéer och perspektiv när vi ses på AWs, resor, julbord och andra gemensamma aktiviteter.

Vi pratar inte bara om att vi bryr oss om våra medarbetare – hela vår verksamhet bygger på dem. Vår ambition är att skapa rätt förutsättningar för just dig.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
TL;DR




→ You own the data layer, ingestion, transformation, and modeling that makes analytics, reporting, and AI possible.




→ Consultant role across the Nordics, with clients spanning industries from financial services to manufacturing.




→ You work in cross functional delivery teams alongside Platform, Analytics and GenAI Developers, each owning a distinct layer of the stack.




→ Certifications without a debate. Colleagues who are obsessed with the craft. Freedom to grow your way.







What you'll do




As a Data Engineer, you’ll be building the data platforms that everything else depends on. From raw source systems to production-ready models consumed by analysts, ML pipelines, and business users. You own the path data takes to become useful. The work is hands-on Python and SQL, heavy on PySpark, and grounded in Kimball modeling and lakehouse architecture.




You come in before the solution is defined. Together with the client, you shape what gets built, then you build it. You'll work in cross-functional delivery teams alongside platform engineers and AI developers, each owning a distinct layer of the stack.




A few examples of what this looks like in practice:




Building a Kafka-based ingestion framework for a major insurance group, onboarding several thousand tables at scale so multiple downstream teams can actually use the data they need.
Lifting an on-prem data warehouse into a cloud-based Databricks lakehouse for a Nordic logistics company, standing up CI/CD, integration, modeling, and quality assurance, then scaling for new use cases.
Auditing a manufacturing company's data platform against best practices, delivering a migration plan, then staying on to bring in new sources, optimize workflows, and build out analytics models.




The bar for what "done" means here is high: production-grade, observable, secure and used.







What you get




Assignments that accelerate you. Every 6–18 months you're in a new engagement. New industry, new architecture decisions, new stakeholders to earn trust from. You'll face more distinct technical challenges in two years here than most engineers see in five.




Work that's hard to come by elsewhere. Modern data platforms underpinning enterprise AI is one of the most technically demanding spaces right now. The problems here aren't solved yet, and you'll be among the people solving them.




Your direction, your call. Want to go deep technically, specialise in Databricks and Spark engineering, platform foundations or analytics engineering? Go for it. Want to grow toward Tech Lead or Solution Architect, steering delivery and building long-term client relationships? Also go for it. Both paths are real and equally valued.




People who make you better. The people here are genuinely passionate about technology, not as a job, but as something they care about. Engineers who go deep because they want to, follow the space obsessively, and get restless when things stop moving. That's what keeps Redeploy consistently ahead, and it's what you'll feel from day one.




The Perks. 30 days vacation · hybrid work and flexible hours · private medical insurance · pension (ITP1) · wellness allowance 5,000 SEK · free choice of tools and tech · free breakfast, soda and snacks · yearly gatherings and AW's · a team with genuine interests outside work — gaming, food, running, padel, golf, football, cycling.







Who you are




You take ownership, hold yourself to a high standard, and bring real opinions to the table. You communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, and you're energised by the consulting pace, new challenges, new contexts, and the pressure to deliver.




We care more about how you think, build and collaborate than whether your background ticks every box. Maybe you come from software development and want to move closer to data. Maybe you've never worked in consulting but you're curious about the variety it brings. Humble enough to play for the team, sharp enough to push back when it matters.







What you bring




A background in Computer Science, IT, Engineering or equivalent university degree
Strong programming skills in Python, with solid experience in SQL and Spark, and a sound understanding of software engineering principles
Experience with cloud-based data solutions (Azure preferred, but AWS is also highly relevant)
Solid understanding of data structures, modeling and processing
Familiarity with data architectures like Lakehouse and Medallion
AI-assisted development (you actively use tools like Claude Code, Codex, or similar in your daily work)




Strong plus: Databricks or Microsoft Fabric, CI/CD pipelines for data, data governance, graph databases, prior consulting experience, Swedish language skills.







About Redeploy




Redeploy is where cloud, data, and AI come together in production. We help Nordic enterprises design, build, and operate modern tech platforms and AI solutions that are secure, scalable, and production-ready. Engineers at heart, we work hands-on across Azure, AWS, and Databricks from strategy to operations.

Title: Retail Analytics Manager
Description:
Job Description



WHAT YOU’LL DO



As a Retail Analytics Manager, you will lead and develop a team of Data Analysts within the Retail unit. You combine people leadership with operational contribution, ensuring both strong delivery and continuous competence development.



Key responsibilities include:



Build, coach, and develop a high-performing analytics team while fostering a culture of curiosity, experimentation, and continuous improvement.
Define and evolve the analytics vision by establishing long-term measurement frameworks, KPI governance, and a roadmap for advanced analytics that supports retail strategy.
Act as a strategic partner to senior leaders, proactively identifying opportunities where analytics can unlock value and influence key decisions.
Turn complex business challenges into structured analytical initiatives aligned with commercial priorities.
Set standards for data quality, analytical accuracy, and impactful storytelling, ensuring reliable and actionable insights across the organization.
Oversee analytics projects across functions, ensuring insights are timely, actionable, and embedded in strategic decision cycles.





WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH​



You will work closely with stakeholders across Retail and beyond, and peer Data Analyst Managers to ensure strong collaboration and drive aligned, data-driven decision-making.







WHO YOU ARE



We are looking for people with…



→ Years of experience in analytics and/or business development



→ Deep expertise in data analytics, strong problem-solving capabilities and the ability to turn complexity into clear, actionable insights



→ Proven experience leading complex analytics initiatives and projects



→ Strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to build relationships across functions and contribute to Communities of Practice



→ Experience leading, coaching and developing others



→ A strong understanding of fashion retail, with deeper knowledge of the physical store environment



And people who are…



→ Strategic thinkers who combine analytical rigour with clear and effective communication



→ Proactive and confident in driving initiatives forward while delivering high-quality outcomes



→ Engaging and structured leaders who create clarity, accountability and high-performing teams



→ Open and flexible, thriving in dynamic and evolving environments



→ Collaborative and motivated by shaping the future together with others







WHO WE ARE



H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices, and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many, and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.  



We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together, we want to use our power, our scale, and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. Read more about us here.  







JOIN US  



Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.   







Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.  







This is a permanent position based in Stockholm. Please submit your CV in English as soon as possible. Also note that, due to the summer holiday period, the recruitment process may take slightly longer than usual.







*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
At Qred, we’re building the world's best bank for small businesses. Since launching 11 years ago, we’ve grown from startup to profitable fintech scale-up, now generating over 1 billion SEK in annual revenue and supporting 50,000+ entrepreneurs across Northern Europe. We combine smart technology, real data, and human judgment to make financing simple, fast, and fair. With bold growth plans and strong momentum across multiple markets, we’re now looking for a Data Engineer for the next phase of growth.

About The Role

As a Data Engineer, you will own the end-to-end design and execution of our data architecture, directly impacting how our financial systems scale. This mission offers a clear view of data flows across the organization, giving you the autonomy to bridge the gap between technical infrastructure and real-world business impact.

Key responsibilities


Design and optimize robust ETL/ELT pipelines to automate high-quality data integration from diverse financial sources.
Translate complex business requirements into clean, performant, and analytics-ready data models within our warehouse.
Implement robust data governance standards, including testing frameworks, data cataloging, and clear data lineage.
Partner with analysts, engineers, and business leaders to align data delivery with critical commercial goals.
Deploy scalable infrastructure updates using Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tools within our cloud environment.



What we’re looking for

We are looking for a structured, proactive professional who thrives on driving technical alignment and building scalable frameworks. You take initiative to champion engineering best practices, understanding that data reliability and clear cross-functional communication are vital to our velocity.

Qualifications


Several years of professional experience in data engineering, building and optimizing production-grade data pipelines.
Strong proficiency in Python and SQL, with deep experience in schema design and dimensional modeling.
Hands-on experience with modern data warehousing tools, ideally Redshift, and cloud platforms like AWS.
Experience with modern analytics engineering workflows, such as dbt, alongside Git and data quality monitoring frameworks.
Academic background in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.



Why Qred?

This is the place to be if you’re looking for a place to grow. Qred is growing fast, and our Qredsters along with it. With a non-bureaucratic organization and delegated responsibilities, we make sure there’s a short path from idea to action. In addition to our great culture, you get to work with the latest cutting-edge techniques, full ownership, and last but not least a bunch of great competent colleagues to learn from!

One Last Thing

This is a full-time, permanent position based in our headquarters in Stockholm. We believe our culture thrives when we work together, which is why we have an office-first approach. To balance this with some flexibility, we have the option of working remotely one day per week. We review applications on a rolling basis and while the start date is flexible, the right candidate can join us immediately.

Qred celebrates diversity and does not discriminate based on ethnicity, religion, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, disability status, or any other applicable characteristics protected by law.



Qred is an international FinTech scale-up that specializes in digital financing solutions for small businesses, with operations in the Nordics, Benelux, Germany and Brazil. We are passionate about helping small businesses thrive and grow, by providing them with business loans and credit cards. Right now we’re in an exciting phase of our journey that focuses on growth through smart product development and technology fueled by international expansion.

Title: Dataanalytiker till Arbetsmiljöverket
Description:
På Arbetsmiljöverket jobbar vi tillsammans för att ingen ska bli sjuk, skadas eller dö av jobbet. Därför hjälper vi arbetsgivare att uppfylla de krav som finns och informerar om hur arbetsmiljöarbetet blir en naturlig del av vardagen. Med arbetsmiljölagen som grund tar vi fram regler, inspekterar arbetsplatser och sprider kunskap om arbetsmiljöfrågor. Uppdraget är allvarligt men vår vardag präglas av arbetsglädje och vi är omtänksamma och hjälpsamma kollegor.

Ditt nya jobb

Vi söker nu en dataanalytiker för ett vikariat på ett år till enheten för statistik. I rollen kommer du att bidra till myndighetens utveckling mot ett mer datadrivet, riskbaserat och kunskapsbaserat arbetssätt.

Detta innefattar bland annat att:


Identifiera och integrera externa datakällor
Genomföra kvantitativa analyser av större datamängder
Utveckla indikatorer och på sikt prediktionsmodeller
Kvalitetssäkra data och analys
Bidra med ett kvantitativt perspektiv i tvärfunktionella samarbeten



En viktig del av arbetet är även att säkerställa god dokumentation av data, kod, analyser och processer, så att arbetet blir reproducerbart, spårbart och möjligt att vidareutveckla.

Då arbetet till stor del kommer innebära att delta i utvecklingen av myndighetens arbetssätt söker vi dig som vill vara med och skapa nytt. Som tycker om att arbeta självständigt och som trivs i föränderliga miljöer som är under utveckling. Arbetet kommer att kräva samarbete med olika delar av myndighetens verksamhet och du bör trivas med att skapa nya kontakter för att utföra ditt uppdrag.

Rollen är både strategisk och operativ och innefattar även arbete kopplat till myndighetens officiella statistik.

Enheten för statistik består av 12 medarbetare som har i uppdrag att skapa officiell statistik över arbetsskador och arbetsmiljö och ansvarar också för att ta fram olika former av analyser och beslutsunderlag. Vi arbetar även med verksamhetsdata för myndighetens behov samt är statistiskt och dataanalytiskt stöd till myndighetens verksamhet.

Din bakgrund

Rollen kräver att du har:


Högskoleexamen inom exempelvis statistik, datavetenskap, matematik, nationalekonomi eller annat område relevant för tjänsten. Alternativt annan eftergymnasial utbildning tillsammans med mångårig relevant arbetslivserfarenhet som arbetsgivaren bedömer likvärdig
Flerårig aktuell erfarenhet av kvantitativ analys av större datamängder
Aktuell erfarenhet av att arbeta med data i hela kedjan (identifiera möjliga datakällor, bearbetning, analys, dokumentation)
Erfarenhet av programmering för dataanalys (t.ex. SQL, Python, R eller motsvarande)
Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska i både tal och skrift
Goda kunskaper i engelska



Det är meriterande om du även har:


Erfarenhet av arbete i statistikverktyget SAS
Erfarenhet av statistisk modellering eller maskininlärning
Erfarenhet av att ha arbetat vid en statlig myndighet



Som person ser vi att du kommer att trivas och lyckas i rollen om du har mycket god förmåga att självständigt lösa problem och att skapa struktur i ditt arbete. Du kan med lugn och tilltro till din egen förmåga driva arbetet framåt även när ramarna inte alltid är helt givna eller förutsägbara. Du kan snabbt anpassa dig efter nya omständigheter och förändra beslut och prioriteringar när det krävs. Vidare har du god förmåga att analysera och tolka kvantitativ data, strukturera och kvalitetssäkra data samt förstå vilken information som kan förädlas till data.

Då arbetet kommer att bedrivas i tvärfunktionella samarbeten ser vi vidare att du har god samarbetsförmåga och utbyter information och kunskaper i syfte att uppnå gemensamma mål.

Vid urvalet kommer vi att lägga stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.

En anställning hos oss kan innebära att befattningen placeras i säkerhetsklass. Säkerhetsprövning med eller utan registerkontroll kan därför komma att göras före beslut om anställning. För en del av våra arbetsuppgifter i säkerhetsklass krävs svenskt medborgarskap. Säkerhetsprövning genomförs enligt Säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585).

Vad vi erbjuder dig

Arbetsmiljöverket är en trygg och stabil arbetsgivare och en myndighet som hela tiden förändras med samhället vi verkar. Vi är stolta över att få använda vår kunskap och vårt förtroende i vårt viktiga samhällsuppdrag. Tillsammans skapar vi en inkluderande kultur, som rymmer hjärta, mod och hjärna. Hos oss blir hela du en viktig del i något större.

Anställningen är ett vikariat på ett år, på heltid och är för närvarande placerad på vårt kontor i Solna. Vi tillämpar individuell lönesättning och provanställning på sex månader.

Vi strävar efter att alla medarbetare ska ha en hållbar arbetsmiljö och en balans mellan jobb och fritid. Därför har vi flexibla arbetstider eller tillämpar förtroendearbetstid och ger möjlighet till distansarbete där verksamheten så tillåter.

Om oss och våra förmåner och villkor

Så rekryterar vi

Vi använder kompetensbaserad rekrytering, både för att kvalitetssäkra rekryteringen och för att motverka diskriminering. Vi ber dig därför att endast bifoga ett CV, och svara på några frågor istället för ett personligt brev när du registrerar din ansökan. Vi pratar svenska på myndigheten, därför ber vi dig att skicka din ansökan på svenska. Vi använder arbetspsykologiska tester i den här rekryteringen. Planen är att intervjuer sker omkring V. 35 - 36. Rekryterande chef och HR-partner återkommer från semester för att kunna svara på frågor från och med 1 augusti.

Du har också tillgång till vanliga frågor och svar: Om att jobba hos oss

Vi tycker att det är viktigt att även du får göra din bedömning av oss. Därför välkomnar vi frågor före såväl som under rekryteringsprocessen. Fackliga kontaktpersoner är Adam Jansson för Saco, Mats Johansson för ST samt Jenny Bengtsson för Seko. Samtliga nås via Arbetsmiljöverkets växel: 010-730 90 00.

Vill du jobba tillsammans med oss för att ingen ska bli sjuk, skadas eller dö av jobbet?

Välkommen med din ansökan senast den 5 augusti 2026!

Rekryteringen sköts av Arbetsmiljöverkets rekryteringsfunktion varför vi undanber oss säljsamtal från rekryteringsföretag eller annonssäljare.
