Uppdragen varierar, men du kommer typiskt att arbeta i miljöer där hybridlösningar möter molnet, där migrering från äldre plattformar är aktuellt och där datakvalitet och governance spelar allt större roll.


Vem är du?

Du är en självgående och nyfiken data-profil som trivs med att ta ansvar och driva tekniska lösningar framåt. Du är bekväm i dialog med såväl en teknisk arkitekt som med en verksamhetschef och du har koll på varför det du bygger faktiskt spelar roll.


Vi ser gärna att du har ett par eller flera av nedan punkter:

  • Flerårig erfarenhet som Data Engineer, gärna i konsultroll
  • Kompetens inom Microsofts dataplattformar - Azure Data Factory, Synapse Analytics och/eller Microsoft Fabric. Databricks går också bra!
  • Erfarenhet av datamodellering, ETL/ELT och dataintegration
  • Van att jobba med Kimball- och/eller Inmon-modellering
  • Förmåga att arbeta självständigt och driva tekniska lösningar framåt
  • Flytande svenska i tal och skrift, ett krav då uppdragen är mot svenska kunder


Låt oss prata! Vi uppskattar att du delar dina erfarenheter med oss. Nya möjligheter dyker upp kontinuerligt och om det inte passar perfekt just nu, kanske det passar perfekt längre fram.

Title: Advanced Pricing Analyst
Place: Vejle, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join AutoProff – Help Build Trust in the Price Models Powering Our European Growth! 🚗

We’re looking for an Advanced Pricing Analyst to join our team, based out of our Vejle headquarters.

At AutoProff, pricing is at the heart of how we serve customers and drive commercial performance. As our pricing capabilities continue to evolve, we need stronger ownership of how our price models perform in production. This role will be central to creating transparency around pricing quality, spotting issues before they become escalations, and ensuring we understand why our models behave the way they do.

As Advanced Pricing Analyst, you will own the reporting, monitoring, and investigation of price model performance across our production setup. You will help define the right benchmarks and reporting views to evaluate performance over time, identify weakspots or unusual behaviour, and support both existing and new model evaluations. You will also take the lead on investigating specific pricing cases raised by Pricing teams, using data and system-level understanding to determine whether issues stem from data quality, model behaviour, system integration, or whether the model output is in fact well supported by market observations.

This role sits at the intersection of pricing, analytics, and technical investigation. You will work closely with Pricing, Data Science, and other stakeholders to ensure that pricing behaviour is monitored, understood, and communicated clearly across the organisation. You will also contribute to building and strengthening the pricing setup that supports AutoProff’s continued growth across Europe.

Let’s dive into your key responsibilities, you will


Own the reporting and monitoring framework for price model performance in production, including defining the KPIs, benchmarks, and views needed to create a clear overview of model quality and behaviour.
Analyse model performance across segments, markets, and versions to identify weakspots, anomalies, drift, and other signals of deteriorating quality before they become business issues.
Lead investigations into unusual price outcomes and determine whether the underlying cause relates to data quality, model behaviour, system integration, or stakeholder interpretation.
Build a strong understanding of how price models are productionised and consumed through APIs, and validate that models are being supplied with the correct data, format, and values.
Communicate findings and recommendations clearly to Pricing, Data Science, and management, while supporting the quality assurance and benchmarking of existing and new price models.


The need-to-have skills


Strong SQL skills and experience working independently with large datasets to build reporting, monitor performance, and investigate pricing behaviour.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and visualisation skills, with the ability to structure investigations and turn complex findings into clear and actionable insights.
Hands-on Python experience for data investigation, exploratory analysis, and technical debugging.
A good understanding of how machine learning models work in practice, including how input quality, feature behaviour, formatting issues, or drift can affect outputs.
Comfort working with APIs, payloads, and technical system flows, combined with strong communication skills and confidence in challenging assumptions with data and evidence.


The nice-to-have skills


Experience working with pricing models, recommendation models, or other predictive models in production.
Experience investigating model-serving issues, feature quality issues, or data pipeline issues affecting live model outputs.
Familiarity with model monitoring concepts such as drift detection, segmentation analysis, and performance benchmarking over time.
Ability to reproduce or inspect price requests locally through technical tools, services, or APIs.
Experience working closely with Data Science teams and translating between analytical, technical, and business perspectives.


We’re eager to hear from you! If this sounds like a great match and sparks your interest, don’t wait, apply today!

About AutoProff

AutoProff was founded in 2013 with the aim to digitalize B2B used car trading, helping car dealers increase sales and improve profits.

in August 2022, AutoScout24, the leading specialized pan-European online car marketplace, acquired a majority stake in AutoProff. The partnership has supported the rapid expansion of AutoProff's solutions across Europe and enables the creation of Europe's first fully integrated C2B-B2B-B2C dealing solution for used cars.

At AutoProff, we specialize in providing online B2B used-car dealing solutions designed to improve the buying and selling experience for professional car dealers and their customers.

We are the emerging European leader with more than 150,000 B2B used cars on our online trading platforms annually.

With a focus on effective solutions, digitization and customer service, we are committed to being the home of B2B car dealing for European car dealers.

Title: Unsolicited: Business Intelligence & Data Science
Description:
Work With BI/data Science In An International Software Company
Are you motivated by making a difference and contributing to tomorrow’s BI/data science solutions? In which case you might be our new colleague.

You will create a strong foundation for strategic decisions
In your Business Intelligence or data science role, you will be working with various solutions that support Systematic’s business areas.

This involves the development of products and solutions for our customers and other business units – all in close collaboration with highly skilled colleagues.

Some of the tasks you will be working with involve:


Identifying and understanding the needs of both the customer and the product organisation through close dialogue
Using data to create value for our customers
Developing, coding and implementing BI/data science solutions
Cleaning and analysing data, and implementing and developing machine learning models
Facilitating workshops and building data models that support domains, user behaviour and software
Being part of a dynamic working environment characterised by collaboration, development and quality.



You can expect to work with a range of technologies, such as Microsoft Power BI, Python, Azure Cloud, Microsoft SQL Server, T-SQL, Microsoft SSIS and SSAS, data warehousing, data modelling and analysis.

High level of interdisciplinary expertise and collaboration
We are committed to acquiring knowledge and developing methods based on a well-functioning group of BI/data science colleagues.

We enjoy a very high level of job satisfaction, and the dynamics between experienced experts and young talents contribute to a high level of development and progress.

We work closely together across the different disciplines and areas of expertise, with teams comprising project managers, consultants, data scientists, software architects and business developers.

A key characteristic of the teams at Systematic is that we dare to challenge and develop ourselves and each other. We focus on knowledge-sharing in our work, and always find more innovative ways of doing things.

You understand the importance of BI and data science
You are creative, good at spotting opportunities and think out of the box. You have a solid understanding of the interplay between people, technologies and processes.

You have strong communication skills combined with sound business acumen, and you enjoy being involved in a wide variety of assignments.

You stay calm when faced with numerous tasks and requirements, and you maintain an overview and understand how to prioritise the tasks at every stage of the project.

You are organised, analytical and you are driven by the quality of your work. You enjoy presenting new ideas and have what it takes to garner the support of your colleagues and business partners.

At the same time, you are a team player who enjoys the prospect of actively contributing to the team and participating in development activities.

You are fluent in English, both orally and in writing. It is an advantage if you speak Danish, but this is not a requirement.

We work with security classified projects and therefore, as a Systematic employee, you need to have security clearance. Read more here:
https:
//systematic.com/en-gb/careers/security-clearance/ .

Any questions?

Please feel free to get in touch by emailing us at job@systematic.com. Unsolicited applications will continually be assessed according to current vacancies.
We store your details for up to six months, and if in that period your profile matches our requirements for a new job, you will be contacted to arrange an interview.



Connect with us!

Title: Data Analytics Specialist
Description:
Job Description:

What the job is:

At Satair , data and analytics are among our most powerful levers for driving a culture of continuous improvement across the entire organization. They play a vital role in enhancing operational efficiency, boosting competitiveness, engaging employees, and most importantly, delivering outstanding customer satisfaction.

As a Data Analytics Specialist , you will join our dynamic Data & Infrastructure team , where we are dedicated to empowering the business with self-service tools, robust data foundations, and scalable cloud infrastructure solutions. Our mission also includes upskilling teams across the organization to build long-term capabilities and promote a data-driven mindset.

In this role, you will collaborate closely with functional teams across all areas of the Satair organization. You’ll act as a trusted advisor, partnering with business analysts and domain experts to provide technological insights, define data standards, conduct diagnostics, and apply advanced analytics to optimize processes and maximize value for our customers, as well as making sure the underlying infrastructure and pipelines are running smoothly and as effective as possible.

Our team is truly global, reflecting Satair’s international footprint with colleagues and offices all across the globe. This means you will work in small, agile, cross-functional teams, focused on delivering practical, high-impact solutions side-by-side with the business.

Task and responsibilities:


Improve and maintain our key pipelines running on the Airbus Skywise platform - a customized version of Palantir Foundry. Main tasks will be focusing on optimisation, automation and improving the backend pipelines and flows.
Support the business with in-depth data analysis and experimentation to uncover valuable business insights and support data-driven decision-making.
Engage with functional teams to understand end-to-end processes, identify pain points, and uncover opportunities for improvement through data and emerging technologies.
Translate analysis into actionable outcomes by developing automated tools, visual dashboards, reports, or workflows that enhance productivity and business value.
Design and deliver Minimum Viable Products (MVPs) to quickly test, validate, and address critical business challenges.
Act as a coach and advisor to cross-functional teams, guiding them on data quality improvement plans, and supporting them in developing their own MVPs and analytics use cases.


How will you contribute to the team:

We are looking for a dynamic, business-savvy individual who is passionate about data and technology, with a strong drive to deliver impactful results through collaboration and innovation.


You are a quick learner, passionate about your work and driven to get things done with a collaborative way of working.
You bring a pragmatic, solution-oriented mindset-comfortable with rapid experimentation and iteration when building digital products and tools.
You understand the intersection of data and business processes and are passionate about solving real-world industrial and operational challenges.
Robust coding abilities (Python, SQL, Java) & experience in working with APIs, algorithms, data structures would be a plus.
Experience with DevOps tools / modern cloud stack & practices would be a plus.
Experience working with Data Mesh principles such as decentralized data ownership, domain-aligned data products, and self-serve data infrastructure would be a strong plus.
Prior experience with Patantir Foundry would be a big plu
A university degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field is preferred.
3-5 years of experience in an analytics, data science, or similar role within a complex business environment.
Strong knowledge of English, both orally and in writing


What does your next challenge look like?

At SATAIR, we believe in sustainable aviation that allows people to travel and connect across the globe.

We are on a mission to provide world class material solutions that keep aircraft flying and extend their lifespan.

In Satair, you can challenge the status quo, pioneer new ways of working, and generate real value for airlines and suppliers worldwide.

Being part of the Airbus family gives us the opportunity to live a global mindset, working in international and diverse teams and collaborating across functions, sites and cultures.

If you want to work as one team embracing a can-do attitude in a demanding environment, then we offer a great place to explore, learn, and grow, making an impact on aviation’s digital and sustainable transformation.

Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company!

About SATAIR

Satair is an Airbus Services company offering first-class material management solutions in the aviation aftermarket industry. We take pride in delivering an excellent customer experience around the clock, driving innovation, and contributing to aviation’s digital and sustainable transformation.

With our global functional organisation spanning 12 sites and locations worldwide, and a team of over 2,000 people supporting a $3.9 billion turnover, you will be part of a truly global company that continues to grow year after year.

Welcome to the Satair family!

If you want to know more about our business, have a closer look at our website www.SATAIR.com or check out our SATAIR Youtube Channel at www.youtube.com/sataircompany !

What do we offer at SATAIR?

At SATAIR, you will find much more than a job - we offer you the possibility of growing both your personal and professional capacities in a fun, supportive and informal working environment, where you will be encouraged to engage, share knowledge and ideas.

SATAIR is committed to achieving workforce diversity and creating an inclusive working environment. We love to bring in people with different backgrounds and experiences as we believe it enhances our performance immensely!

Here is a selection of some of the benefits we offer our employees in Copenhagen:

Financial Benefits: Competitive salary, Employee Stock Ownership Program, Success sharing, Health insurance, Group accident insurance, Employee discounts to local businesses

Employee Health and Wellbeing: On-site gym, App-based health & wellbeing platform, Amazing canteen at a subsided price, Massage scheme

Learning and Development: Airbus Leadership University, Large number of e-learning and training offers for personal and professional development, Promotion of professional development (e.g. postgraduate studies, technician training), Possibility of mentoring, Gain international experience

Work-life blend and life phase support: Possibility to work remotely, 4 care days, Senior days, Working from home policy (2 days remote per week)

Come on board and join us at Satair, an Airbus Services Company

Please send us your application as soon as possible to get the opportunity to become part of an exceptional global team!

We welcome all applications irrespective of social and cultural background, age, gender, disability, sexual orientation or religious belief.

When applying for the position, you accept our data privacy statement: http://www.SATAIR.com/privacy

This job requires an awareness of any potential compliance risks and a commitment to act with integrity, as the foundation for the Company’s success, reputation and sustainable growth.

Permanent

Professional

Title: Data Analyst - 10-month maternity cover
Description:
Driving Digital Transformation Through Insightful Analytics – Viby J.

Do you enjoy turning complex data into trusted insights that help an international business make better decisions? We are looking for a Data Analyst to join STARK Group IT in a 10-month maternity cover position from 1 October 2026 until 31 July 2027. As part of one of Northern Europe's largest digital transformation programmes, you will help turn business needs into trusted data, actionable insights, and scalable analytics solutions used across STARK Group.

If you are motivated by creating clarity from complexity and enjoy diving into data with a delivery focused mindset, this could be the role for you.

This role is based in Viby J, Aarhus.

Your Impact And What You Will Deliver

You will join the STARK Group IT Data Management team, consisting of four Data Analysts and two Data Management Specialists, where you will collaborate closely with experienced colleagues and contribute to continuously strengthening our data management and data analysis capabilities.

While you will be part of the Data Management team, your day-to-day work will be closely integrated with project teams and business stakeholders. The role follows a hybrid working model with three days per week in the office and two days working from home. To support collaboration and knowledge sharing, the Data Management team works together from the office every Tuesday. The remaining office days are typically spent alongside your assigned project team and key stakeholders.

In this role, you will work closely with Product Owners, business stakeholders, architects, data engineers, and BI developers. You will play a key role in translating business needs into data requirements, identifying and validating data sources, establishing source-to-target mappings, and helping ensure a common understanding of business rules, KPIs, and data definitions across business and technical teams.

A key assignment during this maternity cover will be supporting reporting initiatives within Athene, STARK Group's large-scale digital transformation programme consisting of 16 interconnected projects across the business. Working across multiple projects and stakeholders, you will help ensure that reporting needs are understood early, data dependencies are identified, and future reporting solutions are supported by reliable and well-structured data.

In this role you will:


Translate business needs into clear data requirements, user stories, acceptance criteria, and supporting documentation.
Identify, assess, and validate data sources, source-to-target mappings and data transformation logic together with business and technical stakeholders.
Use SQL and analytical tools to explore, analyse, and validate data while identifying patterns, discrepancies, and data quality issues.
Define and document KPIs, metrics, calculation logic, business rules, and data definitions to ensure a shared understanding across teams.
Support Agile delivery activities such as backlog refinement, sprint planning, and change assessments by providing analytical input and data expertise.


You will be successful in this role when stakeholders experience you as a trusted analytical partner who brings structure, clarity, and practical insight into complex data topics. By ensuring that requirements are actionable solutions, data mappings and definitions are well understood, and data is reliable and fit for purpose, you help create confidence in both the data and the decisions built upon it.

What You Bring

We are looking for someone who combines strong analytical skills with curiosity, drive, and a desire to make an impact from day one. You are comfortable navigating ambiguity, asking questions when requirements need clarification, and balancing business value, data quality, and pragmatic delivery.

You bring:


Experience working as a Data Analyst, Business Analyst, or in a similar data-focused role, preferably within a complex or international organization
Strong analytical skills, including experience working with requirements gathering, data validation, data mapping data quality, KPIs, metrics, and business rules.
Solid SQL skills and experience analysing and validating data.
Strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to communicate effectively across business and technical stakeholders.
A structured, collaborative, and delivery-focused mindset, with the ability to work independently and drive analysis activities from clarification to implementation.


You will work with tools including Azure Databricks, Power BI, Azure DevOps, Kafka, and Open Metadata (Data catalog). To set you up for success in this role, we expect you to bring hands-on experience with several of these technologies and the ability to quickly apply your knowledge in a business context.

In addition, experience with Python, ServiceNow, Scrum or Kanban, or finance-related business processes is an advantage but not a requirement. We expect you to hold a relevant Masters degree within IT, Data & Analytics, Business Administration, Finance, Engineering, or a related field. Equivalent practical experience may also be considered.

Welcome to Group IT

When you join Group IT, you join a collaborative international organisation where technology and business go hand in hand. We are replacing legacy systems and building a modern, modular IT landscape to support STARK Group's continued growth. You will become part of a community that values knowledge sharing, professionalism, and building solutions that make a clear difference to colleagues across Europe.

Want to know more and apply?

We will be happy to answer any questions you may have regarding the position and your options in STARK Group. You are welcome to contact Kristine Grann at Kristine.grann@stark.dk for more information.

As many of our colleagues will be on summer holiday throughout July, please do not expect your application to be reviewed during this period. We will resume the recruitment process in early to mid-August.

STARK Group 

STARK Group is a growing private equity-owned company with a strong legacy. STARK Group was first established in Denmark in 1896 and has today evolved into a leading European retailer and distributor of heavy building materials with net sales of approx. EUR 9 billion. With a dedicated team of more than 20,000 employees and +1,150 branches and distribution centres across the Nordics, Greenland, Germany, Austria and the UK, we live and breathe to serve professional craftsmen with heavy building materials all the way from the Austrian Alps to Tromsø, close to the Arctic circle and everywhere in between. STARK Group is committed to reach net zero emissions by 2050 across the entire business, is a signatory to the UN Global Compact, and works within fully 2030 validated science-based targets to keep global warming below 1.5°C At STARK Group IT, we believe in the richness diversity brings. We invite individuals of all backgrounds to join us in creating and inclusive and welcoming environment. We believe in a workplace where everyone can bring their authentic selves to work. 

 

Agency Policy 

STARK Group IT appreciates the interest of all candidates. However, we prefer to directly engage with individuals seeking employment opportunities. Therefore, we kindly request recruitment agencies, head-hunters, and third-party staffing services to refrain from contacting us regarding this job posting. All applications should be submitted directly by the candidates through our official application process. We value direct communication and look forward to reviewing your application.

Title: Data Analyst - Trust
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Trustpilot, we're on an incredible journey. We're a profitable, high-growth FTSE-250 company with a big vision: to become the universal symbol of trust. We run the world's largest open customer review platform, and while we've come a long way, there's still so much exciting work to do. Come join us at the heart of trust!

Trustpilot’s growth has been exponential - from a successful IPO to building out new teams, we never stop working hard to fulfill our mission to create better experiences for everyone through our platform. But to do that, we need data! That’s where Analytics comes in - you’ll help us measure, uncover insights, and predict, to get data everywhere in Trustpilot. We’ve built a world-class analytics team that spans two continents and four cities - are you ready to join a fun, diverse global team?

 

Trustpilot is on a mission to create better experiences for everyone through our platform. But to do that, we need to protect the integrity of our community and deeply understand how our consumers perceive trust. We’ve built a world-class analytics team that spans continents and cities - and now, we’re looking for a data analyst to support our regulatory duty and protect trust for our platform and consumers.

 

Trust

This team is the guardian of our platform. They use data to stay one step ahead of bad actors, working with the teams that build the models and detection systems that identify fake reviews and ensure Trustpilot remains a universal symbol of integrity.

 

It is a huge part of what we do at Trustpilot - it’s in the name! We need to ensure that the content on our platform is real content from real people. That means eliminating scam and spam, and identifying fraudulent behavior. We have several cross functional teams devoted to Trust. You’ll be tightly aligned to their goals, helping teams such as Content Integrity, Legal & Compliance, Corporate Comms & Investor Relations, Fraud & Investigations to investigate requests on fraudulent activity, prepare various reports including core publicly shared company and regulatory reports.

 

As a Data Analyst at Trustpilot, you will collaborate with Trusties in our Trust, Comms and Marketing teams and spend your time digging through our data to provide them with data-driven insights, as well as analytics and dashboards/reporting. You’re keen to help them use data to answer their toughest questions, and you can proactively identify areas that need a little data support. You’ll be an internal data consultant, with loads of amazing data in our data warehouse at your fingertips, working with analytical tools including BigQuery and Looker.  

 

What you’ll be doing:

Be the main contact point for all regulatory and compliance reporting
Delivering expert analytics and insights to different parts of the business, through both workstream-driven initiatives and ad-hoc inquiries
Building data sets and dashboards that support our Trust data integrity and auditability requirements
Picking the right solution for the problem you’ve got, from your arsenal of analytical tools and methods
Working closely with the Comms, Investor Relations, Marketing, Compliance or Trust teams to understand their data requests and their goals to deliver awesome solutions
Presenting and promoting your work to the wider business, to enable data-informed decision making
Scoping and documenting projects
Standardising and automating key reports ensuring integrity, governance and data quality

 

Who you are:

Knowledge of reporting best practices and experience delivering actionable insights leveraging BI tools (we use Looker)
Experience in stakeholder and project management, with the confidence to deliver results back to the business
Advanced SQL skills (we use BigQuery)
Data modelling experience: DBT or similar tools used for building data pipelines
Experience with Python is desirable 
Familiarity of key analytical and statistical methodologies, such as model-based analyses (regression, clustering etc.), cohort analysis 
Familiarity with Airflow 
Familiarity with A/B testing
Outstanding written and verbal communication skills

About us

Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever — to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial — we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.

Today, we have more than 350 million reviews and 60 million monthly active users across the globe, with 149 billion annual Trustpilot brand impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1000 employees and we’re headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in  Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York. 

We’re driven by connection. It’s at the heart of what we do. Our culture keeps things fresh –– it’s built on the relationships we create. We talk, we laugh, we collaborate and we respect each other. We work across borders and cultures to be the universal symbol of trust in an ever-changing world. With vibrant office locations worldwide and over 50 nationalities, we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace with diverse perspectives and ideas. 

Our purpose to help people and businesses help each other is a tall order, but we keep it real. We’re a great bunch of humans, doing awesome stuff, without fuss or pretense. A successful Trustpilot future is driven by you –– we give you the autonomy to shape a career you can be proud of. If you’re ready to grow, let’s go.

Join us at the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive and where different viewpoints and experiences are valued and respected. Trustpilot will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, religious beliefs, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, neurodiversity, disability, age, parental or veteran status. Together, we are the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is a global company and our data practices are designed to ensure that your personally identifiable information is appropriately protected. Please note that your personal information will be transferred, accessed, and stored globally as necessary for the uses and disclosures stated in our Privacy Policy.

If you have a disability and would like to discuss any adjustments you might need either in submitting your application, or to the recruitment process more generally, please let us know by contacting our Talent Acquisition Team (talent.acquisition@trustpilot.com) quoting the role you wish to apply for.

Title: Product Analytics Engineer
Place: Aarhus Municipality, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
The role

We are looking for a Product Analytics Engineer to join our product team in Aarhus.

This is not a role where you only build pipelines. You own Candeno's data and analytics layer within your domain: the data warehouse, the BI and dashboards, and the instrumentation that turns product usage and AI interactions into reliable data. Part of the work is figuring out the right question before you build the answer. The rest is building it well.




We work AI-augmented. Modern AI tools are a natural part of how we work, so more of the routine building is handled for you. That makes the harder, more valuable work the judgement: what to measure, how to model it, and whether the numbers can be trusted. We use AI to move faster, and we believe people are what make sure we build the right things and trust the right data.




Your work will reach hundreds of thousands of people across the Nordics: job seekers navigating labour market reforms, employment professionals adapting to new digitalisation strategies, and the institutions managing that change. The dashboards and data you build inform decisions inside Candeno and in the hands of our customers, so keeping them trustworthy matters.




What you will work on

Candeno serves municipalities, unemployment funds, and educational institutions across Denmark, Norway, Sweden, and Finland. The platform has three sides: candidates navigating the labour market, advisors guiding them through digitalisation and compliance workflows, and companies recruiting talent. One platform, many contexts. Our products increasingly run on data and AI, and this role builds the foundation underneath them.




Discovery (analysis and design)

•    Work with product people and customers to understand what a dashboard or a data model actually needs to answer, before you build it. AI helps us move fast, so the harder work is knowing what is worth measuring.

•    Model the data. Decide access patterns and weigh the tradeoffs between flexibility and control, with architecture direction from our Lead Architect.




Delivery (build and operate)

•    Build and maintain the data warehouse, data pipelines, and ETL or ELT flows between Candeno's platform and the warehouse.

•    Build and maintain internal and customer-facing dashboards, so the right data reaches the right audience in a form that drives decisions.

•    Instrument our AI chats, agents, and product usage so requests and usage signals become structured, first-class data, and work with engineers to keep it accessible for AI and ML use.

•    Write clean, maintainable code, using AI tools where they help and applying judgement to what they produce.




Quality and craft

•    Keep data trustworthy. Care about data quality and make it something the team can rely on.

•    Meet regulatory requirements in a data-sensitive sector, with guidance from our legal advisor.

•    Share what you know and help raise data competence across the team. This is knowledge-sharing, not people management.




Who we are looking for

You are a data and analytics engineer who is as comfortable with the question as with the query. Your foundation is solid delivery in data and BI, and your edge is knowing what is worth measuring before you build it.




You will need:

•    A few years building and maintaining data warehouses and BI or dashboards, ideally in SaaS or a product company. Full build experience matters more than years.

•    Solid data modelling skills, and comfort navigating the tradeoffs between flexibility and control.

•    Experience building data pipelines and ETL or ELT flows. Modern transformation tooling such as dbt is a plus.

•    An understanding of what AI and ML need from data, in structure and quality. You can grow into this.

•    A considered approach to AI in your workflow. You use modern tools well, and you check what they produce critically.

•    Clear communication. You can explain a data tradeoff to a product manager, an engineer, and a customer, and make it feel simple.

•    Comfort working with customers directly.




The kind of person who does well here:

•    Curious about the question, not only the query.

•    Ownership mindset. You follow a problem all the way through.

•    Comfortable making decisions without a perfect foundation, and honest about the tradeoffs when you do.

•    Direct. You share work early, give feedback clearly, and push back when something is not right.




What the first six months look like

After one month

You understand Candeno's platform, our BI setup and data flows, and the team. You have taken ownership of your first scoped tasks and you are active in how the team works.




After three months

You are building key parts of the warehouse and BI independently and contributing to product and AI instrumentation. You are starting to engage with customer analytics needs, with support.




After six months

You reliably own and deliver within the data and analytics domain. The dashboards and instrumentation you build are in real use, and you are a dependable go-to for product analytics questions, working within architecture direction from the Lead Architect.




Working at Candeno

We are a scale-up. That means real ownership, real impact, and a product that is still being shaped by the people who work on it.

We are based in Aarhus, and this is an on-site role. The team is small, cross-functional, and works closely together. You will have real ownership of your work and close proximity to product, engineering, and our customers.

We are direct and focused on doing the right thing for users. We share work early and expect you to do the same.




What we offer

•    Pension scheme and health insurance

•    5 extra days off after 2 years of seniority (10 in total)

•    Daily lunch from Meyers Madhus

•    A shared milestone programme that celebrates successes big and small

•    An active social life: team events, clubs, and an engaged social committee




The process

•    A short screening call

•    A first interview, a deeper conversation about your experience and the role

•    A case interview, a practical brief sent in advance

No cover letter. You will answer three short questions when you apply.




About Candeno

Candeno is a Nordic employment technology company based in Aarhus. We build the platform that connects candidates, advisors, and companies across municipalities, unemployment funds, and educational institutions in Denmark, Norway, Sweden, and Finland. Our work sits at the intersection of public sector compliance and modern product thinking, and we take both seriously.

You reliably own and deliver within the data and analytics domain. The dashboards and instrumentation you build are in real use, and you are a dependable go-to for product analytics questions, working within architecture direction from the Lead Architect.


Working at Candeno

We are a scale-up. That means real ownership, real impact, and a product that is still being shaped by the people who work on it.

We are based in Aarhus, and this is an on-site role. The team is small, cross-functional, and works closely together. You will have real ownership of your work and close proximity to product, engineering, and our customers.

We are direct and focused on doing the right thing for users. We share work early and expect you to do the same.


What we offer

•    Pension scheme and health insurance

•    5 extra days off after 2 years of seniority (10 in total)

•    Daily lunch from Meyers Madhus

•    A shared milestone programme that celebrates successes big and small

•    An active social life: team events, clubs, and an engaged social committee


The process

•    A short screening call

•    A first interview, a deeper conversation about your experience and the role

•    A case interview, a practical brief sent in advance

No cover letter. You will answer three short questions when you apply.


About Candeno

Candeno is a Nordic employment technology company based in Aarhus. We build the platform that connects candidates, advisors, and companies across municipalities, unemployment funds, and educational institutions in Denmark, Norway, Sweden, and Finland. Our work sits at the intersection of public sector compliance and modern product thinking, and we take both seriously.

Title: Data Portfolio Analyst for Markets Data Office
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Copenhagen HQ, Denmark

Looking for your next career opportunity within Data Governance, where you can expand your critical data expertise in a fintech organisation?

Join us in building a new data capability in Markets

At Saxo, we are building the Markets Data Office as part of the Data Governance Programme. The function is structured across four domain areas Products, Operations, Counterparty Risk, and Market Risk reflecting how critical data is used across Markets.

As a Data Portfolio Analyst, you will work within one of these domain areas and help improve how critical data is defined, monitored, and controlled. Your work will contribute to establishing consistent ways of working across the Markets Data Office, while supporting domain-specific needs. While your focus will be within a specific domain area, you will operate as part of a single Markets Data Office with shared standards, tooling, and governance practices.

This is an opportunity to join early, shape how we work, and make a real impact on how data is governed at scale.

What You Will Do

You will work close to business stakeholders, data owners, data stewards, and technology teams to improve the quality and governance of critical data within your assigned domain area. Depending on the domain, this may include data such as instrument reference data, market data, price data, custody information, election data, trades, positions, margin utilisation, or model data.

Your Responsibilities Will Include


Identify and document Critical Data Attributes (CDAs) and support the governance of Critical Data Elements (CDEs) in your domain area
Analyse data to assess quality, identify issues, and support root cause investigations
Define and maintain data definitions, rules, thresholds, and supporting documentation used in monitoring and control activities
Contribute to ensuring data is accurate, consistent, and fit for business use
Monitor quality outputs, track remediation activities, and escalate blockers where data issues are not being resolved
Collaborate with data owners, stewards, and stakeholders across business and technology
Support the implementation of data governance practices and controls


A key part of the role is ensuring that documentation is clear, structured, and maintainable, enabling transparency and consistency across the organisation.

Who You Are

We are looking for someone who is structured, curious, and motivated to work with data in a business context:


Experience in data analysis, data quality, or a related field
Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail
Ability to understand data in the context of business processes
Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across teams
Ability to produce clear and structured documentation
Experience with SQL or similar tools is an advantage
Experience with Great Expectation or Databricks is an advantage


What We Offer


The opportunity to help build a new data function from the ground up
Exposure to key business domains and critical data flows
A collaborative and learning-focused environment
A role where your work directly improves decision-making and operational quality


You will join Markets Data Office in the early stages of its journey, where your role will grow alongside the organisation. This means exposure to new challenges, increasing responsibility, and the opportunity to shape how data is managed across the business.

Inclusion at Saxo

We are committed to creating an inclusive workplace where different perspectives are valued. We encourage applications from all backgrounds and experiences.

We get curious people invested in the world

When you work at Saxo, you become a Saxonian and part of a purpose-driven organisation, where good ideas are always taken seriously, and where you can make a true impact. We are invested in your development, and you can expect a robust career from day one when you join Saxo – no matter which role you take on.

You will join 2,500 other ambitious colleagues across 15 countries and become part of an international organisation. Working in Saxo, you will get to meet colleagues from many different cultures and backgrounds, and you should know that we value diversity and inclusion and see it as a genuine source of strength to drive growth, foster innovation and position us for long-term success.

We encourage an open feedback culture and supportive team environments enabling employees to grow and fulfil their career aspirations.

When you bring passion, curiosity, drive and team spirit, your learning journey will be dynamic and your career opportunities in Saxo will be immense.

At Saxo we don’t just offer a job – we offer an opportunity to invest in your future!

How To Apply

Click here to create an account and upload your resume and a short motivation. We look forward to getting to know you better!

Title: Fraud Detection Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Trustpilot, we're on an incredible journey. We're a profitable, high-growth FTSE-250 company with a big vision: to become the universal symbol of trust. We run the world's largest open customer review platform, and while we've come a long way, there's still so much exciting work to do. Come join us at the heart of trust!

We’re looking for a curious and analytical Fraud Detection Analyst to join our global Fraud & Investigations team. You’ll analyse data, spot patterns, and write detection rules that help stop fraud in its tracks — playing a vital role in protecting consumers, businesses, and the integrity of our platform.

You’ll work on complex, often ambiguous challenges in the ever-evolving world of online trust. If you love solving problems, diving into data, and making a real difference - this is your chance.

You’ll be joining a collaborative, inquisitive team that values transparency, fairness, and a good sense of humour. As part of the wider Trust & Transparency team, we’re driving change across the business—and working to make Trustpilot the universal symbol of trust online.

What you’ll be doing:

Identify fraud trends and develop a deep understanding of fraudulent behavior on our platform through the use of SQL, Google BigQuery, and Google Looker.
Formulate rules based on your analysis to mitigate fraudulent behavior across our platform.
Carry out investigations of escalated cases related to potential misuse of our platform and assist with media, legal, and customer inquiries.
Partner with our data science experts to advance our technological and procedural approaches to identifying fraudulent activity.
Work closely with our engineering teams to enhance internal tools and databases that enable us to combat fraud and scale effectively.
Act as a key resource for other departments within Trustpilot by providing analysis of customer and reviewer behavior on the platform.
Present and communicate the results of analyses and investigations to a variety of stakeholders across the department and wider company.
Work closely with and report to our Lead Fraud & Investigation Analyst.

Who you are:

Experience in data analytics or a similar analytical role—this could be through formal education (e.g., a degree in Science, Mathematics, Computer Science) or equivalent practical experience.
Comfortable working with and interpreting large datasets.
A knack for answering difficult questions using data and the ability to translate complex findings into clear, actionable reports.
Proficiency in writing queries and preparing reports using SQL or a similar language.
Motivated by self-learning and the desire to grow your skills when faced with new challenges.
Prior knowledge of formulating rules to combat fraudulent behavior is a plus, but not a requirement.
The ability to manage multiple priorities effectively with a proactive, forward-thinking mindset

What’s in it for you:


A range of flexible working options to dedicate time to what matters to you
Competitive compensation package + bonus
25 days holiday per year, with an extra 5 days holiday allocated after your 1 year anniversary (prorated) 
Two (paid) volunteering days a year to spend your time giving back to the causes that matter to you and your community
Rich learning and development opportunities are supported through the Trustpilot Academy and Blinkist
Comprehensive health package, pension, and full access to Headspace, a popular mindfulness app to promote positive mental health
Paid parental leave
Central office with  a laid-back vibe and constant buzz of different languages being spoken everywhere you go It’s complete with a coffee bar, canteen, and table tennis and has a wide variety of refreshments available - you can opt-in for breakfast and lunch at an affordable price
Regular opportunities to connect and get to know your fellow Trusties, including company-wide celebrations and events, ERG activities, and team socials
Opportunity to join the Trustpilot Social Club for a small monthly fee and attend regular social and leisure activities like go-karting, cooking classes, and escape rooms 
Employee discounts to restaurants and shops 

Still not sure?

We want to be a part of creating a more diverse, equitable, and inclusive world of work for all. We’re excited to hear about your experiences as well as how you will contribute to our working culture. So, even if you don’t feel you don't meet all the requirements, we'd still really like to hear from you!

About us

Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever — to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial — we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.

Today, we have more than 350 million reviews and 60 million monthly active users across the globe, with 149 billion annual Trustpilot brand impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1000 employees and we’re headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in  Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York. 

We’re driven by connection. It’s at the heart of what we do. Our culture keeps things fresh –– it’s built on the relationships we create. We talk, we laugh, we collaborate and we respect each other. We work across borders and cultures to be the universal symbol of trust in an ever-changing world. With vibrant office locations worldwide and over 50 nationalities, we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace with diverse perspectives and ideas. 

Our purpose to help people and businesses help each other is a tall order, but we keep it real. We’re a great bunch of humans, doing awesome stuff, without fuss or pretense. A successful Trustpilot future is driven by you –– we give you the autonomy to shape a career you can be proud of. If you’re ready to grow, let’s go.

Join us at the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive and where different viewpoints and experiences are valued and respected. Trustpilot will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, religious beliefs, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, neurodiversity, disability, age, parental or veteran status. Together, we are the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is a global company and our data practices are designed to ensure that your personally identifiable information is appropriately protected. Please note that your personal information will be transferred, accessed, and stored globally as necessary for the uses and disclosures stated in our Privacy Policy.

If you have a disability and would like to discuss any adjustments you might need either in submitting your application, or to the recruitment process more generally, please let us know by contacting our Talent Acquisition Team (talent.acquisition@trustpilot.com) quoting the role you wish to apply for.

Title: Advanced Data Analyst with focus on semantic data at Novo Nordisk Engineering
Place: Virum, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Advanced Data Analyst with focus on semantic data for Data Engineering department at Novo Nordisk Engineering

If you’re energised by bringing structure to complex data and enabling a shared understanding across disciplines, and you thrive on bridging business needs with technical solutions, we’d love to hear from you!

Who We Are

We are a leading pharma engineering company shaping the future of API pharma engineering globally. Leveraging the expertise of 2,000+ employees across Denmark, India, and the US, we design, build and optimise pharmaceutical manufacturing facilities that are essential to the health and well-being of patients everywhere.

Our success is driven by passion, innovation, and collaboration — with our customers and across our teams and disciplines. If you share our dedication to a noble cause and our passion for API pharma engineering, then NNE is the place for you.

About The Role

We are looking for an Advanced Data Analyst with focus on semantic data to join our Engineering Data & Systems team of 4 passionate colleagues.

As a team, we are working to strengthen the data foundation across engineering projects and support the transition from document-based to data-driven work. We focus on improving the structure and quality of data and enabling better decision-making through dashboards and visualizations.

You will act as the bridge between business and IT – translating engineering needs into semantic concepts and models, while collaborating closely with Knowledge Graph Engineers in IT responsible for technical implementation.

You will own and drive the semantic agenda from a business perspective, ensuring that our semantic models are usable, adopted, and create real value in projects.

Location Denmark - Virum

Experience Minimum 3 Years

Department Data Engineering & Systems

How You Might Spend Your Days

As a Data Analyst with focus on semantic data, you’ll be:


Defining and driving the semantic data structure from a business perspective, ensuring it reflects real operational needs
Facilitating alignment on terminology, concepts, and relationships across disciplines to enable a shared data understanding
Leading the development of ontology models with domain experts and translate business knowledge into structured, implementable data models
Collaborating closely with IT and Knowledge Engineers to ensure correct and scalable technical implementation
Establishing guidelines and best practices while driving consistent adoption and use of semantic models across projects and teams



Who You Are

We care about who you are as a person. In the end, how you work, and your energy is what impacts the results we achieve as a team. As a person, you are:


Possessing a structured and analytical mindset with the ability to structure complex information into clear models
Thriving in a role that requires a proactive approach and taking ownership of tasks from start to finish
A skilled facilitator and communicator that can translate between business needs and technical representation
Comfortable working across disciplines with both internal and external stakeholders
Motivated by creating clarity in changeable and at times complex environment



What You Bring To The Role

In all positions, there are some things that are preferred, and others, a bonus. If you do not meet all the qualifications listed below, you are welcome to offer alternative experience that you think would be valuable in this role:


Minimum 3-5 years of relevant experience within data modelling, semantics, or similar disciplines
Relevant educational degree within data, engineering, IT or similar
Great knowledge of business processes and data structures
Proven experience with stakeholder management across domains including facilitating workshops and driving alignment
Fluency in English



Life at NNE

NNE is short for Novo Nordisk Engineering. And for all of us who work here, it means so much more.

At NNE, you become part of a vibrant, project-based environment built on close day-to-day collaboration. The role is therefore primarily on-site, with physical presence at the workplace expected five days a week. Limited flexibility for occasional remote work may be accommodated in line with business needs.

We are, at our core, a people business and, as such, committed to continuously developing our people and the way we work together. We do not expect you to know it all from day one, and we truly believe a great start makes a real difference. We therefore welcome new colleagues with our award-nominated onboarding programme, supported by a range of in-house talent and development initiatives designed to support your long-term growth.

You can read more about the benefits we offer here .

Did we spark your curiosity? If you’re ready to join our passion, let’s hear from you.

Deadline of application is 22th July 2026

Questions before applying? Send us a quick text to arrange a convenient time to chat. Contact Manager, Mayes Ali on +45 3191 4302 or Director, Niels Karim Høst-Madsen on +45 3075 2705.

Between week 28-30 you can expect a delay in reply due to summer holiday. Therefore, we encourage you to call before Monday 6 July if you have questions.

We kindly refrain from questions regarding the potential fit into the role - we encourage you to apply to the position even if you are a bit in doubt. You might just be the one we are looking for!

If you have any questions regarding the application process, please write to recruit@nne.com and we will assist you as soon as possible.

Please note:


We only accept applications submitted through our online recruitment system
You do not need to attach a cover letter, but please include a few sentences about why you are applying in your resume or CV
We invite to interviews on a running basis.



Fair hiring:

At NNE, we are committed to an inclusive recruitment process and equal opportunities for all job applicants. To maintain fairness, we request that you refrain from including a personal photo in your CV.

Still searching for the right opportunity?

If this position isn’t quite what you're looking for, but you know someone who would excel in this role, please share this job post. And don’t forget to explore our other exciting opportunities — your future role at NNE might be just around the corner! Visit our Career Site

  • You do not need to attach a cover letter, but please include a few sentences about why you are applying in your resume or CV
  • We invite to interviews on a running basis.


Fair hiring:

At NNE, we are committed to an inclusive recruitment process and equal opportunities for all job applicants. To maintain fairness, we request that you refrain from including a personal photo in your CV.

Still searching for the right opportunity?

If this position isn’t quite what you're looking for, but you know someone who would excel in this role, please share this job post. And don’t forget to explore our other exciting opportunities — your future role at NNE might be just around the corner! Visit our Career Site

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Kolding, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Every day, we get opportunities to make a positive impact – on our colleagues, partners, customers and society. Together, we’re pioneering the solutions of the future and unlocking the full potential of precious resources. Trusted to act on initiative, we challenge conventional thinking to develop world-leading technologies that inspire progress in vital areas, including energy, food, water and shipping.

As we push forward, the innovative, open spirit that fuels our 140-year-old start-up culture and rapid growth also drives our personal growth. So, as we shape a more resourceful, less wasteful world, we build our careers too.

About The Job

As a Data Analyst, you’ll provide data analytics and support for business and process data, working with large datasets (including product master data in PLM and ERP systems). You’ll be part of a cross-functional R&D team (60+ colleagues), collaborating closely with manufacturing, distribution, and product management teams.

Responsibilities Include


Data analysis and exploration
Collaborate with cross functional teams to understand the data
Acquire, preprocess, and clean data from various sources to ensure quality and reliability
Analyze business and process data to identify trends and patterns impacting operations
Create informative dashboards and visualizations (primarily in Excel, Power BI).
Stay current with industry trends and best practices


What You Bring


Bachelor’s degree in information systems, Engineering, or similar, or equivalent experience.
Strong skills in Excel (required) and Power BI (nice to have).
Understanding of PLM (Product Lifecycle Management) and product master data management.
Experience in data governance and data quality management.
Language skills: English and Danish (preferred)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Excellent organizational and cross-functional collaboration skills.
Ability to manage and drive changes in a global, multi-site manufacturing environment.
Effective communication and training abilities.


Who You Are


Systematic, detail-oriented, and collaborative. You thrive in diverse teams, balance independence with teamwork, and contribute positively to our culture.
You have a keen eye for detail, strong data discipline, and the ability to understand and manage complex data structures across multiple platforms.


We are conducting a continuous review of the applications received. We do not accept applications via email, they will be deleted, due to General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

Please send your application and resume, no later than 18th of July 2026. We look forward to hearing from you soon!

We care about diversity, inclusion, and equity in our recruitment processes. We also believe behavioral traits can provide important insights into a candidate's fit to a role. To help us achieve this we apply Pymetrics assessments, and upon application you will be invited to play the assessment games.

Title: Junior Analytics Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Do you have a passion for Sports & Outdoor?

Do you thrive at the intersection of data, automation, and AI?

Do you love finding ways to make complex processes simpler through data?

Apply now and become part of our great team at DANISH ENDURANCE®!

We are looking for a talented and ambitious Junior Analytics Engineer to help drive our automation and AI journey - and to contribute to the continuous development and growth of our brand of endurance and outdoor gear.

Who is DANISH ENDURANCE®?

DANISH ENDURANCE® is a global endurance & outdoor brand within e-commerce. With a focus on Danish design and sustainable manufacturing in Europe and Asia, we are a young high-growth company, with a flat structure and a friendly, dynamic working environment. We are a truly international team consisting of 70 professionals with 25 different nationalities based in Copenhagen at Holmen and Gramrode near Horsens.

We have a scalable and unique business model, our ambitions are rocket-high, and we strongly believe that everything is possible. That is why we need you!

What can you expect from the job?

As Our Junior Analytics Engineer, You Will


Build and maintain ELT pipelines, and model the ingested data to business needs in dbt
Drive process optimization and automation initiatives
Help take automations and agentic workflows into production - and grow into the architecture and hosting behind them
Build data products people use - dashboards, automated reports, and ad-hoc analyses
Ensure data quality and maintain documentation
Participate in ML/AI projects and help to execute on our AI roadmap


What do we expect from you?


Curious - you want to understand how things work end to end, not just make them run
Reliable and solution-oriented - you take ownership and see things through
Eager to learn and quick to adapt in a fast-moving environment
Design, implement, and maintain automation pipelines that reduce manual work across the business
Develop (agentic) solutions using modern AI tools and frameworks
Transform complex business requirements into efficient, scalable technical solutions
Proactively identify opportunities for process improvement and see them through to delivery
Collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams


Qualifications

You don’t need to tick every box — but solid hands-on experience with several of the following is what we’re after:


Degree in Data Science, Business Analytics, Computer Science, or a related field
Working knowledge of SQL
Familiarity with data modelling and a cloud data warehouse (we use BigQuery)
Experience with agentic development/AI automations
Experience with dbt and version control (Git)
Experience with data analysis and visualization tools
Experience with Python
Business process optimization experience
Bonus: exposure to deploying solutions in production - hosting, architecture, CI/CD
Passion for sports is a bonus


Additional Facts

Weekly working hours: Full time

Location: Copenhagen Holmen, at Talent Garden Rainmaking

Deadline: We will review applications on an ongoing basis, the sooner you apply, the better

Start date: As soon as possible

How To Apply

Please apply via the Hub. Please include both your CV and motivational letter in one document, as you can only uploade one document to your application.

Read more about DANISH ENDURANCE® at www.danishendurance.com

KEEP YOUR PERSONAL DATA SAFE

Danish Endurance takes responsibility for the protection of your personal data. Accordingly, upon receiving your job application and additional material you choose to submit, we keep it in a safe and secure environment and ensure confidentiality.

You also have a role to play in keeping your personal data safe. We request you only submit personal data about yourself in your job application, CV or other relevant material you choose to provide to us that is relevant for the specific position you are applying for.

(Please consider whether there is personal information you are about to submit, such as health information, that is not necessary for our recruitment process or the position you are applying for. Please also be aware of certificates or other documents that are not necessary for the vacancy.

Title: Business Intelligence (BI) Developer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
As Our Next Business Intelligence (BI) Developer You Can Play a Key Role in Shaping Our Company Strategy

You will work with diverse modern data platforms and support valuable data-driven decision-making. The role involves responsibility for designing, developing, and optimizing data pipelines while creating insightful reports and dashboards for a variety of stakeholders.

Get To Know Us

The RiskPoint Group is a fast-growing global Managing General Underwriter (MGU), providing specialist insurance solutions across multiple lines of business in close partnership with leading insurance companies.

We are a global company of more than 325 employees, collaborating across borders while leveraging local market expertise in 17 locations. We combine deep underwriting expertise with hands-on, in-house claims handling, and high-quality internal business support, to deliver outstanding service and results to the market.

With us, you are trusted with responsibility. We invest in your development, value initiative, and give you the opportunity to shape your role, grow your expertise, and make a real impact.

About Your Team

You will join our Business Intelligence team, which currently consists of three members across Europe. You will join a small, highly skilled sub-team within our growing global IT function, working within the global Finance and Operations department. Our IT team brings together talented IT professionals across Denmark and the UK, combining strong technical talent with extensive insurance industry experience.

Your colleagues form part of our wider global IT team, operating across 17 countries, with 23 experts internationally.

What Will You Do?

Your work will directly to support data-driven decisions across the entire company and therefore we are looking for someone who is detail-oriented and a proactive problem-solver with strong communication skills.

You will collaborate with colleagues across the entire company and work closely with key stakeholders, including analysts, to ensure a solid understanding of data requirements and delivery of optimized reports and dashboards that provide actionable insights.

Key responsibilities include:


Collaborating with analysts & internal stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver optimized reports
Designing, developing, and maintaining scalable data platforms to support various business needs
Implementing data integrations solutions using ETL tools
Ensuring data quality and integrity across all data platforms
Monitoring and troubleshooting data issues to maintain reliability
Staying up-to-date with the latest industry trends to continuously improve the data architecture and BI capabilities


This position is newly created to support our growth strategy and add further value to the team. You will have the opportunity to bring your experience and insights, whilst learning new skills, and developing your career.

This position will be based in Copenhagen and is office based.

About You

We are looking for someone with a strong technical background and a proven ability to deliver results that support the business, while working effectively with stakeholders across an international organization. You should be a self-starter, who is curious, comfortable working autonomously, and has ambition to drive change. An interest in coaching and developing others is also an advantage.

To be successful in this role, you should have:


A Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field
5 or more years of professional experience in BI development
An excellent understanding of SQL with professional experience on top data platforms (e.g., PostgreSQL, Microsoft SQL, Snowflake)
Experience deploying and managing data solutions on cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud)
Proficiency in at least one programming language; e.g., Python, Go, or Java
Experience creating reports and dashboards using BI tools like Power BI, Terraform, or Tableau
Hands-on experience designing and implementing ETL processes
A strong understanding of data modeling, data warehousing, and database design
A proven track record of working effectively in a team, communicating technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders, and solving complex data problems


Experience with the following is preferred but not required:


Large-scale data processing technologies (e.g., Hadoop, Spark, Iceberg)
Modern data tools like Trino and dbt (data build tool)
Terraform for infrastructure as code
DevOps methodologies and tools (CI/CD pipelines, version control)


You must have current residency in Denmark with the necessary work and residency permits in place before applying.

Spoken and written fluency in English is essential for this role, ideally together with a basic understanding of Danish. Other languages are an advantage.

How Do You Apply?

We are looking forward to receiving your CV and responses to the application questions. Please apply in English.

We wish to receive your application by August 3, 2026.

For more information, please contact Zubair Sabir, CTO via *protected email*.

Please note that we do not accept applications via e-mail. All further communication will be in English.

About The RiskPoint Group

The RiskPoint Group is one of Europe’s largest Managing General Underwriters (MGUs), providing best in class insurance solutions to businesses and their advisors globally. The RiskPoint Group operates as RiskPoint in Europe and Asia and RP Underwriting in the rest of the world.

With 325+ employees and 18 locations in Europe, North America, and Asia Pacific, the RiskPoint Group employs expert underwriting and specialized, in-house claims teams within Accident & Health, Transactional Risk, Property & Construction, Renewable Energy, Offshore Upstream, and Liabilities, including Financial Lines, Cyber, and Casualty.

The RiskPoint Group is a Coverholder with Lloyd’s of London and is backed by a strong panel of well-reputed insurance companies. The RiskPoint Group core values are: trust, openness & honesty, respect & tolerance, drive & dedication as well as being part of a winning team in a competitive environment.

Title: Digital Solution Engineer – Offshore Wind Industry
Place: Vejle, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you passionate about developing, maintaining, and analysing data? Do you want to work in the offshore wind industry where your skills and expertise will help secure a sustainable future based on renewable energy? Then keep reading – Cadeler is looking for a Digital Solution Engineer with your skills and interests!

Cadeler is a global partner in the offshore wind industry, specializing in wind farm construction and maintenance. We are part of an industry that is now, more than ever, both relevant and play a critical role in the energy transition with a strong long-term outlook. As our industry continues to develop, so do we! We are now looking for a Digital Solution Engineer to help optimise our operational performance within jacking and monopile installation.

What will you do?

As our new Digital Solution Engineer, you will act as a bridge between offshore operational teams and onshore engineering by translating operational data into actionable insights. Furthermore, you will work hand-in-hand with the BI team regarding the data platform, data validation, and the development of dashboards.

Your Main Tasks Include


Analyse operational data from vessel systems, jacking logs, geotechnical data, and installation records.
Develop dashboards and visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Python-based dashboards, or similar) based on Cadeler’s data platform.
Identify trends, deviations, and performance improvement opportunities based on installation and jacking data. Support root cause analysis of operational events using data-driven methodologies.
Identify, propose, and refine engineering performance metrics in collaboration with the Operational Excellence and BI teams
Identify opportunities for performance optimization, cost reduction, and risk mitigation using data analytics.
Engage with clients when data-driven reporting or performance analysis is required


To succeed in this role

Being part of the Cadeler community means you will have a natural interest and care for the environment and our shared mission to speed up the green energy transition. Your environmental considerations and the safety of our people and the planet will be a key priority in your daily work.

We are looking for someone with a strong analytical and structured mindset, who possesses strong communication skills and is driven by curiosity and motivation to improve operations through digital solutions.

We think you will be a good match if you:


2–5 years of relevant experience
Academic degree in engineering (civil, mechanical, geotechnical, marine) or data science with strong technical/engineering exposure.
Experience with data analysis tools such as Python, Power BI, SQL, or similar.
Hands-on experience working with cloud-based data platforms (e.g., Snowflake or equivalent technologies)
Experience in offshore wind, marine operations, heavy lift, or jack-up vessels is an advantage but not mandatory.
Experience working with large datasets and operational performance analysis.


Come work with us!

By becoming a Cadeler employee, you will be part of a rapidly growing company with a diverse and energetic team. We offer an exciting position focused on delivering excellence in the face of interesting new challenges, within a positive and rewarding work environment in an international company with great development possibilities.

Cadeler is headquartered in Copenhagen, but we also have offices in Vejle, UK, US, and Taiwan, as well as colleagues working offshore aboard our fleet of Wind Farm Installation Vessels (WFIV).

Meet some of our colleagues here: https://www.cadeler.com/career

Apply

Use the APPLY button to apply for this position. For data protection purposes, applications are only accepted via the link and are assessed continuously until we find the right candidate, so please don’t hesitate to send in your application as soon as possible and no later than the 10th of August. You can expect to hear back from us after that date, due to vacation.

If you have any questions regarding the position, please contact Geotechnical & Jacking Engineering Team Lead, Can Mollaibrahimoglu, on (+45) 2979 9623, and for practical matters please contact peopleandculture@cadeler.com.
