Title: Procurement Data & Business Analyst
Place: Tønder, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Hydro Extrusions is a world-leading aluminium extrusion business counting around 100 production sites in 40 countries and employing 20,000 people. Through our unique combination of local expertise, global network, and unmatched R&D capabilities, we can offer everything from standard profiles, to advanced development and manufacturing for most industries.




About The Job

We are seeking a highly analytical Procurement Data & Business Analyst to join the Hydro Extrusions Procurement team, a strategic organization that oversees spend within several important indirect categories across sites all over the world.

In close collaboration with Category Managers, you will play a critical role ensuring that procurement decisions are based on accurate data and insightful analysis. In this position, you will have to structure and analyze complex datasets to provide actionable recommendations that will support sourcing decisions, cost optimizations and supplier performance.

This role operates across EU and NA regions, in a fast-paced and imperfect data environment. It requires strong problem-solving skills and the ability to bring structure to complexity with a pragmatic, solution-oriented and results-driven mindset.




What you will be doing

In close partnership with Category Managers, you will play a key role in driving procurement excellence through data.

Data analysis:

Collect, clean, structure and analyze procurement data from multiple sources (multiple ERP systems, supplier reports, sites databases).
Develop and maintain Excel-based tools (databases, bid sheets, assessments files) to run wide tenders and support decision-making.
Analyze supplier performance, pricing, cost structure and spend to provide actionable insights and identify savings opportunities.
Contribute to supplier negotiations and strategic decisions with clear and structures analysis.
Create and maintain dashboards to track savings and categories procurement strategies implementation.

Stakeholder management:

Support execution of complex tenders involving cross-functional teams and stakeholders in different locations.
Provide solutions and recommendations even when data is incomplete or imperfect, ensuring projects move forward with pragmatic and data-driven recommendations.
Uphold high personal standards and expectations for quality and impact.




What will make you successful?

Required Skills & Qualifications

Degree in Mathematics, Business, Economics, Engineering or similar analytical fields.
Experience in Procurement, Project Management or related Business-oriented role is a plus.
Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel (pivot tables, VLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH, data modeling, complex formulas and VBA Macros).
Ability to work in project-based environment, interpret large datasets, and turn complexity into clear insights and pragmatic recommendations, while ensuring continued projects execution.
Excellent communication skills to present insights clearly to different stakeholders across multiple locations (proficiency in both Excel and PowerPoint).
Comfortable working with imperfect data and making sound decisions under uncertainty while maintaining accuracy and delivering results.
Fluency in English is essential, while proficiency in other European languages is a plus.

If you recognize yourself in the description below, don’t wait to apply!

You enjoy solving complex problems and turning data into clear, actionable insights.
You are comfortable working with imperfect and incomplete data and thrive finding and bringing structure into it.
You know how to build comprehensive analyses and present them in a structured, synthetic, clear and visual way.
You are curious, proactive, adaptable and like to challenge the status quo.
You are comfortable working in a fast-paced project-based environment handling multiple tasks in parallel.
You are excited to navigate a dynamic matrix organization and travel up to 25%-30% of time.
Self-motivated, you are able to perform under time constraints and pressure.
Excel is your playground; experience with Power Query, Power BI is a plus.




What we offer you

Whether it’s developing skills or advancing your expertise even further, we’ll support you with continuous learning and development opportunities, to help discuss and steer your long-term Norsk Hydro career path. You’ll be welcomed from day one into an inclusive, trusting environment guided by three core values – Care, Courage and Collaboration
Opportunity to work in an international, multi-site automotive environment
Professional development in procurement analytics and category management
Hybrid working model
Stable employment in a global industrial organization




Equal opportunities

Hydro values diverse skills and perspectives among employees. We encourage all qualified candidates to apply. Qualified applicants will be considered regardless of race, religion, nationality, ethnicity, age, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, protected veteran status, or disability. We strive to provide equal opportunities for all to contribute and succeed with us.

Diversity improves our ability to act in accordance with The Hydro Way. Therefore all applications will be considered with equal suitability. Please apply online in ONE with your CV and optionally a cover letter.

Title: Dataanalytiker
Place: Odense Kommune, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vil du være med til at skabe et stærkere datagrundlag for beslutninger i Odense Letbane?

 

I Odense Letbane arbejder vi målrettet på tværs af organisationen med at styrke vores beslutningsgrundlag gennem data. Derfor søger vi en dataanalytiker med interesse for datakvalitet, datamodeller og analyse.




Du kommer til at arbejde med tre centrale dataområder:

Passagerdata 
Driftsdata 
Finansielle data 




En væsentlig del af rollen er at skabe indsigt i Odense Letbanes passagerer, rejsemønstre og brug af letbanen. Samtidig vil du bidrage til et fælles og troværdigt datagrundlag for opfølgning på drift og økonomi.

Stillingen refererer direkte til økonomidirektøren (CFO) og er centralt placeret i organisationen.




Dine primære opgaver




Datakvalitet

Sikre høj kvalitet og konsistens i passager-, drifts- og finansielle data 
Identificere og håndtere fejl, mangler og uoverensstemmelser i data 
Sikre ensartede datadefinitioner og opgørelsesmetoder for centrale nøgletal 
Samarbejde med FynBus, Keolis, Odense Kommune og øvrige samarbejdspartnere om datakvalitet og datagrundlag




Datamodeller

Udvikle og vedligeholde datamodeller for passager-, drifts- og finansielle data 
Sammenstille data fra forskellige kilder 
Dokumentere data, KPI'er og datadefinitioner 
Sikre fælles forståelse af centrale data og nøgletal på tværs af organisationen




Dataanalyse

Udvikle dashboards, der giver overblik over passagerer og passageradfærd – og som kan bruges aktivt i løbende opfølgninger og beslutninger 
Identificere og vurdere nye datakilder, der kan styrke indsigten i passagerer, drift og økonomi 
Analysere driftens performance og centrale driftsmæssige nøgletal 
Analysere sammenhænge mellem passagerer, drift og indtægter 
Udarbejde analyser, KPI'er og beslutningsgrundlag til ledelse og organisation




Din profil

Vi lægger større vægt på erfaring, interesse og faglig nysgerrighed end på en bestemt uddannelsesmæssig baggrund.




Vi forestiller os, at du:

Har erfaring med data og analyse 
Har interesse for datakvalitet, datamodeller og strukturering af data 
Er nysgerrig på data og motiveres af at forstå sammenhænge og mønstre 
Har mod på at anvende AI som en naturlig del af det daglige arbejde 
Kan omsætte komplekse datasæt til klare konklusioner og anvendelig indsigt 
Arbejder systematisk og har fokus på kvalitet og detaljer 
Trives med selvstændigt ansvar og tager ejerskab for dine opgaver 




Det er ikke afgørende, hvilke værktøjer du har arbejdet med tidligere. Det vigtigste er, at du har lyst til at arbejde med data og evnen til at skabe struktur, kvalitet og indsigt.




Vi tilbyder

En central rolle med fokus på passager-, drifts- og finansielle data  
Selvstændigt ansvar inden for et centralt fagområde 
Korte beslutningsveje 
Mulighed for at præge udviklingen af Odense Letbanes arbejde med data 
Et stærkt fagligt og socialt fællesskab med engagerede kolleger 
En hverdag i moderne rammer centralt i Odense 
Pensionsordning 
Fleksibilitet i hverdagen og mulighed for hjemmearbejde, når arbejdsopgaverne tillader det 




Du bliver en del af Odense Letbane, som i dag har cirka 25 medarbejdere. Organisationen er i udvikling som følge af arbejdet med letbanens etape 2 og de nye opgaver, projektet medfører. Vi hjælper hinanden på tværs af fagområder og løfter i fællesskab de opgaver, der opstår.




Praktiske oplysninger




Arbejdssted: Odense

Ansøgningsfrist: 9. august 2026

Samtaler: Samtalerne gennemføres med økonomidirektør Remi Thomsen og den kommercielle chef Lotte Stensberg. Vi forventer at afholde første samtalerunde fredag d. 14. august og anden samtalerunde torsdag d. 20. august.

Test og case: Kandidater, der går videre til anden samtalerunde, vil blive bedt om at gennemføre en personligheds- og færdighedstest samt løse en mindre case. Resultaterne indgår som ét af flere elementer i den samlede vurdering.

Tiltrædelse: Efter aftale.

Spørgsmål: Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte økonomidirektør Remi Thomsen på 40 28 94 58 via sms. Du vil herefter blive kontaktet telefonisk efter den 28. juli.




Mangfoldighed 

Odense Letbane betragter mangfoldighed som en styrke og opfordrer derfor alle kvalificerede til at søge uanset alder, køn, seksuel orientering, religion, etnisk tilhørsforhold, handicap mv. 

Odense Letbane er desuden medlem af Mangfoldighedscharter Odense.

Title: Advanced Analytics Specialist, Supply Chain Intelligence
Place: Ballerup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you passionate about driving high-impact projects in a global supply chain? Do you want to play a key role in delivering transformational initiatives that shape how Ambu scales, operates, and delivers value to patients worldwide?

Ambu is transforming – and this is your opportunity to shape how intelligence drives our supply chain.

We have recently evolved our analytics function into a Supply Chain Intelligence team within our Global Supply Chain organization, and we are seeking an Advanced Analytics Specialist to join us. The team’s ambition is clear: moving from describing what happened to knowing what to do next. You will play a central role in delivering this journey; building predictive and prescriptive models, enabling what-if scenarios and decision support, and strengthening the data foundation that underpins it all; while helping build a data-driven culture across the global supply chain.

At Ambu, we are on an ambitious growth journey - scaling our operations and capabilities to meet the rising global demand for our innovative medical solutions. As part of this journey, you will join a highly motivated team that values initiative, impact, and collaboration; and you will have a front-row seat in maturing how data and intelligence power our global supply chain.

The role

As an Advanced Analytics Specialist, your primary responsibility is to design, build, and deploy analytics solutions that move our global supply chain from descriptive dashboards to predictive and prescriptive intelligence. You will work across our five focus areas: data foundation, reporting & visibility, advanced analytics, decision support, and analytics enablement; with the greatest emphasis on advanced analytics and decision support.

This is a hands-on role where technical depth meets business-centric problem-solving. Working closely with the Advanced Analytics Manager, GSC, you will partner with stakeholders across corporate functions and manufacturing sites – bridging business and IT – to ensure that data becomes a strategic asset embedded in how Ambu’s supply chain makes decisions.

Key Responsibilities

Advanced analytics & decision support (primary focus)


Develop, deploy, and maintain predictive and prescriptive models (e.g., forecasting, anomaly detection, optimization) that address concrete supply chain challenges across planning, operations, and distribution
Build what-if scenario and simulation capabilities that give leadership actionable decision support; not just reporting
Translate ambiguous business questions into structured analytical problems, and analytical results into clear recommendations for stakeholders and leadership
Automate recurring analyses and workflows to free capacity across the organization for higher-value work
Take ownership of solutions end-to-end: from problem framing and data preparation to model deployment in collaboration with back-end IT, monitoring, and adoption


Data foundation, visibility & analytics enablement


Contribute to advancing our data foundation toward a single source of truth; improving data quality, governance, and data models in collaboration with IT
Develop and enhance Power BI dashboards and standard GSC reporting where they create genuine visibility and value
Support upskilling across the global supply chain through training, documentation, and self-service enablement; helping embed a data-driven culture
Stay curious about new technologies (AI, machine learning, modern analytics tooling) and proactively bring relevant opportunities into the team


Suggested candidate profile

Skills

Skills and experience can be gained in many ways, and we understand that the best fit for the role cannot be described on paper. You are, however, likely to succeed in this position if you have:


2–5 years of experience in analytics, data science, or business intelligence roles. You may have gained your experience in a consultancy, an industrial company, or a data-driven scale-up
Strong hands-on skills in SQL and Python (or R) for data preparation, analysis, and modelling
Solid experience with Power BI – including DAX and data modelling – and an eye for visualizations that drive decisions
Experience building predictive models (e.g., forecasting or machine learning) and bringing at least one into real operational use; you know the difference between a notebook and a solution people rely on
Strong business translation skills; you can turn a vague business question into an analytical approach, and an analytical result into a clear recommendation
A working understanding of supply chain processes (e.g., planning, manufacturing, logistics, or distribution); you don't need to be a supply chain expert, but you can speak the language of operations and understand the physical flows behind the data
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills; you build trust quickly and collaborate effectively across functions and levels
A proactive, structured and independent way of working: you thrive in a maturing environment where you help build the way, not just follow it


It is an advantage if you have experience with the following:


Deeper experience within supply chain planning, S&OP, or distribution analytics
ERP data (e.g., Infor), cloud data platforms (e.g., Azure, Fabric), or optimization and simulation techniques
Experience in integrating new data (e.g., through API/EDI) into database and semantic models
Experience in training or upskilling non-technical colleagues
Exposure to a GxP-regulated industry


Please note that due to the upcoming summer holiday period, the recruitment process may take slightly longer than usual. We expect to begin the interview process at the beginning of August.

Ambu – a visionary and international workplace where your efforts matter

Ambu is a company that expands rapidly and has ambitious growth targets. We meet these targets through interdisciplinary teamwork between motivated and highly skilled employees. Your contributions and efforts are crucial to our success and by joining us you will get the opportunity to work in an international head office with an informal working environment.

We offer great opportunities for personal development. Furthermore, we offer a wide range of professional, social and financial employee benefits in addition to exciting job challenges and continuous professional and personal development.

Title: Datakonsulent til udvikling af fælles dataløsninger inden for indkøb og logistik
Description:
Datakonsulent til udvikling af fælles dataløsninger inden for indkøb og logistik,

Vil du være med til at udvikle datadrevne løsninger, der understøtter indkøbs-og logistikfunktionerne i Region Midtjyllands sundhedsvæsen?

Samt være en del af et datateam i udvikling?

Vi udvider teamet fra fire til fem medarbejdere og søger derfor en dygtig datakonsulent eller BI-udvikler, der vil være med til at udvikle fælles dataløsninger til fællesfunktionen for Indkøb og Medicoteknik.

Vi søger dig der har stærke tekniske kompetencer, har et skarpt blik for de fælles digitale løsninger og evnen til at omsætte komplekse data til indsigter, der skaber værdi for organisationen.

Du bliver en del af et stærkt datateam

I Indkøb & Medicoteknik kommer du til at arbejde i afdelingens datateam, der er en del af en tværgående stabsfunktion. Teamet har specialiseret sig inden for indkøbs- og logistikdata samt data omkring medicoteknisk udstyr.

Datateamet arbejder med hele BI-processen – fra ETL, datakvalitet og datamodellering til automatiseringer, rapportering og visualisering. Datateamet spiller en vigtig rolle i forhold til behovs- og procesafklaring omkring anvendelse af data i afdelingen. Teamet har ofte en aktiv rolle i hele processen fra koncept til endelig implementering af dataløsninger.

Du vil derfor få en bred kontaktflade til afdelingens mange fagspecialister inden for indkøb, logistik, bæredygtighed og medicoteknik.

Du kan forvente at skulle arbejde både selvstændigt ude i organisationen og som en del af et teknisk stærkt team, hvor du får mulighed for faglig sparring og udvikling. Vi har ligeledes tætte samarbejdsflader med regionens hospitaler og regionens afdeling for Digitalisering og IT.

Primære arbejdsopgaver i stillingen

For at sikre en stærk og robust datafunktion søger vi en kollega med kompetencer inden for især anvendelse af indkøbs- og logistikdata.

Udvikling og implementering af datadrevne løsninger via regionens standardværktøjer er hovedfokus i stillingen. Disse værktøjer er MSSQL til databaser, Tableau til visualiseringer og UiPath/Python til automatiseringer, hvilket derfor også vil være dine primære værktøjer i arbejdet. Vores indkøbs- og logistikdata stammer primært fra SAP S/4HANA-platformen, hvorfor kendskab til denne vil være en fordel.

Vores Opgaveportefølje Er Bred, Men Ofte Bygger Vi Driftsorienterede Værktøjer Med Udgangspunkt i Data Og Afdelingens Strategiske Målsætninger. Herunder Kan Nævnes Nogle Eksempler På Opgaver


Automatiseret berigelse af masterdata i vores varekatalog.
Automatisering af manuelle processer i forbindelse med de administrative dele af varehåndteringen.
Beslutningsstøtteværktøjer til varelogistikdrift for regionslager og ude på hospitalerne.
Løbende udvidelse og anvendelse af datagrundlag forbundet med hele processen fra indkøb til anvendelse af produkter fra leverandørerne.
Udvikling og implementering af KPI’er til opfølgning på tværs af hele indkøbsprocessen.


Vi søger en kollega, der


Har en relevant akademisk uddannelse inden for økonomi, logistik eller samfundsvidenskab, f.eks. Cand.merc., Cand.oecon. eller tilsvarende uddannelser på bachelorniveau.
Kan arbejde selvstændigt med kompleks databehandling og datamodellering samt skabe robuste dataløsninger.
Har et par års erfaring med Python, SQL og/eller Tableau.
Har kendskab til RPA-software (ideelt UiPath) eller VBA. Det vil være et plus, men ikke et krav.
Har dokumenteret erfaring med at omsætte komplekse problemstillinger gennem datamæssig forståelse og viden til konkrete tiltag og nye løsninger.
Kan drive processer gennem en vedholdende, imødekommende og positiv tilgang og kan lide at kommunikere med forskellige faggrupper.
Er initiativrig, engageret, ansvarsbevidst og er nysgerrig på den organisation, du bliver en del af.


Vi kan tilbyde dig

Indkøb & Medicoteknik tilbyder en attraktiv arbejdsplads og et meningsfyldt arbejde. Vi arbejder for, at det skal være nemt at være medarbejder og sikkert at være patient i Region Midtjylland.

Du kommer til at arbejde et sted med spændende og varierende opgaver, dygtige kolleger og samarbejdspartnere samt med gode muligheder for personlig og faglig udvikling.

Humor, trivsel og en uformel kultur vægtes højt på vores arbejdsplads. Det samme gør fleksibilitet. Det er vigtigt, at du har tid til et liv ved siden af arbejdet. Vi har derfor en flekstidsordning og mulighed for at arbejde hjemmefra op til 2 dage om ugen.

Derudover har vi et sundhedsudvalg, der løbende afholder forskellige arrangementer.

Løn- og ansættelsesvilkår

Hovedtjenestestedet er Indkøb & Medicoteknik, Olof Palmes Allé 15, 8200 Aarhus N.

Stillingen er en fast fuldtidsstilling på 37 timer inkl. betalt frokostpause og med flekstidsordning.

Ansættelsen sker med reference til områdelederen i Økonomi- og Dataenheden.

Dine løn- og ansættelsesvilkår fastsættes i henhold til gældende overenskomst. Lønnen består af en grundløn evt. suppleret med funktions- og/eller kvalifikationstillæg, som fastsættes ud fra stillingens indhold samt dine kompetencer, erfaring og kvalifikationer.

Vi ønsker at ansætte med opstart 1. oktober 2026 eller hurtigst muligt herefter.

Yderligere oplysninger

Yderligere oplysninger om stillingen kan fås ved henvendelse til:


Områdeleder Astrid Kriegbaum Westphael på tlf. 2334 7977 eller via mail astwes@rm.dk
BI-udvikler Kim Skov Rasmussen på tlf. 3036 3169 eller via mail kimsrs@rm.dk


Grundet ferieafholdelse bedes du sende en sms i ugerne 29 til 31, så finder vi et tidspunkt til en snak.

Ansøgningsfrist

Ansøgningsfristen er tirsdag den 18. august 2026.

Samtaler afholdes mandag den 24. august 2026.

En eventuel 2. samtalerunde afholdes onsdag den 26. august 2026. Der indkaldes med kort varsel til begge samtalerunder.

Ansøgninger modtages kun via vores elektroniske rekrutteringssystem. Vælg det anførte link for at søge stillingen.

Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig.

Bliv en del af en spændende afdeling

Indkøb & Medicoteknik Er En Regionsdækkende Enhed Med Ca. 200 Ansatte, Som Har Ansvaret For Indkøb, Logistik Og Medicoteknik i Hele Region Midtjylland


Vi står for alle regionens udbud, indkøbsaftaler og kontrakter,
Vi har ansvaret for varelogistikken, lige fra varen bestilles, til den leveres rette sted i regionen, hvilket også indebærer regionens fælleslager, der drives af en privat partner,
Vi anskaffer, drifter og vedligeholder det medicotekniske udstyr på regionens hospitaler og rådgiver hospitalerne på området.


Desuden arbejder vi for en bæredygtig udvikling af regionen. Vi indtænker regionens mål om mindre CO2-aftryk, mindre affald og mere genanvendelse i opgaveløsningen på alle vores ansvarsområder. Sammen med Center for Bæredygtige Hospitaler, der er en del af Indkøb & Medicoteknik, dækker vi næsten hele opgaven med Cirkulær Økonomi i Region Midtjylland.

Læs mere om, hvordan det er at arbejde hos os på vores karriereside.

Eller få et indblik i vores hverdag på LinkedIn.

Da Region Midtjylland løbende gennemfører besparelser og omorganiseringer, vil medarbejdere i omplacering, der søger stillingen, have fortrinsret.

Region Midtjylland er jf. EU’s løngennemsigtighedsdirektiv, der trådte i kraft 7. juni 2026 forpligtet til at oplyse dig om startløn eller et interval for startløn for en stilling inden du tilbydes ansættelse.

Her finder du Region Midtjyllands lønkatalog for de fleste faggrupper og stillinger, som findes i Region Midtjylland: Gennemsigtighed forud for ansættelse - Medarbejder.rm.dk

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
I am working with a leading global quantitative investment firm that is looking to hire a Data Scientist to join its Commodities business in Aarhus.




This is a front-office facing role sitting at the intersection of Research, Trading, and Engineering, focused on transforming complex datasets into high-value research and trading inputs used across systematic commodities strategies.




The successful candidate will work closely with traders, quantitative researchers, and data engineers to onboard, transform, and productionise both traditional and alternative datasets that directly support investment decisions.




What you'll be working on

Partnering with Trading and Research teams to understand and deliver data requirements
Designing and implementing scalable data pipelines supporting systematic commodities strategies
Onboarding and integrating new datasets into the firm's research platform
Developing data transformation processes to create high-value, investment-ready datasets
Building tools and workflows to extract, clean, normalise, and aggregate data from diverse raw sources and formats
Managing the end-to-end lifecycle of new data acquisitions and integrations
Driving data quality, validation, and monitoring across critical research datasets
Troubleshooting complex data challenges and reducing time-to-production for new data sources
Researching and implementing innovative methods for data extraction and acquisition
Working closely with stakeholders across Trading, Research, Engineering, and external data vendors




This is a highly visible position offering direct exposure to front-office teams and the opportunity to work on some of the most interesting data challenges within quantitative investing.




What they're looking for

3+ years of experience in Data Science, Data Engineering, Quantitative Analytics, or a related data-focused role
Advanced Python programming skills, including strong experience with Pandas and NumPy
Experience designing and maintaining data pipelines and data transformation workflows
Experience working with financial, market, commodities, or alternative datasets
Postgraduate degree in Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, Statistics, or another quantitative discipline
Demonstrable interest in financial markets and data-driven investment processes
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to operate in a fast-paced, high-performance environment
Excellent communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical and business teams
Prior experience within systematic trading, quantitative finance, hedge funds, asset management, commodity trading, market data, or alternative data environments would be advantageous




Please apply directly.




They are open to people looking to relocate, but you must have the expertise described above.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
We’re looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to join our Copenhagen office and bring structure to a fragmented data landscape - building a foundation that can be used across the business, by people and by AI. As part of our Revenue Operations team, you will work closely with IT and colleagues across the company to make business data consistent, understandable, and trustworthy. You’ll be joining a newly established function in a growing SaaS company.




The role

This is a hands-on, foundational role: practical execution, not data science. That said, no central data platform exists here yet, so “hands-on” includes real decisions about architecture and tooling - this isn’t a job where you just plug into something that’s already built. Dashboards and reporting matter, but they’re an output. The real goal is data structured and reliable enough to power AI, automation, and better decisions.

This position is part of our Revenue Operations team and reports to Martin Rafn Møller. We’re looking for a candidate with around 3+ years of hands-on experience.




What you’ll do




Platform setup

Own the end-to-end design of our data platform; picking and setting up the stack across ingestion, storage, modeling, and delivery.




Data ingestion

Map the business systems where company data lives (CRM, marketing, project delivery, product, support, and other operational platforms), and bring that data in via managed ELT connectors (e.g. Fivetran, Airbyte, or Weld) or custom-built pipelines, depending on what each source supports.
Work with IT on the custom integrations this requires - API ingestion, database connections, or source-specific flows. As a result of ongoing mergers and acquisitions, expect this to be a regular part of the job, not a rare exception.




Modeling, quality, and documentation

Turn raw source data into a clear, maintainable foundation of cleaned, modeled, reusable datasets, with data models that reflect how the business actually works and clear definitions for entities, relationships, and metrics.
Implement practical data quality checks so missing, duplicated, outdated, or inconsistent data is caught before it affects reporting or downstream automation.
Document sources, transformations, metric definitions, ownership, and access so the foundation is trusted across the business.




Reporting, automation, and AI use cases

Prepare data so it’s genuinely AI-ready, meaning structured, clearly defined, documented, and reliable enough that AI can be applied broadly. Not only for analysis, but for automation, assistants, and AI-driven workflows across the business, as well as for human use.
Replace manual exports and conflicting definitions with automated, repeatable data flows - and build dashboards and reports on top of trusted models where they’re needed.




The company

For over 20 years, Omnidocs has built software for document generation. Born as a consultancy and turned into a SaaS business, the company now helps customers worldwide get the best results from their documents and presentations.

Today, the Omnidocs Group has a strong portfolio comprised of several products and almost 200 professionals across various office locations such as Denmark, The Netherlands, UK, Switzerland and more. Following a strategy of acquiring new companies and technologies, in the past 3 years we welcomed Dania Software, Office Consult, Xential, Eformity, Presentation Solutions and officeatwork to the group.

As we mature into a more product- and data-oriented company, getting our data foundation right has become essential to how we operate and how we make decisions. That’s where you come in.




Your qualifications




The essential:

Hands-on experience with data from real business systems, not just sample datasets or isolated reports
Strong SQL for querying, transforming, modeling, and validating data
Python or another scripting language for data processing, automation, and operational data tasks
Experience building data pipelines into a central data platform - using managed ELT connectors (such as Fivetran, Airbyte, or Weld) and custom-built integrations depending on the source, with a solid grasp of ETL/ELT concepts and data quality principles
Experience with a modern data warehouse, lakehouse, or cloud data platform
A clear grasp of how to structure raw data into clean, reusable models that support reporting, analysis, automation, and AI - including a basic sense of what that asks of data quality and structure
Ability to translate unclear business questions into practical data structures and outputs, with good judgment about when to use a standard connector, coordinate something custom, or leave a process manual for now
Strong communication skills in English




It would be great if you also have an interest and/or experience in:

The modern data stack: dbt for modeling and documentation, Fivetran/Airbyte/Weld for ingestion, Snowflake/BigQuery/Fabric/Databricks for the platform, Dagster/Airflow for orchestration, Git for version control
Hands-on experience building or deploying AI-powered features themselves (e.g. a RAG pipeline, an AI agent, or semantic search) - beyond just preparing data for them
Building data foundations where data structures, ownership, and reporting logic are still immature
Building dashboards in tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or Metabase
Access control, privacy, and basic data governance




Although you must be versatile across the data engineering process, we’re not looking for a “ninja” or “unicorn”: we expect you to know your strengths and be interested in working on your weaker points. Be open about your core competencies, and where you’d like to improve. Our needs are diverse, and we can accommodate different professional profiles.




Soft skills

You are structured, curious, and pragmatic - comfortable with ambiguity and with imperfect systems and incomplete data, creating order over time. You work independently, ask sharp questions, and challenge requirements when the underlying data logic is unclear. You care whether data is useful, reliable, and reusable, not just whether a dashboard looks good. You use modern tools, including AI, naturally in your work - not as a gimmick, but to move faster, improve documentation, debug, and explore data. We appreciate proactivity, adaptability, and a strongly cross-functional, collaborative approach.




What we offer

Flexible work hours: 9-to-5 or 11-to-19, it’s your choice
Hybrid work arrangements
Work-life balance, in true Scandinavian style
Optional lunch scheme in partnership with a local French brasserie
Discounts in gym membership and work glasses
Partially subsidized weekly massage therapy in the office
Social activities like wine tasting, seasonal parties and team events
Sport activities like running groups and participation in the DHL Relay CPH
Career development opportunities
An informal work environment in our office in the heart of the historic Christianshavn neighbourhood, surrounded by the vibrant life of cafes, restaurants, and cultural sights




Why is this a great time to join

You won’t have to advocate for the value of data: from senior management down, there’s clear recognition of why this foundation matters
This is the first step in building out our data capability - you’ll be an important part of it, and you’ll help set the standards for how data engineering fits in as we mature into a product- and data-oriented company
You will directly influence not only the company’s data foundation, but also your own role, tooling, and processes
You’ll be the first dedicated data hire - no data team to lean on day-to-day, but IT is a close, responsive partner for integrations and infrastructure, and you’ll work closely with your manager for sparring and ensuring progress.
We’re not asking you to build this without the right tools - what you need to do the job well is part of the conversation once you’re in the role




Application

Please apply via the dedicated application link with your CV and brief written answers to a few short questions in the application form. That’s all we ask - no cover letter or portfolio needed. Keep your answers short and to the point; if we move forward, we’ll explore more in the interview.

As we strive for a thorough and unbiased recruitment process, we also kindly ask you to:

Use the dedicated application link (do NOT send your resume over e-mail)
Do NOT send a picture of yourself
Do NOT share your age or birth date




Recruitment steps:

First interview - initial screening with Martin Rafn Møller starting from the 27th of July
Second interview - a deeper conversation with the team, plus a take-home behavioural assessment (aprox. 30 min) and a follow-up with People & Culture
Final call to discuss additional matters, if needed




Questions?

Feel free to reach out to Martin at mrm@omnidocs.com.

Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis. We’ll do our best to get back to you within 3-4 working weeks, but please be aware that this timeframe may be extended.




Processing of personal data

By registering in our HR system, BambooHR, and uploading both application and CV, you accept that Omnidocs stores the registered data about you and the data included in the uploaded documents. All information is used solely for recruitment purposes and will be deleted within 12 months of upload. Please do not include person-sensitive data in the application and CV, e.g. CPR number, information about race, religion, trade union conditions, and health information. You can read about Omnidocs’ guidelines for processing personal data here:

https://omnidocs.com/processing-of-personal-data-in-the-context-of-recruitment/

HTML:
We’re looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to join our Copenhagen office and bring structure to a fragmented data landscape - building a foundation that can be used across the business, by people and by AI. As part of our Revenue Operations team, you will work closely with IT and colleagues across the company to make business data consistent, understandable, and trustworthy. You’ll be joining a newly established function in a growing SaaS company.


The role

This is a hands-on, foundational role: practical execution, not data science. That said, no central data platform exists here yet, so “hands-on” includes real decisions about architecture and tooling - this isn’t a job where you just plug into something that’s already built. Dashboards and reporting matter, but they’re an output. The real goal is data structured and reliable enough to power AI, automation, and better decisions.

This position is part of our Revenue Operations team and reports to Martin Rafn Møller. We’re looking for a candidate with around 3+ years of hands-on experience.


What you’ll do


Platform setup

  • Own the end-to-end design of our data platform; picking and setting up the stack across ingestion, storage, modeling, and delivery.


Data ingestion

  • Map the business systems where company data lives (CRM, marketing, project delivery, product, support, and other operational platforms), and bring that data in via managed ELT connectors (e.g. Fivetran, Airbyte, or Weld) or custom-built pipelines, depending on what each source supports.
  • Work with IT on the custom integrations this requires - API ingestion, database connections, or source-specific flows. As a result of ongoing mergers and acquisitions, expect this to be a regular part of the job, not a rare exception.


Modeling, quality, and documentation

  • Turn raw source data into a clear, maintainable foundation of cleaned, modeled, reusable datasets, with data models that reflect how the business actually works and clear definitions for entities, relationships, and metrics.
  • Implement practical data quality checks so missing, duplicated, outdated, or inconsistent data is caught before it affects reporting or downstream automation.
  • Document sources, transformations, metric definitions, ownership, and access so the foundation is trusted across the business.


Reporting, automation, and AI use cases

  • Prepare data so it’s genuinely AI-ready, meaning structured, clearly defined, documented, and reliable enough that AI can be applied broadly. Not only for analysis, but for automation, assistants, and AI-driven workflows across the business, as well as for human use.
  • Replace manual exports and conflicting definitions with automated, repeatable data flows - and build dashboards and reports on top of trusted models where they’re needed.


The company

For over 20 years, Omnidocs has built software for document generation. Born as a consultancy and turned into a SaaS business, the company now helps customers worldwide get the best results from their documents and presentations.

Today, the Omnidocs Group has a strong portfolio comprised of several products and almost 200 professionals across various office locations such as Denmark, The Netherlands, UK, Switzerland and more. Following a strategy of acquiring new companies and technologies, in the past 3 years we welcomed Dania Software, Office Consult, Xential, Eformity, Presentation Solutions and officeatwork to the group.

As we mature into a more product- and data-oriented company, getting our data foundation right has become essential to how we operate and how we make decisions. That’s where you come in.


Your qualifications


The essential:

  • Hands-on experience with data from real business systems, not just sample datasets or isolated reports
  • Strong SQL for querying, transforming, modeling, and validating data
  • Python or another scripting language for data processing, automation, and operational data tasks
  • Experience building data pipelines into a central data platform - using managed ELT connectors (such as Fivetran, Airbyte, or Weld) and custom-built integrations depending on the source, with a solid grasp of ETL/ELT concepts and data quality principles
  • Experience with a modern data warehouse, lakehouse, or cloud data platform
  • A clear grasp of how to structure raw data into clean, reusable models that support reporting, analysis, automation, and AI - including a basic sense of what that asks of data quality and structure
  • Ability to translate unclear business questions into practical data structures and outputs, with good judgment about when to use a standard connector, coordinate something custom, or leave a process manual for now
  • Strong communication skills in English


It would be great if you also have an interest and/or experience in:

  • The modern data stack: dbt for modeling and documentation, Fivetran/Airbyte/Weld for ingestion, Snowflake/BigQuery/Fabric/Databricks for the platform, Dagster/Airflow for orchestration, Git for version control
  • Hands-on experience building or deploying AI-powered features themselves (e.g. a RAG pipeline, an AI agent, or semantic search) - beyond just preparing data for them
  • Building data foundations where data structures, ownership, and reporting logic are still immature
  • Building dashboards in tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or Metabase
  • Access control, privacy, and basic data governance


Although you must be versatile across the data engineering process, we’re not looking for a “ninja” or “unicorn”: we expect you to know your strengths and be interested in working on your weaker points. Be open about your core competencies, and where you’d like to improve. Our needs are diverse, and we can accommodate different professional profiles.


Soft skills

You are structured, curious, and pragmatic - comfortable with ambiguity and with imperfect systems and incomplete data, creating order over time. You work independently, ask sharp questions, and challenge requirements when the underlying data logic is unclear. You care whether data is useful, reliable, and reusable, not just whether a dashboard looks good. You use modern tools, including AI, naturally in your work - not as a gimmick, but to move faster, improve documentation, debug, and explore data. We appreciate proactivity, adaptability, and a strongly cross-functional, collaborative approach.


What we offer

  • Flexible work hours: 9-to-5 or 11-to-19, it’s your choice
  • Hybrid work arrangements
  • Work-life balance, in true Scandinavian style
  • Optional lunch scheme in partnership with a local French brasserie
  • Discounts in gym membership and work glasses
  • Partially subsidized weekly massage therapy in the office
  • Social activities like wine tasting, seasonal parties and team events
  • Sport activities like running groups and participation in the DHL Relay CPH
  • Career development opportunities
  • An informal work environment in our office in the heart of the historic Christianshavn neighbourhood, surrounded by the vibrant life of cafes, restaurants, and cultural sights


Why is this a great time to join

  • You won’t have to advocate for the value of data: from senior management down, there’s clear recognition of why this foundation matters
  • This is the first step in building out our data capability - you’ll be an important part of it, and you’ll help set the standards for how data engineering fits in as we mature into a product- and data-oriented company
  • You will directly influence not only the company’s data foundation, but also your own role, tooling, and processes
  • You’ll be the first dedicated data hire - no data team to lean on day-to-day, but IT is a close, responsive partner for integrations and infrastructure, and you’ll work closely with your manager for sparring and ensuring progress.
  • We’re not asking you to build this without the right tools - what you need to do the job well is part of the conversation once you’re in the role


Application

Please apply via the dedicated application link with your CV and brief written answers to a few short questions in the application form. That’s all we ask - no cover letter or portfolio needed. Keep your answers short and to the point; if we move forward, we’ll explore more in the interview.

As we strive for a thorough and unbiased recruitment process, we also kindly ask you to:

  • Use the dedicated application link (do NOT send your resume over e-mail)
  • Do NOT send a picture of yourself
  • Do NOT share your age or birth date


Recruitment steps:

  1. First interview - initial screening with Martin Rafn Møller starting from the 27th of July
  2. Second interview - a deeper conversation with the team, plus a take-home behavioural assessment (aprox. 30 min) and a follow-up with People & Culture
  3. Final call to discuss additional matters, if needed


Questions?

Feel free to reach out to Martin at mrm@omnidocs.com.

Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis. We’ll do our best to get back to you within 3-4 working weeks, but please be aware that this timeframe may be extended.


Processing of personal data

By registering in our HR system, BambooHR, and uploading both application and CV, you accept that Omnidocs stores the registered data about you and the data included in the uploaded documents. All information is used solely for recruitment purposes and will be deleted within 12 months of upload. Please do not include person-sensitive data in the application and CV, e.g. CPR number, information about race, religion, trade union conditions, and health information. You can read about Omnidocs’ guidelines for processing personal data here:

https://omnidocs.com/processing-of-personal-data-in-the-context-of-recruitment/

Title: Quantitative analyst for Short-term Power Trading
Description:
Experienced in quantitative modeling for energy or finance? Skilled in programming? Join us as a quantitative analyst for Intraday Analysis and make a real impact.




Here’s what the job entails

In this role, you will help optimise our trading performance by developing quantitative models that impact the decision-making process across our trading teams.




More specifically, your responsibilities include:

developing and enhancing quantitative models to drive trading decisions
taking active part in coding algorithmic trading infrastructure and optimisation engines used for live trading
conducting market research and strategy analysis aimed at improving overall PnL and operational robustness
collaborating with team members to ensure the operational stability of algorithms and tools developed by the team




Meet the team

As our new quantitative analyst, you will join our Intraday Analysis team, where we support the trading and asset optimisation teams by building quantitative models and trading tools. Our primary area of responsibility is to develop strategies, trading tools, and quantitative models that enable decision-making for our proprietary trading and asset business.




We are a team of 6 specialists in energy trading with backgrounds in economics and mathematics. We are a curious, dedicated, and highly analytical team who always strive to improve processes, systems, and strategies to fine-tune the infrastructural engine of our power trading business.




But enough about us… over to you!

As you will be developing models and analyses used for decision-making, we are looking for someone who has a data-driven approach and an eye for market risks while also evaluating other factors in your analyses. Also, as we operate in an ever-changing market, we see you as someone who thrives in a dynamic environment and can quickly adapt to market conditions.




We also imagine that you:

have an educational background in a quantitative field, for example, economics, mathematics, physics, statistics, data science, or engineering
have experience in a similar position, either in the energy industry, the financial markets or similar
have solid coding skills with the ability to write production-level Python code
have great communication skills as you should be able to explain your analyses to stakeholders who do not have the same insights as our team
are proficient in English - both written and verbal




Please note that prior knowledge of the energy sector is not required for this role.




We’ll take good care of you

At Danske Commodities, we take great care of our people. Joining us means you’ll get a lot of great perks – including social events, cultural experiences, tasty food and benefits to better your health, your life and your future.




Here are some of the benefits we offer our employees:

Shape your development tailored growth plans, 100+ DC University courses & Harvard Learning access

100+ yearly events from office parties to DC Sports events, CS:GO team, student network & more

Global career paths as part of our parent company, Equinor’s 20,000-strong international network

Flexible work life adjustable hours, part-time options & up to 2 work from home days/week

Fuel Fridays one paid Friday off every month (July and December excluded)

Top-tier parental leave up to 26 weeks paid, pension during leave & post-leave support

Unlimited paid child's sick days because family comes first

Extra time off 5 additional days on top of your 5 weeks

Senior days extra time off if you’re 55+

Attractive package 10% pension, health insurance, bonus scheme & more

Share savings the option to join Equinor’s attractive share programme

Home setup covered paid internet, phone & IT gear

Free tickets & discounts culture, family fun, hotels, restaurants & more

Organic meals light breakfast, lunch, snacks, dinner & take-away

Barista café high-quality coffee, always on us

On-site wellness state-of-the-art gym, physio, chiro and massage therapy

New HQ in the heart of Aarhus with stunning views, inspiring architecture and close to public transport

…. and much more

Title: Digital Solution Engineer – Offshore Wind Industry
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you passionate about developing, maintaining, and analysing data? Do you want to work in the offshore wind industry where your skills and expertise will help secure a sustainable future based on renewable energy? Then keep reading – Cadeler is looking for a Digital Solution Engineer with your skills and interests!

Cadeler is a global partner in the offshore wind industry, specializing in wind farm construction and maintenance. We are part of an industry that is now, more than ever, both relevant and play a critical role in the energy transition with a strong long-term outlook. As our industry continues to develop, so do we! We are now looking for a Digital Solution Engineer to help optimise our operational performance within jacking and monopile installation.

What will you do?

As our new Digital Solution Engineer, you will act as a bridge between offshore operational teams and onshore engineering by translating operational data into actionable insights. Furthermore, you will work hand-in-hand with the BI team regarding the data platform, data validation, and the development of dashboards.

Your Main Tasks Include


Analyse operational data from vessel systems, jacking logs, geotechnical data, and installation records.
Develop dashboards and visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Python-based dashboards, or similar) based on Cadeler’s data platform.
Identify trends, deviations, and performance improvement opportunities based on installation and jacking data. Support root cause analysis of operational events using data-driven methodologies.
Identify, propose, and refine engineering performance metrics in collaboration with the Operational Excellence and BI teams
Identify opportunities for performance optimization, cost reduction, and risk mitigation using data analytics.
Engage with clients when data-driven reporting or performance analysis is required


To succeed in this role

Being part of the Cadeler community means you will have a natural interest and care for the environment and our shared mission to speed up the green energy transition. Your environmental considerations and the safety of our people and the planet will be a key priority in your daily work.

We are looking for someone with a strong analytical and structured mindset, who possesses strong communication skills and is driven by curiosity and motivation to improve operations through digital solutions.

We think you will be a good match if you:


2–5 years of relevant experience
Academic degree in engineering (civil, mechanical, geotechnical, marine) or data science with strong technical/engineering exposure.
Experience with data analysis tools such as Python, Power BI, SQL, or similar.
Hands-on experience working with cloud-based data platforms (e.g., Snowflake or equivalent technologies)
Experience in offshore wind, marine operations, heavy lift, or jack-up vessels is an advantage but not mandatory.
Experience working with large datasets and operational performance analysis.


Come work with us!

By becoming a Cadeler employee, you will be part of a rapidly growing company with a diverse and energetic team. We offer an exciting position focused on delivering excellence in the face of interesting new challenges, within a positive and rewarding work environment in an international company with great development possibilities.

Cadeler is headquartered in Copenhagen, but we also have offices in Vejle, UK, US, and Taiwan, as well as colleagues working offshore aboard our fleet of Wind Farm Installation Vessels (WFIV).

Meet some of our colleagues here: https://www.cadeler.com/career

Apply

Use the APPLY button to apply for this position. For data protection purposes, applications are only accepted via the link and are assessed continuously until we find the right candidate, so please don’t hesitate to send in your application as soon as possible and no later than the 10th of August. You can expect to hear back from us after that date, due to vacation.

If you have any questions regarding the position, please contact Geotechnical & Jacking Engineering Team Lead, Can Mollaibrahimoglu, on (+45) 2979 9623, and for practical matters please contact peopleandculture@cadeler.com.

Title: Bliv dataanalytiker i Data Governance Execution & Technology hos Nykredit
Description:
Har du solide analytiske færdigheder og erfaring med at skabe overblik over komplekse datalandskaber? Motiveres du af at afdække dataflows, forstå forretningsprocesser og omsætte teknisk indsigt til tydelig dokumentation og konkrete data governance-leverancer? Og har du lyst til at være med til at optimere vores arbejdsgange og udforske, hvordan AI kan skabe mere effektive og værdiskabende data governance-processer? Så er du måske vores nye kollega.

Nykredit-koncernen er ejet af en forening af vores kunder, Forenet Kredit. Det betyder, at vi kan dele vores fremgang med vores kunder, når det går os godt, og at vi påtager os et særligt ansvar over for det samfund, vi er en del af. Det vil du også opleve, når vi kan byde dig velkommen som dataanalytiker.

Bliv en del af Data Governance Execution & Technology

Du bliver en vigtig del af et centralt team, der arbejder med at omsætte data governance-principper til praksis på tværs af Nykredit-koncernen. I tæt samarbejde med stakeholders fra forretning, IT og andre centrale funktioner skal du bidrage til at skabe transparens, dokumentation og fremdrift i arbejdet med datakvalitet, metadata, kritiske dataelementer, kilder og lineage.

Vi arbejder samtidig målrettet med at optimere og effektivisere vores processer, blandt andet ved at udforske og anvende AI som en naturlig del af data governance-arbejdet. Med din dataforståelse og forretningsmæssige nysgerrighed vil du bidrage til at skabe sammenhæng mellem forretningsprocesser, datakilder og regulatoriske krav.

Du forventes at kunne sætte dig ind i nye og komplekse forretningsområder, forstå værdikæder og identificere, hvor data skabes, transformeres og anvendes. Arbejdet kræver dataforståelse, analytisk struktur og evnen til at samarbejde med kolleger på tværs af forretning, IT, risikoområder og vores internationale teams. Du vil også være med til at identificere muligheder for at arbejde smartere, reducere manuelle processer og øge kvaliteten i vores leverancer – eksempelvis gennem bedre brug af værktøjer, standarder, automatisering og AI-understøttelse. Data Governance Execution & Technology er placeret i Aalborg og København, og da vi arbejder tæt sammen med vores søsterteam i Polen, er det vigtigt, at du kan kommunikere professionelt på engelsk.

Dine vigtigste arbejdsopgaver bliver:


Afdække, analysere og dokumentere datakilder, dataflows og lineage i tæt dialog med forretning, IT og relevante dataejere.
Bidrage til arbejdet med datakvalitet, metadata, kritiske dataelementer og autoritative kilder, så data bliver mere forståelige, anvendelige og styrbare.
Omsætte komplekse data- og forretningsforhold til tydelig dokumentation, beslutningsgrundlag og anbefalinger for både tekniske og ikke-tekniske interessenter.
Samarbejde tæt med stakeholders på tværs af forretning, IT og internationale teams for at sikre fremdrift, fælles forståelse og compliant løsninger.
Facilitere dialog med stakeholders for at afklare dataejerskab, forretningsbetydning, anvendelse og governance-behov.
Bidrage til optimering og effektivisering af data governance-processer, herunder identificere muligheder for automatisering og relevant brug af AI til at understøtte dokumentation



Er det dig, vi leder efter?

Du har en relevant uddannelse eller solid erfaring inden for dataanalyse, business analysis, systemanalyse, data governance eller lignende. Du trives med at arbejde i krydsfeltet mellem forretning og teknologi og har lyst til at dykke ned i komplekse datamodeller, kildesystemer og værdikæder. Erfaring fra den finansielle sektor, regulatoriske miljøer eller arbejde med Data Governance er en fordel, men ikke et krav. Det vigtigste er, at du kan skabe struktur, stille de rigtige spørgsmål og omsætte kompleksitet til brugbar viden.

Vi forventer, at dit CV afspejler, at du har:


erfaring med dataanalyse, SQL, SAS eller lignende værktøjer og evnen til at arbejde selvstændigt med komplekse datasæt,
forståelse for, hvordan data bevæger sig fra kildesystemer til anvendelse, og evne til at analysere og dokumentere dataflows, afhængigheder og transformationer,
erfaring med at kortlægge forretningsprocesser, værdikæder og dataflows for at identificere, hvor og hvordan data skabes, ændres og bruges,
interesse for Data Governance, herunder datakvalitet, metadata, lineage, kritiske dataelementer og autoritative kilder,
evne til at kommunikere klart med både tekniske specialister, forretningskolleger og ledelsesinteressenter,
gerne minimum fem års erfaring med dataanalyse, governance, dokumentation, stakeholder-samarbejde eller arbejde i en reguleret branche,
interesse for optimering, automatisering og anvendelse af AI som støtte til analyse, dokumentation og mere effektive arbejdsgange.



Dit nye team

Vi er et team med høj faglighed, uformel omgangstone og frihed under ansvar. Vi arbejder målrettet med at skabe fremdrift, struktur og gennemsigtighed i Nykredit-koncernens data governance-arbejde – fra teknisk afdækning og dokumentation til konkrete leverancer, der understøtter compliance, datakvalitet og bedre anvendelse af data. Vi har samtidig fokus på at arbejde smartere og mere effektivt gennem standardisering, optimering, automatisering og relevant brug af AI. Vi er et nyetableret team med fokus på eksekvering og teknologi, og vi lykkes gennem tæt samarbejde, nysgerrighed og en fælles ambition om at skabe orden i komplekse datalandskaber.

Vil du vide mere?

Du er velkommen til at kontakte mig, funktionschef Maria Whelan Mortensen på tlf. 26 86 50 32, hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen. Du kan også finde mig på LinkedIn her: Maria Whelan Mortensen | LinkedIn.

Har du spørgsmål til rekrutteringsprocessen, kan du kontakte rekrutteringsteamet på nytjob@nykredit.dk

Vi inviterer løbende til samtaler og ser frem til at modtage din ansøgning og dit CV.

HTML:
Har du solide analytiske færdigheder og erfaring med at skabe overblik over komplekse datalandskaber? Motiveres du af at afdække dataflows, forstå forretningsprocesser og omsætte teknisk indsigt til tydelig dokumentation og konkrete data governance-leverancer? Og har du lyst til at være med til at optimere vores arbejdsgange og udforske, hvordan AI kan skabe mere effektive og værdiskabende data governance-processer? Så er du måske vores nye kollega.

Nykredit-koncernen er ejet af en forening af vores kunder, Forenet Kredit. Det betyder, at vi kan dele vores fremgang med vores kunder, når det går os godt, og at vi påtager os et særligt ansvar over for det samfund, vi er en del af. Det vil du også opleve, når vi kan byde dig velkommen som dataanalytiker.

Bliv en del af Data Governance Execution & Technology

Du bliver en vigtig del af et centralt team, der arbejder med at omsætte data governance-principper til praksis på tværs af Nykredit-koncernen. I tæt samarbejde med stakeholders fra forretning, IT og andre centrale funktioner skal du bidrage til at skabe transparens, dokumentation og fremdrift i arbejdet med datakvalitet, metadata, kritiske dataelementer, kilder og lineage.

Vi arbejder samtidig målrettet med at optimere og effektivisere vores processer, blandt andet ved at udforske og anvende AI som en naturlig del af data governance-arbejdet. Med din dataforståelse og forretningsmæssige nysgerrighed vil du bidrage til at skabe sammenhæng mellem forretningsprocesser, datakilder og regulatoriske krav.

Du forventes at kunne sætte dig ind i nye og komplekse forretningsområder, forstå værdikæder og identificere, hvor data skabes, transformeres og anvendes. Arbejdet kræver dataforståelse, analytisk struktur og evnen til at samarbejde med kolleger på tværs af forretning, IT, risikoområder og vores internationale teams. Du vil også være med til at identificere muligheder for at arbejde smartere, reducere manuelle processer og øge kvaliteten i vores leverancer – eksempelvis gennem bedre brug af værktøjer, standarder, automatisering og AI-understøttelse. Data Governance Execution & Technology er placeret i Aalborg og København, og da vi arbejder tæt sammen med vores søsterteam i Polen, er det vigtigt, at du kan kommunikere professionelt på engelsk.

Dine vigtigste arbejdsopgaver bliver:

  • Afdække, analysere og dokumentere datakilder, dataflows og lineage i tæt dialog med forretning, IT og relevante dataejere.
  • Bidrage til arbejdet med datakvalitet, metadata, kritiske dataelementer og autoritative kilder, så data bliver mere forståelige, anvendelige og styrbare.
  • Omsætte komplekse data- og forretningsforhold til tydelig dokumentation, beslutningsgrundlag og anbefalinger for både tekniske og ikke-tekniske interessenter.
  • Samarbejde tæt med stakeholders på tværs af forretning, IT og internationale teams for at sikre fremdrift, fælles forståelse og compliant løsninger.
  • Facilitere dialog med stakeholders for at afklare dataejerskab, forretningsbetydning, anvendelse og governance-behov.
  • Bidrage til optimering og effektivisering af data governance-processer, herunder identificere muligheder for automatisering og relevant brug af AI til at understøtte dokumentation


Er det dig, vi leder efter?

Du har en relevant uddannelse eller solid erfaring inden for dataanalyse, business analysis, systemanalyse, data governance eller lignende. Du trives med at arbejde i krydsfeltet mellem forretning og teknologi og har lyst til at dykke ned i komplekse datamodeller, kildesystemer og værdikæder. Erfaring fra den finansielle sektor, regulatoriske miljøer eller arbejde med Data Governance er en fordel, men ikke et krav. Det vigtigste er, at du kan skabe struktur, stille de rigtige spørgsmål og omsætte kompleksitet til brugbar viden.

Vi forventer, at dit CV afspejler, at du har:

  • erfaring med dataanalyse, SQL, SAS eller lignende værktøjer og evnen til at arbejde selvstændigt med komplekse datasæt,
  • forståelse for, hvordan data bevæger sig fra kildesystemer til anvendelse, og evne til at analysere og dokumentere dataflows, afhængigheder og transformationer,
  • erfaring med at kortlægge forretningsprocesser, værdikæder og dataflows for at identificere, hvor og hvordan data skabes, ændres og bruges,
  • interesse for Data Governance, herunder datakvalitet, metadata, lineage, kritiske dataelementer og autoritative kilder,
  • evne til at kommunikere klart med både tekniske specialister, forretningskolleger og ledelsesinteressenter,
  • gerne minimum fem års erfaring med dataanalyse, governance, dokumentation, stakeholder-samarbejde eller arbejde i en reguleret branche,
  • interesse for optimering, automatisering og anvendelse af AI som støtte til analyse, dokumentation og mere effektive arbejdsgange.


Dit nye team

Vi er et team med høj faglighed, uformel omgangstone og frihed under ansvar. Vi arbejder målrettet med at skabe fremdrift, struktur og gennemsigtighed i Nykredit-koncernens data governance-arbejde – fra teknisk afdækning og dokumentation til konkrete leverancer, der understøtter compliance, datakvalitet og bedre anvendelse af data. Vi har samtidig fokus på at arbejde smartere og mere effektivt gennem standardisering, optimering, automatisering og relevant brug af AI. Vi er et nyetableret team med fokus på eksekvering og teknologi, og vi lykkes gennem tæt samarbejde, nysgerrighed og en fælles ambition om at skabe orden i komplekse datalandskaber.

Vil du vide mere?

Du er velkommen til at kontakte mig, funktionschef Maria Whelan Mortensen på tlf. 26 86 50 32, hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen. Du kan også finde mig på LinkedIn her: Maria Whelan Mortensen | LinkedIn.

Har du spørgsmål til rekrutteringsprocessen, kan du kontakte rekrutteringsteamet på nytjob@nykredit.dk

Vi inviterer løbende til samtaler og ser frem til at modtage din ansøgning og dit CV.

Title: Product Owner & Analytics Engineer
Description:
Vil du være med til at forme fremtidens dataplatform i en af Danmarks største energivirksomheder?

Har du evnen til at forstå forretningens behov, prioritere de vigtigste indsatser og omsætte dem til dataløsninger, der skaber værdi i praksis? Så er du måske vores nye Product Owner & Analytics Engineer.

En nøglerolle i udviklingen af OK's dataplatform
Du bliver en del af Data Engineering & BI, som har en central rolle i at gøre data til et strategisk aktiv på tværs af OK. Vi er i gang med en ambitiøs transformation, hvor vi bygger en moderne dataplatform og udvikler nye måder at arbejde med data på – med fokus på skalerbarhed, kvalitet, genanvendelighed og forretningsværdi.

I rollen kombinerer du Product Ownership og Analytics Engineering. Du sætter retningen for dit område, prioriterer indsatserne og sikrer, at teamet arbejder med det, der skaber størst værdi. Samtidig arbejder du hands-on med datamodellering, forretningslogik og udviklingen af data products, der omsætter data til konkrete indsigter og løsninger for forretningen.

Du får en bred kontaktflade på tværs af organisationen og stor indflydelse på både prioriteringer, arbejdsformer og udviklingen af OK's dataplatform.

Vi søger en profil, der forener kompetencer fra både Product Owner- og BI-udviklerrollen. Du kan kombinere forretningsforståelse, produktledelse og analytiske kompetencer for at sikre, at data omsættes til løsninger, der bliver anvendt og skaber værdi.

Dine opgaver er at:

Drive data- og analyseinitiativer fra idé til forretningsværdi.
Eje og prioritere backloggen for dit område.
Omsætte forretningsbehov til data products, KPI'er og analyser.
Udvikle genanvendelige datamodeller og forretningslogik.
Sikre høj anvendelse af data gennem stærke løsninger og tæt forretningsdialog.
Udfordre behov og skabe alignment mellem forretning, IT og Data.
Bidrage til udviklingen af OK's moderne dataplatform.

Du balancerer forretning, teknologi og samarbejde
Du trives i krydsfeltet mellem forretning og teknologi og motiveres af at omsætte behov til løsninger, der skaber værdi. Du tager naturligt ejerskab, skaber fremdrift og formår at omsætte komplekse problemstillinger til konkrete initiativer. Samtidig bygger du stærke relationer, udfordrer konstruktivt og skaber tillid på tværs af organisationen.

Vi ser gerne, at du har erfaring med:

Product Ownership eller lignende roller med ansvar for prioritering, roadmap og backlog.
At omsætte forretningsbehov til data products, analyser og konkrete løsninger.
Stakeholder management og samarbejde på tværs af forretning og IT.
Datamodellering, forretningslogik og udvikling af genanvendelige dataløsninger.
At skabe værdi og adoption gennem datadrevne løsninger.
Datakvalitet, governance og dokumentation.

Vi ser også gerne, at du har erfaring med følgende teknologier og værktøjer:

Databricks
dbt
Power BI
SQL

Det er en fordel, men ikke et krav, hvis du har erfaring med:

Microsoft SQL Server BI (SSIS, SSAS og SSRS)

Derfor skal du søge
Du bliver en del af et område i stor udvikling, hvor du får mulighed for at sætte dit præg på både vores dataplatform og den måde, vi arbejder med data på.

Vi tilbyder blandt andet:

En central rolle med stor indflydelse på OK's dataområde
Mulighed for at præge vores dataplatform og arbejdsmetoder
Et stærkt fagligt fællesskab med dygtige og hjælpsomme kolleger
Stor kontaktflade og tæt samarbejde med forretningen
Gode muligheder for faglig og personlig udvikling
Fleksible arbejdsforhold, sundhedsforsikring og attraktive personalegoder




Lyder det som noget for dig?
Så glæder vi os til at høre fra dig. Send dit CV og fortæl os kort, hvorfor netop du motiveres af at skabe værdi gennem data.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du meget velkommen til at tage fat i Senior Manager, Data Engineering, Peter Marcker på tlf.nr.: +4561680118 (ferie uge 30-32).

Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig.

Title: Advanced Data Analyst to join our Data Engineering department at Novo Nordisk Engineering
Place: Virum, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
If you’re energised by turning complex data into clear insights and you thrive on building structured, scalable BI solutions, we’d love to hear from you!

Who We Are

We are a leading pharma engineering company shaping the future of API pharma engineering globally. Leveraging the expertise of 2,000+ employees across Denmark, India, and the US, we design, build and optimise pharmaceutical manufacturing facilities that are essential to the health and well-being of patients everywhere.

Our success is driven by passion, innovation, and collaboration — with our customers and across our teams and disciplines. If you share our dedication to a noble cause and our passion for API pharma engineering, then NNE is the place for you.

About The Role

We are looking for an Advanced Data Analyst to join our Engineering Data & Systems team of 4 passionate colleagues.

As a team, we are working to strengthen the data foundation across engineering projects and support the transition from document-based to data-driven work. We focus on improving the structure and quality of data and enabling better decision-making through dashboards and visualizations.

You will play a key role in establishing how BI and reporting solutions are developed and managed across our VP area. As we are in an early phase, you will work hands-on across the full lifecycle – from understanding business needs to building and maintaining dashboards and reports. You will act as the go-to person for BI and visualization, supporting teams across our VP area with delivering high-quality, scalable solutions.

Over time, you will help shape standards and ways of working within BI and visualization as the area matures, with the opportunity to take on increased responsibility as the function grows.

Location Denmark - Virum

Experience Minimum 3 Years

Department Data Engineering

How You Might Spend Your Days

As an Advanced Data Analyst, you’ll be:


Leading the development of Power BI dashboards, turning business needs into clear and impactful visualizations
Designing reusable BI templates and ensure consistency in structure, layout, KPIs, and naming
Working with data from the Fabric workspace and apply the right data access and integration approach
Developing and maintain DAX calculations to enable scalable and reliable reporting
Ensuring high quality, performance, and continuous improvement of dashboards while supporting the team in reusing solutions



Who You Are

We care about who you are as a person. In the end, how you work, and your energy is what impacts the results we achieve as a team. As a person, you are:


Thriving by taking ownership, leading tasks from start to finish independently
Great at understanding the ability to collaborate across business and IT
Structured and quality-conscious approach to solving tasks with attention to detail
Pragmatic and solution-oriented mindset, and motived by creating value to the line of business
Able to translate complex data into simple insights and act as a trusted sparring partner to the team



What You Bring To The Role

In all positions, there are some things that are preferred, and others, a bonus. If you do not meet all the qualifications listed below, you are welcome to offer alternative experience that you think would be valuable in this role:


Solid experience (3-5 years) within data analysis or Business Intelligence, combined with a relevant educational background in data, IT, engineering, or similar
Extensive experience in Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, and data visualization
Experience working with modern data platforms (e.g. Fabric, Azure, or similar) and building scalable BI solutions
Great understanding of data flows, data models, and hands-on experience with SQL and data processing
Fluency in English



Life at NNE

NNE is short for Novo Nordisk Engineering. And for all of us who work here, it means so much more.

At NNE, you become part of a vibrant, project-based environment built on close day-to-day collaboration. The role is therefore primarily on-site, with physical presence at the workplace expected five days a week. Limited flexibility for occasional remote work may be accommodated in line with business needs.

We are, at our core, a people business and, as such, committed to continuously developing our people and the way we work together. We do not expect you to know it all from day one, and we truly believe a great start makes a real difference. We therefore welcome new colleagues with our award-nominated onboarding programme, supported by a range of in-house talent and development initiatives designed to support your long-term growth.

You can read more about the benefits we offer here .

Did we spark your curiosity? If you’re ready to join our passion, let’s hear from you.

Deadline of application is 22th July 2026

Questions before applying? Send us a quick text to arrange a convenient time to chat. Contact Manager, Mayes Ali on +45 3191 4302 or Director, Niels Karim Høst-Madsen on +45 3075 2705.

Between week 28-30 you can expect a delay in reply due to summer holiday. Therefore, we encourage you to call before Monday 6 July if you have questions.

We kindly refrain from questions regarding the potential fit into the role - we encourage you to apply to the position even if you are a bit in doubt. You might just be the one we are looking for!

If you have any questions regarding the application process, please write to recruit@nne.com and we will assist you as soon as possible.

Please note:


We only accept applications submitted through our online recruitment system
You do not need to attach a cover letter, but please include a few sentences about why you are applying in your resume or CV
We invite to interviews on a running basis.



Fair hiring:

At NNE, we are committed to an inclusive recruitment process and equal opportunities for all job applicants. To maintain fairness, we request that you refrain from including a personal photo in your CV.

Still searching for the right opportunity?

If this position isn’t quite what you're looking for, but you know someone who would excel in this role, please share this job post. And don’t forget to explore our other exciting opportunities — your future role at NNE might be just around the corner! Visit our Career Site

Title: Analytics Specialist
Description:
📍Copenhagen or Aarhus

📆 October, 2026

👩🏽‍💻 Office: Min. 3 Days a Week

📚Our Recruitment Process

Please Note: Due to the summer holiday period, our response times may be longer than usual. We appreciate your patience and look forward to reviewing your application.

We’re now looking for an Analytics Specialist to join our Danish team. In this role, you’ll help shape the data foundation and strategy for leading brands, driving app tracking and analytics initiatives alongside a skilled team.

With solid experience in analytics and data platforms, you have a passion for creating tracking and measurement solutions. You thrive on building strong partnerships and enjoy collaborating with both colleagues and clients to share insights and expertise.

We offer a dynamic, curiosity-driven culture where innovation is at the forefront. You’ll work with some of the industry’s best in digital marketing and martech, all driven to explore new frontiers in a supportive, collaborative environment.

What You Will Be Doing 💼


Design and implement comprehensive measurement plans, driving analytics solutions across both websites and mobile apps.
Maintain high data quality by diagnosing, troubleshooting, and resolving tracking and technical issues.
Innovate and contribute new ideas, conducting research to advance analytics capabilities and methodologies.
Provide strategic guidance to clients, helping them optimize their analytics architecture and enhance the depth and accuracy of insights.


WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR ✔️


Expertise in Tracking Methodologies: Expertise in tagging, data layers, and server-side tracking, especially within the Google stack (Google Analytics, Tag Manager, BigQuery).
Technical Proficiency: Confident in Javascript, web technologies (e.g., cookies, HTTP requests), and product APIs.
App Analytics Knowledge (preference): In-depth understanding of app analytics tools and platforms (e.g., Firebase, Appsflyer/Adjust, Amplitude/Mixpanel, SKAdNetwork).
Stakeholder Communication & Project Management: Skilled in engaging with stakeholders and managing project timelines effectively.
Experience: 2+ years of hands-on experience with tracking technologies.
High Technical Skills: Capable of guiding customers seamlessly through the integration process.


WHAT WE OFFER🌟

You can read our full offering in our handbook.
