Title: Data og AI-konsulent til Forskningsservice på Aalborg Universitet
Description:
Skab overblik, indsigt og konkrete løsninger sammen med engagerede specialister



Vil du arbejde i krydsfeltet mellem data, analyse og kunstig intelligens - og bruge dine kompetencer til at skabe konkrete forbedringer i hverdagen? Har du digital forestillingskraft? Trives du med at samarbejde tæt med faglige specialister og omsætte indsigt til nye digitale løsninger? Så er stillingen som Data- og AI-konsulent måske noget for dig!



Om stillingen som Data- og AI-konsulent



Som Data- og AI-konsulent får du en central rolle i at skabe overblik over data på tværs af Forskningsservice på Aalborg Universitet. Din organisatoriske forankring er i sekretariatet, men får din daglige gang i ”hele huset”, i alle Forskningsservices fem områder. Du skal arbejde tæt sammen med kolleger, afdelingscheferne for de fem områder, vicedirektøren og partnere på tværs af universitetet. Dit arbejde får direkte betydning for den måde, vi træffer beslutninger på og planlægger vores aktiviteter fremover.



Stillingen kombinerer to hovedopgaver, som tilsammen skal styrke beslutningsgrundlag og arbejdsgange i Forskningsservice:

Data management og indsigt, hvor du skaber overblik over afdelingens aktiviteter, opgaver og ressourceanvendelse.
AI-konsulentarbejde, hvor du sammen med dine kollegaer udvikler og afprøver AI-værktøjer, der understøtter konkrete opgaver og behov.

Du bliver bindeled mellem data, faglighed og teknologi og får en central rolle i at sikre, at data og AI anvendes målrettet, ansvarligt og med tydelig værdi for organisationen.



Dine primære arbejdsopgaver



Data management og indsigt



Det er en hovedopgave at opbygge og forankre en samlet datastruktur for Forskningsservice. Det indebærer, at du identificerer og samler de centrale data, som i dag er spredt på tværs af enheder og systemer. Du er med til at definere, hvordan data skal struktureres, vedligeholdes, anvendes og udstilles fremadrettet. Du arbejder både med at skabe et stærkt datagrundlag og med at sikre, at det giver mening for ledelse og modtagere i praksis:

Kortlægge, strukturere og vedligeholde data om afdelingens aktiviteter, opgaver og processer.
Udvikle analyser, oversigter og visualiseringer (fx dashboards i Power BI), der skaber gennemsigtighed og understøtter ledelsesbeslutninger.
Identificere mønstre og udvikling i opgaveporteføljen med henblik på prioritering, kapacitetsplanlægning og forbedring af arbejdsgange.
Bidrage til fælles forståelse af data, begreber og nøgletal på tværs af organisationen.

Du samarbejder med andre centrale afdelinger og administrative funktioner om adgang til og anvendelse af data, og du bidrager til, at data bruges på en ensartet og gennemtænkt måde på tværs af Forskningsservice. Du vil både arbejde med at opbygge nye løsninger og med løbende at sikre datakvalitet og opdaterede rapporter. Derudover kommer du til at spille en vigtig rolle i at formidle tal og analyser, så de er forståelige og anvendelige i den ledelsesmæssige hverdag.



AI-konsulent og opgaveudvikling



Den anden hovedopgave er identificere opgaver og arbejdsgange, hvor AI-værktøjer kan understøtte eller forenkle arbejdet. Du skal eksperimentere i samarbejde med dine kollegaer, som ofte ved, hvor potentialet er, men som ikke nødvendigvis ved, hvordan man prompter og bygger en agent:

Omsætte behov til konkrete AI-baserede løsninger og prototyper.
Bidrage til, at kollegaer udvikler deres forestillingskraft og udviklingsmod
Rådgive om anvendelse af generativ AI og andre AI-værktøjer med fokus på kvalitet, ansvarlighed, etik og datasikkerhed ud fra universitetets politikker og kodeks.
Understøtte implementering og forankring af nye løsninger i praksis, herunder formidling og kompetenceopbygning.

Vi lægger vægt på, at du

Har en relevant videregående uddannelse, anden relevant uddannelse eller tilsvarende kompetencer opnået gennem erfaring, inden for fx dataanalyse, samfundsvidenskab, IT eller digitalisering. 
Har erfaring med – eller stærk interesse for – dataanalyse, visualisering og struktureret arbejde med data.
Har kendskab til og erfaring med AI-værktøjer og deres praktiske anvendelse i opgaveløsning.
Trives i en rådgivende rolle og kan samarbejde med både tekniske og faglige specialister.
Kan formidle komplekse sammenhænge klart og anvendeligt for forskellige målgrupper.
Arbejder selvstændigt, struktureret og med blik for, hvordan løsninger skaber værdi i praksis.

Det er ikke et krav, at du er teknologispecialist fra dag ét – vigtigst er din analytiske tilgang, samarbejdsevne og lyst til at omsætte data og AI til konkrete forbedringer.



Vi tilbyder

En alsidig og udviklende stilling i krydsfeltet mellem data, analyse og AI.
Mulighed for at præge, hvordan data og AI anvendes i organisationen.
Tæt samarbejde med engagerede medarbejdere og ledelse.
Et fagligt miljø med plads til læring, eksperimenter og videndeling.
Fleksible rammer og gode muligheder for kompetenceudvikling.

Om os



Forskningsservice er en del af Fælles Service.



Forskningsservice består af enhederne Fundraising & Projektledelse, AAU Ph.d., Kontraktenheden og Missionssekretariat.



Vi yder administrativ understøttelse til AAU’s forskning og samarbejde med omverdenen. Dette sker blandt andet gennem:

Fundingrådgivning og administrativ projektledelse af større forskningsprojekter.
Juridisk rådgivning, udarbejdelse og forhandling af aftaler i forbindelse med forskningssamarbejde og databeskyttelse.
Administrativ håndtering af PhD studerendes uddannelsesforløb.
Understøttelse af udvalgte satsninger inden for missionsdrevet forskning, som AAU leder eller deltager i.

Ansøgning



Din ansøgning skal indeholde følgende:

Ansøgning, hvori du redegør for, hvilke faglige og personlige kompetencer samt erfaringer der gør dig særligt kvalificeret til stillingen
CV
Eksamensbevis
Evt. relevante bilag

Du kan læse mere om krav til din ansøgning her.



Ansøgning sendes via Aalborg Universitets rekrutteringssystem, som kan tilgås under stillingsopslaget på Aalborg Universitets hjemmeside.



Aalborg Universitet ønsker at afspejle det omgivende samfund og har diversitet som en kerneværdi. Derfor opfordres alle uanset personlig baggrund og orientering til at søge stillingen.



Har du spørgsmål?



Hvis du har spørgsmål, er du meget velkommen til at kontakte os. Du finder kontaktpersoner nederst i opslaget.



Samtaler forventes afholdt i uge 34, 2026.



Øvrige informationer



Du kan læse mere om vores rekrutteringsproces her.



Ansættelsesprocessen på Aalborg Universitet kan omfatte en risikovurdering som et værktøj til at identificere potentielle risici forbundet med nyansættelser, der sikrer arbejdspladsens sikkerhed, compliance og integritet.



Løn- og ansættelsesvilkår



Der er tale om en fast stilling med ugentlig arbejdstid på 37 timer.



Stillingen ønskes besat pr. 1. september eller 1. oktober, evt. snarest derefter.



Løn- og ansættelsesvilkår er enten i henhold til OAO-S-fællesoverenskomsten og cirkulære om organisationsaftale for IT-medarbejdere (PROSA) eller henhold til overenskomst for akademikere i staten. Der er mulighed for forhandling af kvalifikationstillæg.



Der er 3 måneders gensidig prøvetid i stillingen.



Aalborg Universitet - Viden for verden



Aalborg Universitet er en international arbejdsplads med mere end 3700 ansatte. Vi udbyder virkelighedsnære uddannelser og skaber forskningsresultater i verdensklasse gennem samarbejde mellem forskere, studerende og offentlige og private virksomheder. Sådan opnås erkendelser, nye løsninger på samfundets problemer og viden, der forandrer verden. Vores hovedcampus ligger i Aalborg, men vi har også campus i Esbjerg og København.

App Analytics Knowledge (preference): In-depth understanding of app analytics tools and platforms (e.g., Firebase, Appsflyer/Adjust, Amplitude/Mixpanel, SKAdNetwork).
Stakeholder Communication & Project Management: Skilled in engaging with stakeholders and managing project timelines effectively.
Experience: 2+ years of hands-on experience with tracking technologies.
High Technical Skills: Capable of guiding customers seamlessly through the integration process.

An intense learning environment where you will be able to grow and develop your skills and passions alongside some of the brightest minds in the industry.
A culture that is built on collaboration, trust and innovation. We value having fun together - that’s why we also have regular afterworks, team events and a yearly festival.

  • A culture that is built on collaboration, trust and innovation. We value having fun together - that’s why we also have regular afterworks, team events and a yearly festival.
  • Transparent salary setting, you can find all our salary bands in our Handbook - this role is an L2 role within the Analytics column. Check it out!
  • 30 days paid leave, flexible public holidays, parental pay, a solid pension scheme and key insurances. Or different offices also offer different perks such as health care contributions, healthy snacks & great coffee, EAP programs and your choice of PC or Mac.
  • A diverse environment with a high focus on inclusion and belonging. We speak over 27 native languages around Precis and our internal inclusion score is 4.3 out of 5.

We are committed to building an inclusive and fair workplace that attracts talent and advances its people, regardless of their background, experiences or perspectives. We believe that a diverse set of minds will continue to contribute to our innovative culture and our ability to reinvent ourselves constantly.

🇬🇧 To make things as smooth as possible, please ensure your CV is in English.

We update all candidates on their applications by email. If you don't receive an email from us, please check your spam folder.

Title: Finance & Settlement Data Analyst
Place: Høje Taastrup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
We are looking for a Finance & Settlement Data Analyst to own the financial and settlement data that sits between our trading desk, our counterparties, and the books — turning messy settlement data into clean, trusted numbers the business can act on.




Why this role matters

Hybrid Greentech is a fast-growing, tech-first energy trading scale-up building the data and algorithmic infrastructure behind the green transition. As we trade across more European power markets, accurate financial and settlement data becomes mission-critical — it underpins cash forecasting, risk reporting, and the trust our counterparties place in us. This role exists to make that data clean, reliable, and automated as we scale.




The role

You will be the bridge between trading operations and finance — reconciling traded volumes against settlement statements from TSOs, exchanges, and counterparties; investigating breaks; and turning messy settlement data into clean, trusted numbers the business can act on. As an individual contributor working closely with traders, finance, quants, and engineering, your work will directly shape financial accuracy, cash forecasting, and risk reporting.




What you will do




Lead the settlement and reconciliation cycle across power markets end-to-end (day-ahead, intraday, balancing, ancillary services) matching trades, invoices, and TSO/exchange statements, and leading the customer settlement process, including invoicing and cash balances.
Investigate and resolve settlement breaks with counterparties, exchanges, and internal desks, driving root-cause fixes rather than one-off patches.
Build and maintain SQL/Python pipelines that ingest counterparty and market data, calculate expected settlements, and surface discrepancies automatically.
Partner with finance on month-end close — accounting attribution, accruals, fee validation, and cash forecasting.
Support finance in cash analysis and cash flow forecasting to facilitate efficient cash management of flows end-to-end.
Support new market and product launches by mapping their settlement mechanics and standing up the data flows before go-live.




What you bring




Experience and skills (Required)

3–6 years in a finance, settlement, middle-office, or data analyst role — ideally in energy trading, commodities, banking, telecom, or another market-facing environment.
Strong SQL and working Python (polars) and comfortable wrangling large, imperfect datasets without reaching Excel as a first resort.
Solid grasp of the trade lifecycle and settlement mechanics: matching, netting, fees, invoicing, and breaks.
Numerate and precise, you notice when a number is 0.3% off, and you care why.
A clear written communicator in English, able to explain a settlement break to a trader and to a controller in the same week.




Nice to have

Direct experience with European power markets (EPEX, Nord Pool) or balancing/ancillary services.
Exposure to dbt, Airflow, or similar data tooling, and familiarity with cloud data warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Clickhouse).
Language: English (required); Danish (plus).
Understanding how settlement activities flow through financial accounting.




What we offer

A central seat in an ambitious, fast-scaling energy trading business with real, direct impact on the green transition.
Close collaboration with traders, quants, and engineering — short paths from idea to production, and room to grow into either a deeper data/engineering track or a broader finance leadership track.
Competitive salary and pension; freedom under responsibility.
Hybrid working from our Copenhagen office, with flexibility around the trading day.
The opportunity to work with forward-looking customers and partners driving electrification and the green transition in Northern Europe.




Ready to apply?

If you like the idea of owning the numbers that keep a fast-moving trading business honest — and turning messy settlement data into something the whole company trusts — we would love to hear from you. Send your CV and a short note on why this role fits. We review applications on a rolling basis and aim to respond within one week. Hybrid Greentech is an equal opportunity employer, and we welcome applicants from all backgrounds.




Deadline

Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis until 27 July 2026. We reserve the right to close this vacancy earlier if a suitable candidate is identified and accepted.




About Hybrid Greentech

Hybrid Greentech helps Battery Energy Storage Systems and flexible assets perform across their full lifecycle, from project readiness to real-time multi-market trading. We are a vertically integrated VPP, BRP, and retailer. We combine analytics, EPC management, asset integration and automated trading to turn flexibility into reliable value for the grid and for asset owners. We are a diverse, international team of 40+ based in Høje Taastrup, building technology that makes 100% renewable energy workable in practice.

Title: Commercial Analyst
Description:
Are you motivated by data, product performance and commercial decision-making? Do you want to work close to the commercial heart of a growing E-Commerce business, turning sales data, stock insights and product performance into clear commercial actions?

Then you might be the Commercial Analyst we are looking for. This is a newly created role in VILA E-Commerce, where you will develop at the intersection of Business Analysis and Buying. As part of our growing online business, you will work across our own site and Partner Program, including Zalando, About You and ASOS. You will also support NOISY MAY as part of our shared E-Commerce portfolio, gaining exposure to two strong fashion brands and multiple digital sales channels.

TOGETHER, WE ARE THE BEST

You will be part of a dynamic and business-driven E-Commerce department consisting of 14 employees today, who are specialists in each of their areas of responsibility. We take pride in our tasks, both individually and as a team. On top of this, we have a central E-Commerce team within BESTSELLER that we collaborate with on a weekly basis. 

A COMMERCIAL ROLE WITH A STRONG ANALYTICAL FOUNDATION

You will work at the intersection of Business Analysis and Buying across our own site and Partner Program, collaborating closely with our Business Analyst and Senior E-commerce Buyer. Through reporting, KPI follow-up, commercial analysis and NOOS insights, you will help turn data into concrete commercial actions and support product planning and buying decisions. As you build experience, you will take on greater ownership of product performance, stock depth, sell-through and assortment planning across styles and colours, becoming an increasingly important sparring partner in the buying process.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with numbers and performance, but who also wants to move closer to products, buying and commercial decision-making

Your key responsibilities


Follow up on sales, stock, availability and sell-through performance
Use product performance and NOOS-related insights to support commercial recommendations
Identify product opportunities, missed sales and replenishment potential
Support product-level planning across styles, colours, width and depth
Follow up on performance across own site and Partner Program


What We Offer


At VILA, you will gain broad exposure across Business Analysis, Buying and product planning, giving you a unique opportunity to develop your commercial skillset in E-Commerce.
As you grow in the role, you will take on increasing responsibility and play a bigger part in product planning and buying decisions.
Flexible working hours and the opportunity to work remotely are part of our culture.


YOUR PROFILE

You are structured, curious and motivated by understanding what drives sales. You enjoy working with numbers and details, but you are also interested in products, fashion and consumer behaviour. You may come from either an analytical or commercial background - what matters most is that you are curious, structured and motivated by turning data into better product decisions.

We imagine that you:


Have experience from E-Commerce, buying, merchandising or a similar commercial role, where you have worked with products, performance, analysis or planning. 
Are strong in Excel and confident working with numbers
Are curious about product performance and what drives E-Commerce sales
Have an interest in buying, product planning and commercial performance
Enjoy working closely with different stakeholders
Have a digital mindset and use data to drive decisions
Are fluent in English, both written and spoken
Experience with Power BI, NOOS, Partner Program, marketplace performance or buying support is an advantage, but not a requirement.


What Success Looks Like

Success in this role means that our commercial decisions become more data-driven, our product follow-up becomes sharper, and our buying preparation is supported by clear, actionable insights. You will help us understand what is working, where we see potential, and how product performance should influence future decisions on stock, width, depth, replenishment and commercial priorities.

WE ARE LOOKING FORWARD TO HEARING FROM YOU

For questions, please contact Rasmus Bech at +4530852934. Please send your application and CV as soon as possible, but no later than July 14, 2026. Applications are processed on an ongoing basis, and we reserve the right to remove the ad when we find the right candidate. We accept both Danish and English applications.

About Vila

The design philosophy of VILA is based on a feminine approach to fashion, uniting femininity and trend insights. VILA’s signature style is discovered in the mix of prints, delicate cuts and fine details. VILA was established in 1994 as part of BESTSELLER, a 100% family- owned company founded in 1975.Today, VILA is sold through 100+ chain stores all over Europe and 2000 multi-brand stores. VILA also covers vila.com and shop-in-shop. For more information, please visit www.vila.com or www.bestseller.com.

Title: Data Engineer - Supply Chain Advanced Analytics - Viby
Description:
Are you excited by building scalable data solutions that directly impact Supply Chain decisions across a global organization? Curious about how data can truly drive business decisions - and not afraid to challenge the status quo to get there?

You will be part of a collaborative Advanced Analytics team where we co-create data-driven solutions with the business and continuously improve our platforms and way of working. We value curiosity, dialogue and the courage to challenge ideas to create better outcomes.

How You Will Make an Impact?

As part of the Supply Chain Advanced Analytics product team, you will work across a broad analytics landscape supporting functions such as SC Finance, Logistics, Planning, QEHS and Procurement.

You will contribute to building and maintaining robust data solutions while collaborating closely with business stakeholders to deliver real value. You will not only implement solutions but also seek to understand business challenges, ask the right questions and help continuously improve how data is used across the organization.

Your Key Responsibilities Will Include


Build and maintain end-to-end data pipelines (ingestion, transformation and delivery)
Implement enhancements to improve functionality and performance of supported solutions
Provide technical feedback and drive improvements to operational practices
Lead and coordinate UAT and PAT with business stakeholders, ensuring solutions are challenged, validated and aligned with real business needs
Share and document knowledge to support team development and future scalability


What Will Make You Successful?

We are looking for a data-focused IT Developer with strong analytical and collaboration skills, who thrives in a self-managing, collaborative and business-driven environment. You are curious about both data and the business behind it and motivated to understand how solutions create value. You have strong communication skills and can translate between technical and business perspectives as well as an eagerness to learn and continuously develop your technical and business skills. Further, we are looking for below professional competencies


Hands-on experience with Azure data services (e.g., Databricks, Data Lake, Synapse, Azure SQL, Data Factory, Azure Data Explorer)
Good understanding of Azure as a platform
Solid experience with data systems, analytics and databases
SQL proficiency and working knowledge of relational databases
Basic to intermediate Python knowledge
Familiarity with data modelling and data pipeline technologies
Experience with Git and branching strategies
Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and deployments
Experience working closely with business stakeholders (requirements gathering, alignment, etc.)


What Do We Offer?

You will join a team of 21 specialists working across Data Foundation, Power BI, SAP BW, SAP Analytics Cloud, BODS and Power Apps. Together, we deliver business-driven analytics solutions that strengthen decision-making across the Supply Chain.

We offer


A collaborative and diverse product team working closely with the business
Exposure to a wide range of technologies and data platforms
Opportunities to shape future analytics capabilities
A strong culture of knowledge sharing and continuous improvement
A direct impact on global Supply Chain transformation


Our team delivers solutions such as BW reports, Power BI dashboards, Power Apps, curated datasets and machine learning models - with AI agents as our next step. All solutions are built in close partnership with stakeholders, ensuring real business value.

Would you like to join us?

If you are passionate about data engineering and want to make a real impact in a global Supply Chain organization, we encourage you to apply.

Please apply as soon as possible, as we process applications on a continuous basis.

For more information, please contact Magda Merecka at magmg@arlafoods.com

Shape the Future of Dairy

Arla is a global leader in the dairy industry, committed to enabling good food choices that make life better, providing people with natural, sustainable nourishment, while taking care at every step to ensure Arla is a choice they can feel good about. If you are looking to shape the future with an ambitious global cooperative that truly cares about your growth, where everyone feels valued and empowered, and collaboration is the core of culture, Arla is a choice you can feel good about.

Title: Business Analytics Consultant
Description:
Twoday er den førende digitale transformationspartner i Nordeuropa med en global tilstedeværelse. Med cirka 3.000 teknologer samarbejder vi med de mest beundrede private og offentlige organisationer for at levere banebrydende digitale løsninger. Vores dybe brancheekspertise omfatter Data & AI, softwareudvikling, digitale oplevelser og forretningsapplikationer. Vores team, der opererer i hele Norden og Litauen, genererede en omsætning på 280 millioner euro i 2023. Vi betjener over 8.000 kunder og understøtter deres digitale transformationsrejser.

Vil du være med på Danmarks stærkeste team af Business Analytics-konsulenter? Vil du arbejde med nogle af Danmark største virksomheder? Tænder du på at gøre komplekse problemstillinger til simple løsninger for kunderne? Så er twoday Data & AI det rigtige sted for dig! Vi søger nye Business Analytics-konsulenter til et af vores kontorer i Danmark.

Om stillingen

Som Business Analytics-konsulent vil du indgå i et stærkt team med en varierende hverdag – du vil få ansvar, frihed og faglig udvikling. Vores konsulenter besidder hver især stærke kompetencer, og du vil opleve kolleger, der er specialister på deres område.

Du vil sammen med vores kunder afklare de forretningsmæssige behov og derefter designe, udvikle og implementere løsningen. Alt efter kompleksitet og størrelse af løsningen arbejder du enten i et team eller som primær konsulent på løsningerne.

Vi holder faste firmadage, hvor sparring, idéudveksling og kollegialt sammenhold er i højsædet. Du vil opleve at blive udfordret, men du vil også få mulighed for at udfordre dine kolleger.

Fristet, men stadig i tvivl?


Turbo på det faglige! Hos twoday Data & AI er vores medarbejdere kendt for at have et skyhøjt fagligt niveau. Samtidig med at vi har det godt socialt.
Lækre kontorer! twoday Data & AI har lækre og moderne kontorer centralt placeret i København, Aarhus, Aalborg og Kolding.
Tid til fordybelse! Vi har fire årlige fagdage, hvor idéudvikling, udvikling og sparring er i højsædet.
Uddannelse! Hos twoday Data & AI får du mulighed for efteruddannelse og certificeringer.
Attraktiv lønpakke! Vi tilbyder en attraktiv lønpakke med branchens bedste bonusordning.
Fantastisk kaffe! Hos twoday Data & AI har du adgang til fitness, frugt og fantastisk god kaffe.


Om dig

Du har flere års relevant erhvervserfaring og brænder for hele tiden at blive bedre. Du har en baggrund som BI- eller datakonsulent, controller i en større virksomhed eller noget lign. Du har erfaring med Azure Data Platform, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks og/eller SQL Server. Endvidere har du kendskab til én eller flere front-end værktøjer såsom Power BI, Excel eller lign.

Du ved, at livet som konsulent er udfordrende og til tider meget krævende. Vores vigtigste aktiv er vores kunder og medarbejdere, og du er klar til at give, hvad der skal til, for at skabe et godt forhold både internt og eksternt samt at udvikle Business Intelligence-løsninger i verdensklasse.

Din uddannelse kan være cand.merc., cand.merc.dat eller cand.oecon – eller for den sags skyld noget helt fjerde. Det vigtigste er, at du brænder for Business Analytics og ønsker at gøre en forskel.

Dine kommunikative evner er i top, og du skriver og taler flydende dansk og engelsk.

Vi begejstrer med data – måske vil du være med?

Så send din ansøgning i dag, de vil løbende blive læst.

Der vil løbende blive indkaldt til indledende samtaler. Du ansøger ved at klikke på knappen nedenfor. Alle ansøgninger behandles fortroligt.

Hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen, er Ann Brahe-Orlandi, Talent Acquisition Manager, klar til at hjælpe. Hun glæder sig til at høre fra dig på ann.brahe-orlandi@twoday.com.

Hvad kan vi tilbyde?

Twoday er placeret i moderne lokaler på 30 steder i Norge, Sverige, Danmark, Finland og Litauen. Vi har et stærkt og unikt socialt miljø med højt engagerede medarbejdere. Hvis du er den kandidat, vi leder efter, vil du få en central rolle med spændende muligheder for at udvikle dig i en international og innovativ gruppe, med spændende produkter og tjenester inden for teknologi og software. Du vil også have fleksibiliteten til at "jumpe" og arbejde fra et af vores hovedkontorer i Oslo, Stockholm, København, Helsinki eller Vilnius til tider.

HTML:
Twoday er den førende digitale transformationspartner i Nordeuropa med en global tilstedeværelse. Med cirka 3.000 teknologer samarbejder vi med de mest beundrede private og offentlige organisationer for at levere banebrydende digitale løsninger. Vores dybe brancheekspertise omfatter Data & AI, softwareudvikling, digitale oplevelser og forretningsapplikationer. Vores team, der opererer i hele Norden og Litauen, genererede en omsætning på 280 millioner euro i 2023. Vi betjener over 8.000 kunder og understøtter deres digitale transformationsrejser.

Vil du være med på Danmarks stærkeste team af Business Analytics-konsulenter? Vil du arbejde med nogle af Danmark største virksomheder? Tænder du på at gøre komplekse problemstillinger til simple løsninger for kunderne? Så er twoday Data & AI det rigtige sted for dig! Vi søger nye Business Analytics-konsulenter til et af vores kontorer i Danmark.

Om stillingen

Som Business Analytics-konsulent vil du indgå i et stærkt team med en varierende hverdag – du vil få ansvar, frihed og faglig udvikling. Vores konsulenter besidder hver især stærke kompetencer, og du vil opleve kolleger, der er specialister på deres område.

Du vil sammen med vores kunder afklare de forretningsmæssige behov og derefter designe, udvikle og implementere løsningen. Alt efter kompleksitet og størrelse af løsningen arbejder du enten i et team eller som primær konsulent på løsningerne.

Vi holder faste firmadage, hvor sparring, idéudveksling og kollegialt sammenhold er i højsædet. Du vil opleve at blive udfordret, men du vil også få mulighed for at udfordre dine kolleger.

Fristet, men stadig i tvivl?

  • Turbo på det faglige! Hos twoday Data & AI er vores medarbejdere kendt for at have et skyhøjt fagligt niveau. Samtidig med at vi har det godt socialt.
  • Lækre kontorer! twoday Data & AI har lækre og moderne kontorer centralt placeret i København, Aarhus, Aalborg og Kolding.
  • Tid til fordybelse! Vi har fire årlige fagdage, hvor idéudvikling, udvikling og sparring er i højsædet.
  • Uddannelse! Hos twoday Data & AI får du mulighed for efteruddannelse og certificeringer.
  • Attraktiv lønpakke! Vi tilbyder en attraktiv lønpakke med branchens bedste bonusordning.
  • Fantastisk kaffe! Hos twoday Data & AI har du adgang til fitness, frugt og fantastisk god kaffe.

Om dig

Du har flere års relevant erhvervserfaring og brænder for hele tiden at blive bedre. Du har en baggrund som BI- eller datakonsulent, controller i en større virksomhed eller noget lign. Du har erfaring med Azure Data Platform, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks og/eller SQL Server. Endvidere har du kendskab til én eller flere front-end værktøjer såsom Power BI, Excel eller lign.

Du ved, at livet som konsulent er udfordrende og til tider meget krævende. Vores vigtigste aktiv er vores kunder og medarbejdere, og du er klar til at give, hvad der skal til, for at skabe et godt forhold både internt og eksternt samt at udvikle Business Intelligence-løsninger i verdensklasse.

Din uddannelse kan være cand.merc., cand.merc.dat eller cand.oecon – eller for den sags skyld noget helt fjerde. Det vigtigste er, at du brænder for Business Analytics og ønsker at gøre en forskel.

Dine kommunikative evner er i top, og du skriver og taler flydende dansk og engelsk.

Vi begejstrer med data – måske vil du være med?

Så send din ansøgning i dag, de vil løbende blive læst.

Der vil løbende blive indkaldt til indledende samtaler. Du ansøger ved at klikke på knappen nedenfor. Alle ansøgninger behandles fortroligt.

Hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen, er Ann Brahe-Orlandi, Talent Acquisition Manager, klar til at hjælpe. Hun glæder sig til at høre fra dig på ann.brahe-orlandi@twoday.com.

Hvad kan vi tilbyde?

Twoday er placeret i moderne lokaler på 30 steder i Norge, Sverige, Danmark, Finland og Litauen. Vi har et stærkt og unikt socialt miljø med højt engagerede medarbejdere. Hvis du er den kandidat, vi leder efter, vil du få en central rolle med spændende muligheder for at udvikle dig i en international og innovativ gruppe, med spændende produkter og tjenester inden for teknologi og software. Du vil også have fleksibiliteten til at "jumpe" og arbejde fra et af vores hovedkontorer i Oslo, Stockholm, København, Helsinki eller Vilnius til tider.

Title: Business Analyst
Description:
BORG Automotive is a leading European supplier of both remanufactured and new automotive spare parts, serving the independent aftermarket with quality, sustainability, and innovation. We are driven by our core values of competence, responsibility, transparency, interdependency, and continuous improvement. With a flat organizational structure and open management style, we empower our people to take initiative, collaborate across functions, and make a real impact on our business.

Your Role

As a Business Analyst, you will take on a key staff role reporting to our Aftermarket Sales Director. You will be an integral part of our commercial organization, supporting data-driven decision-making across the business. Your role will focus on transforming data into clear insights that enable the Sales team to make the right strategic and operational decisions. You will collaborate closely with stakeholders across the organization in a dynamic and international environment.

Key Responsibilities


Conduct data-driven analyses to support strategic and operational decisions within the sales department
Develop, maintain, and improve reporting, dashboards, and performance tracking
Identify trends, risks, and business opportunities across markets, customers, and products
Support forecasting, budgeting, and business planning processes
Collaborate with Sales and other stakeholders to understand business needs and ensure relevant analysis
Drive continuous improvement of analytical tools, data quality, and working methods


What We’re Looking For


Experience working with data analysis in a commercial or business-oriented environment
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to convert data into business insights
Advanced Excel skills and experience with reporting and BI tools (e.g., Power BI is an advantage)
Strong communication skills and ability to present complex data in a clear and structured way
A proactive, structured, and solution-oriented approach
Collaborative mindset and ability to work with multiple stakeholders


What We Offer


A flat and open organizational structure where initiative is encouraged
A collaborative culture that supports your personal and professional growth
The opportunity to influence business decisions
A dynamic and international work environment with strong focus on development and learning
Attractive employment conditions aligned with your qualifications


Ready to Make an Impact?

If you are motivated by turning data into business value and want to play a key role in supporting commercial decisions, we would love to hear from you. Join BORG Automotive - where "the most important part is you" is not just our motto, but how we work.

We will process the applications ongoing, but due to summer holidays we will take the first look at candidates medio August.

Title: Business Intelligence Consultant
Company: SameSystem - Retail WFM | Part of ECIT
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
BI Consultant




Please note that fluency in Danish is a requirement for this position.

Due to the summer holidays, please expect a longer processing time.




SameSystem

Are you ready to turn data into real commercial value for some of Europe's largest retail and hospitality brands? At SameSystem, we empower companies like Bestseller, Nike, Normal, and Matas to optimize their operations daily with our cutting-edge AI-driven Workforce Management Solution. We're looking for a BI Consultant who is as comfortable building a clean dashboard as sitting across the table from a customer explaining what it means for their business.

This is a full-time role, and you'll join our Customer Success team in Copenhagen. You'll own the business intelligence and reporting side of our platform: building and maintaining reports in our BI tool, Sisense, and being the person our customers and colleagues turn to when a number needs to make sense. We've deliberately placed this role in Customer Success rather than in a back-office data team, because the reporting is only worth as much as the commercial insight it drives. This role suits someone who is happy behind the screen building things properly, but who does not want to only sit behind the screen.

This is a new role, so the right person will have real influence over how we build, package, and deliver the analytics side of our platform.




Key responsibilities

Building the reports

Design, build, and maintain BI reports and dashboards in Sisense, with strong attention to data integrity, performance, and usability
Translate customer and internal requirements into robust data models and reporting logic
Own reporting quality across accounts, including validation, troubleshooting, and iterative improvement
Develop new reporting capabilities and scope custom development work as our customers' needs grow




Working with customers

Join customer meetings and represent the BI and analytics part of the SameSystem platform
Talk specific development and scoping with customers, and translate their business questions into what we can actually build
Help customers see the value in their data, and support Customer Success and Sales in retention, upselling, and expansion through analytics
Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Sales, and Support to feed customer needs back into the platform




What we're looking for

Proven experience building reports and dashboards in a BI tool. Experience with Sisense is a strong plus; solid experience with a comparable tool (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, Qlik) is very welcome
Enough experience under your belt to own your work end to end
Strong SQL and data modelling skills, and the instinct to build things properly rather than just quickly
Commercial acumen. You understand that a report exists to drive a decision, and you're comfortable talking value and scope with customers
Confidence in client-facing settings, presenting clearly to both operational and managerial stakeholders
The balance we keep coming back to: someone who can do focused, hands-on build work and also step into a customer meeting and hold their own
A self-starter who takes ownership and isn't afraid to share ideas. Execution matters
Nice to have: comfort scoping and pricing development work. If pricing is new to you, that's fine, we can teach it
Fluency in Danish and English; additional European languages are a plus
Familiarity with retail operations or workforce management is a plus




What you'll get

At SameSystem, we are committed to transforming retail and hospitality through cutting-edge technology. Joining our team means:

Working in a dynamic environment where innovation drives success.
Contributing to the success of over 185,000 employees across 12,500+ stores in 24 countries.
Access to professional growth opportunities in a company that values expertise and collaboration.
Being part of a supportive culture that prioritizes work-life balance and employee satisfaction.
Lots of social activities and fun parties as well as "clubs" run by employees where SameSystem sponsors going to a sauna, playing badminton, drinking port wine, or whatever else the team can think of.
Lunch and as much coffee, soda or energy drink as you can muster.




If you have any questions about this position, please reach out to Nikolai May Yde at nikolai.yde@samesystem.com.

Title: Data Scientists for Big Data, IoT and AI projects
Description:
Stillingsbeskrivelse

Do you like working creatively with data analytics, big data, AI, visualisation, NoSQL and databases? Would you like to join an international organisation where we already work with data analytics and machine learning projects? Join BEUMER Group and exploit the chance to innovate, develop and test concepts for products and tools and most importantly, develop your own skill.

You will team up with colleagues, so you can share and grow your knowledge within the data science field. It also means focusing on using data from an already existing infrastructure where we collect data from more than 350 sites worldwide.

Join our Customer Diagnostics Center

You become part of our Data Analytics department, which is part of our Customer Diagnostics Center (CDC). In the CDC, which is part of our Customer Support division, we are tasked with making sure our installed base of systems around the globe are healthy and running smoothly.

Here, you join a well-established but agile team of highly sought for data scientists. You have the freedom to develop solutions across the organisation focusing on creativity and innovation on the conceptual stages of possible new products, services and tools.

In the CDC, you will find a multitude of skills and competences, and we enjoy an informal atmosphere where we combine our strengths to create brand new solutions for colleagues and customers. Moreover, you become part of a data analytics forum where we work closely with universities to expand our knowledge.

Create value through data across the organisation

Through a continuous dialogue with our business and product development, you will be responsible for defining new products, services and tools based on data and machine learning. We are a large organisation with several specialist fields, which means that we can enjoy the value of data analysis across disciplines.

Your Tasks Will Include


Initiating mining of existing data from material handling systems running in production, looking for possible bottlenecks, optimizations etc.
Processing, cleansing, and verifying the integrity of data used for analysis.
Participating in the maintenance of the existing data pipeline.
Participate in defining and evaluating new techniques for data collection and analysis.
Drive the rollout and extension of our data collection to all sites in the BEUMER Group.


We have a strong preference for open-source software and are working with platforms and tools such as Kubernetes, ArgoCD, MLFlow, vLLM, Elastic stack, Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, Numaflow, Python/Java/Golang, Jupyter notebooks and Opentelemetry. You will test and implement prototypes, e.g. for customer solutions or for your colleagues to provide them with the right tools to perfect their work. As such, you bridge the worlds of product development, subject specialists and technology to innovate the business.

Experience with data driven decision making, big data and concept development?

Role

Most important to this role is that you have a good all-round technical foundation, allowing you to implement software, configure tools, understand our domain and be a sounding board in the creative process. To do so, we also imagine that you:


Hold a master's degree or PhD as a computer scientist, engineer, physicist or similar.
Have a great understanding of and hands-on experience with using data analytics for system and concept development.
Speak and write English fluently and it is a big plus if you understand Danish as well.


Personality-wise, you are a strong communicator who can work across the organisation and with customers. Your strength is within innovation and creative thinking, which shows itself in added value in the concept development process and tests.

Interested?

If you have any questions regarding this position, please do not hesitate to contact Head of Global Data Analytics Anders Lildballe at +45 30 17 93 48. Please apply online via beumergroup.com/career.

We look forward to meeting you.

BEUMER Group

Conveying, loading, palletising, packing, sortation and distribution – BEUMER Group is a leader within the development and manufacture of high-technological intralogistics systems for global markets. Our employees stand out from others thanks to their ability to supply innovative solutions to our customers. They can do this because they know the industry and are passionate about continuously developing and expanding their knowledge. We support these high standards through teamwork, mutual respect and a working culture based on trust that fosters stability and security for all our employees. Our common goal is to implement outstanding and innovative projects worldwide.

Title: REVENUE & ANALYTICS MANAGER
Place: Kastrup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
CARGO REVENUE AND ANALYTICS MANAGER

Join SAS Cargo and help shape the future of our performance.

SAS Cargo is a premium multi-market airline driving sustainable profits on our way towards a consolidated future. SAS Cargo’s services are based on the cargo capacity of SAS network, extended by dedicated truck operations. Besides expanding our presence in main hub Copenhagen, we are transforming how we make commercial decisions by using more data, acting faster, and serve better.

If you’re passionate about analytics, commercial optimization, and creating measurable business impact, this is your opportunity.

You will be part of Commercial’s RM and Pricing team, a small and diverse team responsible for optimizing SAS Cargo’s revenue and contribution, and ensuring that SAS Cargo is offering a competitive network, relevant products and competitive rates on SAS capacity.

What You’ll Do

In-depth analysis


Analyze performance across yields, load factors, revenue drivers, and flights to identify trends and improvement opportunities
Conduct scenario modelling and forecasting together with the Finance Business Controller and Partner and Strategic Pricing Manager to support strategic decisions and reporting outlooks
Contribute to our ongoing ML/AI initiatives, enhancing our predictive capabilities and enabling smarter, data‑driven decision‑making.
Support or lead development of business cases and business follow-up across SAS Cargo
Act as an internal ambassador for data-driven decision making within SAS Cargo.


Revenue Management


Optimize revenue contribution on SAS belly capacity on your own route portfolio through optimal rates, overbooking decisions and sales mix analysis
Work cross functional with Operations managers and planners, and Sales on day-to-day route optimization
Monitor and adjust steering (rates, capacity allocation) based on market changes and input from stakeholders
Evaluate and set up allotment allocations
Respond to special rate requests (spot market)
Work with Global Sales and Strategic Pricing Manager to set rates correctly and win deals
Work with our RM architect and rest of the RM-team to continuously develop best practice and adoption of our upcoming new RM system enhancements.


What You Bring


Required: 1-2 years of experience in an analytical or technical role. Minimum BSc degree or equivalent. Previous experience in revenue management, data/business analysis, or a related field within aviation, logistics, transportation, hospitality or other network industries is big plus.
Preferred: Experience with statistical modeling, RM systems, programming, or quantitative analytics will make you stand out.


Your strengths


Strategic & Analytical Thinking: You thrive on solving complex problems, making data-driven decisions, and turning insights into strategies that drive business growth.
Clear Communication: You excel at explaining complex data insights in a way that’s both clear and compelling, connecting with colleagues across departments.
Proactive & Results-Driven: You believe in making things happen! You take the initiative, move quickly from analysis to action, and consistently deliver impactful results.
Why You Should Apply: At our company, we believe in supporting each other and championing everyone’s potential. If you're passionate about what you do and want to work in a place that values personal development, well-being, and empowerment, this is the role for you.


Our Culture At SAS Cargo And What We Offer

At SAS Cargo, you will join a collaborative and supportive team where you can take initiative, influence key commercial processes, and contribute to creating value across the organization.

We Offer


A role with real responsibility and opportunity to drive improvements in revenue and decision-making
A strong team culture where we collaborate across SAS Cargo and the wider SAS organization, recognize each other’s efforts, and share successes
A structured onboarding program with support from experienced colleagues and your manager
Workplace flexibility, including the possibility of some remote work and option to access a gym
Social activities with colleagues in and outside of work
Access to staff tickets and discounts. You can read more at https://www.sasgroup.net/career/working-at-sas/benefits-and-compensations/


Additional Information 


Interviews will not take place before 2nd half of August. Application deadline 16 august 2026. Note that we cannot handle any applications that are sent by email, due to GDPR regulations.
Desired start date: As soon as possible.
Position: Fulltime (100%) starting with a 3-month probation period
This position is based at SAS Cargo’s new HQ facility at Copenhagen airport, and you will be expected to work onsite. Some remote work may also be an option, depending on the agreement made with your manager.
To be eligible for this position you need an EU-work permit. Please note that we ask you to inform us if you currently have a valid EU-work permit.


SAS Cargo is the leading provider of air freight solutions to, from and within Scandinavia, delivering world class quality and customer care. SAS Cargo’s services are based on the cargo capacity of SAS network, extended by dedicated truck operations.

  • Work with Global Sales and Strategic Pricing Manager to set rates correctly and win deals
  • Work with our RM architect and rest of the RM-team to continuously develop best practice and adoption of our upcoming new RM system enhancements.

What You Bring

  • Required: 1-2 years of experience in an analytical or technical role. Minimum BSc degree or equivalent. Previous experience in revenue management, data/business analysis, or a related field within aviation, logistics, transportation, hospitality or other network industries is big plus.
  • Preferred: Experience with statistical modeling, RM systems, programming, or quantitative analytics will make you stand out.

Your strengths

  • Strategic & Analytical Thinking: You thrive on solving complex problems, making data-driven decisions, and turning insights into strategies that drive business growth.
  • Clear Communication: You excel at explaining complex data insights in a way that’s both clear and compelling, connecting with colleagues across departments.
  • Proactive & Results-Driven: You believe in making things happen! You take the initiative, move quickly from analysis to action, and consistently deliver impactful results.
  • Why You Should Apply: At our company, we believe in supporting each other and championing everyone’s potential. If you're passionate about what you do and want to work in a place that values personal development, well-being, and empowerment, this is the role for you.

Our Culture At SAS Cargo And What We Offer

At SAS Cargo, you will join a collaborative and supportive team where you can take initiative, influence key commercial processes, and contribute to creating value across the organization.

We Offer

  • A role with real responsibility and opportunity to drive improvements in revenue and decision-making
  • A strong team culture where we collaborate across SAS Cargo and the wider SAS organization, recognize each other’s efforts, and share successes
  • A structured onboarding program with support from experienced colleagues and your manager
  • Workplace flexibility, including the possibility of some remote work and option to access a gym
  • Social activities with colleagues in and outside of work
  • Access to staff tickets and discounts. You can read more at https://www.sasgroup.net/career/working-at-sas/benefits-and-compensations/

Additional Information 

  • Interviews will not take place before 2nd half of August. Application deadline 16 august 2026. Note that we cannot handle any applications that are sent by email, due to GDPR regulations.
  • Desired start date: As soon as possible.
  • Position: Fulltime (100%) starting with a 3-month probation period
  • This position is based at SAS Cargo’s new HQ facility at Copenhagen airport, and you will be expected to work onsite. Some remote work may also be an option, depending on the agreement made with your manager.
  • To be eligible for this position you need an EU-work permit. Please note that we ask you to inform us if you currently have a valid EU-work permit.

SAS Cargo is the leading provider of air freight solutions to, from and within Scandinavia, delivering world class quality and customer care. SAS Cargo’s services are based on the cargo capacity of SAS network, extended by dedicated truck operations.

Title: Business Analyst, AI Fashion Planning
Description:
Join BESTSELLER TECH and revolutionize fashion with machine learning!

Do you want to be part of shaping the future of fashion? BESTSELLER TECH is looking for a skilled and dynamic individual to join our AI Fashion Planning team as a Business Analyst.

Become a part of our mission to drive data-driven fashion innovation. Your key to success in this role is your passion for supply chain planning and your ability to understand our planners' processes and their ways of working to ensure new technology can be embedded and taken up by them.

YOUR TEAM

By joining our AI Fashion Planning team, you will be at the forefront of developing state-of-the-art machine learning solutions for some of BESTSELLER’s most strategically important projects within demand planning and inventory optimisation.

YOUR ROLE

As the Business Analyst in our AI Fashion Planning team, you will be fostering close collaboration between data scientists, the product owner, and stakeholders to prioritize and develop the most impactful solutions. You will focus on ensuring that machine learning products developed by the team are fully embedded in user workflows, driving adoption and value realization. Furthermore, your tasks will include:


Designing next-level planning processes and solutions in close collaboration with data scientists and planners
Working closely with our de-centralized planning organisation in the brands to drive change and adoption of our solutions
Driving data analysis and help derive key metrics for product improvement
Supporting the team and stakeholders with demand planning and inventory management expertise and knowledge of best practices
Creating visualizations, dashboards, and presentations to support data-driven decisions.


Your Benefits


You will be working in one of the world’s largest fast fashion companies, and our AI Fashion Planning team is at the forefront of innovation.
We believe in empowering our team members to explore new ideas, solve complex challenges, and make a real impact.
You will be part of a vibrant, supportive environment with passionate colleagues and plenty of opportunities to grow your career.


YOUR PROFILE

This role is perfect for someone with real-life experience as a planner or supply chain specialist with a passion for data science and change management. In addition, we hope that you have one or more of the following:


Experience in driving product adoption within user communities
A good understanding of data analysis, interpreting and presenting insights
Strong communication and people skills, bridging the gap between technical teams and business stakeholders.


READY TO MAKE A DIFFERENCE?

If you’re excited by the opportunity to shape the future of fashion through machine learning solutions, we’d love to hear from you! Apply today or no later than July 14 2026, and join us on our journey to transform the industry.

If you have questions about the position, you are welcome to reach out to Mathias Fisker on +45 28 30 36 25. We review applications on an ongoing basis and reserve the right to take down the advert when we find the right candidate.

WORKPLACE: This role is based out of our Aarhus office.

About Bestseller Tech

We are more than 400 people strong, located in Denmark, Spain and Rwanda. We are excited about what we do, but there’s more to us than our tech stack. We are young at heart and thrive in a flat organization. Our teams are agile and empowered with end-to-end responsibility. We want to be at the forefront of the newest technologies, and we move forward together. Every day. If you want to play a significant part in empowering our business, then welcome to BESTSELLER TECH. BESTSELLER TECH is part of the worldwide fashion company BESTSELLER. For more information, please go to www.bestseller.com.

Title: Revenue Operations Analyst
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Revenue Operations Analyst

United Fintech is building a global platform that connects financial institutions with modern, scalable technology solutions. Through acquisitions and partnerships, we bring together market-leading fintech products that serve banks, exchange groups, brokerages, and investment firms worldwide.
 
If you enjoy working with data and have a keen interest in commercial operations within a fast-paced fintech environment, then you might be what we are looking for to join our team in Copenhagen.
 
What You Will Contribute
 
We are looking for a Revenue Operations Analyst to join our Revenue Operations team at United Fintech. 
 
United Fintech is scaling fast, and our commercial data needs to scale with it. We are looking for someone who does not just report what happened but helps the business understand why and what to do next.
 
You will be responsible for defining the analytical roadmap for our commercial departments, ensuring that data is not just reported but used as a competitive advantage to drive growth and efficiency. This means working across our commercial data infrastructure, spanning CRM, BI tooling, and the integrations that connect them, with a clear view of how systems speak to each other and how data flows across the customer journey. You will also be expected to continuously improve how the team operates, using AI tools and automation to make analytical workflows faster and more scalable.
 
This is a hands-on, highly operational role with real ownership and significant growth potential for someone looking to build expertise in revenue analytics, systems integration, and commercial data architecture within a fast-growing fintech environment.
 
Key responsibilities include:

Analytics and Insight - Translate commercial data into clear, decision-ready insight for leadership and commercial teams. Cover pipeline, churn, revenue trends, and ad hoc requests as they come.
Analytical Roadmap - Define the analytics agenda for our commercial departments. Identify where data is missing or misread and work proactively to close those gaps.
Systems and Data Integration - Own the data flow between CRM, BI tools, and the rest of the commercial stack. Fix problems at the source, not around them. This is the layer the team has been missing.
Workflow Optimisation – Spot manual and repetitive processes across the commercial data stack and build better alternatives. Use AI tools actively to automate analytical work, improve reporting turnaround, and free up capacity for higher-value tasks.
Data Architecture and Visualisation - Design and maintain the reporting structures that support commercial decision-making. Build scalable dashboards and contribute to our Tableau Next implementation.
Data Quality and Forecasting - Monitor CRM and other commercial data quality, identify issues, and support forecasting with structured analysis and materials for leadership review cycles.
Collaboration - Work with the RevOps team and commercial stakeholders to ensure data and insight serve real decisions. Contribute beyond your primary focus area as part of a small, high-ownership team.
 
 You will bring: 

2 to 4 years of experience in RevOps, Sales Ops, analytics, BI, or a technically oriented commercial role. A technical academic background is equally welcome.
Strong commercial understanding. Pipeline coverage, conversion rates, churn, ARR, and NRR are part of how you think.
Hands-on experience with BI tooling such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar.
Proven experience using AI tools such as Claude, ChatGPT or Copilot to optimise workflows and accelerate output, including data analysis, documentation, and process automation. Familiarity with workflow automation tools is a plus.
CRM experience is a requirement. You are comfortable working with CRM objects, fields, and data structures on the reporting and data side. Salesforce specifically is a strong bonus.
A systems thinker who understands how data moves between platforms and how to fix it upstream.
Structured, independent, and direct. You own your work and communicate findings clearly to non-technical audiences.
 
Location & Working Arrangements
This role is based in Copenhagen. The position is full-time and will be reporting to the Senior Revenue Operations Manager. 
You will join a three-person RevOps team and play a key role in building the analytics capability that supports commercial decision-making across United Fintech.
 
What We offer 

Key Role in Growth – Be instrumental in preparing us for the next stage of growth as we expand and integrate new businesses.
Collaborative Environment – Work alongside entrepreneurial minds and experts in the fintech sector, where teamwork and innovation are valued.
Opportunities for Development – Access personal and professional growth opportunities within a fast-paced, dynamic organisation.
Work-Life Balance – Flexibility to balance professional achievements with personal ambitions while contributing to a supportive and inclusive culture.
Engaging Culture – Participate in team-building activities, global events, and opportunities to connect with colleagues.
Lunch at our office in central Copenhagen K
 
Application Process
Please submit your CV by 20 of July. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, so early submissions are encouraged. The first round of interviews is expected to take place end of July.
 
We encourage candidates of all backgrounds to apply and are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and supportive working environment. 
 
About United Fintech
Founded in 2020 and backed by the industry, United Fintech is a Digital Transformation Platform where global Financial Institutions and cutting-edge technology providers come together to unleash their full potential and enable the future of finance. United Fintech remains on the frontier of innovation by acquiring engineering-led fintechs within Capital Markets, Wholesale Banking, and Wealth Management under a central umbrella. In just over four years, the company has acquired six fintechs. United Fintech has received strategic investments from Barclays, BNP Paribas, Citi, Danske Bank and Standard Chartered and Danish Growth Capital.

Title: BI Business Analyst I
Place: Birkerød, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join us to Make an Impact

Innovation is in our DNA. We invented supercontinuum lasers and photonic crystal fibers and we constantly reimagine how our products can be used and improved. Our customers use our products in medical and life science research, quantum computing and space exploration.

We seek curious, creative and collaborative people to work in our cutting-edge company. Can you see yourself working in teams and providing solutions for innovators?

If you wish to apply, please contact us with your CV / Résumé. If you have a cover letter, please save this in one document with your CV / Résumé as you are only able to upload one document.

We look forward to hearing from you!

About The Job

Excited to make a real impact while diving into the finance world? Are you passionate about turning data into actionable insights? As a BI Business Analyst, you will play a key role in empowering colleagues with business intelligence and driving data-driven decision-making across the organization.

This is more than just a job - it is an opportunity to develop valuable skills within BI and finance, collaborate across teams, and contribute to impactful projects within life sciences, renewable energy, and fundamental research.

We are involved in projects that will transform the way we live. With more than fifteen years of expertise and innovation, NKT Photonics a Hamamatsu Company strives to remain the market leader in everything we do.

Responsibilities

As a BI Business Analyst, you will be transforming numbers into insights by working intensively within Power BI and Snowflake. You will play a key role in transforming the organization into a data-driven company creating value through insights.


Act as the go-to person for BI support of new insights and issue resolution
Transform raw data into meaningful insights using Power BI and Snowflake
Gather and prioritize requirements from finance and stakeholders
Develop and maintain dashboards with intuitive visualizations
Improve and modernize Power BI solutions
Automate manual finance processes
Ensure data models support business needs
Collaborate closely with IT and business teams
Manage tickets and resolve data issues
Stay up to date with Power BI and data storytelling best practices


In you, we hope to find


Strong experience with Power BI and Snowflake
Strong ability to define and implement KPIs that measure and drive performance across key business drivers
Experience with D365 is an advantage
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Ability to translate data into insights
Experience with financial datasets
Strong communication skills in English


You are a proactive and hands-on team player who thrives in a collaborative environment, balancing business understanding with strong technical execution.

Our culture is positive, pragmatic, and focused on delivering both quick wins and long-term value. We collaborate closely across the BI team, Finance, and the wider organization to translate data and insights into tangible business impact. Within the BI team, we cover both frontend development in Power BI and backend development in dbt/Snowflake, working in close partnership with our colleagues in IT to ensure structured, reliable data from source systems.

Because collaboration is central to how we work, we primarily work from the office.

Keep your personal data safe

Hamamatsu ("The Company") take responsibly for the protection of your personal data. Accordingly, upon receiving your job application and additional material you choose to submit, the Company keeps a safe and secure environment and ensures confidentiality.

You also have a role to play in keeping your personal data safe. We request you only submit personal data about yourself in your job application, CV / Résumé or other relevant material you choose to provide to us that is relevant for the specific job opening you are applying for.

Please consider whether there is personal information you are about to submit, such as health information, information on family relations, information on trade union membership, etc. that is not necessary for our recruitment process or the job opening you are applying for. Please also be aware that certificates or other documents are not necessary for the vacancy. We urgently request you to redact/cross out such information before you submit your job application.

Thank you.

Title: BI Consultant
Description:
Group BI & Analytics

Are you curious about how data can create real business value? Do you enjoy working with people, understanding business challenges, and turning insights into solutions?

At BORG Automotive, we are looking for a BI Consultant to join our Group BI & Analytics team. Whether you are early in your career or already have some experience within BI, analytics, or reporting, this role offers the opportunity to building broad expertise within BI & Analytics and developing deeper technical and non-technical competencies.

The Role

As a BI Consultant, you will work with BI and analytics solutions, from understanding business needs and gathering requirements to supporting development, testing, and implementation. You will act as the bridge between business stakeholders and the BI & Analytics team, helping translate opportunities into scalable solutions that support better decision-making across BORG Automotive.

This combination of requirement gathering, solution development, analytics, and domain specialization gives you the opportunity to specialize within specific domains such as supply chain, production, commercial, or finance, while deepening both your technical and non-technical competencies within BI & Analytics.

Key Responsibilities


Clarify business requirements and translate them into valuable BI and analytics solution designs in collaboration with technical specialists.
Support development, testing, and rollout of dashboards, reports, and analytics solutions.
Train and support key users, ensuring relevant knowledge, guidance, and documentation are available to end users.
Take part in the team’s support responsibility through weekly rotating shifts, helping ensure stable and reliable BI services.
Promote data-driven decision-making by identifying opportunities where BI and analytics can improve processes, transparency, and business performance.
Contribute to governance, standardization, and continuous improvement of BI solutions.


Who You Are

You are naturally curious and enjoy understanding how businesses operate. You are comfortable engaging with stakeholders and asking the right questions before jumping to solutions.

You Bring The Following Qualifications And Strengths


Have a relevant degree within Business, Economics, Engineering, IT, Data Analytics, or similar.
Enjoy combining business understanding with analytical thinking
Have strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
Are structured and proactive in your approach.
Are motivated by learning and continuous development.
A problem solver who is persistent in turning needs into practical solutions
Help simplify data structures and processes through curiosity and active engagement with business stakeholders.


Experience with SQL, BI tools, reporting, analytics, or data modelling is an advantage, but the most important factor is a genuine interest in helping the business create value through data.

What We Offer


A role with significant interaction across functions, countries, and business areas.
The opportunity to build deep expertise within a selected business domain.
A strong learning environment focused on capability building and knowledge sharing.
Exposure to modern BI and analytics technologies and business-critical initiatives.
Clear opportunities to develop your skills, grow your expertise, and shape your future career path within BI & Analytics.


About Group BI & Analytics

Group BI & Analytics is a team of 8 employees responsible for enabling data-driven decision-making across BORG Automotive. We work closely with the business to transform data into trusted insights, scalable data products, and improved decision-making capabilities. Our mission is to help the organization turn data into business value through strong collaboration, governance, and analytics expertise.

We will process the applications ongoing, but due to summer holidays we will take the first look at candidates medio August.

  • Are structured and proactive in your approach.
  • Are motivated by learning and continuous development.
  • A problem solver who is persistent in turning needs into practical solutions
  • Help simplify data structures and processes through curiosity and active engagement with business stakeholders.

Experience with SQL, BI tools, reporting, analytics, or data modelling is an advantage, but the most important factor is a genuine interest in helping the business create value through data.

What We Offer

  • A role with significant interaction across functions, countries, and business areas.
  • The opportunity to build deep expertise within a selected business domain.
  • A strong learning environment focused on capability building and knowledge sharing.
  • Exposure to modern BI and analytics technologies and business-critical initiatives.
  • Clear opportunities to develop your skills, grow your expertise, and shape your future career path within BI & Analytics.

About Group BI & Analytics

Group BI & Analytics is a team of 8 employees responsible for enabling data-driven decision-making across BORG Automotive. We work closely with the business to transform data into trusted insights, scalable data products, and improved decision-making capabilities. Our mission is to help the organization turn data into business value through strong collaboration, governance, and analytics expertise.

We will process the applications ongoing, but due to summer holidays we will take the first look at candidates medio August.

Title: Business Intelligence Engineer
Place: Hillerød, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Help shape the future of Data & Analytics at Ellab




Ellab is on an exciting growth journey and data is at the heart of our success. We're building a unified, company-wide Business Intelligence platform that will empower better decision-making across our global organisation.

To help us achieve this vision, we're looking for a Business Intelligence Engineer who is passionate about turning data into business value.

As part of our growing Data & Analytics team, you'll work alongside Data Warehouse Engineers, BI specialists and analysts who own the entire data lifecycle - from extracting data from operational systems to delivering actionable insights through modern reporting solutions.

You will help drive the evolution of Ellab's reporting landscape from fragmented reports and ad hoc data extracts to a centralized, scalable BI platform.




What You'll Be Doing




This is more than a traditional BI role. You'll play a key part in shaping how data is consumed and leveraged across Ellab. You will:




Partner with stakeholders across the business to understand data and reporting requirements.
Gather and translate business requirements into scalable BI and data platform solutions.
Develop, maintain and optimize semantic models within our Power BI platform, enabling analysts and business users to generate meaningful insights.
Act as a trusted advisor to business users, helping them unlock the full potential of data and analytics.
Collaborate closely with colleagues across functions and geographies to establish best practices and drive adoption of modern BI solutions.




Why This Role Is Exciting




You'll have a direct impact on Ellab's global data strategy.
You'll help build and shape a modern BI platform from the ground up.
You'll work with stakeholders across multiple business areas, including manufacturing, consulting, operations and corporate functions.
You'll join a company experiencing significant growth, where data-driven decision-making is becoming increasingly important.
You'll have the opportunity to influence both technology choices and ways of working.




What You'll Bring




You combine strong technical skills with a genuine interest in understanding business challenges.

You ideally have:




3 - 6 years of experience working with Power BI and SQL.
Experience designing semantic models and translating business requirements into analytical solutions.
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.
A passion for helping users become more data-driven and self-sufficient.




It would be an advantage if you also have:




Experience with Microsoft Fabric and the wider Microsoft data ecosystem.
Experience working in complex, enterprise-scale environments.
Exposure to multinational organizations and diverse business domains.
Knowledge of finance, supply chain, procurement, resource planning or related business functions.




The hiring process




If you are ready to take on this exciting opportunity, apply now to join our team!

We aim to hire the best fit as soon as possible - interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis until a mutually suitable match has been identified.
