Title: Business Developer
Description:
Commercial decisions are only as good as the insights behind them. At SELECTED, we're building a new Business Development team to help our markets make even better commercial decisions.

As one of the first members of the team, you'll work at the intersection of business and data. Together with commercial stakeholders across Europe, you'll turn insights into action and help shape how commercial decisions are made.

YOUR TEAM

You will join a newly established Business Development team at SELECTED. Together, we work to strengthen commercial decision-making by connecting business understanding, data and technology. Joining us from at an early stage, you'll have the opportunity to influence how we work, build strong relationships across the organisation and make an impact from day one.

Working closely with Country Sales Managers, Sales Representatives, Merchandise Managers and Partner Performance Managers across our European markets, you'll help shape how data creates value in the business. You'll also collaborate with colleagues in our buying organisation as well as BESTSELLER Tech to develop commercial data products that support our decision-making across the value chain.

YOUR ROLE

You'll help strengthen commercial decision-making across SELECTED by combining business understanding with data. Working closely with colleagues across our sales organisation and markets, you'll identify opportunities, develop tools and analyses and help colleagues make better and faster decisions across our markets.

More specifically, you will:


Analyse commercial performance across partners and markets to identify opportunities and recommend where actions will create the greatest value
Develop tools, analyses and AI-supported solutions that help our sales organisation make better and faster commercial decisions
Drive the adoption of data-driven ways of working through stakeholder engagement, collaboration and training
Support our buying organisation by bringing commercial insights into product-related decisions and processes
Collaborate with BESTSELLER Tech to shape and continuously improve commercial data products that create value across the business.


Your Benefits

This isn't a role where insights disappear into a report. You'll work closely with the people making commercial decisions, giving you the opportunity to challenge, advise and see the impact of your work first-hand. You'll also:


Help build a new function with room to influence priorities and ways of working
Work closely with commercial stakeholders across Europe, our buying organisation and BESTSELLER Tech
Benefit from flexible working hours and the opportunity to work from home, which is a natural part of how we work at SELECTED.


YOUR PROFILE

You're curious about what drives commercial performance and motivated by making a real impact through data. You enjoy navigating between business and analytics, and you're at your best when collaborating with others to solve complex commercial challenges.

In addition, we imagine that you:


Hold a relevant Master's degree, for example within Economics and Business Administration (cand.merc.), Business Intelligence or Data Science, or a similar field
Have experience working with commercial data and business insights, ideally within fashion, wholesale, retail or a comparable industry
Understand how commercial performance, partner dynamics and product decisions influence business results
Are comfortable working with analytical tools such as SQL, Python or similar, and enjoy using modern technologies, including AI, to develop analyses and practical solutions
Communicate complex insights in a simple and actionable way to stakeholders with different backgrounds
Communicate confidently in English, both written and spoken.


WE LOOK FORWARD TO HEARING FROM YOU

If you have questions about the position, you are welcome to contact Business Development Manager Alex Brink Poulsen. Otherwise, send us your application and CV as soon as possible and no later than 4 August 2026. We review applications on an ongoing basis and reserve the right to close the posting when we find the right candidate.

LOCATION: Brande, Denmark

About Selected

Based in Denmark, SELECTED is an international brand for men and women, providing modern consumers a unique offering of style and craftmanship at an attractive price point. We always strive to add an edgy touch to our progressive styles and aim to offer collections that can be the base to any wardrobe, always in sync with the current tendencies. We aim for quality over quantity, which is why sustainability is a main focus for us. Every collection should be more sustainable than the last. This underlines the responsibility we take for our future environmental footprint. With SELECTED you are never out of style but have a quality that is built to last. SELECTED is part of the worldwide fashion company BESTSELLER. For more information, please go to www.selected.com  or www.bestseller.com.

Title: Data and Invoicing Specialist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Data and Invoicing Specialist til UBsend A/S i København




Er du en nysgerrig og struktureret Data and Invoicing Specialist med flair for IT og en interesse for at skabe værdi gennem data og analyser? Hos UBsend A/S søger vi en engageret Data and Invoicing Specialist til at styrke vores team i København.




Om UBsend A/S




UBsend er specialiseret i transportløsninger og hjælper både store og små kunder med at finde de bedste løsninger til deres specifikke forsendelsesbehov. Vi arbejder med en bred vifte af B2B- og B2C-kunder og er i løbende vækst. Vores ambition er klar: Vi vil være den førende integrationsplatform på markedet.




Vi er en international virksomhed med ca. 80 medarbejdere fordelt på kontorer i Aarhus og København og er den del af Heartland/Bestseller-koncernen. Hos os bliver du en del af en dynamisk og ambitiøs virksomhed med et stærkt fokus på innovation og udvikling.




Dine arbejdsopgaver




Som Data and Invoicing Specialist hos UBsend vil du få en alsidig rolle med fokus på datahåndtering, kontrol samt fakturering, der understøtter vores forretning. Dine primære opgaver vil være:




Data processering
Fakturering
Sikring af datakvalitet gennem afstemning og kontrol
Opfølgning på data med kunder og transportører.
Udarbejdelse af ad hoc-analyser og rapporter.
Bidrag til udvikling og optimering af automatiserede løsninger.




Din profil




Vi leder efter en kandidat, der kan genkende sig selv i følgende:




Du er nysgerrig og har en struktureret tilgang til dit arbejde.
Du har flair for IT og gerne kendskab til data håndtering.
Du er analytisk stærk, detaljeorienteret og god til at arbejde med data.
Du har gode engelskkundskaber både mundtligt og skriftligt.
Du trives i en dynamisk og international virksomhed, hvor tingene udvikler sig hurtigt.







Vi tilbyder




En fuldtidsstilling i København.
En spændende rolle i en virksomhed i vækst, hvor du får mulighed for at præge din egen hverdag.
Et uformelt og internationalt arbejdsmiljø med engagerede kolleger.
Indgåelse i et team som har en faglighed stolthed og sætter stor ære i deres arbejde.
Fokus på faglig og personlig udvikling.




Ansøgning




Hvis dette lyder som jobbet for dig, vil vi meget gerne høre fra dig! Ansøg via LinkedIn hurtigst muligt eller senest den 14. august 2026. Vi behandler ansøgninger løbende, så tøv ikke med at sende din ansøgning.




Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Søren Fynbo, Finance Director på 52 15 40 10.




Bliv en del af UBsend, og vær med til at gøre en forskel i transportbranchen!




MISSION




Vores mission er på en nem, attraktiv og let tilgængelig måde og på tværs af alle leverandører at levere innovative, fleksible og sikre løsninger indenfor transport og betaling.




Vi er drevet af i fælleskab med dig at fokusere på dine kunder så vi sammen genererer den optimale værdi for alle parter. Vi mener det skal være profitabelt at drive sin forretning og vi arbejder derfor altid målrettet på kundetilfredsheden så UBsend er det fortrukne valg ved køb af transport og forsikrings- og betalingsløsning.




Vi fokuserer på udviklingen af vores platform for hele tiden at være på forkant med teknologien og nye trends i markedet. Kulturen i UBsend er baseret på en stærk dedikation for vores kunder og vi arbejder målrettet på at tiltrække og fastholde de rigtige kolleger i virksomheden.

Title: Er du VisitDenmarks nye talstærke analytiker til at hjælpe med at styrke dansk turisme?
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vil du være med til at skabe ny viden, der styrker beslutningsgrundlaget i en branche, hvor intelligent brug af data er afgørende? 





I VisitDenmark arbejder vi hver dag for at gøre Danmark til et foretrukket rejsemål for gæster fra hele verden. Og når vi lykkes, er vi med til at skabe omsætning, vækst og arbejdspladser i Danmark.





Som Danmarks nationale turismeorganisation, tror vi på styrken i et godt samarbejde. Både med offentlige og private aktører brander og markedsfører vi Danmark internationalt. På den måde skaber vi de bedste rammer for, at flere får lyst til at besøge Danmark.





I Insights vil du møde 5 dygtige kolleger som skaber og formidler ny viden om turisternes forbrug, rejseønsker, motiver og adfærd på tværs af de vigtigste markeder for dansk turisme. 





Vi arbejder med modelberegninger af turismens økonomiske og klimamæssige effekter, udforsker nye datakilder (fx booking- og transaktionsdata) og indhenter indsigter gennem vores surveys i Danmark og på vores prioriterede markeder.





Vi leder efter en ny metodestærk og nysgerrig kollega, som kan indgå i teamet.





 





Opgaver og ansvar i jobbet:

Som vores nye analytiker bliver du en drivende kraft i at indhente og afsøge ny viden og nye indsigter om dansk turisme. Du bliver ansvarlig for både større projekter og mindre ad hoc-opgaver med afsæt i kvantitative data og analyser. Du får ansvar for både databearbejdning, analyse og afrapportering af de opnåede indsigter, som bruges af politikere, destinationsselskaber, kolleger og resten af den danske turismebranche.





Du bidrager desuden til at identificere og udvikle automatiserede løsninger, som effektiviserer vores arbejdsprocesser og frigør tid til analyse og udvikling.  





Du vil fra tid til anden få mulighed for at deltage i internationale møder og statistikgrupper. 





Dine primære ansvarsområder og opgaver:






Indhente, behandle og analysere kvantitative data fra både eksterne kilder og vores egne spørgeskemaundersøgelser, herunder udvikling af effektive og automatiserede processer.







Udarbejde og formidle resultaterne i fx dashboards, rapporter, notater og oplæg til kolleger og eksterne.







Support, sparring og rådgivning af kolleger og eksterne aktører i dialog om, hvordan analyseresultater og turismedata skal forstås og anvendes.






 





Dine kvalifikationer:

Udover, at du trives med ansvar og motiveres af at nå i mål, forestiller vi os, at du er har en samfundsvidenskabelig uddannelse som cand.polit., cand.oecon., cand.merc., cand.scient.oecon., cand.scient.pol. eller anden relevant uddannelse med fokus på data og kvantitativ analyse. 





Det er nødvendigt, at du er metodestærk og erfaren med høj faglig troværdighed og en solid værktøjskasse inden for kvantitativ analyse. Du har lyst til at tage ansvar for flere nøgleleverancer. Du har appetit på at udforske nye metoder, datakilder og problemstillinger. Du har erfaring med statistisk software og programmering i Python eller lignende, og du har enten kendskab til dashboardudvikling i Power BI eller lyst til at lære det. 





Du skal kunne arbejde selvstændigt og struktureret, også med rutineopgaver – men der vil være god afveksling i dine arbejdsopgaver. Vi forventer, at du er initiativrig, har gode samarbejdsevner, kommunikerer klart og professionelt og sætter en ære i at skabe de bedst mulige resultater og konstant at forbedre udgangspunktet.





Og vi ser gerne, at du kan tjekke flere af disse af:






Du har udtalt talforståelse og er detaljeorienteret


Du balancerer ordentlighed og grundighed med modet til at skabe nyt. Og du ser styrken i at forene viden og ressourcer med henblik på at samle fremfor at dele.


Du er nysgerrig af natur og ved, at man må lytte for at forstå.


Du tænker løsningsorienteret, tager initiativ og er god til at håndtere flere aktiviteter sideløbende.


Du ser stor værdi i et stærkt kollegaskab og kan både give og tage imod hjælp fra andre, når det er nødvendigt.


Du sætter en ære i at overholde dine deadlines






 





Vi tilbyder:

Vi mener sagtens, det er muligt at være professionelle og samtidig uhøjtidelige. Hos os prioriterer vi at være en moderne, fleksibel og inkluderende arbejdsplads med høj arbejdsglæde. Vi tilbyder stor selvstændighed og mulighed for indflydelse på hverdagen og for at udvikle dine kompetencer.





Og kollegaskabet er helt i top. Samlet set får du cirka 100 nye, gode kolleger fordelt på hovedkontoret i København og vores markedskontorer i udlandet. Vi er særligt stolte af, at vi gennem vores arbejde er med til at skabe udvikling og arbejdspladser i hele Danmark hele året.





Hovedarbejdsstedet er på VisitDenmarks hovedkontor, Islands Brygge 43. Løn forhandles individuelt efter kvalifikationer.





Vi kunne skrive meget mere om alle de gode ting ved at arbejde hos os, som fx en god pensionsordning, solid onboarding, intern mentorordning, en stærk personaleforening, god fleksibilitetspolitik mv. Men lad os høre, hvad der er vigtigt for dig, når vi kommer så langt.





 





Ansøgning:

Vi har brug for dig allerede den 1. oktober, så send ansøgning og CV senest den 17. juli via ”søg stillingen”. Af hensyn til en ensartet, fair og effektiv proces for alle, vil kun ansøgninger indsendt via vores E-rekrutteringssystem blive taget i betragtning. Samtaler forventes holdt i uge 31. og 32. . Send gerne din ansøgning hurtigt, da vi holder samtaler løbende.





Undervejs i processen kan du blive præsenteret for en personprofilanalyse.





Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at ringe til teamansvarlig for Insights, Charlotte Rømmer Rassing på telefon 20 60 38 55.



Vi opfordrer alle kvalificerede kandidater uanset køn, alder, etnicitet, handicap, religion eller seksuel orientering til at søge stillingen.

HTML:
Vil du være med til at skabe ny viden, der styrker beslutningsgrundlaget i en branche, hvor intelligent brug af data er afgørende? 



I VisitDenmark arbejder vi hver dag for at gøre Danmark til et foretrukket rejsemål for gæster fra hele verden. Og når vi lykkes, er vi med til at skabe omsætning, vækst og arbejdspladser i Danmark.



Som Danmarks nationale turismeorganisation, tror vi på styrken i et godt samarbejde. Både med offentlige og private aktører brander og markedsfører vi Danmark internationalt. På den måde skaber vi de bedste rammer for, at flere får lyst til at besøge Danmark.



I Insights vil du møde 5 dygtige kolleger som skaber og formidler ny viden om turisternes forbrug, rejseønsker, motiver og adfærd på tværs af de vigtigste markeder for dansk turisme. 



Vi arbejder med modelberegninger af turismens økonomiske og klimamæssige effekter, udforsker nye datakilder (fx booking- og transaktionsdata) og indhenter indsigter gennem vores surveys i Danmark og på vores prioriterede markeder.



Vi leder efter en ny metodestærk og nysgerrig kollega, som kan indgå i teamet.



 



Opgaver og ansvar i jobbet:

Som vores nye analytiker bliver du en drivende kraft i at indhente og afsøge ny viden og nye indsigter om dansk turisme. Du bliver ansvarlig for både større projekter og mindre ad hoc-opgaver med afsæt i kvantitative data og analyser. Du får ansvar for både databearbejdning, analyse og afrapportering af de opnåede indsigter, som bruges af politikere, destinationsselskaber, kolleger og resten af den danske turismebranche.



Du bidrager desuden til at identificere og udvikle automatiserede løsninger, som effektiviserer vores arbejdsprocesser og frigør tid til analyse og udvikling.  



Du vil fra tid til anden få mulighed for at deltage i internationale møder og statistikgrupper. 



Dine primære ansvarsområder og opgaver:




  • Indhente, behandle og analysere kvantitative data fra både eksterne kilder og vores egne spørgeskemaundersøgelser, herunder udvikling af effektive og automatiserede processer.




  • Udarbejde og formidle resultaterne i fx dashboards, rapporter, notater og oplæg til kolleger og eksterne.




  • Support, sparring og rådgivning af kolleger og eksterne aktører i dialog om, hvordan analyseresultater og turismedata skal forstås og anvendes.



 



Dine kvalifikationer:

Udover, at du trives med ansvar og motiveres af at nå i mål, forestiller vi os, at du er har en samfundsvidenskabelig uddannelse som cand.polit., cand.oecon., cand.merc., cand.scient.oecon., cand.scient.pol. eller anden relevant uddannelse med fokus på data og kvantitativ analyse. 



Det er nødvendigt, at du er metodestærk og erfaren med høj faglig troværdighed og en solid værktøjskasse inden for kvantitativ analyse. Du har lyst til at tage ansvar for flere nøgleleverancer. Du har appetit på at udforske nye metoder, datakilder og problemstillinger. Du har erfaring med statistisk software og programmering i Python eller lignende, og du har enten kendskab til dashboardudvikling i Power BI eller lyst til at lære det. 



Du skal kunne arbejde selvstændigt og struktureret, også med rutineopgaver – men der vil være god afveksling i dine arbejdsopgaver. Vi forventer, at du er initiativrig, har gode samarbejdsevner, kommunikerer klart og professionelt og sætter en ære i at skabe de bedst mulige resultater og konstant at forbedre udgangspunktet.



Og vi ser gerne, at du kan tjekke flere af disse af:




  • Du har udtalt talforståelse og er detaljeorienteret

  • Du balancerer ordentlighed og grundighed med modet til at skabe nyt. Og du ser styrken i at forene viden og ressourcer med henblik på at samle fremfor at dele.

  • Du er nysgerrig af natur og ved, at man må lytte for at forstå.

  • Du tænker løsningsorienteret, tager initiativ og er god til at håndtere flere aktiviteter sideløbende.

  • Du ser stor værdi i et stærkt kollegaskab og kan både give og tage imod hjælp fra andre, når det er nødvendigt.

  • Du sætter en ære i at overholde dine deadlines



 



Vi tilbyder:

Vi mener sagtens, det er muligt at være professionelle og samtidig uhøjtidelige. Hos os prioriterer vi at være en moderne, fleksibel og inkluderende arbejdsplads med høj arbejdsglæde. Vi tilbyder stor selvstændighed og mulighed for indflydelse på hverdagen og for at udvikle dine kompetencer.



Og kollegaskabet er helt i top. Samlet set får du cirka 100 nye, gode kolleger fordelt på hovedkontoret i København og vores markedskontorer i udlandet. Vi er særligt stolte af, at vi gennem vores arbejde er med til at skabe udvikling og arbejdspladser i hele Danmark hele året.



Hovedarbejdsstedet er på VisitDenmarks hovedkontor, Islands Brygge 43. Løn forhandles individuelt efter kvalifikationer.



Vi kunne skrive meget mere om alle de gode ting ved at arbejde hos os, som fx en god pensionsordning, solid onboarding, intern mentorordning, en stærk personaleforening, god fleksibilitetspolitik mv. Men lad os høre, hvad der er vigtigt for dig, når vi kommer så langt.



 



Ansøgning:

Vi har brug for dig allerede den 1. oktober, så send ansøgning og CV senest den 17. juli via ”søg stillingen”. Af hensyn til en ensartet, fair og effektiv proces for alle, vil kun ansøgninger indsendt via vores E-rekrutteringssystem blive taget i betragtning. Samtaler forventes holdt i uge 31. og 32. . Send gerne din ansøgning hurtigt, da vi holder samtaler løbende.



Undervejs i processen kan du blive præsenteret for en personprofilanalyse.



Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at ringe til teamansvarlig for Insights, Charlotte Rømmer Rassing på telefon 20 60 38 55.



Vi opfordrer alle kvalificerede kandidater uanset køn, alder, etnicitet, handicap, religion eller seksuel orientering til at søge stillingen.

Title: Data Scientist
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Too Good To Go, we have an ambitious goal: to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together.

40% of all food produced in the world is wasted. And that has a huge impact on the health of our planet, with 10% of greenhouse gas emissions coming from food waste.

We’re more than an app: we are a certified B Corporation with a mission to empower everyone to take action against food waste, so alongside our marketplace app, we create educational tools, explore new business solutions - such as our Retail Technologies offering, and influence legislation to help reduce food waste.

We’re growing fast: Our community of 133 million registered users and 261,000 active partners across 21 countries, have together already prevented 517+ million meals from going to waste - avoiding over 1.397.000 tonnes of CO2e!

Your mission

We are looking for a Data Scientist to join our Product Analytics and Center of Excellence team at Too Good To Go.

In this role, you will help build and scale data science capabilities across product and business domains, enabling smarter, more automated, and more impactful decision-making.

As a marketplace, many of our most critical challenges revolve around supply-demand dynamics, user behavior, and marketplace efficiency. You will work on problems such as personalisation, optimisation, and forecasting, helping us move from reactive analysis to proactive, model-driven systems.

You will work as part of the Center of Excellence, partnering closely with Product, Analytics, and business teams through high-impact, project-based collaborations.

Your role


Partner with Product and business leaders to identify high-impact opportunities where data science can drive step-change improvements
Develop and deploy machine learning models and statistical methods to solve problems such as: Personalisation and recommendation systems, Marketplace optimisation (e.g. ranking, matching, supply-demand balance) and Demand and supply forecasting.
Translate ambiguous business problems into scalable models, algorithms, and data products
Design and contribute to experimentation and measurement frameworks to evaluate impact
Collaborate closely with Product Analysts, ML Engineers, and stakeholders within the Product Analytics and Center of Excellence, working in an embedded, project-based model
Take end-to-end ownership, from problem framing to model development and impact evaluation
Contribute to building data science best practices, tools, and standards across the company


Requirements


Master’s degree or PhD in Data Science, Statistics, Machine Learning, Econometrics, or a related field
Strong experience applying machine learning and statistical modeling to product or business problems
Experience in at least one of the following areas: Personalisation / recommendation systems, Marketplace or optimisation problems, Forecasting (time series or causal)
Strong programming skills in Python and solid proficiency in SQL
Experience with experimentation (A/B testing) and impact measurement
Ability to operate in ambiguous problem spaces and translate them into structured solutions
Strong communication skills — able to influence product and business stakeholders
High ownership and comfort working in a fast-paced, evolving environment
Fluent in English, with excellent written and verbal communication skills.


Our values


We win together: Food waste is a big beast to fight. We believe in a #oneteam.
We raise the bar: We always push for more. We work smart, smash barriers and elevate one another.
We keep it simple: Our ambitions are bold but our solutions are simple.
We build a legacy: We’re proud of the change we’re driving.
We care: We always look out for each other. Caring is also about the way we do business. We do the right thing.


What We Have To Offer


An opportunity to work in a global social-impact company and certified B Corporation! where you can see a real and tangible impact in your role.
To be an integral part of our Data & Analytics team. We are eager for you to make an impact and contribute to the role of analytics across key decisions and our solution processes. Your insights are valuable, and we are here to listen.
Work-life balance is important to us! Focus on the job to be done, not the hours spent, there is no need for overtime.
We trust in our employees and encourage an autonomous environment that provides several opportunities for employees to contribute, develop and take ownership of their work in a way that works for them.
To be part of an international company, with over 1,300+ colleagues across 19 countries that are on the same important mission.
An informal environment, working alongside 170+ passionate people within Tech & Product
We celebrate our differences and our successes and have a strong values-driven team culture.
We work in a hybrid set-up and value in office work weekly.
We may be able to support you with a working visa in Copenhagen, please be prepared to discuss this topic further as your first stage interview.


How To Apply


We take recruitment very seriously, so please carefully read everything we have written above. Please also check our website and international media in order to get a good overview of Too Good To Go.
Submit your CV in English.
Please note that we only accept applications coming through our platform. No CV will be accepted by email or LinkedIn direct messaging.


Job Ref

A Movement for Everyone

We want to inspire and empower everyone to fight food waste together. With that mission, it’s only natural that we want to build a diverse and inclusive team of highly capable individuals who are passionate about doing things in a better way. We strongly believe we all excel and are more creative when we’re allowed to be ourselves, and we’re committed to a culture where all of us belong.

We are an equal opportunity employer and all employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need. If you need reasonable accommodation at any point in the application or interview process, please let us know.

Title: Energy Analyst
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About Reel

We are rethinking electricity to accelerate the transition to a carbon-free energy system.

Electricity is one of the biggest sources of carbon emissions globally, yet most green power sold on the market today fails move the needle toward decarbonizing electricity production.

Reel empowers companies to move beyond climate commitments and achieve real-world carbon reductions, by bridging the gap between renewable energy producers and consumers.

Join our mission-driven, cross-disciplinary team of developers, builders, and climate experts working to accelerate the transition to a net-zero economy.

How You Can Contribute

We’re looking for an Energy Analyst to support Reel’s commercial, supply, and trading work with strong analytical execution. The role is intended to add dedicated capacity on detailed analytical work, backtests/simulations, modeling, and supporting material.

This is an entry-level profile for someone who is strong analytically, comfortable working across technical and business tasks, and motivated by helping build a modern energy company.

A note on timing: We're actively collecting applications and will begin interviewing in August, as most of our team is on summer holiday in July. We appreciate your patience and look forward to connecting then!

What Your Role Will Look Like


Support the team with detailed analytical work across commercial, supply, and trading topics
Build and maintain models, analysis, and decision-support material
Help structure and prepare reports, business cases, and presentation material
Support analysis related to trading strategies, simulations, and backtests
Work with team members on ad hoc analytical tasks and act as a strong execution partner on high-priority projects
Help create capacity for more senior colleagues by taking ownership of recurring analysis and preparation work
Contribute to market-related work, including areas connected to Germany and potentially other markets over time
Support cross-functional projects where analytical rigor and clear communication are needed


We think you will thrive in this role if you have/are


Academic background in something like Sustainable Energy or Renewable Energy Systems
A strong analytical profile
Comfortable with both technical and business-oriented work
Able to code, with Python being relevant for the role
Strong with Excel
Comfortable working with data, models, and structured analysis
Able to contribute to simulation and backtesting work
Strong written communication skills, including the ability to prepare reports and business cases, and strong slide-making skills/AI-tech savy
Organized, detail-oriented, and motivated to help others move faster
Interested in energy markets and excited to work in a fast-moving environment
Must-have: You are located in Copenhagen, Denmark or near the area


Working at Reel

At Reel, we care deeply about our people. We offer a workplace that values initiative, ownership and kindness, with ample room for personal and professional growth. We believe great teams are built by welcoming people with different backgrounds, perspectives, and lived experiences.

You will join a tight-knit team on an ambitious journey, where the impact of your role will be visible and recognisable on a daily basis. Every colleague - from founders to student assistants - are in this together. We cherish focus and autonomy just as much as team work and setting time aside for having fun together - at our monthly socials, bi-annual offsites and daily coffee chats in our bright and spacious office in central Copenhagen, 2 minutes from the metro.

Read more about working at Reel at our Careers page.

What We Offer You

At Reel, we offer you an organisation that cares as much for its people as for the climate. We are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace where your personal growth and well-being is imperative.


Compensation: A healthy base salary + warrant programme
Vacation: 30 days (5 weeks + 1) of paid holidays to recharge
Pension: Employer-supported pension scheme, including health insurance
Parental leave: A generous parental leave policy
Equipment: Choose your preferred IT setup (Mac, Windows, etc.) with a budget of 25,000 DKK
Office: A vibrant office-first culture in central Copenhagen with a flexible remote work policy
Lunch: A delicious lunch at the office every day — and your daily caffeine fix from our espresso machine
Socials: Monthly social events and bi-annual offsites where we have fun, unwind, and nurture our team spirit
Visa: We try to support non-EU residents by sponsoring visas whenever we can, but are unable to do so for this role, unfortunately


Interested?

We are very excited hearing from you. You can find more information about the interview process here.

You don't feel like you fulfill all the criteria?

We know that 'perfect' candidates do not exist. The only thing you need to ensure is that the problem we are solving at Reel is one you cannot wait to get out of bed for on Monday morning.

We encourage all candidates, regardless of age, gender, sexual orientation, ethnicity, religious beliefs, etc. to apply.

A note on timing: We're actively collecting applications and will get back to you in August, as most of our team is on summer holiday in July. We appreciate your patience and look forward to connecting then.

Title: Senior Analyst
Place: Hellerup, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you an experienced trade flow analyst and curious to bring your skills into play in a commodity trading business? Join our Data & Analytics team as Senior Analyst.




As Senior Analyst, you will join our cross-functional Data & Analytics team covering the full trade flow. Reporting to the Head of Data & Analytics, you will help implement a more data-driven operating model through automation and advanced analytics.

You will work closely with stakeholders across the business to deliver analytical and development initiatives that create tangible value. This is a key role supporting our commodity trading activities, with responsibility for improving trade flow control, transparency, and analytical insight across the transaction lifecycle.




Become part of an engaged team

You will join a lean and highly skilled team of six, working closely across locations. Based in Hellerup, you will collaborate regularly with colleagues in Middelfart and occasionally with IT teams in Aarhus.

You will initiate and implement improvements, playing a pivotal role in optimizing trade flow processes and guaranteeing accurate, timely, and transparent PnL reporting.




In addition, your responsibilities include:

Deliver analysis, reporting, and visualizations that support business decisions
Develop and improve analytical and automation tools across the trade flow
Translate business needs into scalable data solutions and actionable insights
Support transparency, controls, and process quality across transactions
Implement process improvements and help enhance our data & analytics capabilities




An analytical profile with solid business understanding

We are looking for an experienced analyst with technical skills, a commercial mindset, and a structured approach. We imagine that you bring knowledge of trade flows, derivatives, commodities or financial markets i.e., from a bank, international trading house, or energy company. You work independently, collaborate well across functions, and create value ensuring high-quality, accurate, and transparent PnL reporting.




We also assume that you:

Have solid experience (5-10 years) with tools such as Python, SQL, Power BI, Excel with understanding of trade flow, middle office functions, and operational controls
Bring an analytical mindset with the ability to structure, interpret, and explain complex financial and trading data
Work independently in a fast-paced, collaborative environment
Effective stakeholder management and communication skills
Familiarity with trade lifecycle systems, risk systems, or ETRM platforms




What we offer

We offer an exciting role in a global company where you will have the opportunity for both personal and professional development. You will join a collaborative environment with broad business exposure, a high degree of responsibility, and the opportunity to shape how data and analytics support the organization’s continued development.




Apply now and be part of our journey

If you have any questions regarding the position, you are welcome to contact Head of Data & Analytics, Jakob Korsgaard, on +45 40 72 73 32. We are interviewing on an ongoing basis and look forward to receiving your application.

We value a culture of diversity and, therefore, invite all qualified applicants to apply for the position regardless of age, gender, religion, sexuality, disability, country of origin, or ethnicity.




Global Risk Management is a leading provider of hedging solutions for the management of price risk on energy. Combining in-depth knowledge of the energy market, finance, and transport, clients are helped to protect their margins from the risk posed by notoriously volatile energy prices. A large part of the clients operates within the shipping industry, but also airline companies, oil suppliers and industry clients make extensive use of Global Risk Management’s expertise.

Global Risk Management is part of the Danish, family-owned United Shipping & Trading Company (USTC). USTC holds a portfolio of activities that include oil & energy, shipping & logistics, ship owning, risk management, car activities, IT and sustainable energy, environment & recycling and is present in 40 countries with more than 4,000 employees.

As Senior Analyst, you will join our cross-functional Data & Analytics team covering the full trade flow. Reporting to the Head of Data & Analytics, you will help implement a more data-driven operating model through automation and advanced analytics.

You will work closely with stakeholders across the business to deliver analytical and development initiatives that create tangible value. This is a key role supporting our commodity trading activities, with responsibility for improving trade flow control, transparency, and analytical insight across the transaction lifecycle.


Become part of an engaged team

You will join a lean and highly skilled team of six, working closely across locations. Based in Hellerup, you will collaborate regularly with colleagues in Middelfart and occasionally with IT teams in Aarhus.

You will initiate and implement improvements, playing a pivotal role in optimizing trade flow processes and guaranteeing accurate, timely, and transparent PnL reporting.


In addition, your responsibilities include:

  • Deliver analysis, reporting, and visualizations that support business decisions
  • Develop and improve analytical and automation tools across the trade flow
  • Translate business needs into scalable data solutions and actionable insights
  • Support transparency, controls, and process quality across transactions
  • Implement process improvements and help enhance our data & analytics capabilities


An analytical profile with solid business understanding

We are looking for an experienced analyst with technical skills, a commercial mindset, and a structured approach. We imagine that you bring knowledge of trade flows, derivatives, commodities or financial markets i.e., from a bank, international trading house, or energy company. You work independently, collaborate well across functions, and create value ensuring high-quality, accurate, and transparent PnL reporting.


We also assume that you:

  • Have solid experience (5-10 years) with tools such as Python, SQL, Power BI, Excel with understanding of trade flow, middle office functions, and operational controls
  • Bring an analytical mindset with the ability to structure, interpret, and explain complex financial and trading data
  • Work independently in a fast-paced, collaborative environment
  • Effective stakeholder management and communication skills
  • Familiarity with trade lifecycle systems, risk systems, or ETRM platforms


What we offer

We offer an exciting role in a global company where you will have the opportunity for both personal and professional development. You will join a collaborative environment with broad business exposure, a high degree of responsibility, and the opportunity to shape how data and analytics support the organization’s continued development.


Apply now and be part of our journey

If you have any questions regarding the position, you are welcome to contact Head of Data & Analytics, Jakob Korsgaard, on +45 40 72 73 32. We are interviewing on an ongoing basis and look forward to receiving your application.

We value a culture of diversity and, therefore, invite all qualified applicants to apply for the position regardless of age, gender, religion, sexuality, disability, country of origin, or ethnicity.


Global Risk Management is a leading provider of hedging solutions for the management of price risk on energy. Combining in-depth knowledge of the energy market, finance, and transport, clients are helped to protect their margins from the risk posed by notoriously volatile energy prices. A large part of the clients operates within the shipping industry, but also airline companies, oil suppliers and industry clients make extensive use of Global Risk Management’s expertise.

Global Risk Management is part of the Danish, family-owned United Shipping & Trading Company (USTC). USTC holds a portfolio of activities that include oil & energy, shipping & logistics, ship owning, risk management, car activities, IT and sustainable energy, environment & recycling and is present in 40 countries with more than 4,000 employees.

Title: Dataanalytiker med stærke Python-kompetencer til Team Risikoanalyser
Company: Styrelsen for Grøn Arealomlægning og Vandmiljø
Description:
Er du drevet af at omsætte komplekse datasæt til præcise risikovurderinger? Vil du være med til at sikre den grønne omstilling gennem avancerede statistiske modeller? Så er det dig, vi leder efter.

I Styrelsen for Grøn Arealomlægning og Vandmiljø står vi over for en omfattende opgave: At sikre et rent vandmiljø og gennemføre en historisk omlægning af landbrugsarealer til natur og skov. En central del af denne opgave er at sikre en effektiv og korrekt implementering af den grønne trepart, og derfor ønsker vi nu at øge vores analysekapacitet.

Vi søger derfor en analytisk stærk profil til enheden Kvalitet & Risikoanalyse, som kan bringe avancerede programmeringsteknikker og statistisk metodik i spil for at udvikle fremtidens analyse- og kontrolmodeller.

Din rolle: Fra rådata til intelligent risikoanalyse
Som vores nye dataanalytiker bliver du som noget af det første ansvarlig for at bidrage til løsningen af en specifik tværgående analyseopgave. Gennem vores onboarding-program bliver du introduceret til dine primære ansvarsområder, som vil omfatte:

Avanceret databehandling og automatisering: Du vil bruge Python til at automatisere komplekse arbejdsgange, håndtere store datamængder og transformere rådata til brugbare analyseresultater.
Statistisk modellering og analyse: Du vil udføre dybdegående analyser, der understøtter både den daglige kontrol og den overordnede politikudvikling i styrelsen.
Dokumentation og formidling: Du omsætter komplekse tekniske resultater til letforståelig dokumentation og informationsmateriale til både interne interessenter og eksterne partnere, herunder EU-kommissionen.


Din profil: Analytisk dybde og teknisk ekspertise
Vi forestiller os, at du har en relevant kandidatuddannelse inden for f.eks. statistik, data science, økonomi eller lignende kvantitative discipliner. Derudover lægger vi vægt på, at du har:

Stærke Python-kompetencer: Du er fortrolig med at bruge Python til datamanipulation, statistisk modellering og automatisering af analyseprocesser.
Metodisk fundering: Du har en dyb forståelse for statistisk analyse og erfaring med udvikling af prædiktive modeller.
Data-drevet mindset: Du er vant til at arbejde systematisk og struktureret med store, komplekse datasæt og kan navigere i både ustrukturerede og strukturerede data.
Programmeringskompetencer: Udover Python har du erfaring med SQL og herudover gerne kendskab til SAS Enterprise Guide.
Kommunikativ styrke: Du kan formidle tekniske konklusioner klart og præcist, både skriftligt og mundtligt, til kolleger med forskellige faglige baggrunde.


Det er en fordel, hvis du har erfaring med projektledelse og kendskab til forvaltning af EU-regler på miljø- og landbrugsområdet.

Vi tilbyder
Du bliver en del af Team Risikoanalyser i enheden Kvalitet & Risikoanalyse, som består af ca. 10 medarbejdere med forskellig baggrund. Vi arbejder i et miljø, hvor vidensdeling og gensidig sparring er i højsædet, og hvor du indgår i et tæt samarbejde med andre enheder i styrelsen. Som en del af Team Risikoanalyser vil du erfare, at du er med i opgaveløsningen fra start til slut, og det betyder, at du bidrager til hele processen og ikke kun en isoleret delmængde.

Vi tilbyder en fast stilling med stor faglig indflydelse, hvor dine analyser direkte bidrager til den grønne omstilling af Danmark.

Ansættelsesvilkår
Du vil blive ansat som fuldmægtig efter gældende overenskomst for akademikere i staten.

Ansættelsesområdet er Ministeriet for Natur og Dyrevelfærd med tilhørende institutioner, og tjenestestedet er Styrelsen for Grøn Arealomlægning og Vandmiljø, i Nyropsgade 30. 1780 København V.

Stillingen ønskes besat snarest muligt.

Kontakt
Vil du vide mere om stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte kontorchef Henrik Berg på telefon 25 16 82 14.

Du kan læse mere om styrelsen på www.sgav.dk

Ansøgning
Søg stillingen ved at sende en ansøgning inkl. CV, eksamensbevis og evt. referencer online i vores rekrutteringssystem (tryk på ”Søg stilling”) senest mandag den 27. juli 2026.

Vi forventer at afholde ansættelsessamtaler i uge 32.

Skills
Risk Management
Data Analytics
Python (Programming Language)
Statistical Data Analysis
Programming
SQL
Administration
EU Law
Communication
Project Management

Title: Systems Developer, Business Intelligence, Lystrup
Description:
Would you like to play a key role in turning data into decisions that matter — building the dashboards and visual reporting layers that drive performance across a global defense and aerospace company? We are looking for a skilled and business-oriented BI developer to drive front-end BI delivery, serving stakeholders across the full value chain. In this hands-on position your output directly shapes how leaders and teams across Terma understand and act on business performance.




We are facing an exciting future with considerable growth and demanding changes, as we deliver advanced technologies and mission critical solutions for the defense and aerospace industry. Our systems provide security for people on land, at sea and in the air. Therefore, we are looking for a System Developer within Business Intelligence to become part of this journey and contribute to our overall purpose – securing people through advanced technology.




Your role in our team




As our new Systems Developer, you will design, build, and maintain Power BI solutions that translate complex business, operational, and financial data into clear, actionable insights for stakeholders across our functions and business areas.




You will be the bridge between the business and the data, collaborating closely with stakeholders across the value chain – including sales, business development, project delivery, R&D, executive leadership and finance to evolve KPI frameworks, performance metrics, and business narratives into visual formats. Understanding what decisions need to be made and ensuring the right information is available at the right time. Your solutions will include everything from project performance reviews and pipeline reporting to financial performance management and executive dashboards.




You will join Financial Systems & BI, part of our Performance & Financial Insights function — a team dedicated to bringing structure, insight, and analytical depth to performance management across Terma. You and the BI team will collaborate closely with our IT data infrastructure team on the underlying data models and structures, ensuring the data feeding your reports is fit for purpose.




How you will make a meaningful contribution




Your key responsibilities include:




Designing and developing Power BI dashboards and reports that meet the analytical and reporting needs of stakeholders across sales, supply chain, R&D, and finance
Translating business requirements into robust, well-structured BI solutions — working with stakeholders across the value chain from initial scoping and design through to deployment and user adoption
Developing DAX measures and calculation logic in close collaboration with IT, turning underlying data into clear, business-ready insight in Power BI
Maintaining and continuously improving our front-end BI reporting — covering report quality, consistency, visual standards, and performance optimization
Acting as the go-to BI expert within Financial Systems & BI — advising stakeholders on what is possible, guiding self-service capabilities, and enabling broader data literacy across the organisation
Contributing to BI standards and best practices within Financial Systems & BI, ensuring consistency and scalability across all reporting output




Bring your world class expertise into play




You are fluent in English both in writing and verbal at a professional level. Danish is an advantage but not a requirement. Moreover, we are also looking for a new colleague with the following qualifications:




A degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Business Intelligence, or a similar field of study
Preferably between 3–6 years of hands-on experience developing Power BI solutions in a professional, business-facing context
Strong proficiency in DAX and SQL, with a structured approach to building robust measures and calculation logic in collaboration with the teams who maintain the underlying data models
Experience working as an internal BI resource serving multiple functions or business areas, with the ability to balance competing priorities and manage stakeholder expectations
Familiarity with data infrastructure concepts (data warehouses, semantic models, pipelines, star schema) and comfortable working closely with IT on data structure and sourcing
A structured, quality-conscious approach to development — you document your work, build for maintainability, and care about the end-user experience




As a person, you are curious, positive, precise, and genuinely interested in what the data is trying to say. You are comfortable moving between the technical and the human side of BI work — equally at home refining a DAX expression and discussing with a business partner what they actually need to see on a page. You take ownership of your deliverables and follow through from design to adoption. Being part of a fast-paced environment, you are able to manage multiple tasks simultaneously and find the right balance between quality while at the same time delivering effective results and meeting deadlines as well as targets.




We are an international company representing world-class expertise, delivering complex and high-tech products. We value our colleagues and our meaningful contributions to securing people through advanced technology. You will also experience that we have a strong focus on your development, teaching you useful new competences and further strengthening current ones, both professionally and personally. Our company culture is autonomous and engaging, and we acknowledge the importance of flexibility in a great working environment.

When joining Terma, we promise that you will experience: Great people, challenging tasks, world-class expertise, and meaningful contribution in your daily work.




__________________________________________________________________________________




Benefits at Terma




At Terma, we prioritize the well-being of our employees, ensuring they are well taken care of. Joining our team comes with a multitude of perks designed to enhance your social life, cultural experiences, and health.

International Environment: Explore great teamwork and professional sparring with colleagues around the world

Flexible Working Hours: Enjoy the opportunity to work flexibly and the option to work from home up to two days per week. Internet connection at home is paid for by Terma

Comprehensive Benefits Package: Receive a 10% paid pension along with a beneficial health care and insurance package. Enjoy discounts from various business partners

Well-being Perks: Opportunity to enroll in our on-site massage-offer at our locations in Denmark

Social and Professional Events: We have an active staff association that arranges a variety of social activities and events to bring all of our colleagues together

Competence networking across professional experts in Terma: Professional presentations and knowledge sharing across the organization




__________________________________________________________________________________




We are dedicated to fostering a diverse, equitable, inclusive, and welcoming workplace. Our commitment to DEIB (Diversity, Equity, Inclusion, and Belonging) means we actively value and celebrate unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. We strive to create an environment where all employees have equal opportunities to succeed, feel respected and valued, and experience a true sense of belonging.

To support an unbiased evaluation process, we kindly encourage applicants to omit personal details such as photos and age from their applications.




If you would like to take on the challenge of working in an innovative company, we look forward to receiving your application. A resume is required, while a cover letter is optional. To ensure that your application will reach us and is properly processed, we only accept applications via www.terma.com. Applications will be processed continuously.




__________________________________________________________________________________




Do you have any questions regarding the position?

Feel free to contact Hiring Manager on e-mail: KJEN@terma.com




Bear in mind, that we need you to confirm that you can obtain a security clearance by the Danish Defense Intelligence Service, and that all adults in your household are not posing any risk to the related risk-evaluation. This entails among other issues:

That you have as a minimum stayed within NATO/EU/MISWG countries for the last 7 years only interrupted by short stays abroad, and
That you have not committed serious criminal offences




Terma group has headquarters in Denmark and is a high-tech company developing products and systems for defense and non-defense security applications, including command and control systems, radar systems, self-protection systems for aircraft, space technology, and aerostructures for the aircraft industry. TERMA | Allies in innovation

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture — allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to build and evolve Spirii's data platform and pipeline infrastructure. Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll design and maintain reliable ELT pipelines, ensure data arrives in Amazon Redshift correctly and on time, and support the analytics layer that powers insights across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to shape Spirii's data infrastructure from an early stage — establishing standards for ingestion, orchestration, and data reliability as we scale.

Your Responsibilities

Pipeline & Ingestion


Design, build, and maintain scalable ELT pipelines that bring data from product backends, third-party services, and operational systems into Amazon Redshift.
Own ingestion reliability: monitoring, alerting, and incident response for pipeline failures.
Evaluate and improve orchestration tooling and scheduling.


Data Platform & Infrastructure


Evolve Spirii's data platform architecture: storage, compute, access control, and cost optimisation in Redshift.
Implement and maintain infrastructure-as-code practices for data workflows (Terraform/CDK or similar).
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure and transformations.


Data Quality & Governance


Implement data quality checks, freshness monitoring, and anomaly detection across ingestion and transformation layers.
Work with Analytics Engineers and domain teams to define and enforce data contracts and schema standards.
Ensure GDPR-compliant data handling: PII identification, access control, and data retention policies.


Collaboration & Stakeholders


Partner with Engineering and Product teams to instrument events correctly at the source — schemas, identifiers, and change management.
Collaborate with Data team to ensure the analytics layer is built on reliable, well-modelled foundations.
Contribute to a strong data culture by creating and enhancing documentation, runbooks, data lineage, discoverability and incident response capabilities.


About You


You care deeply about data reliability and building pipelines that teams can depend on.
You're hands-on: you build, test, and ship pipelines and infrastructure improvements yourself.
You think of data pipelines as products — you care about uptime, observability, and the downstream consumers who depend on them.
You're pragmatic: you know when to engineer robustly and when to keep things lean.
You collaborate well across teams and communicate clearly about trade-offs and status.


Qualifications


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role with a strong data focus.
Strong SQL skills and solid experience building production data pipelines.
Hands-on experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred; BigQuery/Snowflake/Databricks also relevant).
Experience with orchestration tools (Apache Airflow or similar).
Proficiency in Python for data work (pipeline scripts, transformations, tooling).
Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts, including incremental loading, CDC, and idempotency.
Familiar with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CDK, or similar).
Familiar with data governance concepts: access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices.


Nice to Have


Experience with data observability tooling.
Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
Experience with dbt (understanding of transformation layer and how it interfaces with pipelines).
Machine learning infrastructure experience is a plus, but not required for this role.


Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work-life balance.


Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.


The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but fluent English language skills are required.

About Spirii – Who We Are

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Spirii has recently celebrated its six-year anniversary as a founded company; however the people in Spirii have extensive experience working with electric cars and charging, as well as the development of future transport and energy systems.

Spirii is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences. Spirii strives to create an inclusive and diverse workplace, and we encourage all qualified applicants to apply regardless of age, gender, race, sexual/religious orientation, or disability.
Show more
HTML:
Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture — allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to build and evolve Spirii's data platform and pipeline infrastructure. Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll design and maintain reliable ELT pipelines, ensure data arrives in Amazon Redshift correctly and on time, and support the analytics layer that powers insights across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to shape Spirii's data infrastructure from an early stage — establishing standards for ingestion, orchestration, and data reliability as we scale.

Your Responsibilities

Pipeline & Ingestion

  • Design, build, and maintain scalable ELT pipelines that bring data from product backends, third-party services, and operational systems into Amazon Redshift.
  • Own ingestion reliability: monitoring, alerting, and incident response for pipeline failures.
  • Evaluate and improve orchestration tooling and scheduling.

Data Platform & Infrastructure

  • Evolve Spirii's data platform architecture: storage, compute, access control, and cost optimisation in Redshift.
  • Implement and maintain infrastructure-as-code practices for data workflows (Terraform/CDK or similar).
  • Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure and transformations.

Data Quality & Governance

  • Implement data quality checks, freshness monitoring, and anomaly detection across ingestion and transformation layers.
  • Work with Analytics Engineers and domain teams to define and enforce data contracts and schema standards.
  • Ensure GDPR-compliant data handling: PII identification, access control, and data retention policies.

Collaboration & Stakeholders

  • Partner with Engineering and Product teams to instrument events correctly at the source — schemas, identifiers, and change management.
  • Collaborate with Data team to ensure the analytics layer is built on reliable, well-modelled foundations.
  • Contribute to a strong data culture by creating and enhancing documentation, runbooks, data lineage, discoverability and incident response capabilities.

About You

  • You care deeply about data reliability and building pipelines that teams can depend on.
  • You're hands-on: you build, test, and ship pipelines and infrastructure improvements yourself.
  • You think of data pipelines as products — you care about uptime, observability, and the downstream consumers who depend on them.
  • You're pragmatic: you know when to engineer robustly and when to keep things lean.
  • You collaborate well across teams and communicate clearly about trade-offs and status.

Qualifications

  • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
  • 3+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role with a strong data focus.
  • Strong SQL skills and solid experience building production data pipelines.
  • Hands-on experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred; BigQuery/Snowflake/Databricks also relevant).
  • Experience with orchestration tools (Apache Airflow or similar).
  • Proficiency in Python for data work (pipeline scripts, transformations, tooling).
  • Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts, including incremental loading, CDC, and idempotency.
  • Familiar with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CDK, or similar).
  • Familiar with data governance concepts: access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices.

Nice to Have

  • Experience with data observability tooling.
  • Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
  • Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
  • Experience with dbt (understanding of transformation layer and how it interfaces with pipelines).
  • Machine learning infrastructure experience is a plus, but not required for this role.

Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work-life balance.

  • Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
  • Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
  • Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
  • Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
  • Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
  • Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
  • Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
  • Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.

The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but fluent English language skills are required.

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Title: Datastærke medarbejdere til udvikling af ledelsesinformation
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vi bygger det datafundament, som Skattestyrelsen bruger til at styre sine mål og resultater – og vi mangler en kollegaer, der brænder for at gøre data til beslutninger.




Dit nye kontor – gennemsigtig styring, prioritering og ledelsesinformation

Til datateamet i vores kontor Drifts- og produktionsopfølgning, søger vi nye kollegaer til at understøtte den centrale mål- og driftsstyring i Skattestyrelsen gennem brugervenlig ledelsesinformation. Kontoret består af i alt 14 medarbejdere, der har en bred pallette af både drifts- og udviklingsopgaver inden for målstyring og produktionsopgørelser.

Datateamet består af tre medarbejdere med forskellige kompetencer og profiler. Teamet udvikler dataløsninger i MS SQL Server og har ansvaret for, at ledere og medarbejdere i hele Skattestyrelsen kan tilgå vores dataløsninger. Der er højt til loftet, høje ambitioner og plads til høje grin og sociale arrangementer på tværs af kontoret.




Udfordrende og komplekse opgaver med stor betydning

Inden for rammerne af koncernens koncept for egenudvikling udvikler og vedligeholder teamet dataløsninger til brug for hele Skattestyrelsen. Vi har ambitioner om få skabt et solidt datafundament, så vi på sigt også kan lave mere avanceret analyse. Teamet har derfor en bred samarbejdsflade til styrelsens forskellige fagområder, og opgaverne spænder bredt:

Udvikling af ETL-processer, udtræk fra koncernens data-varehus med SAS og tilrettelæggelse med MS SQL Server.
Modellering af data fra de systemer, som er med i løsningerne, i henhold til koncernens standarder.
Vedligehold af Excel VBA-applikationer til styring af styrelsens økonomiske ramme og aktivitet.
Power BI rapporter til ledelsesinformation.
Kodeversionering med GitHub.
Understøttelse af Compliance for løsningerne, f.eks. adgangsstyring og slettepolitikker.
Dataunderstøttelse og bidrag til besvarelse af spørgsmål fra Folketinget, publikationer, pressehenvendelser og lignende
Kvalitetssikring og dokumentation af data.
Deltagelse i faglige netværk og arbejdsgrupper.




Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio bliver din daglige faste følgesvend. SAS og Excel VBA er under udfasning for at bruge Python til dataudtræk og mindre applikationer i stedet. Vi bruger Visual Studio Code til Python programmering.




Der er flere større udviklingsprojekter undervejs. Koncernens data-varehus skal afløses af en nyere platform frem mod 2030. Der kommer også nye systemer til sagsbehandling og tidsregistrering – data fra disse systemer skal bruges i kontorets ledelsesinformation.




Dine konkrete opgaver i kontoret vil blandt andet være:

Udvikling af databasestruktur -og ledelsesinformation.
Faste afrapporteringer af ledelsesinformation til Skattestyrelsens fagområder og direktører om mål og resultater
Sikring af optimal performance i rapporteringsløsningerne




Analytisk og datastærke profiler med sans for videreformidling

Vi forestiller os at du har en relevant videregående uddannelse inden for fx it, økonomi eller lignende. Vi er interesserede i både erfarne og nyuddannede profiler. Vores forventninger afhænger selvfølgelig af din erfaring, men vi lægger bl.a. vægt på følgende:

Kendskab til datamodellering (Kimball)
Erfaring med programmering af objekter i MS SQL Server
Grundlæggende kendskab til kravene til juridisk compliance i it-udvikling (og gerne adgangsstyring)
Er tryg ved at sætte retning for dataarbejdet




Er du nyuddannet forventer vi, at du har et grundlæggende kendskab til programmering (fordel med SQL og Python) og har lyst til at arbejde i Power BI og med applikationsudvikling.

Derudover forventer vi, at du;

Arbejder systematisk og kan skabe gennemskuelig struktur på komplekse datamængder
Er nysgerrig på, hvordan systemerne i Skattestyrelsen virker. Vi snakker både med procesejere og sagsbehandlere, når vi skal have tingene til at stemme
Kan yde faglig sparring internt i kontoret og med andre kontorer og fagområder i organisationen
Kan formidle komplekse problemstillinger og løsninger til slutbrugere, der ikke har indsigt i data.




Som person arbejder du struktureret med dine opgaver, og du trives, når omhyggelighed og detaljer er på plads. Du tager ansvar for dine arbejdsopgaver og hjælper gerne til, når dine kollegaer har behov for det. Du er god til at samarbejde, men evner også at arbejde selvstændigt.




Kontakt og ansættelsesvilkår

Vi glæder os til at modtage din ansøgning inkl. CV og dokumentation for uddannelse via linket ”Søg stillingen” senest 30. juli 2026.




Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte kontorchef Nick Damgaard Jespersen på 72371870. I uge 28 og 29 kan du kontakte Nick på LinkedIn.




Vi forventer at bruge en personprofilanalyse og en logisk test i forbindelse med rekrutteringsprocessen. Der vil forud for ansættelsen blive indhentet referencer og straffeattest.




Du bliver ansat på overenskomstvilkår i henhold til enhver tid gældende overenskomst for akademikere i staten (AC), overenskomst for kontorfunktionærer, laboranter og It-medarbejdere (HK) eller overenskomst for Fuldmægtige m.fl. (DTS) i Skatteministeriet. Ansættelse vil være i Skatteministeriet med tilhørende institutioner.




Hvis du går videre til ansættelse i rekrutteringsforløbet, skal du være opmærksom på, at vi foretager en undersøgelse af, om du har gæld, der er sendt til inddrivelse hos det offentlige. Vi undersøger ligeledes, om du har afgjorte kontrol-/og eller straffesager på skatte- og afgiftsområdet. Din ansættelse sker under forudsætning af, at vi ikke finder oplysninger om dig, der er uforenelige med en ansættelse i Skatteministeriets koncern.




Tiltrædelse snarest muligt.




Din fysiske arbejdsplads er Copenhagen Towers, Hannemanns Alle 25 2300 København S.




Skat er ikke enkelt. Derfor har vi brug for dig.

I Skattestyrelsen bliver du en del af et fagligt og mangfoldigt fællesskab, hvor du får ansvar, udvikler dig gennem komplekse opgaver og får tæt sparring med dygtige kolleger – alt sammen inden for fleksible rammer.




Skattestyrelsen lægger vægt på at understøtte fleksible arbejdsvilkår, og rammerne for arbejdstilrettelæggelsen drøftes mellem leder og medarbejder med udgangspunkt i den samlede opgaveløsning.




Din udvikling betyder meget for os, og hos os får du gode muligheder for læring og en karrierevej, der er fleksibel og tilpasset dig, uanset hvor i livet du befinder dig.




Vi samarbejder på tværs af generationer, uddannelser, baggrunde og landsdele om at løse vores kerneopgave. Vores opgaver kræver viden, ekspertise og fordybelse. Din læringskurve vil derfor være stejl, og du styrker din faglighed og opbygger kompetencer, der følger dig videre i dit arbejdsliv.




Skattestyrelsen spiller en vigtig rolle i samfundet ved at sørge for, at ingen betaler hverken for meget eller for lidt i skat. Det er ikke en enkel opgave, men den er vigtig, og vi er stolte af at sikre finansieringen af den offentlige sektor. Skat er alles ansvar – og så er det vores arbejde. Og det kan også blive dit.




Skattestyrelsen opfordrer alle kvalificerede uanset alder, køn, seksuel orientering, religion eller etnisk tilhørsforhold til at søge stillingen.

Title: Marketing Automation & Data Specialist
Place: Ballerup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vil du arbejde med data, analyse og marketing automation i praksis? Hos Velliv får du mulighed for både at analysere store datamængder, udvikle nye datagrundlag og omsætte indsigter til automatiseret kommunikation for mere end 400.000 kunder.

Vi har bedt din kommende leder om at sætte ord på jobbet og opgaverneVi søger to nyuddannede Marketing Automation & Data Specialister, der vil være med til at udvikle vores datadrevne kundekommunikation. Du bliver en del af et dygtigt fagligt miljø i Marketing & Brand, hvor vi samarbejder tæt med forretningen, Data Warehouse og IT for at skabe relevante og værdiskabende kundeoplevelser.

I rollen får du ansvaret for at analysere store mængder data og omsætte indsigter til automatiserede kampagner og flows, der skaber mere relevant kommunikation til vores kunder. Du kommer ikke kun til at arbejde med eksisterende data. Du vil også være med til at udvikle og specificere nye datasæt, segmenteringer og dataprodukter på vores Snowflake-platform i samarbejde med Data Warehouse og IT.

Samtidig bliver du en nøglespiller i implementeringen af Adobe Campaign v8 og får dermed en mulighed for at arbejde med en moderne cloud-baseret marketingteknologi – og være med til at bygge setuppet rigtigt fra starten.

Dine primære opgaver bliver:


Analysere store datamængder og omsætte kundeindsigter til segmentering, målgrupper og relevante kundeoplevelser
Udvikle og vedligeholde datagrundlag til marketing- og kommunikationsaktiviteter
Udvikle og optimere automatiserede kampagner og flows
Rapportering og opfølgning på kampagner og aktiviteter
Bidrage til at sikre et solidt datagrundlag, herunder datakvalitet og struktur på tværs af systemer


Du har forudsætningerne og modet til mereDer er styrke i forskellighed. Vi bliver bedre af at bringe forskellige erfaringer, fagligheder og personligheder sammen. Derfor opfordrer vi til, at alle uanset baggrund sender en ansøgning. Vi vurderer dog, at der er nogle grundlæggende forudsætninger, som er vigtige for at lykkes i jobbet:


Er nyuddannet eller har op til 1-2 års erfaring
Har en relevant kandidatuddannelse inden for fx økonomi, data science, matematik, eller statistik
Er nysgerrig på databaser, datamodeller og moderne dataplatforme
Har interesse for data og programmering – gerne erfaring med SQL samt fx DAX eller Python
Trives med at arbejde analytisk og omsætte data til konkrete løsninger
Er nysgerrig og struktureret og har lyst til at lære


Sådan oplever en af dine nye kolleger afdelingen"Her får du hurtigt lov til at tage ansvar og gøre en forskel. Vi hjælper hinanden, sparrer på tværs og deler gerne vores viden, så alle bliver dygtigere. Vi er et hold med forskellige faglige profiler, og det giver både gode løsninger og en hverdag med god energi, højt humør og masser af samarbejde." // Alan Sonne Rasmussen, ansvarlig for Marketing Automation

Plads til at være menneske – plads til at være digVelliv er flere år i træk blevet kåret til at have branchens bedste medarbejderimage og har gennem flere år vundet prisen som Årets Pensionsselskab. Hos os bliver du mødt med åbne arme, og vi gør os umage for, at du kommer godt fra start og hurtigt falder til.

Du får en buddy, der hjælper dig i gang, og en leder, der bidrager med mening, udvikling og retning. Du har mulighed for at melde dig ind i ”Klub Velliv” og deltage i aktiviteter inden for sport, mad, hobby og kultur for medarbejdere og deres børn. I det hele taget kan du forvente en kultur, hvor vi lever vores tre kerneværdier: Optimisme, Ordentlighed og Omsorg.

I Velliv skal der være plads til at være menneske – plads til at være dig.

Vi holder samtaler løbendeSend dit CV og et par ord om dig selv og din baggrund i en kort ansøgning. Vi vil gerne høre fra dig senest den 15. august 2026, men vi holder samtaler løbende fra 3. august 2026, så send gerne din ansøgning hurtigst muligt. Hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen, kan du kontakte Emil Madsen eller Alan Sonne Rasmussen via e-mail: emil.madsen@velliv.dk eller alan.sonne.rasmussen@velliv.dk.
