Vil du lære Velliv bedre at kende? Læs mere her
Show more
HTML:
Vil du arbejde med data, analyse og marketing automation i praksis? Hos Velliv får du mulighed for både at analysere store datamængder, udvikle nye datagrundlag og omsætte indsigter til automatiseret kommunikation for mere end 400.000 kunder.

Vi har bedt din kommende leder om at sætte ord på jobbet og opgaverneVi søger to nyuddannede Marketing Automation & Data Specialister, der vil være med til at udvikle vores datadrevne kundekommunikation. Du bliver en del af et dygtigt fagligt miljø i Marketing & Brand, hvor vi samarbejder tæt med forretningen, Data Warehouse og IT for at skabe relevante og værdiskabende kundeoplevelser.

I rollen får du ansvaret for at analysere store mængder data og omsætte indsigter til automatiserede kampagner og flows, der skaber mere relevant kommunikation til vores kunder. Du kommer ikke kun til at arbejde med eksisterende data. Du vil også være med til at udvikle og specificere nye datasæt, segmenteringer og dataprodukter på vores Snowflake-platform i samarbejde med Data Warehouse og IT.

Samtidig bliver du en nøglespiller i implementeringen af Adobe Campaign v8 og får dermed en mulighed for at arbejde med en moderne cloud-baseret marketingteknologi – og være med til at bygge setuppet rigtigt fra starten.

Dine primære opgaver bliver:

  • Analysere store datamængder og omsætte kundeindsigter til segmentering, målgrupper og relevante kundeoplevelser
  • Udvikle og vedligeholde datagrundlag til marketing- og kommunikationsaktiviteter
  • Udvikle og optimere automatiserede kampagner og flows
  • Rapportering og opfølgning på kampagner og aktiviteter
  • Bidrage til at sikre et solidt datagrundlag, herunder datakvalitet og struktur på tværs af systemer

Du har forudsætningerne og modet til mereDer er styrke i forskellighed. Vi bliver bedre af at bringe forskellige erfaringer, fagligheder og personligheder sammen. Derfor opfordrer vi til, at alle uanset baggrund sender en ansøgning. Vi vurderer dog, at der er nogle grundlæggende forudsætninger, som er vigtige for at lykkes i jobbet:

  • Er nyuddannet eller har op til 1-2 års erfaring
  • Har en relevant kandidatuddannelse inden for fx økonomi, data science, matematik, eller statistik
  • Er nysgerrig på databaser, datamodeller og moderne dataplatforme
  • Har interesse for data og programmering – gerne erfaring med SQL samt fx DAX eller Python
  • Trives med at arbejde analytisk og omsætte data til konkrete løsninger
  • Er nysgerrig og struktureret og har lyst til at lære

Sådan oplever en af dine nye kolleger afdelingen"Her får du hurtigt lov til at tage ansvar og gøre en forskel. Vi hjælper hinanden, sparrer på tværs og deler gerne vores viden, så alle bliver dygtigere. Vi er et hold med forskellige faglige profiler, og det giver både gode løsninger og en hverdag med god energi, højt humør og masser af samarbejde." // Alan Sonne Rasmussen, ansvarlig for Marketing Automation

Plads til at være menneske – plads til at være digVelliv er flere år i træk blevet kåret til at have branchens bedste medarbejderimage og har gennem flere år vundet prisen som Årets Pensionsselskab. Hos os bliver du mødt med åbne arme, og vi gør os umage for, at du kommer godt fra start og hurtigt falder til.

Du får en buddy, der hjælper dig i gang, og en leder, der bidrager med mening, udvikling og retning. Du har mulighed for at melde dig ind i ”Klub Velliv” og deltage i aktiviteter inden for sport, mad, hobby og kultur for medarbejdere og deres børn. I det hele taget kan du forvente en kultur, hvor vi lever vores tre kerneværdier: Optimisme, Ordentlighed og Omsorg.

I Velliv skal der være plads til at være menneske – plads til at være dig.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you a Data Engineer with 2-7 years of experience ready to take the next step? And would you like to work directly with clients while keeping up to date with the newest technologies? Then read on & apply to become part of a dynamic young team of 60 data experts who help companies gain new insights using a wide palette of modern tools.


Contract: Full-time, permanent
Location: Central Copenhagen, near Vesterport Station



Work Assignments

The role requires you to be both a hands-on builder and a point of contact for our clients' data challenges. Specifically, you will:


Build robust data pipelines and models that streamline the ingestion, transformation, and delivery of data.
Implement and develop modern data platforms through DevOps & CI/CD practices.
Collaborate with our clients’ engineers, analysts, & business users to solve business issues and ensure lasting value.



You will learn from our many data engineers with 7-20 years of experience, while taking on a mentoring role for our less experienced consultants.

What You Bring To The Team

Ideally, you have:


2-7 years of experience working as a data engineer.
Proficiency in Python, SQL, APIs, and/or software development.
An analytical approach to problem-solving, with a strong interest in how to drive business improvement using data.
Clear communication skills and the curiosity to engage with clients on projects.
Experience with some of these technologies: Snowflake, Databricks, Fabric, dbt, Azure, AWS, or GCP.
A relevant degree.
A current base near Copenhagen and fluency in Danish and/or English.



If you're excited about this role but you don’t tick all the boxes, we still encourage you to apply.

Why Devoteam Data Driven?

You will join a fantastic team of talented, curious data experts in a collaborative and dynamic workplace. Beyond working with nice colleagues on impactful projects, we offer comprehensive benefits to support your growth:


Continuous Growth & Community: We invest in your talent and dedicate time for learning to ensure you have up-to-date certifications in our technologies.
Work-Life Balance: We prioritize your personal time. In addition to your six weeks of paid vacation, we offer the flexibility of taking unlimited unpaid time off.
Social Events: We offer a variety of social events throughout the year. Recently we have done Laser tag, Pottery, Disc Golf, Book Club, Curling, Board Game Night, Foosball Tournament, Ice Hockey, among many other things.



Our Impact

Below are a few examples of our work:


Migrated Novo Nordisk's global supply chain analytics solution from Alteryx-based workflows to a governed, cloud-native architecture using dbt, Snowflake, and Azure DevOps to support enterprise-scale analytics and collaboration.
Developed a comprehensive end-to-end data warehouse solution, featuring automated pipelines that structured dimensional data from diverse operational systems for unified analytics for a client.
Assessed the current data landscape in Novonesis and implemented a new self-service framework to enable business users to utilise the power of the modern data stack.



Application Process

Apply by uploading your CV no later than August 9th.

We expect to invite selected candidates for interviews between mid-August and mid-September 2026. Note that our recruitment team is off 18/7-9/8.

We encourage all qualified candidates to apply, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or disability.

About Devoteam

Devoteam is a leading digital consultancy with a strong international presence, represented in 25 countries (primarily in Europe) with over 11,000 employees globally. In Denmark, our team comprises around 350 people and is spread across three business units - Data Driven (our unit), a ServiceNow unit, and a Management Consulting unit - each focused on different aspects of digital transformation.

Title: People Analytics Partner
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Europe, North America & Latin America

About The Role

At Maersk, we believe better people decisions start with better data.

We are looking for a People Analytics Partner to support our Regional organizations across Europe, North America and Latin America. As part of the People Data & Analytics team, you will partner with People Partners, business leaders and HR stakeholders to turn workforce data into insights, actions and business outcomes. You will help leaders understand workforce trends, solve people-related business challenges and make more informed decisions. Just as importantly, you will work with your colleagues to develop data solutions, and you will help build the capability of our stakeholders to use data effectively themselves, driving adoption of self-service analytics and fostering a culture of evidence-based decision making. The role emphasizes stakeholder partnership, translating insights into action, educating stakeholders on data products, product ownership, delivering data/insights, and supporting data quality improvement.

Success in this role requires curiosity, strong stakeholder management, collaboration and the ability to translate complex topics into clear, practical recommendations.

What You'll Do


Partner with People Partners and business leaders to understand workforce challenges and identify opportunities for analytics to drive business value.
Deliver meaningful workforce insights through data analysis, storytelling and visualization.
Act as a trusted advisor, helping stakeholders translate insights into decisions and actions.
Drive adoption of self-service reporting, dashboards and analytics products.
Design and facilitate training, workshops and capability-building activities that improve data literacy and analytical confidence.
Partner closely with Product, Data Management and Platform teams to improve People Data products and strengthen data quality.
Contribute to a collaborative, knowledge-sharing culture across the People Data & Analytics team.


What We're Looking For

Core requirements:


Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent stakeholder management and consulting skills.
Ability to communicate insights in a clear and compelling way.
Experience influencing outcomes through collaboration rather than authority.
Experience facilitating discussions, training sessions or workshops.
Strong teamwork mindset and ability to work effectively across functions, geographies and cultures.
Experience in People/Workforce Analytics, other Business Intelligence functions (finance, operations, commercial, supply chain, etc.), HR Consulting, HR Business Partnering or related fields.


Distinct advantages:


Experience supporting large, global organizations.
SME Knowledge of HR domains like workforce planning, recruitment, talent, rewards, engagement, organizational effectiveness & performance management, service delivery, etc.
Hands-on experience and deep familiarity with tools such as Power BI, Workday, Viva Glint, Fabric or similar analytics platforms.
Understanding of HR data models, reporting and data quality concepts.


What success looks like:


Trusted partner to regional People and business leaders.
Workforce insights that influence decisions and drive action.
Increased adoption of self-service analytics solutions.
Improved stakeholder capability and confidence in using People Data.
Strong partnerships across People Data & Analytics and the wider People Function.


Why join us?

You'll join a global team helping shape how workforce decisions are made across one of the world's leading integrated logistics companies. You'll work on meaningful business challenges, influence senior stakeholders and help build a more data-driven People Function—while enabling others to do the same.

Maersk is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, and we embrace different styles of thinking. Maersk is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applicants without regard to race, colour, gender, sex, age, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, marital status, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy or parental leave, veteran status, gender identity, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with all legal requirements.

We are happy to support your need for any adjustments during the application and hiring process. If you need special assistance or an accommodation to use our website, apply for a position, or to perform a job, please contact us by emailing accommodationrequests@maersk.com.

Title: Business Consultant – Data & Process
HTML:
(Senior) Business Consultant – Data & Process

med fokus på forretningsdata, processer og driftsnære kundeleverancer


Trives du i krydsfeltet mellem data, processer og forretning – og motiveres du af at skabe løsninger, der fungerer både på tegnebrættet og i den daglige drift?


Hos COMPA Mobility søger vi en erfaren profil, der kan forstå kundedata, stille de rigtige spørgsmål og omsætte komplekse input til praktiske løsninger, der fungerer i den daglige drift.


Rollen passer til dig, der ikke kun ser data som tal, filer eller systemfelter – men som vigtig forretningsinformation, der skal bruges korrekt for at sikre kvalitet, compliance og gode kundeoplevelser.


Det vigtige er, at du har stærk forretningsforståelse, arbejder struktureret og kan stå sikkert i dialogen med både kunder, kollegaer og samarbejdspartnere.


Vi forventer derudover, at du er stærk i Excel og har erfaring med blandt andet pivottabeller, avancerede formler, datamodeller, Power Query og analyse af større datamængder. Erfaring med VBA, SQL, BI-værktøjer eller automatisering er en stor fordel.

 


Hvad går jobbet ud på?

COMPA Mobility er i vækst, og vi søger en Business Consultant eller en Senior Business Consultant, der kan være med til at styrke vores arbejde med data, processer og driftsnære kundeleverancer inden for Global Mobility.


Du får en central rolle i at skabe overblik og struktur i komplekse opgaver, hvor data, mennesker, processer og myndighedskrav mødes. Ingen kunder er ens, og det kræver en profil, der kan tilpasse sig, stille de rigtige spørgsmål og få tingene til at fungere i praksis.


Rollen ligger i krydsfeltet mellem dataforståelse, drift, procesdesign og kundedialog. Du vil arbejde tæt sammen med vores specialister inden for skat, immigration, social sikring og international payroll.


Du får blandt andet ansvar for at:

–    forstå, strukturere, validere og kvalitetssikre kundedata fra forskellige kilder;

–    identificere, hvilke data der er nødvendige for en korrekt og effektiv leverance;

–    skabe overblik, når data er uensartede, ufuldstændige eller kommer fra flere systemer og interessenter;

–    sikre gode dataflows mellem HR, løn, økonomi, medarbejdere, udenlandske payrolls og eksterne rådgivere;

–    omsætte komplekse kundebehov til praktiske processer og arbejdsgange;

–    arbejde med processer inden for blandt andet international payroll, shadow payroll, skattepligtig løn og rapportering;

–    være i direkte dialog med kunder og sikre fremdrift, afklaring og kvalitet;

–    samarbejde med interne specialister og bidrage til løsninger, der er effektive, skalerbare og fungerer i praksis.


Hvem er du?

Vi leder ikke efter én bestemt uddannelse eller én bestemt tidligere titel. Vi leder efter en erfaren profil, der kan skabe struktur, stille de rigtige spørgsmål og tage ansvar for komplekse leverancer.


Du har sandsynligvis arbejdet med data, processer, økonomi, payroll, HR, systemer, implementering, controlling, projektledelse eller rådgivning – og du ved, hvad det vil sige at få tingene til at fungere i praksis.


Du behøver ikke komme fra Global Mobility-branchen. Det vigtigste er, at du har solid erfaring, arbejder struktureret og kan repræsentere COMPA professionelt over for kunder.


Din baggrund kan for eksempel være inden for:

–    finance, controlling eller økonomi;

–    payroll, HR operations eller HR-transformation;

–    projektledelse, procesoptimering eller implementering;

–    rådgivning, revision eller konsulentarbejde;

–    ERP, CRM, BI, systemer eller digital transformation;

–    data-, proces- eller forretningsanalyse.


Vi lægger især vægt på, at du:

–    har solid erfaring og professionel ballast;

–    arbejder struktureret og følger opgaver til dørs;

–    forstår data i en forretningsmæssig sammenhæng;

–    kan se, hvad data skal bruges til – ikke kun hvordan de kan behandles;

–    kan stille de rigtige spørgsmål til både kunder og kollegaer;

–    forstår samspillet mellem HR, løn, økonomi, systemer og forretning;

–    trives med kundedialog og kan skabe tillid i relationen;

–    kan navigere i detaljer uden at miste det store overblik;

–    har blik for kvalitet, effektivitet og økonomi;

–    taler og skriver flydende dansk og engelsk.


Det er en fordel, hvis du også har erfaring med:

–    payroll, shadow payroll, HR-data, finance-data eller rapportering;

–    Excel, BI, ERP, CRM, automatisering eller systemimplementering;

–    konsulentarbejde, projektledelse eller komplekse kundeprojekter;

–    internationale processer eller organisationer;

–    miljøer med mange interessenter og komplekse dataflows.


Hvad betyder data i denne rolle?

Data er en vigtig del af rollen, men ikke som en isoleret teknisk disciplin. Hos os handler data om at forstå kundens virkelighed – at vide, hvilke data der er nødvendige, hvad de bruges til, og hvordan de hænger sammen med processer, compliance og forretningsbehov.


Data kan for eksempel være løn- og HR-data, finance-data, kontraktoplysninger, bonusser, benefits, rejse- og arbejdsdage, opholdsdage, cost allocations og skatterelevante oplysninger – ofte i mange formater og fra mange systemer.


Shadow payroll betyder i denne sammenhæng processer, hvor løn- og skattedata opgøres og rapporteres i et arbejdsland, selvom lønnen udbetales fra et andet land.


Du må gerne være stærk teknisk, men det afgørende er, at du kan forstå sammenhængen mellem data, processer, compliance og kundens forretning – og omsætte det til løsninger, der virker i praksis.


Hvem er COMPA Mobility?

COMPA Mobility er en specialiseret Global Mobility-konsulentvirksomhed. Vi hjælper virksomheder med medarbejdere, der arbejder på tværs af landegrænser – til og fra Danmark og mellem andre lande.


Vi er et specialiseret team med høj faglighed, tæt samarbejde og en praktisk tilgang til opgaverne. Vi løser ofte opgaverne helt ud i driften på vegne af vores kunder.


Vi rådgiver og leverer ydelser inden for blandt andet immigration, arbejdstilladelser, registreringer, myndighedskrav, skat, social sikring, posted workers, destination services, international payroll og dataflows mellem HR, løn og økonomi.


Vi tilbyder

Vi tilbyder en erfaren rolle med stor mulighed for at præge, hvordan COMPA arbejder med data, processer og skalerbare kundeleverancer.


Du bliver en vigtig del af et område i vækst og får mulighed for at arbejde tæt sammen med dygtige specialister inden for Global Mobility.


Hos os får du:

–    en central rolle med indflydelse på processer, dataflows og kundeleverancer;

–    mulighed for at være med til at udvikle et vigtigt område i COMPA;

–    komplekse og varierende kundeopgaver;

–    tæt samarbejde med specialister inden for skat, immigration, social sikring og payroll;

–    et uformelt, ambitiøst og fagligt stærkt miljø;

–    mulighed for personlig og faglig udvikling i et specialiseret nicheområde;

–    fleksibilitet i hverdagen, herunder mulighed for hjemmearbejdsdage;

–    arbejdssted i enten Brædstrup eller Aarhus, med udgangspunkt i minimum tre dage om ugen på kontoret.


Interesseret?

Søg via LinkedIn eller send dit CV og gerne et par linjer om din motivation til lof@compamobility.com


Vi behandler ansøgninger løbende og ansætter, når vi har fundet den rette kandidat.


Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Louise Fangel, Director of Projects, Processes & Data: lof@compamobility.com

Title: Data scientist til asset management og digital transformation
Place: Hedehusene, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vil du arbejde med dataanalyse, modellering og visualisering og samtidig være bindeled mellem forretning, IT og vores eksterne leverandører? Så er du måske vores nye kollega.




Hos Vejdirektoratet arbejder vi hver dag for at skabe sammenhæng for de mange trafikanter, der benytter de danske statsveje. I afdelingen Befæstelser søger vi en kollega, der kan omsætte data til viden og være med til at styrke vores brug af data og samarbejdet med IT.




Dine opgaver

Du kommer til at arbejde med vejdata og bidrage til bedre beslutningsgrundlag for drift og vedligehold af vejnettet. Din rolle vil spænde bredt fra analyse og modellering til at skabe overblik og sikre, at behov bliver forstået og omsat i samarbejde med vores IT-afdeling og konsulenter. Vi arbejder med at strukturere og anvende data mere systematisk og har bl.a. løsninger i Azure.




Dine primære arbejdsopgaver bliver bl.a. at:

indhente, strukturere og analysere data om vejtilstand
arbejde med modellering og finde mønstre i data
understøtte økonomisk prioritering af vedligehold
udvikle visualiseringer og formidle resultater (fx i Power BI)
bidrage til bedre og mere effektive dataprocesser
sparre med IT og konsulenter om behov og scope for data, databaser og arkitektur
deltage i internationalt samarbejde med andre vejadministrationer




Din profil

Du har en uddannelse som f.eks. data scientist eller ingeniør, gerne med erfaring i eller uddannelse inden for databehandling eller vejteknik, herunder asset management.




Derudover ser vi gerne, at du:

kan arbejde i Python eller et andet objektorienteret sprog
har erfaring med dataanalyse og modellering fx Azur eller Databricks,
har erfaring med visualisering fx Power BI eller lign.
har forståelse for databaser og dataarkitektur
trives i en rolle, hvor du arbejder i spændet mellem IT og forretning
er nysgerrig på værktøjer og nye måder at arbejde på




Det er en fordel, hvis har praktisk erfaring med tilstandsmålinger, vejteknik eller pavement management systemer, men uanset baggrund sørger vi for at byde dig varmt velkommen til stillingen og give en grundig introduktion til arbejdsopgaverne.




Velkommen til afdelingen Befæstelser

Afdelingen har fokus på digitalisering og teknologi og består primært af ingeniører, der arbejder med vejes funktionelle og strukturelle opbygning og nedbrydning. Vi er en fagligt stærk afdeling med engagerede kolleger og højt til loftet.




Mange af os ved meget om veje, det behøver du ikke gøre, før du begynder. Til gengæld får du en central rolle i at arbejde med vores data i praksis og bidrage til, at samarbejdet mellem fagområdet, IT og leverandører fungerer. Vi er 20 ansatte og ser frem til at byde en ny kollega velkommen.




Dine løn- og ansættelsesvilkår

Du vil blive ansat og aflønnet efter gældende statslig overenskomst med mulighed for forhandling af tillæg alt efter dine kvalifikationer. Du ansættes som udgangspunkt efter én af følgende overenskomster:




Som akademiker ansættes du efter overenskomsten mellem Finansministeriet og AC. Du indplaceres på AC-skalaens basisløntrin efter anciennitet.




Som it-medarbejder ansættes du efter gældende fællesoverenskomst mellem Finansministeriet og Offentligt Ansattes Organisationer (OAO) og organisationsaftale for it-medarbejdere (PROSA) i statens tjeneste. Som udgangspunkt er stillingen placeret i den relevante løngruppe.




Du kan se basisløntrinnene i Statens lønoversigt: loenoversigten-1april-2026.pdf




Du ansættes med stillingsbetegnelsen: Ingeniør.




Forventet ansættelsesdato er d. 1. oktober 2026.




Kontakt Vejdirektoratets HR på rekruttering@vd.dk, hvis du har spørgsmål til lønindplacering. Vi gør opmærksom på, at vi kun oplyser startløn/basisløn for stillingen, og at eventuelle tillæg først forhandles i forbindelse med et konkret løntilbud.




Du bliver en del af en organisation, hvor fleksibilitet, kompetenceudvikling og arbejdsliv i balance står højt på dagsordenen. Der vil være mulighed for ugentlige hjemmearbejdsdage.




Dit ansættelsesområde vil være By-, land- og transportministeriet med de til enhver tid tilhørende institutioner. Tjenestested er Vejdirektoratet i afdelingen Befæstelser, hvor du ansættes i Drift- og Teknikdivisionen på adressen Guldalderen 12, 2640 Hedehusene (Fløng).




Læs mere

Vil du vide mere om Vejdirektoratet, og hvad det vil sige at arbejde hos os kan du læse mere om vores opgaver og medarbejdere på www.vejdirektoratet.dk.




Hvis du vil vide mere om stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte afdelingsleder Ghita Berg på telefon 7244 7141 eller 2290 7024.




Er du interesseret?

Send din ansøgning snarest muligt dog ikke senere end søndag den 2. august 2026. Vi forventer at afholde samtaler onsdag den 12. august 2026.




Søg stillingen ved at trykke på linket ”Søg stillingen” eller via vores hjemmeside www.vejdirektoratet.dk. Vedhæft ansøgning, cv, eksamenspapirer og andre relevante dokumenter.

Vi tror på, at et inkluderende og mangfoldigt arbejdsmiljø styrker fællesskabet og den måde, vi løser opgaverne på. Vi prioriterer en medarbejdersammensætning, der afspejler samfundet og giver lige muligheder for alle. Derfor opfordrer vi alle kvalificerede til at søge stillinger uanset alder, køn, seksuel orientering, religion, politisk anskuelse, handicap, politisk anskuelse eller etnisk tilhørsforhold.

Title: Ansvarlig for data science og analyse (Data scientist)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Lige nu leder vi efter dig, der kan omsætte kvantitative data til meningsfulde indsigter, som understøtter Clevers beslutninger og udvikling af løsninger.

Din rolle

Du vil være med til at drive den grønne omstilling til gavn for hele samfundet. Her spiller virksomheder en vigtig rolle gennem elektrificering af deres bilflåder. For at kunne understøtte denne udvikling er det vigtigt, at vi driver omstillingen baseret på datadrevne beslutninger funderet i kundernes adfærd. Netop dét har vi brug for dig til at hjælpe med. 

Du vil være ansvarlig for at omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Hvad fyldes din dag med?

Rollen er hverken en ren Power BI-rolle eller en rolle, hvor du sidder og bygger tunge ML-modeller. Det er en rolle for dig, der vil være tæt på forretningen og være med til at løfte vores data modenheden i Erhverv, så vores beslutninger bliver skarpere, hurtigere og mindre afhængige af enkeltpersoner. I din hverdag vil du omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata, og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på, hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Konkret betyder det at du:


Driver overgangen fra manuelle Excel-processer til robuste, skalerbare og datadrevne beslutningsgrundlag – f.eks. inden for forecasting, segmentindsigter og prissætning.
Samarbejder med vores Data Enablement-team om at udvikle dataløsninger og omsætte forretningens behov til konkrete data- og analyseleverancer.
Udvikler og videreudvikler Power BI-appen “Clever Erhverv” samt andre rapporterings- og analyseværktøjer, så de bidrager til bedre beslutninger for forretningen.
Sparrer og rådgiver om data for vores produkt-, salgs- og supportteams.
Aarbejder sammen med vores kvalitative indsigtsrolle for at skabe et stærkt beslutningsgrundlag på tværs af kvantitative og kvalitative indsigter.



Dit team

Du bliver en del af teamet Forretningsudvikling for Erhverv. Vi er et udviklingsteam på ni personer med kompetencer inden for økonomiske og kvalitative indsigter, produktudvikling samt salgs- og erhvervsstrategi. Du kommer til at arbejde tæt sammen med den kvalitative indsigtsrolle, produktudviklerne og resten af erhvervsforretningen.

Du vil have berøringsflader med mange forskellige interessenter på tværs af erhvervsforretningen og navigere i et udviklende og tempofyldt område, hvor dine kompetencer vil være efterspurgte. Samtidig bliver du en del af et stærkt fagligt fællesskab med Clevers øvrige specialister inden for kvalitative indsigter, hvor vi sparrer og vidensdeler.

Hvem er du?

Når vi forestiller os vores kommende kollega, ser vi dig, der har:


Fokus på beslutninger frem for rapporter og motiveres af at hjælpe forretningen med at forudse udviklingen og handle på den – ikke blot at visualisere historiske data.
Tekniske dybde for at indgå i en kvalificeret dialog med vores Data Enablement-team.
Kendskab til statistiske og datavidenskabelige metoder, kan arbejde i SQL og Databricks og kan vurdere kvaliteten af dataløsninger, selvom du ikke selv bygger dem.
Erfaring med Power BI og Excel (DAX er en fordel), og ser værktøjerne som middel til at skabe bedre beslutninger.
Stærke formidlingsevner til at oversætte mellem data og forretning og skabe struktur og klarhed for både tekniske og ikke-tekniske interessenter.
Lyst til at drive udvikling og være med til at løfte, hvordan vi arbejder datadrevet i Erhverv.



Vi er ekstra begejstrede, hvis du lytter aktivt, arbejder analytisk og kan skabe struktur og klarhed i komplekse dialoger.

Medledelseskompetencer


Du holder dit ego i skak og ved, at succes handler om at løfte i flok mod fælles mål  
Du tager naturligt ansvar for både dig selv og dine kollegaer, med omsorg og nærvær  
Du er en holdspiller, der ser arbejde som et samarbejde, ikke en konkurrence  



Har du sat mentale flueben hele vejen igennem?

Send gerne din ansøgning afsted allerede i dag – for vi kan godt finde på at lukke opslaget ned før tid, hvis vi møder det rigtige match. Vi håber, at vi gennem din ansøgning kan lære dig at kende og se, hvad du kan bidrage med. Hvis du bruger AI-værktøjer til inspiration, er det fint, men husk at personliggøre den for at skille dig ud.

Vi holder en lang velfortjent sommerferie væk fra computeren og er tilbage i uge 33, hvor du kan forvente at høre fra os. Hvis du har nogle spørgsmål i mellem tiden, så kontakt os på rekruttering@clever.dk .

Du kan læse mere om, hvordan det er at arbejde hos os på: www.clever.dk/velkommen eller vores Instagramkonto @liveticlever.

Er du nysgerrig på vores rekrutteringsproces kan du læse mere om den her: Rekrutteringsprocessen i Clever | Clever .

Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig!

Title: Business Intelligence Manager Specialist
Place: Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

As a Business Intelligence Manager Specialist, you will lead the delivery of high-impact data insights supporting Supplier Services team within the Business Service Organization (BSO). You will own the end-to-end BI lifecycle - from scoping and design to delivery and governance - while acting as a trusted partner to senior stakeholders. At the same time, you will support your team with elevating the standard of BI practice.

This role combines strategic thinking with deep technical expertise. You will drive reporting standardization, lead data-driven initiatives, and shape how the organization uses data to make better informed decisions faster.

This role is based in Billund. No relocation support is offered.


Core Responsibilities

Insight & analytics leadership





Lead the design and delivery of complex analyses across large, multi-source datasets, translating findings into clear, actionable business recommendations
Own the end-to-end reporting lifecycle creation, maintenance, documentation, improvement, and decommissioning of BI reports and dashboards
Identify business trends proactively and drive insight-led recommendations for process improvement and strategic growth
Define and facilitate prioritization of reporting requirements




Power BI & technical excellence





Architect and maintain advanced interactive dashboards and reports with full Power BI proficiency, including complex semantic models and DAX querying - this is a core requirement of the role
Champion data visualization best practices and enforce consistent standards across the team's BI output
Drive reporting standardization and ensure alignment on definitions, metrics, and governance across the organization




Stakeholder partnership





Act as the primary BI partner for senior stakeholders facilitate requirements gathering, manage expectations, and deliver outputs that directly inform leadership decisions
Present findings and recommendations to leadership in a clear, compelling, and audience-appropriate manner
Assist in the design and implementation of data-driven strategies that support business growth objectives




Governance, enablement & continuous improvement





Lead training and ongoing support for business users in BI reporting, data governance, and self-service analytics
Serve as a go-to expert for BI best practices, tools, and emerging technologies -continuously raising the bar within the team




AI-powered efficiency





Champion the adoption of AI tools (including LLM-based co-pilots, generative AI assistants, etc.) across the BI team’s workflows, from report and model development and DAX authoring to documentation, QA, and data storytelling
Identify and implement AI-driven efficiencies in recurring BI processes, including automated documentation generation, insight narration, anomaly detection, and report testing, reducing time-to-insight without compromising quality or governance
Define and role-model responsible AI usage within the BI function, establishing guardrails, sharing best practices with the team, and ensuring AI-assisted outputs meet the organization’s data accuracy and governance standards
Contribute to a culture of data literacy across BSO, enabling stakeholders to engage confidently with data




Play your part in our team succeeding

You will join the Service Experience team within BSO - a collaborative environment that spans Business Account Management, Service Management, Business Process Management and Insights Hub. At Manager Specialist level, you are expected to take ownership, operate with a high degree of independence, and bring others along – supporting colleagues and elevating the standard of BI practice across the team.

Do you have what it takes?





Bachelor's degree in Business Intelligence, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
5 - 7 years of experience in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, or a closely related discipline
Expert-level Power BI proficiency, including advanced DAX modelling - non-negotiable for this role
Demonstrated ability to lead BI projects end-to-end, from requirements definition through to delivery and adoption
Strong command of data visualization principles and the ability to translate complex data into executive-ready narratives
Experience partnering with and influencing senior stakeholders, including leadership-level audiences
Excellent communication skills - able to bridge the gap between technical teams and business decision-makers
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and structured thinking skills, with comfort navigating ambiguity




And even better if you also bring;





Experience with the Databricks platform or equivalent cloud data environments
Exposure to BI governance frameworks, data cataloguing, or data product thinking
Proven track record of driving standardization or center-of-excellence-type initiatives within a BI function
Practical experience using AI tools (e.g. Microsoft Copilot, GitHub Copilot, or similar) to accelerate BI delivery - including report development, documentation, insight generation, etc.




Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

Title: Dataanalytiker til registerdata og kvantitative analyser på uddannelsesområdet
Description:
Vil du være med til at skabe ny viden om børn og unges læring, trivsel og uddannelsesforløb? Har du stærke kompetencer inden for kvantitativ analyse og interesse for registerdata, programmering og machine learning? Motiveres du af at omsætte store datamængder til viden, der kan være med til at udvikle uddannelsesområdet? Så er du måske vores nye kollega.

I Kontor for Viden og Puljer arbejder vi med analyser, undersøgelser og evalueringer, der understøtter udviklingen af dagtilbud, skoler og uddannelser. Vi søger en dataanalytiker, der vil spille en central rolle i vores arbejde med dataklargøring, analyse og formidling af viden på særligt grundskole- og ungdomsuddannelsesområdet.

Vi har samtidig ambitioner om at udvikle nye koncepter for kortere og mere målrettede analyser, der hurtigt kan skabe ny viden om aktuelle problemstillinger på uddannelsesområdet. Her vil du få en nøglerolle i at udvikle metoder, processer og analyser, der kan styrke styrelsens arbejde med data og evidens.

Dine arbejdsopgaver

Som dataanalytiker bliver du en del af et stærkt analysemiljø, hvor du får mulighed for at arbejde med nogle af de mest centrale data på uddannelsesområdet.

Dine Opgaver Vil Blandt Andet Være At


gennemføre registerbaserede analyser af elevernes faglige resultater, trivsel, fravær og uddannelsesforløb
klargøre, bearbejde og kvalitetssikre store datasæt både på uddannelsesområdet og tilknyttede områder som fx socialområdet, beskæftigelse mv.
udvikle og gennemføre analyser i forbindelse med styrelsens arbejde med kvalitet og tilsyn
bidrage til udviklingen af nye koncepter for kortere og målrettede analyser
analysere data fra større spørgeskemaundersøgelser og internationale undersøgelser, som fx PISA-undersøgelsen
bidrage til udviklingsopgaver inden for machine learning og avancerede analysemetoder
udarbejde tabeller, figurer, notater og analyser til ledelse, ministerbetjening og offentlig formidling
samarbejde med forskningsmiljøer, universiteter og øvrige myndigheder om analyser og vidensprojekter.


Analyserne Kan Fx Handle Om


hvordan læringsmiljøet påvirker elevernes faglige resultater, trivsel og skolefravær
hvordan overgangen til og gennemførelse af ungdomsuddannelser har udviklet sig for unge med funktionsnedsættelser
hvilke forhold der kan forklare, at nogle unge ender uden uddannelse og beskæftigelse
hvad skolefravær betyder for elevernes faglige udvikling og videre uddannelsesforløb
hvilke faktorer der har betydning for gennemførelse og frafald på ungdomsuddannelserne
udviklingen i elevernes trivsel, og sammenhængen mellem trivsel, fravær og læring
konsekvenserne af reformer og andre uddannelsespolitiske initiativer.


Du bliver en del af et fagligt stærkt miljø, hvor vi sparrer og samarbejder om at løse vores opgaver. Du kommer også til at arbejde tæt sammen med medarbejdere i hele Undervisningsministeriets koncern.

Din profil

Vi forestiller os, at du har en relevant kandidatuddannelse inden for fx økonomi, statistik, data science, datalogi, sociologi, statskundskab eller lignende.

Derudover lægger vi vægt på, at du:


har stærke kvantitative og metodiske kompetencer
har erfaring med eller stor interesse for registerdataanalyser
har erfaring med dataanalyse i SAS, Stata, R eller Python
kan arbejde systematisk med dataklargøring, dokumentation og kvalitetssikring, herunder forskellige statistiske metoder og gerne også anvendelse af machine learning
har gode analytiske evner og kan omsætte komplekse analyser til klare konklusioner
har gode skriftlige og mundtlige formidlingsevner på dansk
arbejder selvstændigt, struktureret og tager ansvar for egne opgaver
trives med samarbejde og videndeling.


Det er en fordel, hvis du har erfaring med registerdata eller analyser på uddannelsesområdet eller lignende områder.

Om kontoret

Kontor for Viden og Puljer er organiseret i et Vidensteam, et Puljesekretariat og et nyoprettet Videnscenter mod skolefravær.

Vidensteamet arbejder med evalueringer, analyser, undersøgelser og formidling af viden. Vores opgave er at gøre det lettere for skoler og institutioner at bruge data og viden til at videreudvikle deres praksis. Kontorets puljesekretariat administrerer Undervisningsministeriets puljer, som skoler og uddannelser kan søge til forskellige forsøg og udviklingsprojekter. Videnscenter mod skolefravær skal understøtte skoler og kommuner i deres arbejde med at forebygge og håndtere bekymrende skolefravær, så eleverne kan komme tilbage i skole igen.

Kontoret har ca. 35 engagerede og fagligt stærke medarbejdere. Vi holder til i lyse lokaler på den gamle Østre Skole i Holbæk. Du bliver en del af et vidensteamet, hvor vi bl.a. har fokus på det stærke samarbejde gennem fælles fremmødedage, men med gode muligheder for at arbejde hjemmefra eller fra vores lokaler på Kalvebod Brygge i København.

Løn- og ansættelsesvilkår

Vi ønsker at besætte stillingen snarest muligt.

Du bliver ansat som fuldmægtig eller specialkonsulent afhængigt af dine kvalifikationer og erfaring. Ansættelse sker efter fællesoverenskomst for akademikere i staten indgået mellem Finansministeriet og Akademikerorganisationerne.

Arbejdstiden er gennemsnitligt 37 timer om ugen.

Ansættelsessted er Tidemandsvej 1, 4300 Holbæk.

Lyder det som noget for dig?

Send din ansøgning, cv og eksamensbevis senest 5. august 2026. Du søger stillingen via linket i annoncen.

Vi forventer at afholde første samtale onsdag den 12. august 2026. Hvis du går videre til anden samtale, kan du blive bedt om at løse en case, der indgår i den samlede vurdering.

Vil du vide mere?

Hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte teamleder Rune Hejlskov Schjerbeck på 24899432. Send gerne en sms forud for opkaldet af hensyn til ferie.

Du kan også læse mere om Undervisningsministeriet på vores hjemmeside uvm.dk.

Læs mere om, hvordan det er at være ansat og mød en medarbejder her: https://stukuvm.dk/job-i-styrelsen/moed-en-medarbejder/

Vi ønsker, at medarbejdernes sammensætning afspejler samfundets og ser frem til din ansøgning uanset din personlige baggrund – vi ser kun på kvalifikationer og kompetencer.

>

Styrelsen for Undervisning og Kvalitet (STUK) er del af Undervisningsministeriet. Vi bidrager til fagligt stærke uddannelser til alle. Vi fremmer kvalitetsudvikling på uddannelser og dagtilbud på Børne- og Undervisningsministeriets område, varetager tilsyn med kvaliteten på uddannelser og understøtter en effektiv institutionsdrift.

Vi understøtter den løbende implementering af politiske reformer ved at inddrage ny viden og data. I tæt dialog med skoler, institutioner, foreninger og arbejdsmarkedets parter arbejder vi for at gøre det lettere at give god undervisning og skabe gode dagtilbud.

Styrelsen er lokaliseret i København og Holbæk.

Vi ønsker, at medarbejdernes sammensætning afspejler samfundets og ser frem til din ansøgning uanset personlig baggrund – vi ser kun på kvalifikationer og kompetencer.

Application due

05-08-2026

Start date

01-10-2026

Work hours

37

Position category

Workplace

Styrelsen for Undervisning og Kvalitet i Holbæk

Homepage

https://www.stukuvm.dk/

Contact

Rune Hejlskov Schjerbeck

E-mail

E-mail

E-mail

E-mail

Follow us

Title: Junior BI Specialist til Business Intelligence i DLG
Place: Fredericia, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vil du bruge dine kompetencer indenfor data, tech og rapportering til at gøre en reel forskel for medarbejdere og ledere på tværs af DLG Group? Som vores nye Junior BI Specialist træder du direkte ind i hjertet af DLG’s strategi, ”Creating the Future”. Her handler data ikke bare om tal, men om at skabe gennemsigtighed, bæredygtige løsninger og en god brugeroplevelse.

Kort fortalt


I rollen som konsulent vil du få ansvaret for et eller flere data domainer, hvor du driver udvikling af innovative løsninger og tilsikrer succesfuld implementering.
Du vil stå for den løbende dialog med brugere omkring kravspecifikationer og support.
Du bliver en del af et ambitiøst og uformelt BI-team med omkring 8-9 kollegaer i et internationalt setup, der vægter faglighed og trivsel højt.
Det er en udviklingsrolle, som giver dig gode muligheder for at påvirke retningen.


Din hverdag og opgaver

Title: Associate Business Analyst, Commercial Analysis & Insights
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Ready to turn data into commercial insights that shape Pandora’s retail network?

As our new Associate Analyst – Commercial Analysis & Insights, you’ll play a key role in supporting the analytical foundation behind Pandora’s global store portfolio. Based at our Copenhagen HQ, this role offers an exciting opportunity to build your analytical expertise while contributing to business-critical decisions through data, reporting, and commercial insights.

Data analysis meets commercial decision-making

In this role, you will support the delivery of commercial analysis and insights within the Analytics & Data Governance team. Working closely with the team, you’ll execute recurring analytical tasks, maintain high-quality datasets, and contribute to reporting that supports investment decisions and store network planning across Pandora’s global business.

You’ll help ensure the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of data used across key workstreams, including store CAPEX analysis, performance reporting, and network planning initiatives. This is an ideal opportunity for an early-career analyst who enjoys working with data, producing accurate reporting, and supporting commercial decision-making through reliable analysis and high-quality datasets.

Your Key Responsibilities Will Include

Analytical Delivery & Reporting


Supporting recurring analytical processes and reporting activities
Producing regular reports and insights related to store investments, CAPEX, and network performance
Supporting analytical workstreams focused on store age, size, format, and maturity analysis


Data Quality & Maintenance


Collecting, consolidating, and maintaining key datasets used across the team
Performing regular data validation, cleansing, and quality checks
Identifying and resolving data anomalies and inconsistencies
Supporting improvements to data processes and structures


Tracking & Performance Analysis


Maintaining store-level tracking of investment and performance metrics
Supporting dashboard creation and monthly reporting cycles
Assisting with trend analysis, benchmarking exercises, and performance assessments
Producing structured outputs that feed into wider commercial assessments


Collaboration & Development


Working closely with the rest of the team to develop analytical capabilities and business understanding
Collaborating with operational stakeholders to gather information and resolve queries
Broadening scope and autonomy gradually as experience and confidence develop


To Succeed In This Role, You’ll Bring


1–2 years of experience in a business analytics, commercial analytics, finance, or reporting role - alternatively, recent graduate experience combined with relevant internships or student positions
Strong proficiency in Excel, including Power Query
Experience with Power BI and dashboard development
Ability to work confidently with large datasets from multiple sources
Strong data validation and quality assurance skills
Excellent attention to detail and accuracy
Experience within retail, consumer goods, FMCG, commercial finance, operations, or real estate is a plus
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Business Administration, Analytics, Data Science, Economics, Finance, Statistics, or another quantitative discipline


Nice To Have


Understanding of financial concepts such as P&L, CAPEX, ROI, and business performance drivers
Experience with statistical analysis, forecasting, or variance analysis
Exposure to business case development or scenario modelling
Familiarity with retail KPIs and store performance analytics
Knowledge of Power Automate, Python, or R


You Will Thrive In This Role If You Are


Highly proficient in Excel
Detail-oriented with a strong commitment to data accuracy
Structured and organized
Curious and proactive when investigating trends and anomalies
Reliable and focused on delivering high-quality work
Passionate about continuous improvement and automation
Collaborative and eager to learn from experienced colleagues
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, international environment


Your new team

You’ll join Pandora’s Analytics & Data Governance team within Network & Franchise Management. From our Copenhagen HQ, we support strategic decision-making across Pandora’s global store network through trusted data, reporting, and commercial insights. Our team combines analytical expertise with business understanding to help drive profitable growth and operational excellence.

Additional Information

Global career band: 5C – Associate Specialist

Did we get your attention?  

If you see yourself in the position and would like to become a part of Pandora’s future, please do not hesitate to apply. We look forward to hearing from you!  

  

We process applications on a continuous basis, which is why we encourage you to send your application as soon as possible. You can also read more about Pandora on our corporate site www.pandoragroup.com.  

  

Icing on the cake 

You will be working out of our beautiful Head Office in Copenhagen V, established in 2016 and designed to unite and inspire our teams. Besides our beautiful interior design we are also proud of our LEED Gold Certification for Energy and Environmental Design. This means that we use as few natural resources as possible and that we use them with respect. The result is a professional and dynamic space that is unique, comfortable and inspirational – and delivers a true Pandora experience to anyone who works here or comes to visit.  

  

About Pandora  

Pandora is the world's largest jewellery brand, specialising in the design, crafting and marketing of accessible luxury jewellery made from high-quality materials. Each piece is created to inspire self-expression, allowing people to share their stories and passions through meaningful jewellery. Pandora jewellery is sold in more than 100 countries through 7,000 points of sale, including more than 2,800 concept stores.   

    

Headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark, Pandora employs around 39,000 people worldwide and crafts its jewellery with 100% recycled silver and gold. Pandora is committed to leadership in sustainability and has set out to halve greenhouse gas emissions across its value chain by 2030. Pandora is listed on the Nasdaq Copenhagen stock exchange and generated revenue of DKK 32.5 billion (EUR 4.4 billion) in 2025.

Title: Data Analyst (Excel) - Remote
Description:
Job Title: Data Analyst (Excel)

Job Type: Contractor

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input. No prior experience in AI is required — your domain knowledge is what matters.

Key Responsibilities


Conduct in-depth data analysis using Microsoft Excel to extract actionable insights and support decision-making across teams.
Collaborate with project stakeholders to understand data requirements and translate them into meaningful analyses.
Review, clean, and validate datasets to ensure accuracy, consistency, and integrity.
Develop clear, concise reports and visualizations to communicate findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities in complex datasets, contributing to strategic initiatives.
Document methodologies, results, and recommendations with meticulous attention to detail.
Contribute expertise to enhance data-driven decision-making processes within the customer's team.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Proven experience performing data analysis in a business environment using Microsoft Excel as the primary tool.
Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy in data processing and reporting.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to clearly articulate analytical findings.
Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively across teams to drive business outcomes.
Solid understanding of Excel functions, formulas, and data visualization tools.
Proficiency in identifying data inconsistencies and implementing corrective measures.
Experience working in fast-paced, data-driven organizations.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience supporting cross-functional teams with data insights to inform business strategy.
Background in presenting analytical outcomes to diverse audiences.
Familiarity with additional data analysis tools or business intelligence platforms.


Skills: data,decision-making,data training,excel,data analysis,microsoft,microsoft excel,datasets

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst – Banking – London




A Data Analyst is required by a challenger bank in London on a contract basis (Outside IR35) to work with their Data Process team on a Data Excellence project.

We need someone with a good Data Analytics background within a Retail Banking or Corporate Banking environment to work with the team to define Data Quality rules. You must be someone who can explain complex data practices to non-data people. They are building an AI rules engine and need help to define the rules and then crunch the data thereafter to identify and rectify data errors.




You will:

Define Data Quality rules for automated data monitoring systems
Identify data quality issues in process flows




You will require:

Experience of working within a UK bank
A good understanding of UK banking data
Broad knowledge of Data requirements for regulatory reporting
Experience with Cloud Data platforms (ideally GCP)
Data quality best practice




Nice to have:

Data governance and data management experience




This is a key role in the bank’s automation and AI strategy and you will have access to a variety of stakeholders across the business. Sound like your next gig? Then get in touch and let’s chat.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
🚀 Senior Data Analyst | AI Governance & Emerging Technologies

📍 Liverpool, London or Glasgow (Hybrid)




I'm working on a genuinely unique opportunity for a Data Analyst who is excited by the future of AI and wants to help shape how organisations govern, monitor and responsibly adopt AI technologies.




This isn't a traditional reporting-focused Data Analyst position.

The successful candidate will sit within a Data Governance function and play a key role in helping design frameworks, controls and monitoring approaches for AI systems, AI-generated data and AI-assisted workflows as adoption accelerates across the business.




We're looking for someone who combines strong analytical capability with a genuine interest in Machine Learning, Generative AI and emerging technologies.




Key experience we're looking for:

✅ Strong Data Analysis experience

✅ Advanced SQL and Python skills

✅ Experience working with Machine Learning concepts and models

✅ Understanding of Generative AI, LLMs and AI-assisted development tools

✅ Data Governance, Data Quality or Data Management experience

✅ Ability to work with stakeholders and translate complex concepts into practical solutions

✅ Curiosity and the ability to solve problems where there isn't an established blueprint




Highly desirable:

⭐ Snowflake experience

⭐ Experience working with cloud data platforms

⭐ Exposure to AI governance, model governance, risk or control frameworks

⭐ Familiarity with Git, testing principles and structured development practices




This role would suit someone who enjoys asking questions such as:

• How should organisations govern AI systems?

• What data should be captured from AI agents?

• How do we monitor AI-generated outputs?

• What controls are needed to ensure responsible AI adoption?




The hiring team is particularly interested in candidates who can bridge the gap between data analytics, technology and governance, rather than those focused purely on dashboard development or pure data science.




If you're a Data Analyst with strong Python skills, exposure to Machine Learning and AI, and you're interested in working at the forefront of AI governance, I'd be keen to speak with you.




💰 Up to £65,000 (London) / £56,000 (Scotland) / £52,000 (Liverpool)

🎯 Bonus + Excellent Pension + Strong Long-Term Career Growth

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




Overall Ambition




Nando’s is on a journey to Create Lasting Happiness, making a difference to the lives of our people, our customers, and the communities we work in. Originally started in Johannesburg, South Africa, we pride ourselves on creating memorable experiences for everyone involved in Nando’s: from our employees, communities, founders, and suppliers, but most importantly, our customers.

Whilst we always look back on our history and story with pride, the story is still evolving. We have recently been growing our digital capabilities to ensure the Nando’s experience remains relevant to modern-day customers.




You will be joining a tech team that has grown rapidly in the past five years and is determined to enable change within the restaurant industry by bringing innovative thinking and the latest technology tools and practices to solve some of its most complex problems.







The Data and Analysis Team

We are seeking an experienced Data Analyst to join our Data and Analysis team. Our remit is to ensure everyone has access to the right information, at the right time, and that Nando’s gets the best value out of our data to achieve our business goals.




Our current focus is extracting insights from the data and creating a single source of accurate, timely data as we continue to increase our data maturity and advance our predictive analytic capability and explore the role of AI in the business.




You will be part of our work to support our strategic goals and build a data-informed attitude for addressing our opportunities and challenges.







Our Values

At Nando’s, our values guide how we work, make decisions, and show up for each other every day:

Pride – We take pride in everything we do, from the quality of our food to the impact of our work.
Passion – We bring energy, enthusiasm, and a love for what we do.
Courage – We speak up, challenge the status quo, and are not afraid to try new things.
Integrity – We do the right thing, act honestly, and build trust with each other.
Family – We care for and support one another, creating a sense of belonging where everyone can thrive.







What we're looking for:

As a Data Analyst you will work across a wide range of domains from financial and operational data, to kitchen tech and stock, helping our teams make better decisions grounded in sound analysis. As a curious problem-solver, you will be able to move between the technical and the commercial; and be comfortable building data models as well as presenting findings to senior stakeholders.




In this role you’ll work independently across the full data stack, understanding the ‘why’ behind requests before jumping to the ‘what’, and contributing to the translation of business problems into well-defined, actionable work. You’ll be supported by a collaborative team and won’t be expected to deliver everything solo from day one, but you’ll be expected to grow into doing so.

You will…







Write and maintain SQL models (in Dataform) to transform and serve reliable data across our pipelines
Support data engineers in the design, building and testing of data pipelines, able to contribute meaningfully, not just consume the output
Build and iterate on cloud-based reports and dashboards in Looker (or similar BI tool), giving our support teams and restaurants clear, actionable insight into performance
Translate business questions into well-scoped analysis and understand the problem and domain before reaching for the answer
Communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, choosing the right medium for the audience
Peer review colleagues’ work and contribute to data quality standards and documentation
Identify opportunities to automate manual processes and data tasks, and take the initiative to implement them







Skills required

You will have proven experience in the following:

Writing complex SQL to query, transform and model data across our cloud data platform (window functions, CTEs, complex datatypes)
Building and maintaining data models in Dataform, or a similar data transformation tool such as Snowflake or DBT, with an understanding of how they fit into the broader pipeline
Building reports and dashboards in Looker or a similar BI tool, serving reliable, self-serve data to the business
Using Git as standard practice — branching, pull requests and code review
Contributing to the translation of business requests into clearly scoped, actionable work items
Applying data quality techniques — writing tests, identifying issues proactively and communicating data limitations clearly
Documenting data models, definitions and lineage to a production standard
Understanding data governance principles and championing good data management practice within the team
Applying and understanding when to use relevant statistical methods to support analysis
Supporting data engineers in designing, coding and testing data processing pipelines
Using AI-assisted coding tools (e.g. Claude) to improve the speed and quality of your work — critically, not blindly — with an awareness of responsible AI use and data governance implications







You will also display

Clear communication with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, able to explain complex findings in plain language and tell the story behind the data
Genuine intellectual curiosity, actively exploring new tools and methods, including AI, and sharing what you learn with the team
Comfort with ambiguity, able to define the right question and find the “so what” before jumping to answer
A product mindset, understanding the business objective behind every piece of analysis, not just the request on the surface
A proactive approach to improving processes, data quality and ways of working, with the initiative to make it happen, not just flag it
A collaborative team attitude, peer reviewing others’ work, sharing knowledge and contributing to a stronger team




Inclusion

At Nando’s, everyone is welcome. Inspired by our Southern African heritage we know and value the richness that diversity brings to Nando’s. We’re committed to creating an inclusive and respectful culture for all; including our existing and future Nandocas, partners and suppliers, customers and the communities we are a part of. We deliberately work hard to create an environment where every individual is valued, respected and can flourish regardless of who they are, their background or outlook on life.




We welcome applications from a diverse range of individuals and will consider any reasonable adjustments in order to enable candidates to perform as well as possible during the recruitment process.

Title: Data Analyst, Business Intelligence
Description:
As a Data Analyst in our Business Intelligence team at AgileOne, you will be responsible for designing and delivering data-driven insights that support internal teams and global MSP clients.




Working closely with stakeholders across the business, you will build impactful dashboards, develop data models, and translate complex workforce data into clear, actionable insights that drive decision-making and performance







Our Requirement

Design, build, and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports for internal and client ue
Develop interactive visualizations to highlight trends and key workforce metrics
Build and maintain data models (e.g. star schemas, fact and dimension tables)
Create complex DAX measures and calculations for reporting and analytics
Extract, transform, and load data using SQL and Power Query (M)
Ensure data accuracy and consistency across multiple systems
Optimize report performance and usability (UX, drill-throughs, automation, etc.)
Collaborate with data engineering and infrastructure teams
Support automation of reporting processes (e.g. Power Automate)
Document data sources, logic, and reporting standards




Your Profile:

Experience working as a Data Analyst / BI Analyst (preferably within MSP, staffing, or workforce solutions environment
Good understanding of workforce data, including metrics such as spend, bill rates, headcount, tenure, compliance, and supplier performance
Familiarity with VMS tools and data structures (e.g. Fieldglass, Beeline, VNDLY) is highly valued
Strong hands-on experience with Power BI (Desktop & Service)
Advanced knowledge of DAX, Power Query (M), andSQL
Solid understanding of data modeling and analytics best practices
Experience working with large and complex datasets
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
High attention to detail and data accuracy
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Strong communication skills and ability to present data clearly
Comfortable working independently and collaboratively




What We Offer

A collaborative, international work environment with a global leader in MSP/VMS services
Remote work
Mental Wellbeing support, Nectar points, Medical coverage
Pension scheme and Employee referral awards
Supportive leadership and opportunity for long-term career growth

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




Industry: Designer toys and collectible pieces

Location: London, Canary Wharf

Job type: Full time Permanent




About POP MART




POP MART (09992.HK) is a leading global company in the trend culture and entertainment industry. Centered around IP, POP MART has built a comprehensive platform for creative incubation and IP operation, empowering global creators while delivering exciting products, services and immersive entertainment experiences to consumers.




POP MART identifies and nurtures emerging artists and designers worldwide, creating popular character IPs through a well-established development and operation system. Our portfolio includes iconic characters such as MOLLY, SKULLPANDA, DIMOO, THE MONSTERS and Hirono. POP MART operates over 500 physical stores and more than 2,300 Robo Shops across more than thirty countries and regions, and through multiple cross border e commerce platforms reaches audiences in over ninety countries and regions, bringing joy to young consumers around the world.




POP MART has developed a comprehensive IP commercialisation ecosystem that spans multiple industries, deepening emotional bonds between fans and IPs while exploring broader opportunities for value creation. Our ventures include the world’s first immersive intellectual property themed park in the art toy industry, the expansion of its IPs into global markets through licensing and co-branding with leading global brands, and the curation of art toy expos and intellectual property themed exhibitions internationally. In Europe, POP MART has built a strong presence in key markets including the United Kingdom, France, Italy, Germany, the Netherlands and Spain, and is expanding into new high potential markets. Our expansion brings POP MART closer to European fans and offers new ways for them to connect with our IPs and stories.




For more information, please visit www.popmart.com and our LinkedIn Career page.




What to Expect




POP MART is a fast-paced company with a young and multicultural team where you can expect to:




Contribute to a fast-growing European business that combines retail, art, and creative storytelling
Work with passionate teams across different countries, all connected by a shared love for designer toys and unique customer experiences
Grow your skills through hands-on projects, collaborative learning, and opportunities to explore new ideas




Benefits




POP MART offers our employees:




Monthly appreciation gifts featuring our latest POP MART designs
A structured bonus scheme linked to both company and individual performance
25 days of annual leave, in addition to bank holidays
4x life insurance, together with group medical and travel insurance
A confidential Employee Assistance Programme, alongside access to a discount platform for selected online retailers
Training and development programmes, together with regular team events that encourage learning, collaboration, and celebration




The Role and Candidate




We are looking for a data driven and detail oriented professional to support business performance monitoring and analysis across POP MART’s European business.

This role focuses on operational reporting, performance tracking, and business insight generation across key revenue channels, including physical stores, vending machines, and online business. You will support the business by monitoring core operational indicators, identifying trends and issues through data, and helping teams better understand performance through clear, accurate, and practical analysis.

You will also work closely with teams including Finance, Retail Operations, Marketing, Merchandising, and Business Development to support regular reporting, operational reviews, and day to day data requests. In parallel, the role will contribute to improving reporting consistency, data quality, and the practical use of data in business operations.

This position would suit someone with solid analytical capability, structured thinking, and a hands on approach, who is comfortable working with detailed data while supporting wider business needs in a fast paced environment.




Responsibilities




Daily Operation Monitoring and Performance Review




Establish daily, weekly and monthly monitoring for core operational indicators, including revenue, gross profit, expense ratio, payment collection, labour efficiency and store efficiency, across all revenue channels such as physical stores, vending machines and online business
Prepare daily, weekly, monthly and quarterly operational reports in a timely manner, benchmark performance against budget, year on year and month on month data, and analyse the causes of performance gaps
Track key business indicators including sales volume, average transaction value, conversion rate and profit margin, and provide regular operational analysis to support performance improvement
Analyse the performance of different markets, stores and vending machines, identify trends and issues, and support the development of targeted actions to improve profitability
Support the Business Development team with data input for store and vending machine site selection, based on market data and operational analysis
Support monthly and quarterly operational review meetings by preparing review materials and presentations, highlighting business progress, key issues and improvement priorities, and tracking follow up actions
Work closely with the Finance team to verify operational, cost and expense data, align statistical definitions, resolve data discrepancies, and ensure management reporting is accurate and reliable




Business Insights and Reporting




Work closely with business teams on a daily basis to understand key business pain points, and support practical data solutions based on business needs and operating scenarios
Conduct data analysis across sales operations, marketing and consumer insights, identify the business logic behind the data, and provide insights to support business optimisation
Improve the quality and depth of recurring insight reports by moving beyond data listing and strengthening business focused conclusions and recommendations
Build and maintain structured data records and archives to support data traceability, consistency and reusability
Extract and process data from multiple sources, including business systems, CRM and ERP systems, and use SQL for regular queries, data cleaning, deduplication, correlation and aggregation
Build and maintain basic dashboards and automated reports to reduce manual reporting, improve data visibility, and support more efficient access to information




Cross Functional Collaboration and Data Governance




Support Sales, Marketing, Product, Supply Chain, Finance and other teams with day to day data requests, including customised queries, report exports and basic analysis support
Provide guidance to business teams on basic data viewing and analysis methods to improve the practical use of data in daily operations
Support the standardisation of data fields, statistical rules and analysis templates, and help establish data logs and archiving practices to strengthen data governance
Contribute to the development of a more consistent and practical data culture across the business




Required Qualifications




3–5 years of business analysis experience; candidates with experience in management consulting, internet business analysis, retail operations analysis, or other relevant analytical experience, or those with extensive professional experience are preferred




Excellent communication and organisational skills, capable of handling complex issues with structured thinking and analytical abilities




Proactive, hands-on, and entrepreneurial mindset with experience working in a fast-paced business environment




Outstanding problem-solving, analytical, written and verbal communication skills




Data-sensitive, able to identify business trends and issues through data analysis; proficiency in data analysis and visualisation tools such as Excel, SQL, Tableau is preferred, but strong willingness to learn is a must




Proficiency in English (listening, speaking, reading and writing), with extensive experience in cross-team project collaboration and communication







Desirable but Not Essential




Experience in the toy industry or pop culture retail sector
Familiarity with Local and European retail landscapes
Understanding of designer toy and collectible trends, particularly in the art toy market
Experience supporting operational reporting across multiple countries or regions
Understanding of retail KPIs including conversion rate, ATV, margin, labour efficiency and inventory performance
Exposure to data governance, reporting automation, or process improvement initiatives
Experience within retail, consumer goods, eCommerce, or multi-site operations environments
Experience working with ERP, CRM, POS or business intelligence systems
Familiarity with dashboard tools such as Tableau, Power BI or Looker










If you are excited to be part of POP MART’s journey and believe you have the skills and passion to contribute, click Apply Now and send us your CV.
