Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Senior Data Analyst – Tax Technology




Base Location: Canary Wharf, London OR Glasgow based (Hybrid – 2 days per week in office)




KPMG UK offices | KPMG UK




The KPMG Tax Technology & Innovation are made up of Technology Consultants, Business Analysts, Product Owners, Data Architects & Engineers, Multi-disciplinary Engineering teams and IT Service delivery professionals working on a large portfolio of systems used to improve productivity and solve key business problems across Tax & Legal.




This business has 2500+ tax & legal professionals covering multiple disciplines. We serve many thousands of clients and private individuals by providing a modern, market-leading suite of technology solutions that provide an efficient digital experience from routine to complex tax advice.




KPMG is one of the world's largest and most respected consultancy businesses, we've supported the UK through times of war and peace, prosperity and recession, political and regulatory upheaval. We've proudly stood beside the institutions and businesses which make the UK what it is.




Why Join the KPMG Tax Technology & Innovation team as a Senior Data Analyst?




Currently KPMG Tax and Legal provide services to some of the world’s largest companies.




KPMG is investing $5bn over 5 years in technology and our goal is to achieve the highest standards of quality and to deliver excellent solutions for our clients.




We are a team of 300+ technologists providing technology enablement services and solutions across the Tax and Legal business. This business has 2,500 tax & legal professionals covering multiple disciplines. We serve many thousands of clients and private individuals by providing a modern, market-leading suite of technology solutions that provide an efficient digital experience from routine to complex tax advice.




Tax is highlighted frequently as an industry where generative AI will have a significant impact on how services are provided. You will be working as a team at the forefront of navigating that change and redefining the way in which tax is determined in the UK and globally, making sure people and companies pay the right amount of tax with the minimum amount of effort.




What will you be doing?




As a Senior Data Analyst in the Tax Technology team, you will be an integral part of a team delivering analytics solutions to clients across KPMG’s Tax & Legal practice. You will be working with a mixture of UK-based and India-based data engineers and data analysts and in this role you will:




Play a key role in the design and implement high quality analytics solutions for internal and external clients.
Work with teams of analysts using Alteryx and related tools to automate a variety of ETL and reporting processes for internal and external clients.
Work with teams of BI Developers to develop visualisations, providing insights to our clients.
Coach and mentor others to help them achieving their potential.
Engage with a range of stakeholders, both internally and clients.




The role can be based anywhere in the UK, though preference will be given to candidates in London. Regular travel to London will be required.




Whilst there is no expectation of existing knowledge of tax, we would expect you to develop a degree of domain knowledge over time.




What will you need to do it?




Previous experience in a client facing / consulting role as a Data Analyst or Analytics Engineer.
Recent experience using advanced analytics to solve complex business problems.
Excellent knowledge of Alteryx, ideally with the Alteryx Designer certification.
Experience manipulating large data sets.
Awareness of data visualisation tools eg. Power BI, Tableau etc.
Experience of SQL Server and/or Azure SQL and an understanding of SQL programming.
Expert problem-solving skills with the ability to logically analyse complex requirements, processes and systems.

Job Title: Senior Data Analyst – Tax Technology


Base Location: Canary Wharf, London OR Glasgow based (Hybrid – 2 days per week in office)


KPMG UK offices | KPMG UK


The KPMG Tax Technology & Innovation are made up of Technology Consultants, Business Analysts, Product Owners, Data Architects & Engineers, Multi-disciplinary Engineering teams and IT Service delivery professionals working on a large portfolio of systems used to improve productivity and solve key business problems across Tax & Legal.


This business has 2500+ tax & legal professionals covering multiple disciplines. We serve many thousands of clients and private individuals by providing a modern, market-leading suite of technology solutions that provide an efficient digital experience from routine to complex tax advice.


KPMG is one of the world's largest and most respected consultancy businesses, we've supported the UK through times of war and peace, prosperity and recession, political and regulatory upheaval. We've proudly stood beside the institutions and businesses which make the UK what it is.


Why Join the KPMG Tax Technology & Innovation team as a Senior Data Analyst?


Currently KPMG Tax and Legal provide services to some of the world’s largest companies.


KPMG is investing $5bn over 5 years in technology and our goal is to achieve the highest standards of quality and to deliver excellent solutions for our clients.


We are a team of 300+ technologists providing technology enablement services and solutions across the Tax and Legal business. This business has 2,500 tax & legal professionals covering multiple disciplines. We serve many thousands of clients and private individuals by providing a modern, market-leading suite of technology solutions that provide an efficient digital experience from routine to complex tax advice.


Tax is highlighted frequently as an industry where generative AI will have a significant impact on how services are provided. You will be working as a team at the forefront of navigating that change and redefining the way in which tax is determined in the UK and globally, making sure people and companies pay the right amount of tax with the minimum amount of effort.


What will you be doing?


As a Senior Data Analyst in the Tax Technology team, you will be an integral part of a team delivering analytics solutions to clients across KPMG’s Tax & Legal practice. You will be working with a mixture of UK-based and India-based data engineers and data analysts and in this role you will:


  • Play a key role in the design and implement high quality analytics solutions for internal and external clients.
  • Work with teams of analysts using Alteryx and related tools to automate a variety of ETL and reporting processes for internal and external clients.
  • Work with teams of BI Developers to develop visualisations, providing insights to our clients.
  • Coach and mentor others to help them achieving their potential.
  • Engage with a range of stakeholders, both internally and clients.


The role can be based anywhere in the UK, though preference will be given to candidates in London. Regular travel to London will be required.


Whilst there is no expectation of existing knowledge of tax, we would expect you to develop a degree of domain knowledge over time.


What will you need to do it?


  • Previous experience in a client facing / consulting role as a Data Analyst or Analytics Engineer.

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Job Title: Data Analytics Intern (Remote)

Company: Stealth (Confidential)




About the Role

We are hiring on behalf of a fast-growing Stealth Startup looking for a curious and detail-oriented Data Analytics Intern. This is an exciting opportunity to work closely with founding teams and gain hands-on experience in extracting insights, building dashboards, and driving data-backed decision-making in a high-impact environment.




Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, and analyze structured and unstructured data
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends and patterns
Build and maintain dashboards and reports for business insights
Write efficient SQL queries to extract and manipulate data
Assist in defining KPIs and metrics for business performance
Collaborate with product, marketing, and business teams
Present insights in a clear and actionable manner




Required Qualifications

Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or related field
Strong knowledge of Excel/Google Sheets
Basic proficiency in SQL
Familiarity with Python (Pandas, NumPy) or similar tools
Understanding of data visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, etc.)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills




Preferred Qualifications

Experience with real-world datasets or analytics projects
Knowledge of business metrics and performance tracking
Exposure to A/B testing or statistical analysis
Familiarity with startup or fast-paced environments




Key Skills

Data Analysis & Interpretation
SQL & Data Querying
Dashboarding & Visualization
Critical Thinking
Communication & Storytelling with Data




What We’re Looking For

A data-driven thinker who can translate numbers into meaningful insights
Someone who thrives in a fast-paced, ambiguous startup environment
High ownership and willingness to learn quickly
Strong attention to detail




Location

Remote




Duration

Internship (3–6 months, with potential extension or full-time opportunity)




Compensation

Competitive stipend (based on experience and client budget)




Why Join?

Work directly with a stealth-mode startup solving real-world problems
Gain hands-on experience with live data and business decision-making
Opportunity to convert into a full-time role based on performance

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: CRM Data Analyst

Location: London

Duration: 12 Months FTC

Work Model: Hybrid 4- days onsite in a week.

Purpose:

We are seeking a highly analytical and technically skilled CRM Data Analyst to join our growing Global Customer team. In this role, you will leverage data to drive meaningful customer engagement, optimize CRM strategies, and support the brand’s ambition to deliver exceptional client experiences.

You will work closely with CRM, Data Science, and Digital teams to translate data into actionable insights, using advanced analytics tools and coding in Databricks to structure and analyse large datasets. The ideal candidate has a balance of technical expertise and business acumen, with a passion for the luxury and fashion industry.




Responsibilities:

Build, maintain, and optimize customer data models and segmentation frameworks in Databricks
Identify customer insights and trends that inform personalization, targeting, and strategic decision-making.
Design and maintain interactive dashboards (preferably using Tableau and/or Looker and/or PowerBI), ensuring data accuracy and usability for key stakeholders
Ensure data governance and consistency across global CRM and analytics platforms
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support omnichannel initiatives and clienteling tools, particularly with Data Science,




Core accountabilities:

Update weekly, monthly, and quarterly reports to share with top management, providing insights and recommendations to inform strategic decision
Perform ad hoc customer analyses to support the business in accelerating key customer metrics, particularly retention and purchase frequency
Partner with CRM and Marketing teams to measure One to One and One to Many Campaign effectiveness, looking at Global and Regional performance
Support regional teams by providing training on CRM tools, dashboards, and best practices, as well as performing ad hoc analyses to help them drive local customer engagement




Experience:

4+ years of experience in CRM Analytics, Data Analytics, or related roles, ideally within the fashion, retail, or luxury sector
Strong proficiency in Databricks (SQL, Python, or PySpark) for data transformation and analysis
Solid understanding of CRM principles, customer segmentation, and campaign measurement.
Experience working with customer databases, CDPs, and marketing automation systems (e.g., Salesforce, Braze)
Proven experience creating and maintaining reports and dashboards in Tableau and Looker
Strong analytical mindset and attention to detail, with the ability to interpret complex data into clear business insights
Excellent communication skills and stakeholder management, with the ability to present findings to non-technical audiences
A passion for fashion, luxury, and customer experience

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
About our Team: LexisNexis is a data and analytics company with 10,500 colleagues serving customers in more than 150 countries. We’re one of the largest information and analytics companies on the planet. We design solutions that help our customers increase productivity, improve decision-making and outcomes, and be more successful.




About the role: The senior data analyst consults with internal stakeholders to clarify business problems, gather requirements, and collect, analyze, and interpret data to support data-driven decisions. This role develops advanced insights and recommendations within their domain and uses analytics tools to curate data, build models, create visualizations, and communicate findings to business audiences. The Sr. Data Analyst I independently leads high-complexity analytics projects and mentors junior analysts.




Responsibilities

Partner with stakeholders to understand business needs and identify the most relevant analyses, KPIs, and success measures.
Support stakeholders in setting appropriate goals and defining measurable outcomes aligned to business objectives.
Prepare and transform data using advanced blending, refinement, and quality techniques (including large/complex datasets).
Apply statistical methods and intermediate data models/scenarios to evaluate trends, drivers, and potential outcomes.
Create clear, compelling data visualizations and narratives tailored to the target audience.
Lead and execute analytics projects independently, including scoping, planning, and managing deliverables to deadlines.
Serve as a subject matter expert within an assigned domain and coach/mentor junior team members.
Develop working knowledge of adjacent disciplines and how they influence analytics needs and interpretation.
Leverage approved AI tools to accelerate analysis and deliverables (e.g., drafting analyses, summarizing findings, generating code/queries, documenting work) while maintaining accountability for accuracy and quality.
Develops data products that meets the data consumer where they are in their data literacy journey (e.g. chatbot/genie for metrics questions, reusable dashboards).




Requirements:

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Analytics, Data Science, Mathematics, or a related field; or equivalent practical experience.
Significant experience in analytics, reporting, or data science-adjacent roles.
Ability to understand complex data structures and apply advanced data preparation, blending, refinement, and quality techniques (including big data).
Experience applying intermediate statistics to business problems.
Significant experience leveraging SQL and Python for data querying, collection, transformation, and analysis.
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau and/or Power BI; ability to design dashboards and narratives for business audiences.
Familiarity with advanced analytics/data platforms (e.g., Databricks or equivalent).
Strong project execution skills: able to structure work into modular tasks, manage multiple projects in parallel, communicate progress, and resolve blockers.
Ability to craft effective prompts, iterate toward higher-quality outputs, and incorporate domain context and constraints.







Work in a way that works for you

We promote a healthy work/life balance across the organisation. We offer an appealing working prospect for our people. With numerous wellbeing initiatives, shared parental leave, study assistance and sabbaticals, we will help you meet your immediate responsibilities and your long-term goals.

Working flexible hours - flexing the times when you work in the day to help you fit everything in and work when you are the most productive




Working for you

We know that your wellbeing and happiness are key to a long and successful career. These are some of the benefits we are delighted to offer:

Generous holiday allowance with the option to buy additional days
Health screening, eye care vouchers and private medical benefits
Wellbeing programs
Life assurance
Access to a competitive contributory pension scheme
Save As You Earn share option scheme
Travel Season ticket loan
Electric Vehicle Scheme
Optional Dental Insurance
Maternity, paternity and shared parental leave
Employee Assistance Programme
Access to emergency care for both the elderly and children
RECARES days, giving you time to support the charities and causes that matter to you
Access to employee resource groups with dedicated time to volunteer
Access to extensive learning and development resources
Access to employee discounts scheme via Perks at Work




About the Business

LexisNexis Legal & Professional are a leading global provider of legal and regulatory intelligence. We give organisations the business information and analytics they need to make better, more impactful decisions. We are building powerful new decision tools that use machine learning, natural language processing, visualisation and artificial intelligence that aid and enhance decision making.

  • Significant experience in analytics, reporting, or data science-adjacent roles.
  • Ability to understand complex data structures and apply advanced data preparation, blending, refinement, and quality techniques (including big data).
  • Experience applying intermediate statistics to business problems.
  • Significant experience leveraging SQL and Python for data querying, collection, transformation, and analysis.
  • Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau and/or Power BI; ability to design dashboards and narratives for business audiences.
  • Familiarity with advanced analytics/data platforms (e.g., Databricks or equivalent).
  • Strong project execution skills: able to structure work into modular tasks, manage multiple projects in parallel, communicate progress, and resolve blockers.
  • Ability to craft effective prompts, iterate toward higher-quality outputs, and incorporate domain context and constraints.



Work in a way that works for you

We promote a healthy work/life balance across the organisation. We offer an appealing working prospect for our people. With numerous wellbeing initiatives, shared parental leave, study assistance and sabbaticals, we will help you meet your immediate responsibilities and your long-term goals.

  • Working flexible hours - flexing the times when you work in the day to help you fit everything in and work when you are the most productive


Working for you

We know that your wellbeing and happiness are key to a long and successful career. These are some of the benefits we are delighted to offer:

  • Generous holiday allowance with the option to buy additional days
  • Health screening, eye care vouchers and private medical benefits
  • Wellbeing programs
  • Life assurance
  • Access to a competitive contributory pension scheme
  • Save As You Earn share option scheme
  • Travel Season ticket loan
  • Electric Vehicle Scheme
  • Optional Dental Insurance
  • Maternity, paternity and shared parental leave
  • Employee Assistance Programme
  • Access to emergency care for both the elderly and children
  • RECARES days, giving you time to support the charities and causes that matter to you
  • Access to employee resource groups with dedicated time to volunteer
  • Access to extensive learning and development resources
  • Access to employee discounts scheme via Perks at Work


About the Business

LexisNexis Legal & Professional are a leading global provider of legal and regulatory intelligence. We give organisations the business information and analytics they need to make better, more impactful decisions. We are building powerful new decision tools that use machine learning, natural language processing, visualisation and artificial intelligence that aid and enhance decision making.

Title: Data & Analytics Analyst
Place: Kingston Upon Thames, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Manpower are currently seeking an interim Data & Analytics Analyst to work with our global FMCG client The Magnum Ice Cream Company, renowned for brands such as Magnum, Ben & Jerry's, Cornetto and Wall's, and become an integral part of their fast-paced FMCG environment.




The position is based in Kingston upon Thames, Surrey. This is a full-time temporary role to run for 6 months, requiring 36.25 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Compensation for this role is competitive.




The role currently offers a mix of remote and onsite working, subject to adjustment based on business requirements.







Data & Analytics Analyst – Wholesaler Sales

The Magnum Ice Cream Company is on a mission to create the ultimate snacking company. A place where growth, innovation, founder and ownership mentality, and performance are cultivated and rewarded. And where we craft extraordinary ice cream experiences - transforming ordinary moments into lasting memories. Because we know, life tastes better with ice cream.




ABOUT THE MAGNUM ICE CREAM COMPANY

The Magnum Ice Cream Company is all about growth. Growing our business. Growing our customers’ businesses. Growing our people’s careers. Growth begins with empowerment. So, we free our people to be innovative, responsible entrepreneurs, driven and equipped to give our consumers more amazing products and unforgettable moments – and having fun doing it. With 19,000 expert ice cream colleagues and iconic brands like Wall’s, Cornetto and Ben & Jerry’s, loved in 76 countries, we are the world’s largest Ice Cream company leading the industry.




Position Overview

We’re looking for a Data & Analytics Analyst to unlock the value of wholesaler and customer sales data across Magnum’s UK business.




You will play a critical role in connecting multiple wholesaler data sources, working with Databricks and pipelines, and transforming raw sales data into clear, actionable insights. This includes enabling visibility on sell-in, sell-out, distribution, range, and performance across wholesale partners.




You will work closely with data engineers to ensure data is ingested, structured, and ready for analysis, while acting as a Power BI super user to deliver dashboards that drive commercial decisions across sales, account management, and operations.




Key Responsibilities

Connect & Integrate Wholesaler Data

Connect and consolidate multiple wholesaler sales data sources (including but not limited to EPOS, shipment/sell-in, CRM, partner data)
Work with Databricks to query, combine, and transform large-scale sales datasets
Connect to and leverage data pipelines to access reliable, up-to-date wholesaler data
Partner with data engineers to onboard new wholesale partners and data feeds
Ensure consistent, accurate mapping of SKU, customer, and channel data




Data Ingestion & Pipeline Collaboration

Support and oversee data ingestion processes for wholesaler and partner data
Work closely with data engineers to bring in new data sources and improve data availability
Validate and sense-check incoming data to ensure accuracy and completeness
Help ensure data pipelines deliver timely, structured, analysis-ready datasets

Data Visualisation & Commercial Insights

Act as a Power BI super user, building dashboards focused on wholesale performance
Deliver reporting on key commercial metrics (e.g. sales trends, distribution, range compliance, customer performance)
Create intuitive data visualisations for sales and account teams
Translate complex sales data into clear insights and actions

Make Data Usable for the Business

Develop clean, structured datasets to support sales, CRM, and commercial reporting
Contribute to a single source of truth for wholesaler and customer data
Ensure consistency in definitions (e.g. sales metrics, customer views, SKUs)
Enable self-serve analytics for commercial teams

Set Us Up for Success

Support scalable solutions that accommodate new wholesalers and growing data complexity
Contribute to data documentation, definitions, and data dictionaries
Ensure high standards of data quality, governance, and trust
Identify opportunities to improve data coverage, accuracy, and reporting

Business Partnering & Opportunity Identification

Work closely with sales, account managers, and CRM teams to answer key business questions
Identify trends and opportunities within wholesaler performance and distribution
Proactively highlight risks and opportunities (e.g. gaps in distribution, declining accounts, growth pockets)
Translate business needs into analytical solutions




Required Skills

Advanced Power BI expertise (data modelling, DAX, dashboard delivery)
Experience working with Databricks desirable (data querying, transformation)
Strong SQL skills desirable and ability to work with large, complex sales datasets
Experience with data pipelines and ingestion processes
Proven ability to connect and combine multiple data sources
Experience working with sales, wholesaler, or FMCG data is highly desirable
Strong understanding of commercial metrics and reporting
Experience collaborating with data engineers and non-technical stakeholders
Commercially minded with strong curiosity for sales performance drivers
Detail-oriented with a strong focus on data accuracy and consistency
Proactive and solutions-driven
Ability to manage conflicting priorities and short deadlines
Strong communicator, able to translate data into actionable business insight




Success in This Role Looks Like

Seamless integration of wholesaler sales data across multiple partners
Trusted, high-quality datasets supporting commercial decisions
Widely adopted Power BI dashboards across sales and account teams
Strong collaboration between analytics, engineering, and commercial teams
Clear visibility of wholesaler performance, risks, and growth opportunities

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
💻 Job Title: Data Analyst

💰 Salary: up to £55-60k

📍 Location: Chancery Lane (3 office days/week)

💵 Industry: FS / AI




This AI intelligence / research company is building the global benchmark for AI adoption in banking and insurance. Backed by top-tier tech investors and covered by the New York Times, they're scaling fast - and their data team is at the core of everything they do. They're now looking for a Data Analyst, ready to take their career to the next level 📊




In this role, you will work closely with various stakeholders across the business to build out self-serve data assets, automate index processes and deliver high quality datasets. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of dealing with business stakeholders, working with SQL/Python and have a high attention to detail. This is a fantastic opportunity to join a fast growing team and be involved in all aspects of data from engineering to analysis.




✅ Must have requirements:

Proficiency in SQL or Python (both would be ideal)
Experience building and maintaining analysis ready datasets and automated data workflows
High attention to detail especially in terms of data quality and governance
Great communication skills and stakeholder management skills
Genuine interest in AI - ideally showcased by professional or side projects




👍 Bonus points for:

Experience with dashboarding and BI tools (Tableau, Looker, PowerBI, Omni etc.)
Familiarity with cloud infrastructure (GCP preferred)
Experience working in Financial Services or consulting
Google Apps Script / Javascript
Working in small, fast-paced and scaling environments




🛂 Please note: unfortunately, this role does not offer VISA sponsorship.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Summary




The Senior Data Analyst supports the organisation's objective of enhancing supervisory effectiveness through better use of entity and regulatory data. The role helps transform how Supervision teams identify risks, prioritise interventions and monitor firms, delivered through data-driven insights, dashboards, analytical tools and supervisory intelligence. It sits within a live testing programme for a regulatory data reporting regime. The analyst contributes to systems acceptance, firm feasibility and supervisory usability of the data being collected. The outcome is greater confidence in the regulatory data build through faster, more thorough testing.




Key Responsibilities




Supporting Supervisory Outcomes: Analyse risk indicators, alerts and supervisory insights to enable risk-based supervision and decision making.
Regulatory Data Analysis: Analyse authorisation, holdings, transactions, legal entity and regulatory reporting data. Create integrated views across firms and entities so supervisors see a coherent picture rather than fragmented sources.
Supervisory Intelligence and Risk Analytics: Identify trends, anomalies, concentrations and emerging risks. Support thematic reviews, quantitative investigations and synthesis of alerts.
Data Quality and Improvement: Assess data quality, implement validation controls and validate legal entity identifiers (LEIs). Enrich core datasets using external sources such as GLEIF, Companies House and market data proxies.
Reporting and Decision Support: Develop Tableau dashboards, management information and self-service analytics for Supervision teams, moving towards reusable, automated reporting.
Business Analysis and Stakeholder Engagement: Gather requirements, facilitate workshops and translate supervisory challenges into analytical solutions. Cohere disparate and conflicting business needs into workable plans through engagement and governance forums.
Future State Capability: Support the move towards automated reporting, advanced analytics, continuous monitoring and early-warning indicators, building a mature supervisory intelligence capability.




Essential Skills Technical




SQL and large-scale data analysis across complex, multi-source datasets.
AWS services such as S3, DynamoDB, Athena, etc.
Data visualisation and dashboarding in Tableau or Power BI.
Data quality assessment, validation and improvement.
Stakeholder management across business and technical audiences.
Regulatory or financial services experience.







Behavioral (must-have)




Highly proactive and self-driven — sets direction and momentum without close supervision.
Strategically astute — operates across different business areas, building knowledge and relationships rapidly.
Adaptable — pivots to address new challenges at short notice as contexts change.
Data-fluent — experience driving data-focused projects with a solid grasp of common data challenges in large organisations.
Coheres competing needs — reconciles disparate business requirements into deliverable plans via engagement, workshops and governance.




Desirable Skills




Familiarity with regulatory or financial data reporting regimes.




Eligibility Criteria




UK Citizen & SC Cleared
3 days mandatory work from London Client Site.










Why Join Us?

We’re a purpose‑driven organisation committed to creating positive impact and championing inclusion.
You’ll join a culture that encourages innovation, collaboration, and continuous growth.
We celebrate difference and believe diverse perspectives lead to stronger solutions.
Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply — great talent comes from a variety of backgrounds.
Your lived experience, unique strengths, and personal story matter — they help us design better outcomes for the communities we serve.
We celebrate diversity of experience, knowledge, backgrounds, and perspectives, and believe these differences enable us to create meaningful impact. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to fairness and inclusion for all, regardless of sex, race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy or related conditions, or any other protected characteristic. If you require any reasonable adjustments or additional support during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Hey! We're Huel, and we're pleased to meet you 👋

Our culture thrives on high performance, and we set the bar high for new Hueligans who join us.

We invite you to read the advert below for our new Data Analyst position to understand the role, and explore our 7 Huel values before applying to ensure we have the right working environment for you!




The role

Huel is looking for a Data Analyst to turn raw behavioural and commercial data into clear, actionable insights for Marketing, Trading, Product, and Retail teams. You’ll build measurement frameworks, run causal analyses, and create self-serve dashboards, helping the business move fast and make confident, data-driven decisions.




Key responsibilities include:

Owning end-to-end analysis – translate open-ended questions from Marketing, Trading, Product, and Retail into clear analytical briefs, run the analysis, and land unambiguous recommendations.
Designing & evaluating experiments – design A/B tests and causal-inference studies (lift analysis, hold-out tests) and communicate results in plain language.
Defining measurement frameworks – set the right KPIs and establish how new initiatives will be measured, ensuring every launch has a clear success metric and data plan from day one.
Scale our data foundations – team up with Engineering to design efficient, accurate data sets and schemas, define requirements for new data feeds, and automate the checks that keep everything running smoothly.
Championing self-serve analytics – develop and maintain live dashboards, document datasets, and run training sessions so non-analysts can answer routine questions without waiting in a queue.
AI & Automation - Champion the adoption of AI-powered analytics, using AI tools to enhance analysis, automate routine activities, uncover deeper insights, and improve productivity, while enabling colleagues to self-serve trusted data and answer business questions independently.
Spotting growth opportunities – combine behavioural and marketing data with commercial metrics to size new products, markets, and campaigns, prioritising by impact and effort.




This role is based at our HQ in Tring, Hertfordshire. We operate a hybrid working model, with office days on Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays, and the flexibility to work from home on Wednesdays and Fridays.




Not yet sure if this is the right role for you? Click HERE to read a more detailed job description.




What we're looking for in you

We’re looking for a confident, curious analyst who’s comfortable working with statistics, behavioural data, and experiment design. You can move seamlessly between SQL, product analytics tools, and storytelling through slides. You’re proactive, independent, and always looking for high-impact opportunities to raise the bar on how we use data. In a fast-paced environment, you adapt quickly and communicate clearly - collaborating with both technical and non-technical teams to get things done.

SQL – you can write quick exploratory queries or optimise a multi-table join on Snowflake without breaking a sweat.
Product analytics – comfortable in platforms such as Heap, Amplitude or Mixpanel and happy to set up events, funnels and cohorts from scratch.
Statistical – solid on A/B testing, experiment design and the basics of causal inference; you know when a result is worth acting on.
Measurement mindset – you’ve built tracking plans and KPI frameworks that let teams see what “good” looks like before they ship.
Data - experience modelling data in dbt (or similar), automating checks and keeping feeds clean and accurate as they scale.




And as always, we’re on the lookout for new Hueligans who love a fast-paced environment and are always striving to be their best. We want people who aren’t afraid to think differently and push boundaries. If you’re all about setting big goals and believe that amazing things happen through creativity and teamwork, you’ll fit right in at Huel!




What do we offer in return?

We know that at times, our teams face demanding pressures, and exceptional effort deserves meaningful rewards. That's why we've created a world-class perks and benefits program designed to support our Hueligans in achieving their best, both professionally and personally, while celebrating the global impact they're making!




🕖 Hybrid working - We spend Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays in the office together. The remaining two days have the option to be worked from home.

🌴 30 days annual leave PLUS bank holidays

🥤 Free Huel to keep you going

🏖️ 2 weeks a year to work remotely from anywhere!

🐾 Dog friendly

🙋 Paid Volunteering Days

🏋️ Free on-site gym with free classes, and we will give you your own nutrition plan

🧘 Free on-site wellness area offering infrared saunas and an ice bath🧘

🧠 Free 1-on-1 therapy provided by Self-Space.

🏥 Private Medical and Health insurance for you and your loved ones, including free life insurance covering up to 4x your salary

⚡ Electric Car Scheme with onsite charging

🚴Cycle to Work Scheme

🤰 Enhanced Family Leave

👪 Workplace Nursery Scheme

🎓 Huel Academy

💸 Paid Employee Referral Scheme

🎉 Biannual events to celebrate success - Have you heard about Huelchella?




We are Hueligans

We know that diversity isn't just important; it’s essential, and it makes us stronger. We're all about embracing our differences, celebrating what makes us unique, and bringing together Hueligans from all walks of life.

Whilst we all share the 7 values of Huel, it’s our individual differences that truly enhance our culture of belonging. We seek out Hueligans from around the world, encouraging authenticity, diverse views, and fresh ideas to create products that our global customers love.

Meet our teams here.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Company Description

TripBooka.com is building the platform that helps independent travel advisors compete with the biggest names in travel.




For too long, independent advisors have relied on word of mouth, social media and fragmented tools to grow their businesses, while large online travel companies have dominated through technology and marketing spend. We believe travellers deserve more choice, better service and holidays designed by real experts who genuinely care about their clients.




TripBooka combines marketplace technology, workflow automation and AI to help independent travel advisors attract new customers, manage bookings, build lasting customer relationships and grow their businesses, all from one platform.




We're still at the beginning of our journey, making this an exciting opportunity to join an ambitious startup and help shape the future of independent travel.




Role Description

We're looking for our first permanent Data Analyst to join TripBooka.




This is a rare opportunity to join an early-stage startup and help build our data capability from the ground up. You'll work directly with the founders and collaborate with Product, Engineering, Marketing and Customer Success to turn data into meaningful insights that drive better decisions.




Whether it's understanding customer behaviour, measuring feature adoption, identifying growth opportunities or improving the customer experience, your work will directly influence the future of TripBooka.




This is a full-time remote role for candidates based in the UK. While you'll primarily work remotely, we'd love someone who enjoys getting together with the team in Newcastle upon Tyne from time to time for planning sessions, collaboration and team meetups.




Responsibilities
Analyse product, customer and business data to uncover meaningful insights.
Build reports and dashboards that help the team make informed decisions.
Monitor key business and product metrics.
Identify trends, opportunities and areas for improvement.
Work closely with Product, Engineering, Marketing and Customer Success to answer important business questions.
Help measure the success of new features and initiatives.
Present findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Recommend improvements based on data and customer behaviour.




Qualifications

We're more interested in your curiosity, analytical thinking and problem-solving ability than whether you've used a particular tool or worked in a specific industry.




You'll likely have:

Strong analytical skills with the ability to explore data, identify patterns and turn findings into meaningful insights.
An understanding of statistics and how data can be used to support decision-making, experimentation and performance measurement.
The ability to communicate complex ideas clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Experience organising, analysing or modelling data to answer business questions.
A high level of accuracy, attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities while working independently in a remote environment.




You may also have experience with:

SQL.
Python, R or another analytics or programming language.
Dashboard and visualisation tools such as Power BI, Tableau or Looker Studio.
Data modelling, reporting or business intelligence.
Marketplace, SaaS, e-commerce or travel industry data.




A degree in Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science or another quantitative discipline is beneficial, but it's not essential. We also welcome candidates who have developed their analytical skills through work experience, personal projects, bootcamps or self-directed learning.




If this role excites you, we'd encourage you to apply even if you don't meet every qualification. We'd much rather speak to someone with strong potential and a passion for solving problems than miss out on a great candidate because they don't tick every box.




Why Apply?
Be TripBooka's first permanent Data Analyst and help build our data capability from the ground up.
Work directly with the founders and influence product and business decisions.
Turn data into insights that genuinely improve the experience for customers.
Work across Product, Engineering, Marketing and Customer Success, gaining exposure to every part of the business.
Join an ambitious startup using AI and technology to transform independent travel.
Competitive salary, equity package and significant opportunities for career progression as TripBooka grows.




If you're looking for an opportunity to make a genuine impact in a fast-growing technology startup, where your ideas matter and your work directly influences the future of the business, we'd love to hear from you.

Hybrid | Central Bristol 3x Per Week

£40,000–£50,000 + Bonus + Industry Leading Benefits




We're excited to be recruiting exclusively for a Data Analyst to join a startup-style team within a global, data-driven organisation. Think fast-moving, well-funded, plenty of autonomy, and a collaborative culture that's committed to building innovative data products.

Strong SQL skills, with Python for automation and data analysis
Experience in data validation, QA and working with financial data
Excellent analytical skills and attention to detail
Confident working with both technical teams and customers

Title: APP Data Analyst
Description:
Job description:

INTRODUCTION

At Burberry, we believe creativity opens spaces. Our purpose is to unlock the power of imagination to push boundaries and open new possibilities for our people, our customers and our communities. This is the core belief that has guided Burberry since it was founded in 1856 and is central to how we operate as a company today.

We aim to provide an environment for creative minds from different backgrounds to thrive, bringing a wide range of skills and experiences to everything we do. As a purposeful, values-driven brand, we are committed to being a force for good in the world as well, creating the next generation of sustainable luxury for customers, driving industry change and championing our communities.

JOB PURPOSE

To provide data analytics and gap analysis of key areas of compliance in the business that would leave us exposed from a risk perspective if not highlighted. This reporting is to be provided across multiple departments within the business.

RESPONSIBILITIES

 

Maintain, run and improve automated KPI and trend analysis reporting and flagging relevant issues to key stakeholders.
Continue to build a suite of reports to highlight exceptions and/or breaches in key areas utilising both SAP and IntelliQ.
Supporting regional APP with evidence gathering during investigations.
System Maintenance to include: monitoring daily data loads and clearing of load errors; weekly gap analysis; regular updates of key data streams from HR, product and finance to ensure reporting is accurate and up to date. Troubleshooting as required to fix issues.
Continue to build a Sharepoint site for the APP team to provide training materials and support for using the system. 
Maintenance of the APP (Asset and Profit Protection) Operations database to include keeping store master and HR data up to date.
Supporting the wider regional team with a growing list of daily, weekly and monthly reports including :
Store KPI Reporting, Adjustment Reporting, Incident Reporting, Digital Fraud Reporting, AML Reporting, Retail Operations Exception Reporting.
Support the team with biannual stock loss reporting to include data cleansing, performing analysis on required areas of concerns and creating store level posters.
Provide ad hoc support to Digital Fraud team with chargebacks.
PERSONAL PROFILE

Essential

Previous Experience with IntelliQ or other data mining systems (This is a mandatory requirement for the role)
Advanced Office 365 Suite, in particular Excel
Data minded
Experience in retail fraud prevention 
Retail chargeback processing
Experience with SQL
Experience with Microsoft Access and Power BI
Previous experience with SAP
Experience using Power Platform
MEASURES OF SUCCESS FOOTER

Burberry is an Equal Opportunities Employer and as such, treats all applications equally and recruits purely on the basis of skills and experience.

 

Posting Notes: United Kingdom || Not Applicable || London || CORPORATE AFFAIRS || CORPORATE - ASSET & PROFIT PROTECTION || n/a ||

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
VOY is a fast growing Healthtech scale-up, aiming to be the healthcare provider that millions of people trust as their partner to a long and healthy life. Our platform allows users to quickly and safely diagnose possible conditions, get treatment and support them afterwards to live a higher quality life. To do this we are building an all in one healthcare platform which brings together world leading medical expertise and technical excellence.

We are looking for a talented Data Analyst to join our growing Data team and help drive smarter, data-informed decisions across the organisation.

The Opportunity

This is a high-impact role where the insights you surface and the dashboards you build directly influence how our teams operate and how well we support our patients.

Who you are


You have strong SQL skills and can write queries confidently to extract, transform and analyse data
You're comfortable with data visualisation tools and understand how to design dashboards that are intuitive and actionable
You think carefully about data -what metrics mean, what the data is actually telling you, and how to communicate it clearly
You're curious and self-driven, comfortable exploring datasets to uncover insights and answer difficult questions
You're a natural communicator who can translate analytical findings into clear, actionable recommendations
You collaborate well with both technical and non-technical stakeholders and take ownership end-to-end
You're excited about working in a fast-paced environment and motivated by the idea that your work helps improve patient outcomes at scale


What you will do

Translate data into insight


Conduct ad-hoc analyses to answer business questions from stakeholders across Operations, Finance, Clinical and Programme teams
Identify trends, patterns and anomalies in operational and clinical data that impact service delivery and patient outcomes
Present findings in clear, compelling ways that drive decision-making across the organisation


Build and maintain dashboards & reports


Design and build dashboards in Looker that track key metrics across the business - from programme delivery KPIs to financial performance
Develop automated reports that keep stakeholders informed without requiring manual updates
Ensure dashboards are accurate, performant and easy to navigate for end users


Contribute to data quality & modelling


Write clear, optimised SQL to query and manipulate data reliably
Collaborate with the Data Engineering team on data modelling, dbt transformations and data governance
Identify and escalate data quality issues and help ensure consistency across systems
Help define and track metrics that matter, ensuring every KPI is well understood and correctly calculated


Partner across teams


Work closely with stakeholders across Operations, Clinical, Finance and Programme Delivery to understand their data needs
Communicate complex data concepts in a way that's accessible to non-technical stakeholders
Help drive adoption of data-driven thinking and self-serve analytics across the organisation


What we need from you


2-4 years of experience in a data analyst, business analyst or similar analytics role
Proficiency in SQL - you can write queries to explore, filter, join and aggregate data reliably
Experience with at least one BI/visualisation tool (Looker, Tableau, Power BI, or similar)
Familiarity with modern data stacks and tools (BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, dbt are all helpful)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with an eye for detail
Excellent communication skills - able to explain complex findings clearly to diverse audiences
This is a hybrid role requiring 3 days per week in the office


Experience you may have


A degree in a quantitative subject (Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, etc.)
Experience in healthcare, public health or regulated industries
Exposure to Python or R for data analysis and statistical work
Experience working in fast-growing, high-impact environments
Familiarity with dbt or other data transformation frameworks


Please note that this is a hybrid role with a requirement of in office presence, 3 days per week

Healthcare is broken. We’re fixing it — and we need your help.

Back in 2019, we launched MANUAL to tackle men's health (starting with hair loss and sexual health). A few years later, we launched Voy with a focus on weight loss. But our ambition has always been bigger. Healthcare too often treats conditions in isolation, not people as a whole, and we're on a mission to prove there's a better way.

In 2025, we evolved from MANUAL into Voy, a brand built for the long game: supporting people through every stage of life, not just a single diagnosis. Today, we support over 1 million members across the UK, Germany and Brazil, delivering personalised care across weight loss, menopause, hair loss and TRT (with more categories on the way).

Ready to join us and change how healthcare feels for millions of people?

  • Familiarity with modern data stacks and tools (BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, dbt are all helpful)
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with an eye for detail
  • Excellent communication skills - able to explain complex findings clearly to diverse audiences
  • This is a hybrid role requiring 3 days per week in the office

Experience you may have

  • A degree in a quantitative subject (Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, etc.)
  • Experience in healthcare, public health or regulated industries
  • Exposure to Python or R for data analysis and statistical work
  • Experience working in fast-growing, high-impact environments
  • Familiarity with dbt or other data transformation frameworks

Please note that this is a hybrid role with a requirement of in office presence, 3 days per week

Healthcare is broken. We’re fixing it — and we need your help.

Back in 2019, we launched MANUAL to tackle men's health (starting with hair loss and sexual health). A few years later, we launched Voy with a focus on weight loss. But our ambition has always been bigger. Healthcare too often treats conditions in isolation, not people as a whole, and we're on a mission to prove there's a better way.

In 2025, we evolved from MANUAL into Voy, a brand built for the long game: supporting people through every stage of life, not just a single diagnosis. Today, we support over 1 million members across the UK, Germany and Brazil, delivering personalised care across weight loss, menopause, hair loss and TRT (with more categories on the way).

Ready to join us and change how healthcare feels for millions of people?

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
The business is Europe's leading live entertainment platform, owning over 80 festivals including major rock, electronic, and Gen Z-focused events. With F&B playing a huge part in the overall revenue.




Working directly alongside the F&B Strategy Lead, the Data Analyst will help build the evidence base that will shape the company's F&B strategy for the next 5 years.




The Data Analyst will work with messy, live event data from multiple systems and help turn it into clear commercial recommendations.




***This is a 6 months FTC role***




What You Will Actually Do




Hands-on Revenue Analysis

Go beyond top-line revenue. Analyse product mix, per-outlet performance, and site-level variances.
Answer questions like: Why did Bar A outperform Bar B? Was it location, queue times, pricing, product range, or staffing?
Identify the underlying drivers of performance – not just what happened, but why.




Working with Large, Messy Datasets

Pull sales, volume, and margin data from Square POS across multiple festivals and venues – often inconsistent, incomplete, or differently formatted.
Clean, structure, and build insight layers on top of imperfect operational data.
Investigate why "all data is not in one plan" and help build a single source of truth in PowerBI.




Comparative Operating Model Analysis

Model the financial and operational performance of in-house F&B vs outsourced partners (major contract caterers).
Compare good examples vs poor examples within the company's own network.
Benchmark national team performance across different countries – not just totals, but efficiency, throughput, and margin drivers.
You will own the data appendix behind that recommendation – every chart, every driver analysis, every unit economics assumption.




Must-Haves (Non-Negotiable):

Hands-on analysis of revenue streams – you have looked at F&B, product mix, or site-level performance, not just top-line totals.
Evidence of identifying drivers of performance – you can point to a time you figured out why something performed well or poorly, not just reported the number.
Experience working with large / messy datasets – you have built insight layers on top of imperfect operational data.




Nice to Have (But Not Essential):

Experience in live events, festivals, stadiums, or high-volume hospitality.
Familiarity with Square POS or similar EPOS systems.
Basic SQL or Python for ad-hoc data pulls.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Graduate / Early Career Data Analyst




📍Guildford (Hybrid – office & home working)




You’ll be joining a European team focused on improving how we understand and manage supply chain performance. This is a hands-on data role where your work directly supports decision-making across Finance, Technology, and Supply Chain.




In this role, you’ll take ownership of sourcing, validating, and structuring data from multiple international partners. You’ll play a key part in turning complex, fragmented information into clean, reliable datasets that support reporting, forecasting, and business performance insight.




This role would suit someone who enjoys detail, problem-solving, and working with data at scale, particularly someone looking to build their experience and develop a career in data within a global business environment.




✅ What you’ll be doing

Collecting and extracting data from multiple internal and external sources
Loading and transforming datasets into structured formats for analysis
Validating data quality and working with stakeholders to resolve inconsistencies
Maintaining and setting up master data (products, brands, suppliers, locations)
Researching and enriching missing data attributes (e.g. pack size, manufacturer)
Performing data cleansing and quality assurance to ensure accuracy
Preparing datasets for reporting and financial analysis
Building and running reports using SQL to support business insights
Collaborating with European teams across supply chain, finance, and technology




🔎 What we’re looking for

Experience working with data in an analytical or operational environment (academic or professional)
Strong Excel skills and confidence handling large datasets
Working knowledge of SQL for reporting and data extraction
Strong attention to detail and a methodical approach to problem-solving
Clear communication skills in English and ability to work across teams




⭐ Nice to have

Additional European language skills (Spanish is particularly valuable)
Experience working with supply chain or procurement data




🤝 What we offer

A role with real ownership and visibility across European operations
A supportive environment to develop and grow your data skills
Exposure to international stakeholders and large-scale supply chain data
Flexible hybrid working and a collaborative team environment




📩 Ready to apply?

If you’re excited by working with data, solving problems, and contributing to a fast-moving international business, we’d love to hear from you.




We’re committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels respected and valued. We welcome applications from all backgrounds and ensure fair and equal opportunities regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, belief, sexual orientation, marital status, or race.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst (People)




Location: Remote / Leeds

Duration: Initial 4.5 month contract

IR35 Status: Outside IR35

Start Date: Immediate

£400/day




About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Data Analyst to transform complex people and operational data into meaningful insights that support business performance, strategic planning, and continuous improvement.




Working closely with HR, business stakeholders, and data teams, you will develop reports, dashboards, and visualisations that enable informed decision-making and drive operational efficiency across the organisation.




Key Responsibilities

Analyse people and operational data to identify trends, risks, opportunities, and areas for improvement.
Develop, maintain, and enhance reports, dashboards, and KPI tracking using tools such as Power BI, Tableau, SQL, and Excel.
Present insights and findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Support business teams in understanding and effectively utilising data.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and adherence to data governance standards.
Collaborate with stakeholders to improve reporting processes and identify opportunities for automation and efficiency gains.




Required Skills & Experience

Proven experience in a Data Analyst or similar analytical role.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and data interpretation skills.
Hands-on experience with reporting and visualisation tools such as Power BI, Tableau, SQL, and Excel.
Ability to communicate complex data concepts in a clear and accessible manner.
Experience working collaboratively with stakeholders across multiple business functions.




Desirable Experience

Experience within transport, logistics, utilities, infrastructure, or other operationally focused industries.
Knowledge of data warehousing concepts and/or statistical analysis techniques.
Relevant qualification in Data Analytics, Statistics, Data Science, Mathematics, or a related discipline.




Success Measures

Delivering accurate, insightful, and trusted reporting.
Producing high-quality dashboards and reports within agreed timescales.
Supporting data-driven decision-making across the organisation.
Building strong stakeholder relationships and enhancing the overall reporting experience.




Contract Overview

This is an excellent opportunity to join a large, customer-focused organisation undergoing significant transformation and investment in its data capabilities. The successful candidate will play a key role in improving reporting, driving insight-led decision-making, and supporting business performance through effective use of data.

About the Role

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join a growing data and insight team. This role is responsible for developing analysis and reporting capabilities, identifying trends, monitoring business performance, and delivering actionable insights that support strategic decision-making.

Working closely with key stakeholders, you will analyse complex datasets, develop dashboards and reports, improve data quality, and help drive business performance through data-led recommendations.




Key Responsibilities

Analyse data to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for business improvement.
Develop, maintain, and enhance dashboards and reports using Power BI.

Build and maintain data sources from multiple systems.
Support stakeholders by providing meaningful analysis and actionable insights.
Define and implement data acquisition and integration processes.
Monitor data quality and recommend process improvements.
Produce clear documentation and present findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Ensure compliance with data protection, governance, and information security requirements.

Essential

Previous experience in a Data Analyst or similar analytical role.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with relational databases.
Experience developing reports and dashboards using Power BI.
Experience with Python, R, XML, JavaScript, or ETL tools.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
High level of accuracy and attention to detail.
Ability to interpret complex data and present findings clearly.
Experience managing multiple priorities and meeting deadlines.




Desirable

Experience within the financial services sector.
Experience in a B2B environment.
Knowledge of data modelling, data mining, and segmentation techniques.
Experience supporting pricing, underwriting, or commercial analytics.
Relevant degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related discipline.

What's on Offer

Hybrid working arrangement.
Opportunity to work with large and varied datasets.
Collaborative and supportive team environment.
Exposure to a wide range of business stakeholders.
Opportunities for professional development and career progression.




If you are passionate about using data to solve business problems and enjoy turning complex information into meaningful insights, we'd love to hear from you.

  • Foundational knowledge of SQL for querying databases.
  • Familiarity with visualization software like Tableau or Power BI.
  • Strong communication skills to explain findings to stakeholders.

What We Offer

  • Direct mentorship from seasoned data professionals.
  • Access to modern data stacks and corporate tools.
  • Clear pathways to advance into senior analytical roles.
  • Competitive compensation, health benefits, and flexible hours.

Title: Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a strong self-starter with a genuine passion for data and a drive to produce results. The right candidate will thrive in a fast-paced environment, where priorities can shift quickly, and will demonstrate the self-motivation and intellectual curiosity needed to continuously learn and develop within a specialist, high-performing team.




Above all, the successful candidate must embody Lockton's core principles:




Client first
Results and impact driven
Self-motivated
Continuous improvement




Role Purpose

The LPS Analyst will report to the LPS Data Lead, providing hands-on analytical and data support across LPS's monoline facilities and wider Lockton portfolio solutions initiatives. The primary focus of the role will be to:




Continue building and maintaining the data infrastructure underpinning Lockton Portfolio Solutions monoline facilities.
Support opportunity scoping through the interrogation and modelling of premium and claims data.
Support the Data Lead in the preparation of high-quality data packs and submissions for insurance markets.
Build and maintain reporting and dashboards to monitor the performance of LPS facilities.
Provide analytical support across a range of LPS strategic initiatives.




Key Responsibilities

Monoline Facilities – Data & Analytics

Extract, interrogate and analyse data to support the Portfolio solutions Lead in internal scoping discussions with relevant stakeholders, using BI and analytics to identify opportunities and support the PS Lead.
Produce Meaningful BI and reporting to assess the profitability and scope of monoline facility opportunities.
Continue to develop and build on the data infrastructure for the monoline facility initiative




Market Submissions & Engagement

Work with Data Lead to produce professional data packs for submission to market in Excel, PowerPoint and PDF, including a range of analytical exhibits.
Respond to ad-hoc data requests from markets in a timely and accurate manner.
Support data lead on maintaining robust reporting on key facility metrics, ideally delivered via interactive dashboards, including:
Indexation: percentage of in-scope business placed versus not placed.
Premium and claims performance by team and risk code.
Aggregate accumulations versus limits and modelled loss estimates versus limits (using RMS / AIR outputs).
Ensure reporting remains current and reliable as facilities grow and data sources evolve.




Must-Have Skills & Experience

End-to-end development of Power BI reports, including ETL, data modelling, DAX expression writing and report creation.
Proficiency in SQL development and good practices in query writing and optimisation.
Good understanding of relational databases and data architecture.
Advanced Excel, including Power Query and Power Pivot.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear insights.
Strong communication skills — able to present findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.




Desirable Skills & Experience

Experience working in the insurance or financial services sector.
Familiarity with London Market data processes, including BDX, Xchanging or Vipr.
Experience working with large, multi-source datasets and building data pipelines.
Knowledge of Python for data analysis and automation.
Experience building automated reporting solutions or data workflows.
Hands-on experience using Databricks for analytics and reporting workflows

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Business Intelligence Analyst

Kilmarnock, Ayrshire | Full-Time | Permanent




We're looking for a commercially minded Business Intelligence Analyst to play a pivotal role in transforming data into meaningful insights that drive business performance. This is an exciting opportunity to join a growing multi-site business where data is at the heart of strategic and operational decision-making.




As part of a significant investment in data, reporting, and analytics capabilities, you'll lead the development of a central data warehouse and help establish a single source of truth across the organisation. Working closely with Finance, Operations, IT, and senior stakeholders, you'll influence how data is captured, managed, and leveraged to support growth.




What You'll Be Doing




Build Strong Data Foundations

Design and develop a central data warehouse integrating multiple business systems.
Create scalable data models and structures to support reporting and analytics.
Improve data quality, governance, and consistency across the organisation.
Work with internal teams and external providers to develop and maintain data pipelines.




Deliver Insight That Drives Performance

Build dashboards and reporting solutions covering key operational and financial metrics.
Analyse trends, risks, and opportunities to support strategic decision-making.
Enable self-service reporting while maintaining robust data governance.
Present complex findings in a clear, actionable way for non-technical stakeholders.




Support Commercial Growth

Partner with Finance and Operations teams to understand business performance.
Support budgeting, forecasting, investment decisions, and expansion projects.
Identify opportunities to improve profitability, efficiency, and customer outcomes through data.




Drive Innovation

Explore AI-enabled analytics, automation, and predictive modelling opportunities.
Support the transition from historical reporting to forward-looking insights.
Help prepare data infrastructure for future AI and advanced analytics initiatives.




What We're Looking For




Essential

Proven experience in a Business Intelligence, Data Analyst, or similar analytics role.
Strong SQL skills and experience working with structured datasets.
Experience designing or working with data warehouses and centralised data models.
Advanced Excel and analytical skills.
Experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualisation tools.
Ability to communicate complex data clearly to a wide range of stakeholders.




Desirable

Experience within hospitality, retail, or other multi-site environments.
Exposure to cloud data platforms such as Azure, AWS, or Snowflake.
Experience working with data from systems such as EPOS, CRM, reservations, payroll, or finance platforms.
Knowledge of forecasting, predictive analytics, or AI-driven solutions.




About You




You are:

Curious and commercially focused.
Comfortable working with ambiguity and evolving priorities.
Passionate about creating structure and improving processes.
Detail-oriented, with the ability to simplify complexity.
Enthusiastic about innovation, automation, and continuous improvement.




Why Join Us?




Opportunity to build and shape a modern BI function.
Significant influence on business-wide decision-making.
Exposure to senior stakeholders and strategic projects.
A forward-thinking environment investing in data, technology, and AI.
Competitive salary and benefits package.




Ready to turn data into impact? Apply today and help build the foundations for smarter business decisions.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Junior Data Analyst

Location: Birmingham Head Office

Working Hours: 9AM-5PM Monday - Friday, Hybrid working in the office 2 x per week

Salary: Highly Competitive + Excellent Benefits Package + Progression Opportunities

About Us

Join 2 Sisters Food Group, one of the UK's largest food manufacturers, with an annual turnover exceeding £3 billion and approximately 13,000 employees across 16 sites. We have a strong presence in poultry, chilled, and bakery food categories. We are committed to delivering high-quality products to the British public and our retail and food service customers, including major retailers like Aldi, Asda, Co-op, KFC, Lidl, Marks & Spencer, Morrisons, Sainsbury's, Tesco, and Waitrose.

About the Role

We're looking for a Junior Data Analyst to join our Group Technical function. This is an excellent opportunity for someone at the beginning of their analytics career who is looking to gain hands-on experience in data management, reporting, systems support and stakeholder engagement within a large, fast-paced organisation.

This role has a strong administration and system support element, with approximately 70% of the role focused on managing, validating and maintaining data and reporting processes, and 30% focused on analysing data and producing insights. You'll receive ongoing training and development, learning from experienced colleagues while building your technical and analytical capability.

Key Responsibilities

Manage and maintain technical and H&S reporting systems, ensuring information remains accurate, up to date and accessible across the business
Perform regular data imports, validation, cleansing and publication activities to maintain data quality and integrity
Produce and distribute weekly, monthly and quarterly KPI reports for Group and Divisional stakeholders
Investigate data anomalies and work with site teams to resolve errors and ensure reporting accuracy
Support site users with system access, account creation, data profile management and reporting queries

About You

We're looking for an organised and technically minded individual with a genuine interest in data and analytics. This role would suit a recent graduate, an individual with some experience in administration or reporting, or someone looking to develop their career in a junior data-focused position.

You'll be comfortable handling large amounts of information, have excellent attention to detail and enjoy solving problems. Just as importantly, you'll be able to communicate clearly and confidently with colleagues across the business, explaining information and processes in a way that is easy to understand.

Key Skills & Experience

Strong attention to detail
Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex information clearly to different audiences
Strong organisational and planning skills
Strong Excel skills and confidence working with data
Previous experience in an administration, reporting, data or analytical role would be advantageous but is not essential

Benefits

33 days annual leave inclusive of the bank holidays
Refer a friend scheme
Health Assured Employee Assistance Programme and Grocery Aid are free and confidential support services 365 days a year
Boparan Restaurant Group Discount - 50% discount for friends and family

Why Join Us?

As one of the UK's largest food manufacturers, we provide excellent opportunities for career growth and development. This role offers hands-on exposure to systems, reporting and analytics across multiple business functions, with the opportunity to learn from experienced colleagues and develop into a more advanced analytical position over time. We're committed to promoting from within and helping our people build rewarding long-term careers with us.
