Title: Data Analyst - Trust
Description:
At Trustpilot, we're on an incredible journey. We're a profitable, high-growth FTSE-250 company with a big vision: to become the universal symbol of trust. We run the world's largest open customer review platform, and while we've come a long way, there's still so much exciting work to do. Come join us at the heart of trust!

Trustpilot’s growth has been exponential - from a successful IPO to building out new teams, we never stop working hard to fulfill our mission to create better experiences for everyone through our platform. But to do that, we need data! That’s where Analytics comes in - you’ll help us measure, uncover insights, and predict, to get data everywhere in Trustpilot. We’ve built a world-class analytics team that spans two continents and four cities - are you ready to join a fun, diverse global team?

 

Trustpilot is on a mission to create better experiences for everyone through our platform. But to do that, we need to protect the integrity of our community and deeply understand how our consumers perceive trust. We’ve built a world-class analytics team that spans continents and cities - and now, we’re looking for a data analyst to support our regulatory duty and protect trust for our platform and consumers.

 

Trust

This team is the guardian of our platform. They use data to stay one step ahead of bad actors, working with the teams that build the models and detection systems that identify fake reviews and ensure Trustpilot remains a universal symbol of integrity.

 

It is a huge part of what we do at Trustpilot - it’s in the name! We need to ensure that the content on our platform is real content from real people. That means eliminating scam and spam, and identifying fraudulent behavior. We have several cross functional teams devoted to Trust. You’ll be tightly aligned to their goals, helping teams such as Content Integrity, Legal & Compliance, Corporate Comms & Investor Relations, Fraud & Investigations to investigate requests on fraudulent activity, prepare various reports including core publicly shared company and regulatory reports.

 

As a Data Analyst at Trustpilot, you will collaborate with Trusties in our Trust, Comms and Marketing teams and spend your time digging through our data to provide them with data-driven insights, as well as analytics and dashboards/reporting. You’re keen to help them use data to answer their toughest questions, and you can proactively identify areas that need a little data support. You’ll be an internal data consultant, with loads of amazing data in our data warehouse at your fingertips, working with analytical tools including BigQuery and Looker.  

 

What you’ll be doing:

Be the main contact point for all regulatory and compliance reporting
Delivering expert analytics and insights to different parts of the business, through both workstream-driven initiatives and ad-hoc inquiries
Building data sets and dashboards that support our Trust data integrity and auditability requirements
Picking the right solution for the problem you’ve got, from your arsenal of analytical tools and methods
Working closely with the Comms, Investor Relations, Marketing, Compliance or Trust teams to understand their data requests and their goals to deliver awesome solutions
Presenting and promoting your work to the wider business, to enable data-informed decision making
Scoping and documenting projects
Standardising and automating key reports ensuring integrity, governance and data quality

 

Who you are:

Knowledge of reporting best practices and experience delivering actionable insights leveraging BI tools (we use Looker)
Experience in stakeholder and project management, with the confidence to deliver results back to the business
Advanced SQL skills (we use BigQuery)
Data modelling experience: DBT or similar tools used for building data pipelines
Experience with Python is desirable 
Familiarity of key analytical and statistical methodologies, such as model-based analyses (regression, clustering etc.), cohort analysis 
Familiarity with Airflow 
Familiarity with A/B testing
Outstanding written and verbal communication skills

About us

Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever — to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Trustpilot is open, independent, and impartial — we help consumers make the right choices and businesses to build trust, grow and improve.

Today, we have more than 350 million reviews and 60 million monthly active users across the globe, with 149 billion annual Trustpilot brand impressions, and the numbers keep growing. We have more than 1000 employees and we’re headquartered in Copenhagen, with operations in  Amsterdam, Denver, Edinburgh, Hamburg, London, Melbourne, Milan and New York. 

We’re driven by connection. It’s at the heart of what we do. Our culture keeps things fresh –– it’s built on the relationships we create. We talk, we laugh, we collaborate and we respect each other. We work across borders and cultures to be the universal symbol of trust in an ever-changing world. With vibrant office locations worldwide and over 50 nationalities, we’re proud to be an equal opportunity workplace with diverse perspectives and ideas. 

Our purpose to help people and businesses help each other is a tall order, but we keep it real. We’re a great bunch of humans, doing awesome stuff, without fuss or pretense. A successful Trustpilot future is driven by you –– we give you the autonomy to shape a career you can be proud of. If you’re ready to grow, let’s go.

Join us at the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is committed to creating an inclusive environment where people from all backgrounds can thrive and where different viewpoints and experiences are valued and respected. Trustpilot will consider all applications for employment without regard to race, ethnicity, national origin, religious beliefs, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, neurodiversity, disability, age, parental or veteran status. Together, we are the heart of trust.

Trustpilot is a global company and our data practices are designed to ensure that your personally identifiable information is appropriately protected. Please note that your personal information will be transferred, accessed, and stored globally as necessary for the uses and disclosures stated in our Privacy Policy.

We run the world's largest open customer review platform, and while we've come a long way, there's still so much exciting work to do. Come join us at the heart of trust!

Trustpilot’s growth has been exponential - from a successful IPO to building out new teams, we never stop working hard to fulfill our mission to create better experiences for everyone through our platform. But to do that, we need data! That’s where Analytics comes in - you’ll help us measure, uncover insights, and predict, to get data everywhere in Trustpilot. We’ve built a world-class analytics team that spans two continents and four cities - are you ready to join a fun, diverse global team?

 

Trustpilot is on a mission to create better experiences for everyone through our platform. But to do that, we need to protect the integrity of our community and deeply understand how our consumers perceive trust. We’ve built a world-class analytics team that spans continents and cities - and now, we’re looking for a data analyst to support our regulatory duty and protect trust for our platform and consumers.

 

Trust

This team is the guardian of our platform. They use data to stay one step ahead of bad actors, working with the teams that build the models and detection systems that identify fake reviews and ensure Trustpilot remains a universal symbol of integrity.

 

It is a huge part of what we do at Trustpilot - it’s in the name! We need to ensure that the content on our platform is real content from real people. That means eliminating scam and spam, and identifying fraudulent behavior. We have several cross functional teams devoted to Trust. You’ll be tightly aligned to their goals, helping teams such as Content Integrity, Legal & Compliance, Corporate Comms & Investor Relations, Fraud & Investigations to investigate requests on fraudulent activity, prepare various reports including core publicly shared company and regulatory reports.

 

As a Data Analyst at Trustpilot, you will collaborate with Trusties in our Trust, Comms and Marketing teams and spend your time digging through our data to provide them with data-driven insights, as well as analytics and dashboards/reporting. You’re keen to help them use data to answer their toughest questions, and you can proactively identify areas that need a little data support. You’ll be an internal data consultant, with loads of amazing data in our data warehouse at your fingertips, working with analytical tools including BigQuery and Looker.  

 

What you’ll be doing:

  • Be the main contact point for all regulatory and compliance reporting
  • Delivering expert analytics and insights to different parts of the business, through both workstream-driven initiatives and ad-hoc inquiries
  • Building data sets and dashboards that support our Trust data integrity and auditability requirements
  • Picking the right solution for the problem you’ve got, from your arsenal of analytical tools and methods

  • Presenting and promoting your work to the wider business, to enable data-informed decision making
  • Scoping and documenting projects
  • Standardising and automating key reports ensuring integrity, governance and data quality

 

Who you are:

  • Knowledge of reporting best practices and experience delivering actionable insights leveraging BI tools (we use Looker)
  • Experience in stakeholder and project management, with the confidence to deliver results back to the business
  • Advanced SQL skills (we use BigQuery)
  • Data modelling experience: DBT or similar tools used for building data pipelines
  • Experience with Python is desirable 
  • Familiarity of key analytical and statistical methodologies, such as model-based analyses (regression, clustering etc.), cohort analysis 
  • Familiarity with Airflow 
  • Familiarity with A/B testing
  • Outstanding written and verbal communication skills

Trustpilot began in 2007 with a simple yet powerful idea that is more relevant today than ever — to be the universal symbol of trust, bringing consumers and businesses together through reviews. Title: Data Analyst
Description:
A global Insurance company is looking to expand their teams in London. Our client is currently looking for a passionate Data Analyst with an interest in financial services to realize their own vision on how data analytics should be used, this is about bringing unique ideas to the table, not working to a specification.




There is no better time for an ambitious Data Analyst to join a company which leads digital transformations for clients by modernizing and integrating their mainstream IT, and by deploying digital solutions at scale to produce better business outcomes.




The successful applicant will support the development of client analytics, research, and data‑driven insights that inform tehri management strategy. You will conduct detailed quantitative analysis of internal and external data to understand client behavior, insurance programs, market positioning, and peer comparisons




Requirements




· Proven hands-on experience within Data analytics

· A strong experience Reporting and modelling ideally MI/Client intelligence

· Ideally needs to be a strategic thinker

· Must have strong stakeholder management experience

· Must have right to work in the UK




Salary & Package: up to £55,000 per year + variety of other benefits

Location: London 4 days in office required

Role: Permanent




Lawrence Harvey is a specialist recruiter, operating across 8 offices globally. We have several other Data analytics vacancies so please get in touch.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: City Of London, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Data Analyst – London




5-6 Month Contract




Up to £525 per day | Outside IR35




Hybrid Working – 1-2 Days Per Week Onsite




We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join an exciting transformation programme focused on evolving how the organisation uses data to drive better business decisions.

This role will play a key part in enabling self-service analytics by leveraging Looker to create semantic layers across core datasets. These semantic models will support Looker AI Agents (Looker Conversation), Large Language Models (LLMs) and automated push notifications, giving business users greater flexibility to explore data, receive proactive insights and accelerate decision-making.

A significant focus of this role will be working with LLM-powered AI solutions, reviewing large and complex datasets, validating AI-generated outputs and ensuring that insights delivered to the business are accurate, reliable and fit for purpose. You'll be responsible for maintaining rigorous quality assurance standards and will have the confidence to sign off AI-generated outputs before they are released to the business.

Working closely with business stakeholders, data engineering teams and analytics specialists, you'll help shape how AI, semantic modelling and trusted data are used across the organisation.




Key skills and responsibilities,

Looker expertise – Proven hands-on experience developing and working within the Looker platform, including semantic layer creation and optimisation.
Looker AI Agents – Experience working with or supporting AI Agents within Looker (Looker Conversation), helping to optimise semantic models and improve AI-generated responses.
Automated Push Notifications – Experience building or supporting automated push notifications and proactive insight delivery using data-driven workflows.
LLM & AI experience – Strong experience working with Large Language Models (LLMs), AI-powered analytics or conversational AI solutions, ensuring outputs are accurate, relevant and trusted.
Large Dataset Analysis – Extensive experience analysing, interrogating and validating large, complex datasets, identifying anomalies and ensuring data integrity across AI-driven solutions.
Semantic Layer Development – Collaborate with business domain experts to design, build and optimise semantic layers, working closely with Data Engineering to improve performance and scalability.
Quality Assurance – Significant experience testing, validating and quality assuring data products and AI-generated outputs, with exceptional attention to detail and a critical eye for accuracy.
Results Ownership – Confident taking accountability for data quality and formally signing off whether AI-generated insights meet business and delivery standards.
AI & Machine Learning Lifecycle – Previous experience supporting AI/ML initiatives and AI agents from development through to deployment, with an understanding of model validation and continuous improvement.
SQL Proficiency – Strong working knowledge of SQL for analysing, interrogating and validating large datasets.
Stakeholder Engagement – Partner with business stakeholders to understand key business questions and translate requirements into trusted analytical models.
Adaptability – Comfortable working in an agile environment where priorities evolve, AI capabilities mature and continuous learning is encouraged.




This is an exciting opportunity to work at the forefront of AI-enabled analytics, helping shape how the organisation leverages LLMs, Looker AI Agents, semantic modelling and automated push notifications to transform how data is consumed and trusted across the business.




Interested? Please submit your updated CV to Dean Sadler-Parkes at Harvey Nash for immediate consideration.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Workplace: White City, London - Hybrid

Data Analyst, Central Insights

We are seeking a Data Analyst to join the ITV Insight Group’s Central Insights team.

Central Insights plays a crucial role in shaping ITV’s understanding of the UK television market, evolving viewer behaviours and future opportunities in an exciting, highly competitive and increasingly complex media landscape. Our work helps the business understand how viewers engage with ITV’s content across broadcast, VOD and SVOD, and supports key decisions across content, scheduling, marketing, product, strategy and performance planning.

As part of a cross-functional team, you will partner with Insight Managers, Data Scientists, Data Engineers, Analysts and business stakeholders to understand their challenges and help answer the “big questions”. You will use data to create clear, accurate and actionable insight, helping stakeholders make better decisions and supporting ITV’s wider strategic objectives.

As a Data Analyst, you will be responsible for delivering analytical requirements for your business area, working with the wider team to produce high-quality analysis, dashboards, reports and recommendations. You will be encouraged to sharpen your technical skills, think critically about the questions being asked, and communicate insight in a way that is clear, trusted and impactful.

The team

ITV is on a mission to Supercharge Streaming and Optimise Broadcast with data and insight right at the centre of our digital transformation strategy.

The business faces many challenges ahead to achieve this vision: What content should we commission? Which audiences are the most commercially attractive for us? What should our marketing strategy be? How do we optimise our product to improve view through rates? How do we use VOD (Video on Demand) to offset declines to Linear TV?

Whatever the question, ITV Insights is here to lead the business to its most successful future by putting insight with integrity, ingenuity, and impact, at the heart of every key decision.

Responsibilities:


Analysing audience behaviours and providing insight on our viewers to drive business value.
Provision of accurate and insightful analysis on how viewers engage with and consume the nation’s best-loved and most-watched content across broadcast, VOD and SVOD.
Communicating analytics and insight to business stakeholders in a non-technical, strategically-minded and impactful manner.
Creating analysis, dashboards and reports that help stakeholders understand performance against key business KPIs.
Supporting proof of concept analysis for new metrics, methodologies, segmentations or analytical approaches.
As part of a cross-functional team, developing and embedding dashboarding solutions for business stakeholders, enabling a culture of self-service analytics.
Evaluating the performance of content, release strategies, marketing activity and product experiments, and supporting recommendations for future tests and next steps.
Working with large and complex datasets to identify trends, opportunities and risks in viewer behaviour and content performance.
Supporting forecasting and performance analysis across key business KPIs.
Collaborating with subject matter experts in a range of disciplines, including Insight Managers, Data Scientists, Analysts and business stakeholders, to drive projects forward.
Helping to embed analytical best practice across the team, including clear documentation, reproducible analysis and high-quality standards in analytical delivery.


Skills you’ll need (minimum criteria)


Proven experience (or similar exposure) within a data analytics role, with the ability to turn complex data into meaningful insights
Ability to create insightful analysis and dashboards.
Comfortable visualising and presenting analysis across a range of formats, including reports, PowerPoint, dashboards and email.
Experience using SQL to analyse large datasets.
Understanding of analytical techniques and statistical concepts.
Experience using Python for analytics, data transformation or modelling.
Strong attention to detail and a drive to produce accurate, trusted and well-documented analysis.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to tell clear and impactful stories with data.
Ability to work collaboratively with analysts, insight specialists and business stakeholders.
A curious and critical mindset, with the ability to question assumptions and understand the limitations of analysis.


Other things we’re looking for (key criteria)


A passion for TV programmes and an interest in the wider media landscape.
Experience working with cloud-based platforms such as GCP, BigQuery, AWS, Redshift or Databricks.
Experience working with data visualisation tools such as Tableau or Looker Studio.
Experience of working with first and third party data, ideally in a TV, media, streaming, product, marketing or customer analytics environment.
Experience using Jupyter notebooks is preferable.
Experience of forecasting or predictive modelling in a business analytics context is preferable.
Keen problem solver who can demonstrate critical thinking, coupled with strong communication skills.
Interest in improving insight capability through automation, self-serve analytics, documentation or new ways of working.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

We are champions of rail, inspired to build a greener, more sustainable future of travel. Trainline enables millions of travellers to find and book the best value tickets across carriers, fares, and journey options through our highly rated mobile app, website, and B2B partner channels.

Great journeys start with Trainline 🚄

Now Europe’s number 1 downloaded rail app, with over 135 million monthly visits and £6.3 billion in annual ticket sales, we collaborate with 270+ rail and coach companies in over 40 countries. We want to create a world where travel is as simple, seamless, eco-friendly and affordable as it should be.

Today, we're a FTSE 250 company driven by our incredible team of over 1,000 Trainliners from 50+ nationalities, based across London, Paris, Barcelona, Milan, Edinburgh and Madrid. With our focus on growth in the UK and Europe, now is the perfect time to join us on this high-speed journey.

Introducing the Trainline Data Analytics Team 👋

Data Analytics is central to how we build better products, support our customers and grow our business. Our Data Analysts are embedded across cross-functional teams spanning product, commercial and industry initiatives, working closely with Product Managers, Software Engineers, Commercial teams, Embedded Data Scientists and the wider Data organisation to enable confident, evidence-based decision making.

As a Data Analyst in the Travel & Disruption team, you will help us build a better travel proposition for our customers. You'll use data to understand how customers engage with our travel features throughout their journey, helping teams make better decisions that improve the travel experience. Whether customers are enjoying a seamless journey or navigating unexpected disruption, your insights will help shape products and services that make rail travel easier, more reliable and more rewarding for millions of customers.

In this role as the Data Analyst, you will...🚄


Analyse customer behaviour in relation to our travel products.
Translate complex analysis into clear, compelling insights and practical recommendations for technical and non-technical audiences.
Produce high-quality analytical deliverables, dashboards, reports and presentations that help senior stakeholders and external partners make informed decisions.
Work closely with Data Scientists to structure analytical approaches, interpret findings and draw meaningful conclusions from large and complex datasets.
Collaborate across Data, Product and Design to ensure insight is embedded into decision making from early exploration through to delivery.
Help define how we measure success in the Travel space.
Contribute to a growing Data organisation that includes Data Science, Machine Learning, Data Engineering, Business Intelligence and Data Product Management.


We'd love to hear from you if you have...🔍


Experience using analytics to support business decisions, answer complex commercial questions and identify opportunities for improvement.
Strong SQL skills and confidence working with large datasets to produce accurate, reliable and well-structured analysis.
The ability to structure analytical work clearly, navigate ambiguity and turn complex findings into actionable recommendations.
Experience communicating insight to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders and colleagues from technical and non-technical backgrounds.
Strong data visualisation skills, ideally using tools such as Tableau, Power BI or similar.
A thoughtful approach to storytelling, with the ability to create polished deliverables that make insight easy to understand and act on.
Strong organisational skills, with the ability to manage multiple analytical work streams and balance competing priorities.
Ideally, experience with DBT or modern data transformation workflows, and exposure to analysing experiments, pilots or trials.


More Information

Enjoy fantastic perks like private healthcare & dental insurance, a generous work from abroad policy, 2-for-1 share purchase plans, an EV Scheme to further reduce carbon emissions, extra festive time off, and excellent family-friendly benefits.

We prioritise career growth with clear career paths, transparent pay bands, personal learning budgets, and regular learning days. Jump on board and supercharge your career from day one!

We're operating a hybrid model and ask that Trainliners work from the office a minimum of 60% of their time over a 12-week period. We also have a 28-day Work from Abroad policy.

Our Values Represent The Things That Matter Most To Us And What We Live And Breathe Everyday, In Everything We Do


💭 Think Big - We're building the future of rail
✔️ Own It - We focus on every customer, partner and journey
🤝 Travel Together - We're one team
♻️ Do Good - We make a positive impact


We know that having a diverse team makes us better and helps us succeed. And we mean all forms of diversity - gender, ethnicity, sexuality, disability, nationality and diversity of thought. That's why we're committed to creating inclusive places to work, where everyone belongs and differences are valued and celebrated.

Interested in finding out more about what it's like to work at Trainline? Why not check us out on LinkedIn, Instagram and Glassdoor!

More Information

Enjoy fantastic perks like private healthcare & dental insurance, a generous work from abroad policy, 2-for-1 share purchase plans, an EV Scheme to further reduce carbon emissions, extra festive time off, and excellent family-friendly benefits.

We prioritise career growth with clear career paths, transparent pay bands, personal learning budgets, and regular learning days. Jump on board and supercharge your career from day one!

We're operating a hybrid model and ask that Trainliners work from the office a minimum of 60% of their time over a 12-week period. We also have a 28-day Work from Abroad policy.

Our Values Represent The Things That Matter Most To Us And What We Live And Breathe Everyday, In Everything We Do

  • 💭 Think Big - We're building the future of rail
  • ✔️ Own It - We focus on every customer, partner and journey
  • 🤝 Travel Together - We're one team
  • ♻️ Do Good - We make a positive impact

We know that having a diverse team makes us better and helps us succeed. And we mean all forms of diversity - gender, ethnicity, sexuality, disability, nationality and diversity of thought. That's why we're committed to creating inclusive places to work, where everyone belongs and differences are valued and celebrated.

Interested in finding out more about what it's like to work at Trainline? Why not check us out on LinkedIn, Instagram and Glassdoor!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

North West or London, Hybrid

Up to £60,000




Looking for a role where your analysis drives business decisions? Want to join a business where you'll build reporting from the ground up, work with senior stakeholders, and influence how data is used across the organisation? This is an opportunity to become one of the first key hires within a growing Data team, working directly with the Head of Pricing.




You'll help shape the company's reporting capability, build new dashboards, uncover valuable insights and become a trusted partner to teams across the business. This isn't a role where you'll spend your days refreshing the same reports. You'll help build the function.




What you'll be doing as their Data Analyst:

• Design and build dashboards that support business performance.

• Analyse large datasets to identify trends and opportunities.

• Work with stakeholders across pricing, marketing and operations.

• Develop reports that influence commercial decisions.

• Write and optimise SQL queries.

• Use Python to automate analysis and improve reporting.

• Work with Databricks as the business continues to expand its data platform.

• Help improve data quality and develop new analytical capabilities.




Key skills:

• Commercial experience as a Data Analyst.

• Strong SQL skills and experience using Python for analysis or automation.

• Dashboard development experience using Power BI, Tableau or similar.

• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

• Someone who enjoys taking ownership and working directly with stakeholders.

• A proactive mindset with the ability to learn new technologies quickly.

• Insurance or financial services experience is desirable

• Databricks experience is nice to have but not essential

• Experience working with cloud-based data platforms (preferable Azure)




Salary up to 60K, Hybrid working (1-2 days onsite per week), Clear career progression as the team grows, Flexible hybrid working with offices in London and the North West, and much more!




This is an ideal opportunity for someone who wants more responsibility, more ownership and the chance to build something from the beginning instead of maintaining existing reports. Apply today!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst - London - Up to £50K



Hybrid working - 3 days a week onsite

SQL | Power BI | Snowflake

Are you a Data Analyst with strong SQL and Power BI skills looking to make a real impact?

We're partnering with an organisation that's investing heavily in its data capabilities and is looking for a talented Data Analyst to play a key role in transforming data into meaningful business insight.

This isn't just another reporting role. We're looking for someone with excellent data fundamentals-someone who understands how data is structured, modelled and transformed before bringing it to life through intuitive Power BI dashboards. You'll work across the full dashboard development lifecycle, partnering with stakeholders to turn complex business requirements into impactful reporting solutions that drive better decision-making.



The Role



You'll be responsible for:

Designing and developing engaging Power BI dashboards from concept through to deployment.
Gathering requirements, creating wireframes and delivering reporting that answers real business questions.
Writing and optimising SQL queries to extract, manipulate and prepare data.
Analysing complex datasets to identify trends, opportunities and actionable insights.
Working alongside Data Engineers to improve data pipelines and support scalable data models.
Ensuring data quality, accuracy and governance across reporting solutions.
Building strong relationships with stakeholders and acting as a trusted partner for data and analytics.
We're keen to speak with Data Analysts who have a solid understanding of core data principles and enjoy solving business problems through data.

Essential skills include:
Strong SQL skills.
Proven experience building dashboards in Power BI.
Excellent understanding of data fundamentals, including data modelling, data preparation and data quality.
Experience translating business requirements into reporting and dashboard solutions.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Nice to have (but not essential):

Snowflake experience.
Python or R.
Microsoft Azure.

Please apply asap if intersted. GleeIT

Title: Claims Data Analyst
Description:
About Zego



At Zego, we understand that traditional motor insurance holds good drivers back. It's too complicated, too expensive, and it doesn't reflect how well you actually drive. Since 2016, we have been on a mission to change that by offering the lowest priced insurance for good drivers.

From van drivers and gig workers to everyday car drivers, our customers are the driving force behind everything we do. We've sold tens of millions of policies and raised over $200 million in funding and picked up numerous awards, including the Insurance Claims Team Of The Year 2024 as awarded at The Insurance Post's Claims & Fraud Awards. And we're only just getting started.

Purpose of the role



We're looking for a proactive and analytical Claims Data Analyst to help us enhance our Claims and Fraud operations, shaping one of the most vital areas of the insurance industry.

As part of a fast-growing, product-led tech company, you'll play a key role in driving impactful change within our rapidly scaling Claims team. Your insights will power strategic decision-making, improve efficiency, and uncover opportunities for continuous improvement. Expect to work on developing insightful reports and dashboards, collaborating closely with Claims and Fraud teams, and delivering data-backed recommendations to leadership and stakeholders across the business.

If you thrive in a fast-paced, data-centric environment and are eager to make a real difference in the evolution of claims operations, we'd love to hear from you!

What you will be doing


Design & build core data models and reporting to support Claims and Fraud operations
Develop key reports for senior stakeholders, including Zego leadership, investors, and insurance partners
Analyze performance & identify trends, uncovering key drivers to optimise processes
Spot and size opportunities for continuous improvement, tracking the impact of initiatives
Support strategic decision-making for the Claims and Fraud leadership team
Translate data into compelling stories—delivering actionable insights to senior management
Collaborate cross-functionally, supporting Claims, Fraud, Pricing, and external reporting needs
What you will need to be successful


Got a degree? Great! If not, equivalent experience works too (bonus points if it's in a STEM subject)
SQL Skills - You know your way around data extraction and manipulation like a pro
Power BI Expertise - You've built data models, reports, and dashboards before and can turn raw numbers into something meaningful
Data Storytelling - You can take complex insights and turn them into clear, engaging stories that senior stakeholders (think senior managers, investors, Zego's board, and insurance partners) actually understand
Insurance Know-How (Nice to have!) - If you understand the motor insurance market and what drives commercial performance, that's a big plus
Claims & Financial Acumen (Nice to have!) - Familiarity with claims processes, reserving, and settlements? Even better
Actuarial & Reserving Knowledge (Nice to have!) - If you can be the go-to person between Claims, Fraud, and Actuarial teams, we'd love to have you on board!
What's it like to work at Zego?



Joining Zego is a career-defining move. People go further here, reaching their full potential to achieve extraordinary things. 

We're spread throughout the UK and Europe, and united by our drive to get things done. We're proud of our company and our culture - a friendly and inclusive space where we can lift each other up and celebrate our wins every day.

Together, we're setting the bar higher, delivering exceptional work that makes a difference. Our people are the most important part of our story, and everyone here plays a role. There's loads of room to learn and grow, and you'll get the freedom to steer your career wherever you want.

You'll work alongside a talented group who embrace each other's differences and aren't afraid of a challenge. We recognise our achievements, learn from our mistakes, and help each other to be the best we can be. Together, we're making insurance matter. 

Our approach to AI



We believe in the power of AI to meaningfully improve how we work - helping us move faster, think differently, and focus on what matters most. At Zego, we encourage people to stay curious and intentional about how AI is leveraged in their work and teams to drive practical impact every day. This is your chance to do the most meaningful work of your career - and we'll provide you with the tools, support, and freedom to do it well.

How we work



We believe that teams work better when they have time to collaborate and space to get things done. We call it Zego Hybrid.

Our hybrid way of working is unique. We foster a flexible approach that empowers every Zegon to perform at their best. We ask you to spend at least one day a week in our central London office (next to Liverpool Street). We cover the costs for all company-wide events (3 per year), and also provide a separate hybrid contribution to help pay towards other travel costs. We think it's a good mix of collaborative face time and flexible home-working, setting us up to achieve the right balance between work and life.

Benefits



We reward our people well. Join us and you'll get a market-competitive salary, private medical insurance, company share options, generous holiday allowance, and a whole lot of wellbeing benefits. And that's just for starters.

Title: Data Analyst (SQL & Python)
Place: Brighton And Hove, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Legal & General (L&G) is a leading UK financial services group and major global investor.



We’ve been safeguarding people’s financial futures since 1836, and strive to build a better society, while improving the lives of our customers and creating value for shareholders.



We are one of the world’s largest asset managers and provide powerful asset origination capabilities. Together, these underpin our retirement and protection solutions: we are an international player in pension risk transfer, in UK and US life insurance, and in UK workplace pensions and retirement income.



Our Group Functions provide the services that all areas of the business need. This requires a talented and diverse team behind the scenes, who enable everyone at L&G to do what they do best. 



Joining us means helping to improve the lives of our customers and contributing to the success of the business every day.





Job Description



We are seeking a skilled Data Analyst to support the GDFP IFRS 17 reporting platform, focusing on the development, maintenance, and enhancement of Tableau dashboards and data models within a highly controlled reporting environment.



The role will lead the migration of Tableau workbooks and data sources as Snowflake platforms are re‑engineered and moved to a new server, including controlled updates to semantic models to reflect back‑end changes. A strong emphasis will be placed on testing, reconciliation, and documented assurance to ensure reports align with governed data and reporting requirements.



You will work within established SDLC and change processes, producing required artefacts, and contribute to the maturation of Tableau development practices, including the introduction of CI/CD using Git/GitHub and Python‑based Tableau tooling.



What you’ll be doing:



Designing, developing, and maintaining Tableau dashboards, data sources, and extracts on the GDFP IFRS 17 platform, ensuring outputs are accurate, performant, and aligned to governed reporting standards
Delivering Tableau migration activities as Snowflake data sources are re‑engineered and migrated to a new server, including updating connections and applying controlled changes to semantic and data models to reflect back‑end updates
Contributing to the advancement of Tableau development practices by helping implement stronger SDLC controls, including version control (Git/GitHub), CI/CD pipelines, and deployment automation using Python‑based Tableau tooling and agreed engineering standards
Planning and executing comprehensive testing and reconciliation activities to validate Tableau outputs against back‑end data, producing clear, auditable evidence and required artefacts such as test scripts, reconciliation results, and Test Completion Reports
Collaborating closely with the wider IFRS 17 platform teams, including data engineering, QA, governance, and business stakeholders, to gather requirements, triage issues, and deliver change through defined processes and timelines
Providing business‑as‑usual support for Tableau dashboards and data sources, including monitoring, incident and defect investigation, performance optimisation, and timely resolution in line with operational expectations
Using SQL (Snowflake) and Tableau best practices to design and maintain robust datasets and data models, including joins, aggregations, and calculations, to enable consistent reporting and scalable dashboard development
Operating effectively within a controlled and audited environment by adhering to governance and security standards, maintaining comprehensive documentation and artefacts (e.g. Change Requests, Implementation Plans, runbooks, user guides), and ensuring all changes are traceable, peer‑reviewed, and released through approved processes

Who we’re looking for:



A degree in a technical or numerate discipline, a Level 4 apprenticeship in a relevant field, or equivalent professional experience, with a focus on data analysis and visualisation
Strong experience working with high‑volume datasets, demonstrating excellent attention to detail through robust data testing, reconciliation, and clear evidencing of results
Proven expertise in developing and maintaining high‑quality Tableau dashboards, including workbooks, data sources, calculations, extracts, performance optimisation, and best‑practice visual design
Experience designing and maintaining reporting datasets and data models (e.g. star or semantic layers) using SQL, with the ability to adapt models as upstream Snowflake data sources evolve
Demonstrated ability to improve and automate analytics and reporting delivery processes, including repeatable deployments, standardised testing, and reusable components, to reduce manual effort and operational risk
Strong technical capability across SQL (ideally Snowflake) and advanced Tableau features; experience with Python, Git/GitHub, CI/CD concepts, or data preparation tools (e.g. Alteryx) is advantageous
Comfortable working across multiple datasets and environments in a controlled and governed delivery model, collaborating effectively with data engineering and business stakeholders
A disciplined, engineering‑focused mindset, able to manage workload independently while contributing proactively to standards, peer reviews, testing, documentation, and continuous improvement

Whatever your role, we reward performance and behaviour with a package that looks after all the things that are important to you. Here are some of the benefits we offer:



The opportunity to participate in our annual, performance -related bonus plan and valuable share schemes
Generous pension contribution
Life assurance
Healthcare Plan (permanent employees only)
At least 25 days holiday, plus public holidays, 26 days after 2 years’ service. There’s also the option to buy and sell holiday
Competitive family leave
Participate in our electric car scheme, which offers employees the option to hire a brand-new electric car through tax efficient salary sacrifice (permanent employees only)
There are the many discounts we offer – both for our own products and at a range of high street stores and online
We’re creating net‑zero carbon workplaces by 2030 by investing in our sustainable, modern offices across the UK, all designed to bring people together and elevate the in‑person experience


Additional Information



At L&G, we believe it's possible to generate positive returns today while helping to build a better future for all.



If you join us, you’ll be part of a welcoming, inclusive culture, with opportunities to collaborate with people of diverse backgrounds, views, and experiences. Guided by leaders with integrity who care about your future and wellbeing. Empowered through initiatives which support people to develop their careers and excel.



We care passionately about outcomes rather than attendance and are therefore open to discussing all kinds of flexible working options including part-time, term-time and job shares. Although some roles have limited flexibility due to customer demand, we accommodate requests when we can.



It doesn’t matter if you don’t meet every single criterion in this advert. Instead, think about what you excel at and what else you can bring in terms of strengths, potential and connection to our purpose.

  • The opportunity to participate in our annual, performance -related bonus plan and valuable share schemes
  • Generous pension contribution
  • Life assurance
  • Healthcare Plan (permanent employees only)
  • At least 25 days holiday, plus public holidays, 26 days after 2 years’ service. There’s also the option to buy and sell holiday
  • Competitive family leave
  • Participate in our electric car scheme, which offers employees the option to hire a brand-new electric car through tax efficient salary sacrifice (permanent employees only)
  • There are the many discounts we offer – both for our own products and at a range of high street stores and online
  • We’re creating net‑zero carbon workplaces by 2030 by investing in our sustainable, modern offices across the UK, all designed to bring people together and elevate the in‑person experience

Additional Information

At L&G, we believe it's possible to generate positive returns today while helping to build a better future for all.

If you join us, you’ll be part of a welcoming, inclusive culture, with opportunities to collaborate with people of diverse backgrounds, views, and experiences. Guided by leaders with integrity who care about your future and wellbeing. Empowered through initiatives which support people to develop their careers and excel.

We care passionately about outcomes rather than attendance and are therefore open to discussing all kinds of flexible working options including part-time, term-time and job shares. Although some roles have limited flexibility due to customer demand, we accommodate requests when we can.

It doesn’t matter if you don’t meet every single criterion in this advert. Instead, think about what you excel at and what else you can bring in terms of strengths, potential and connection to our purpose.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
🚀 We're Hiring | Data Quality Analyst

📍 Location: Manchester

💼 Contract Opportunity




Are you passionate about turning messy, unreliable data into trusted business assets?




We're looking for a Data Quality Analyst to play a key role in ensuring data across the organisation is accurate, complete, consistent, and fit for purpose. Experience with data profiling, reconciliation, and quality assessments
Proven track record of root cause analysis and remediation
Understanding of ETL processes and Data Warehouse environments
Experience defining data quality metrics, KPIs, and dashboards
Knowledge of Data Governance and Data Stewardship practices
Exposure to Azure Purview, Collibra, Informatica, or similar tools is advantageous
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills




Desirable:

⭐ Experience working within regulated industries such as Utilities, Energy, Financial Services, Insurance, Healthcare, or Government

If this sounds like your next challenge, I'd be keen to hear from you. Please apply directly or reach out for a confidential discussion.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Senior Data Analyst (Customer Insights) | Banking

Location: Remote with occasional travel to Milton Keynes

Rate: £600 per day (Inside IR35)

Duration: Initial 10-month contract




We're looking for an experienced Senior Data Analyst to join a leading UK financial services organisation on an initial 10-month contract.




This role is ideal for an analyst who thrives on turning complex customer data into meaningful insights that influence business decisions. You'll work closely with stakeholders to identify customer opportunities, build insightful reporting, and provide data-driven recommendations that shape strategy and improve business outcomes.




This is an insight-led analytical role rather than a data engineering or data modelling position.

The Role




You'll be responsible for analysing customer and business data to uncover trends, identify opportunities and deliver clear, actionable recommendations. Working with business stakeholders across the organisation, you'll translate analytical findings into insights that drive customer engagement, commercial performance and strategic decision-making.




Key Responsibilities

Analyse customer and business data to identify trends, opportunities and actionable insights.
Produce high-quality reports, dashboards and presentations that support business decision-making.
Identify and develop customer segmentation strategies to support targeted campaigns and business initiatives.
Partner with business stakeholders to understand challenges and translate requirements into analytical solutions.
Present complex analytical findings in a clear, engaging and commercially meaningful way.
Support continuous improvement of reporting and analytical processes using modern data tools.
Use AI tools to improve analytical productivity and streamline day-to-day workflows where appropriate.




Essential Skills & Experience

Proven experience as a Data Analyst or Insight Analyst delivering business-focused analytical solutions.
Excellent SQL skills for data extraction, manipulation and analysis.
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills.
Strong experience producing business reports, dashboards and analytical insight.
Experience translating data into meaningful recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.
Demonstrable experience identifying customer trends, behaviours or segmentation opportunities.




Desirable Experience

Experience within banking or financial services.
Customer analytics, CRM analytics, marketing analytics or customer insight experience.
Power BI or Tableau dashboard development.
Experience working with cloud-based data platforms such as Snowflake or AWS Athena.
Exposure to organisations transitioning from legacy reporting environments to modern cloud-based analytics platforms.




AI & Modern Ways of Working

We're particularly interested in candidates who actively use AI tools such as ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot or Claude to enhance their day-to-day analytical work. You don't need to build AI solutions—we're looking for someone who embraces modern tools to improve efficiency, automate repetitive tasks and accelerate insight generation.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
The Company




We’re Octopus Legacy: one of the fastest growing tech companies in the country, part of the UK’s most exciting and best-trusted group (you may have heard of some of our sister companies…) and we have a mission.




We’re on a path to scale, aiming to become a household name while transforming a long-established industry and helping our customers prepare for, and find support after, death.




But we can’t do it alone, and that’s why we’re assembling a team of top performers to build with us.




Ready to be part of something big?




The Role

We’re looking for a Data Analyst to join our growing data team at Octopus Legacy. You’ll play a central role in helping teams across the business make smarter, faster, more informed decisions.




While you’ll sit within the data team, your day-to-day will involve working directly with stakeholders from across the organisation such as Growth, Product and Operations. You’ll turn messy real-world data into actionable insights, bring structure to ambiguity, and help the business better understand what drives performance.




This is a hands-on, high-impact role in a fast-moving environment. You’ll need to be comfortable navigating shifting priorities, championing a data culture, and bringing rigour to complexity.




We’re still early in our data journey - right now, the focus is on building the foundations: modelling key metrics, creating dashboards, and getting reliable data into the hands of the people who need it. As our data maturity grows, there’ll be opportunities to go deeper into analysis, experimentation, and more advanced analytics.




Key Responsibilities:

Act as a thought partner to teams across the business - helping them define problems, clarify questions, and uncover the metrics that matter
Translate stakeholder needs into end-to-end data solutions: from raw data to dbt models to dashboards in Metabase
Define and document clear, consistent metrics to support better decision-making across departments
Build intuitive, impactful dashboards that make data accessible and actionable for non-technical users
Deliver ad-hoc and project-based analysis to evaluate initiatives in growth, product, operations, and beyond
Collaborate with the data team to improve the reliability, clarity, and accessibility of our data models and pipelines
Operate with a high degree of autonomy - scoping projects, pushing for clarity, and challenging assumptions when needed
Contribute to a culture of curiosity and rigour, laying the groundwork for more advanced analytics and data science over time




Who Are You?

You have 2+ years of experience in a data analytics role, ideally in a startup or a company building out its data capability
You're proficient in SQL and have worked with BI tools (like Metabase, Looker, or Tableau) to build dashboards that help teams make better decisions
You have hands-on experience with dbt or similar tools, and a solid understanding of data modelling best practices
You’re comfortable working with messy or incomplete data, and know how to turn it into something clean, structured, and useful
You enjoy working with non-technical stakeholders - you ask thoughtful questions, clarify ambiguous requests, and help teams think through what they really need
You take ownership of your work, are comfortable scoping projects with limited direction, and aren’t afraid to challenge assumptions
You’re curious, pragmatic, and excited by the idea of shaping a modern data function from the ground up
Bonus: you have an interest in experimentation or data science, and are keen to grow into more advanced analytics over time




Skills and Knowledge




Must haves:

Strong curiosity and proactive mindset, with the ability to independently explore data, ask the right questions, identify opportunities, and drive projects forward without requiring constant guidance.
Experience in data analytics and the ability to extract actionable insights from complex datasets.
Proficiency with SQL, DBT, python is desirable.
Proven ability to work closely with business stakeholders and translate their needs into data-driven solutions.
Experience in developing reports and dashboards to monitor key metrics.
Strong communication skills, including the ability to present complex data in a way that is understandable and actionable for non-technical stakeholders across departments.
Proficiency with data visualisation tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Metabase).




Nice to haves/we can work on these:

Basic programming skills in languages such as Python.
Understanding of statistical methods and their application to business problems.
Experience working in a startup or fast-paced environment.




Bonus if you:

You have a basic understanding of Data Science concepts and are interested in learning more.
You have experience in training or educating non-technical teams on data literacy.




Our Mission

When people think about planning for death they think about wills, life insurance and funerals. We take these cold processes and turn them into something more human. Share more than money: leave voice notes, music, recipes. Shape a legacy that connects you while you're here. And after you're gone.




Founded by Sam after his mum died suddenly, we’re a group of people who work in death because we’ve been affected by it. We know the difference a good plan makes, and what it’s like when there isn’t one.




Death can come between us, leave mess, legal fees, frustration. But it can also make us stronger. We see a world where people talk openly about death, and work out the real meaning of legacy, one that connects to them. We’re here to make that world happen.




Benefits:

Octopus share incentive scheme.
Hybrid working (London Bridge).
Flexible holiday + extra day off for your Birthday.
Work from anywhere in the world for up to 4 weeks per year.
Vitality Health & Life Insurance.
Pension scheme.
Enhanced parental leave.
Free Will & LPAs + discounts on other Octopus services.
Cycle to Work Scheme and access to the Electric Vehicle (EV) Salary Sacrifice Scheme.
Octopus Giving: we match any charitable fundraising that you do up to £500.
Octopus Springboard: where we help our employees become fully-fledged entrepreneurs.
Dog friendly office.
Breakfast every day, snacks and wellness activities




We know that to be truly innovative, we need to have a diverse team around us. That is why Octopus Legacy is committed to creating an inclusive environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At Smart, our mission is to transform retirement, savings and financial wellbeing, across all generations, around the world.

THE ROLE

As part of the Data Analytics team, you will work closely with stakeholders to translate business challenges into data problems, delivering high-quality and clearly articulated analysis, reporting, and data-driven insights that support effective decision-making. We are a small team with big ambitions. We are building the foundations to enable our vision of maximising the value that our extensive dataset and modern data stack can generate for the business. Because we support the entire business, our data is varied and complex. As such, we are seeking a self-starter who is comfortable navigating ambiguity and independently finding solutions within a fast paced environment. If you match our ambitions, are passionate about creating value from data, and want to help drive the evolution of our data capabilities, then we would love to hear from you.

What You Will Do

Stakeholder Partnership & Communication


Define requirements: Partner with stakeholders across the company to translate business questions into clear analytics requirements and identify the right data points.
Data Translation & Storytelling: Explain analytical outputs and insights to stakeholders in clear, non-technical terms and, as required, build a narrative to support effective decision-making.
Drive data literacy: Support initiatives that enable stakeholders to self-serve data more effectively.


Analytics & Business Intelligence


Build BI solutions: Design, build, and maintain reports and dashboards (primarily using Tableau) used across the organisation, including Board-level KPI reporting.
Data Analysis & Insights: Manipulate and analyse complex data to answer business questions and identify trends, insights, and opportunities.


Data Modeling & Quality Assurance


Build robust data models: Develop and maintain optimised and reliable solutions in our data warehouse (Redshift) using tools like dbt, collaborating closely with Data Engineering where required.
Ensure trust & accuracy: Maintain data quality and reporting reliability, manage the impact of upstream changes, and support business-critical processes with direct regulatory or customer impact.
Peer review: Participate in code reviews and collaborative QA to validate the accuracy and value of our analytical output.


Continuous Improvement & Team Culture


Automate & optimise: Identify opportunities to improve efficiency through automation, better data processes and the responsible use of AI.
Be an active team player: Contribute your ideas to team discussions, retrospectives, and future planning to continuously improve how we work together and support the business.


Who We Are Looking For

The skills, experience, and aptitudes we are looking for are listed below but please don’t be discouraged from applying if you don’t meet every single one of these criteria – having a ‘can do’ attitude is sometimes more important than being able to tick every box:

Experience & Attributes


Proven track record: 3+ years of experience in data analysis, with a focus on data manipulation, reporting and insight analytics.
Stakeholder partnership: A proven ability to work with stakeholders to deeply understand business problems, translate requirements into effective analytics solutions, and advise on best approaches.
Data storytelling & coaching: An effective technical communicator who can translate complex data for non-technical audiences and actively coach stakeholders to enhance overall data maturity.
Analytical curiosity: Inquisitive by nature, with sharp attention to detail and a natural drive to look beyond the surface numbers to uncover actionable insights.
Ambitious and agile learner: A fast, curious learner who is eager to independently explore and pick up new datasets, technologies and methodologies to innovate with purpose.
Organised & adaptable: Highly organised with the ability to manage multiple concurrent tasks.
Collaborative team player: A reliable team player with a "team-first" mindset who thrives in a highly collaborative and supportive environment.
Financial Services/Pensions experience (Desirable): Experience within financial services is desirable; exposure to the pensions industry is a plus.


Technical Skills


Advanced SQL (Essential): Extensive experience of writing, troubleshooting, and optimising complex queries.
BI & Data Visualisation (Essential - Tableau Preferred): Strong experience building impactful dashboards and reports in a modern BI tool, with a preference for Tableau.
dbt (Desirable): Experience with dbt for data modeling, transformation, and managing the analytics layer.
Python (Desirable): Experience using Python for data manipulation, advanced analytics or automation.
Git / GitHub (Desirable): experience using version control for managing code, collaborating with others, and reviewing changes.


Who We Are

We work in partnerships with governments and financial institutions in the UK and internationally. Our cloud-native digital platform is revolutionising how people around the world think about, and save for, their retirement.

At heart, we’re a financial technology business. What we do is all about innovation, and using the power of digital change to put the customer first. Our Engineers will tell you that working at Smart gives you the opportunity to play your part in developing world-class technological solutions, working with – and learning from – like-minded people.

You’ll also find that, across our business, our colleagues love Smart’s culture, and how what we do means better financial outcomes for savers. That feels worthwhile, and it means that what we do, collectively, goes way beyond the nine to five of a typical working day.

Don’t just take our word for it – you can see what our colleagues say about working at Smart on LinkedIn Life and Glassdoor.

Benefits

At Smart, one of the eight principles we work to is “We want happy and good people in our team”. We created a list of benefits that helps us achieve this goal:


25 days’ holiday per year, increasing with length of service.
£500 annual training budget to spend on your professional development.
Extensive private healthcare, including dental, eyecare and EAP.
Enhanced sick leave (three months’ pay per year).
Enhanced maternity and paternity (maternity – 6 months fully paid/paternity – 3 weeks fully paid).
Death in service insurance cover.
Fully-paid five-week sabbatical after five years of employment.
In office wellbeing, such as manicures, massages and barbers.
Smart employees also enjoy a 50% discount on orders from our sister company Arena Flowers, Britain's most ethical florist. They offer unique hand-tied bouquets, luxury flowers, letterbox flowers, plants and gifts to spend on friends and loved ones or even for yourself.


At Smart, we are committed to creating an inclusive and equitable workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to do their best work.

We believe that diverse perspectives help us lead the way in transforming retirement, savings, and financial wellbeing. We welcome differences in background, experience, thinking, and identity, and we recognise that innovation is strongest when it is built on inclusion and fairness. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds and experiences and do not discriminate on the basis of any protected characteristic.

If you require any reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process or in the workplace, we encourage you to let us know - we are committed to supporting you.

HTML:
At Smart, our mission is to transform retirement, savings and financial wellbeing, across all generations, around the world.

THE ROLE

As part of the Data Analytics team, you will work closely with stakeholders to translate business challenges into data problems, delivering high-quality and clearly articulated analysis, reporting, and data-driven insights that support effective decision-making. We are a small team with big ambitions. We are building the foundations to enable our vision of maximising the value that our extensive dataset and modern data stack can generate for the business. Because we support the entire business, our data is varied and complex. As such, we are seeking a self-starter who is comfortable navigating ambiguity and independently finding solutions within a fast paced environment. If you match our ambitions, are passionate about creating value from data, and want to help drive the evolution of our data capabilities, then we would love to hear from you.

What You Will Do

Stakeholder Partnership & Communication

  • Define requirements: Partner with stakeholders across the company to translate business questions into clear analytics requirements and identify the right data points.
  • Data Translation & Storytelling: Explain analytical outputs and insights to stakeholders in clear, non-technical terms and, as required, build a narrative to support effective decision-making.
  • Drive data literacy: Support initiatives that enable stakeholders to self-serve data more effectively.

Analytics & Business Intelligence

  • Build BI solutions: Design, build, and maintain reports and dashboards (primarily using Tableau) used across the organisation, including Board-level KPI reporting.
  • Data Analysis & Insights: Manipulate and analyse complex data to answer business questions and identify trends, insights, and opportunities.

Data Modeling & Quality Assurance

  • Build robust data models: Develop and maintain optimised and reliable solutions in our data warehouse (Redshift) using tools like dbt, collaborating closely with Data Engineering where required.
  • Ensure trust & accuracy: Maintain data quality and reporting reliability, manage the impact of upstream changes, and support business-critical processes with direct regulatory or customer impact.
  • Peer review: Participate in code reviews and collaborative QA to validate the accuracy and value of our analytical output.

Continuous Improvement & Team Culture

  • Automate & optimise: Identify opportunities to improve efficiency through automation, better data processes and the responsible use of AI.
  • Be an active team player: Contribute your ideas to team discussions, retrospectives, and future planning to continuously improve how we work together and support the business.

Who We Are Looking For

The skills, experience, and aptitudes we are looking for are listed below but please don’t be discouraged from applying if you don’t meet every single one of these criteria – having a ‘can do’ attitude is sometimes more important than being able to tick every box:

Experience & Attributes

  • Proven track record: 3+ years of experience in data analysis, with a focus on data manipulation, reporting and insight analytics.
  • Stakeholder partnership: A proven ability to work with stakeholders to deeply understand business problems, translate requirements into effective analytics solutions, and advise on best approaches.
  • Data storytelling & coaching: An effective technical communicator who can translate complex data for non-technical audiences and actively coach stakeholders to enhance overall data maturity.
  • Analytical curiosity: Inquisitive by nature, with sharp attention to detail and a natural drive to look beyond the surface numbers to uncover actionable insights.
  • Ambitious and agile learner: A fast, curious learner who is eager to independently explore and pick up new datasets, technologies and methodologies to innovate with purpose.
  • Organised & adaptable: Highly organised with the ability to manage multiple concurrent tasks.
  • Collaborative team player: A reliable team player with a "team-first" mindset who thrives in a highly collaborative and supportive environment.
  • Financial Services/Pensions experience (Desirable): Experience within financial services is desirable; exposure to the pensions industry is a plus.

Technical Skills

  • Advanced SQL (Essential): Extensive experience of writing, troubleshooting, and optimising complex queries.
  • BI & Data Visualisation (Essential - Tableau Preferred): Strong experience building impactful dashboards and reports in a modern BI tool, with a preference for Tableau.
  • dbt (Desirable): Experience with dbt for data modeling, transformation, and managing the analytics layer.
  • Python (Desirable): Experience using Python for data manipulation, advanced analytics or automation.
  • Git / GitHub (Desirable): experience using version control for managing code, collaborating with others, and reviewing changes.

Who We Are

We work in partnerships with governments and financial institutions in the UK and internationally. Our cloud-native digital platform is revolutionising how people around the world think about, and save for, their retirement.

At heart, we’re a financial technology business. What we do is all about innovation, and using the power of digital change to put the customer first. Our Engineers will tell you that working at Smart gives you the opportunity to play your part in developing world-class technological solutions, working with – and learning from – like-minded people.

You’ll also find that, across our business, our colleagues love Smart’s culture, and how what we do means better financial outcomes for savers. That feels worthwhile, and it means that what we do, collectively, goes way beyond the nine to five of a typical working day.

Don’t just take our word for it – you can see what our colleagues say about working at Smart on LinkedIn Life and Glassdoor.

Benefits

At Smart, one of the eight principles we work to is “We want happy and good people in our team”. We created a list of benefits that helps us achieve this goal:

  • 25 days’ holiday per year, increasing with length of service.
  • £500 annual training budget to spend on your professional development.
  • Extensive private healthcare, including dental, eyecare and EAP.
  • Enhanced sick leave (three months’ pay per year).
  • Enhanced maternity and paternity (maternity – 6 months fully paid/paternity – 3 weeks fully paid).
  • Death in service insurance cover.
  • Fully-paid five-week sabbatical after five years of employment.
  • In office wellbeing, such as manicures, massages and barbers.
  • Smart employees also enjoy a 50% discount on orders from our sister company Arena Flowers, Britain's most ethical florist. They offer unique hand-tied bouquets, luxury flowers, letterbox flowers, plants and gifts to spend on friends and loved ones or even for yourself.

At Smart, we are committed to creating an inclusive and equitable workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to do their best work.

We believe that diverse perspectives help us lead the way in transforming retirement, savings, and financial wellbeing. We welcome differences in background, experience, thinking, and identity, and we recognise that innovation is strongest when it is built on inclusion and fairness. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds and experiences and do not discriminate on the basis of any protected characteristic.

If you require any reasonable adjustments during the recruitment process or in the workplace, we encourage you to let us know - we are committed to supporting you.

We think Smart is an awesome place to work. If it sounds like somewhere you’d like to work, too, and if you’re ready to play your part in our continued success in the future, then naturally we’d love to meet you.

Our mission is to transform retirement, savings and financial wellbeing, across all generations, around the world.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst (Freelance, Outside IR35, 1 month initial ) — Fashion Retail

London (Hybrid, mainly remote)




We're supporting a PE-backed fashion retail client on a freelance basis to help bring clarity to their inventory data.




The client's inventory data currently sits across around ten different spreadsheets with no single agreed source of truth. We're looking for a sharp, personable Data Analyst to get under the hood, work directly with the client's team, and build a clean, centralised baseline of their current inventory position.




What you'll be doing:




Working closely with client stakeholders to identify the source of truth across multiple spreadsheets
Asking the right questions to untangle inconsistent, messy data
Using Excel (and ideally AI tools) to build a clear, centralised view of inventory status
Bringing structure and clarity — this is not a data engineering or system-building role




What we're looking for:




Strong Excel skills -non-negotiable
Comfortable using AI tools to speed up data wrangling
Confident, personable, and comfortable working with non-technical, client-side stakeholders
Patient, low-ego, and happy to dig through messy spreadsheets to get to the answer
Fashion retail experience is a bonus, not a must




The details:




Freelance engagement, outside IR35
London-based, hybrid working — mainly remote
Single-stage interview process — moving quickly, with slots available this week

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Manchester, UK

Contract




We’re Hiring a Data Analyst contractor based in Manchester to support our UKPI business, in CDAO Europe. The focus for this analyst will be supporting UKPI's strategic priorities and foundational enables (namely launching new offers and building unified data platforms), as well as our BAU activities (client segmentation for communications, dashboard updates, ad-hoc analysis requests).

Your responsibilities:

Collect, clean, validate, and analyze large datasets from multiple sources to identify trends, patterns, and business insights.
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and visualizations using tools such as Power BI, Tableau, Excel, or other BI platforms.
Translate business requirements into analytical solutions and actionable recommendations.
Conduct exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify opportunities for process improvements and business growth.
Design and execute ad hoc analyses to support strategic and operational decision-making.
Monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) and provide regular performance reports to stakeholders.
Collaborate with business teams, product owners, and management to understand reporting and analytical needs.
Develop and maintain SQL queries, stored procedures, and data models for efficient data retrieval.
Identify data quality issues and work with relevant teams to implement corrective actions.
Support forecasting, trend analysis, and predictive modeling initiatives where applicable.
Ensure compliance with data governance, security, and privacy standards.
Document data definitions, methodologies, and analytical processes.
Present findings and recommendations effectively to technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Continuously improve reporting processes through automation and optimization techniques.




Your Profile

Essential skills:

Solid experience in Data Analytics, including data querying (SQL, Python) and data visualisation (Tableau, PowerBI)
Strong quantification skills (mathematics, behavioural modelling of averages, deviations, outlier detection, etc.)
Problem-solving expertise (ability to work with partners to understand problems to be solved, digest requirements, and turn them into data solutions to generate insights)
Ability to collaborate with a range of cross-functional teams across business and technology (personable, strong verbal and written communication skills, good facilitation experience)
Proactive, engaged, ‘get stuff done’ mindset, and a fast learner




Desirable skills/knowledge/experience:

SQL, Python, Tableau, PowerBI
